dc-testament/dc/76.51: They are they who received the testimony of Jesus, and believed on his name and were baptized after the manner of his burial, being buried in the water in his name, and this according to the commandment which he has given—
dc-testament/dc/76.52: That by keeping the commandments they might be washed and cleansed from all their sins, and receive the Holy Spirit by the laying on of the hands of him who is ordained and sealed unto this power;
dc-testament/dc/76.53: And who overcome by faith, and are sealed by the Holy Spirit of promise, which the Father sheds forth upon all those who are just and true.
dc-testament/dc/76.54: They are they who are the church of the Firstborn.
dc-testament/dc/76.55: They are they into whose hands the Father has given all things—
dc-testament/dc/76.56: They are they who are priests and kings, who have received of his fulness, and of his glory;
dc-testament/dc/76.57: And are priests of the Most High, after the order of Melchizedek, which was after the order of Enoch, which was after the order of the Only Begotten Son.
dc-testament/dc/76.58: Wherefore, as it is written, they are gods, even the sons of God—
dc-testament/dc/76.59: Wherefore, all things are theirs, whether life or death, or things present, or things to come, all are theirs and they are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s.
dc-testament/dc/76.60: And they shall overcome all things.
dc-testament/dc/76.61: Wherefore, let no man glory in man, but rather let him glory in God, who shall subdue all enemies under his feet.
dc-testament/dc/76.62: These shall dwell in the presence of God and his Christ forever and ever.
dc-testament/dc/76.63: These are they whom he shall bring with him, when he shall come in the clouds of heaven to reign on the earth over his people.
dc-testament/dc/76.64: These are they who shall have part in the first resurrection.
dc-testament/dc/76.65: These are they who shall come forth in the resurrection of the just.
dc-testament/dc/76.66: These are they who are come unto Mount Zion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly place, the holiest of all.
dc-testament/dc/76.67: These are they who have come to an innumerable company of angels, to the general assembly and church of Enoch, and of the Firstborn.
dc-testament/dc/76.68: These are they whose names are written in heaven, where God and Christ are the judge of all.
dc-testament/dc/76.69: These are they who are just men made perfect through Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, who wrought out this perfect atonement through the shedding of his own blood.
dc-testament/dc/76.70: These are they whose bodies are celestial, whose glory is that of the sun, even the glory of God, the highest of all, whose glory the sun of the firmament is written of as being typical.
dc-testament/dc/76.71: And again, we saw the terrestrial world, and behold and lo, these are they who are of the terrestrial, whose glory differs from that of the church of the Firstborn who have received the fulness of the Father, even as that of the moon differs from the sun in the firmament.
dc-testament/dc/76.72: Behold, these are they who died without law;
dc-testament/dc/76.73: And also they who are the spirits of men kept in prison, whom the Son visited, and preached the gospel unto them, that they might be judged according to men in the flesh;
dc-testament/dc/76.74: Who received not the testimony of Jesus in the flesh, but afterwards received it.
dc-testament/dc/76.75: These are they who are honorable men of the earth, who were blinded by the craftiness of men.
dc-testament/dc/76.76: These are they who receive of his glory, but not of his fulness.
dc-testament/dc/76.77: These are they who receive of the presence of the Son, but not of the fulness of the Father.
dc-testament/dc/76.78: Wherefore, they are bodies terrestrial, and not bodies celestial, and differ in glory as the moon differs from the sun.
dc-testament/dc/76.79: These are they who are not valiant in the testimony of Jesus; wherefore, they obtain not the crown over the kingdom of our God.
dc-testament/dc/76.80: And now this is the end of the vision which we saw of the terrestrial, that the Lord commanded us to write while we were yet in the Spirit.
dc-testament/dc/76.81: And again, we saw the glory of the telestial, which glory is that of the lesser, even as the glory of the stars differs from that of the glory of the moon in the firmament.
dc-testament/dc/76.82: These are they who received not the gospel of Christ, neither the testimony of Jesus.
dc-testament/dc/76.83: These are they who deny not the Holy Spirit.
dc-testament/dc/76.84: These are they who are thrust down to hell.
dc-testament/dc/76.85: These are they who shall not be redeemed from the devil until the last resurrection, until the Lord, even Christ the Lamb, shall have finished his work.
dc-testament/dc/76.86: These are they who receive not of his fulness in the eternal world, but of the Holy Spirit through the ministration of the terrestrial;
dc-testament/dc/76.87: And the terrestrial through the ministration of the celestial.
dc-testament/dc/76.88: And also the telestial receive it of the administering of angels who are appointed to minister for them, or who are appointed to be ministering spirits for them; for they shall be heirs of salvation.
dc-testament/dc/76.89: And thus we saw, in the heavenly vision, the glory of the telestial, which surpasses all understanding;
dc-testament/dc/76.90: And no man knows it except him to whom God has revealed it.
dc-testament/dc/76.91: And thus we saw the glory of the terrestrial which excels in all things the glory of the telestial, even in glory, and in power, and in might, and in dominion.
dc-testament/dc/76.92: And thus we saw the glory of the celestial, which excels in all things—where God, even the Father, reigns upon his throne forever and ever;
dc-testament/dc/76.93: Before whose throne all things bow in humble reverence, and give him glory forever and ever.
dc-testament/dc/76.94: They who dwell in his presence are the church of the Firstborn; and they see as they are seen, and know as they are known, having received of his fulness and of his grace;
dc-testament/dc/76.95: And he makes them equal in power, and in might, and in dominion.
dc-testament/dc/76.96: And the glory of the celestial is one, even as the glory of the sun is one.
dc-testament/dc/76.97: And the glory of the terrestrial is one, even as the glory of the moon is one.
dc-testament/dc/76.98: And the glory of the telestial is one, even as the glory of the stars is one; for as one star differs from another star in glory, even so differs one from another in glory in the telestial world;
dc-testament/dc/76.99: For these are they who are of Paul, and of Apollos, and of Cephas.
dc-testament/dc/76.100: These are they who say they are some of one and some of another—some of Christ and some of John, and some of Moses, and some of Elias, and some of Esaias, and some of Isaiah, and some of Enoch;
dc-testament/dc/76.101: But received not the gospel, neither the testimony of Jesus, neither the prophets, neither the everlasting covenant.
dc-testament/dc/76.102: Last of all, these all are they who will not be gathered with the saints, to be caught up unto the church of the Firstborn, and received into the cloud.
dc-testament/dc/76.103: These are they who are liars, and sorcerers, and adulterers, and whoremongers, and whosoever loves and makes a lie.
dc-testament/dc/76.104: These are they who suffer the wrath of God on earth.
dc-testament/dc/76.105: These are they who suffer the vengeance of eternal fire.
dc-testament/dc/76.106: These are they who are cast down to hell and suffer the wrath of Almighty God, until the fulness of times, when Christ shall have subdued all enemies under his feet, and shall have perfected his work;
dc-testament/dc/76.107: When he shall deliver up the kingdom, and present it unto the Father, spotless, saying: I have overcome and have trodden the wine-press alone, even the wine-press of the fierceness of the wrath of Almighty God.
dc-testament/dc/76.108: Then shall he be crowned with the crown of his glory, to sit on the throne of his power to reign forever and ever.
dc-testament/dc/76.109: But behold, and lo, we saw the glory and the inhabitants of the telestial world, that they were as innumerable as the stars in the firmament of heaven, or as the sand upon the seashore;
dc-testament/dc/76.110: And heard the voice of the Lord saying: These all shall bow the knee, and every tongue shall confess to him who sits upon the throne forever and ever;
dc-testament/dc/76.111: For they shall be judged according to their works, and every man shall receive according to his own works, his own dominion, in the mansions which are prepared;
dc-testament/dc/76.112: And they shall be servants of the Most High; but where God and Christ dwell they cannot come, worlds without end.
dc-testament/dc/76.113: This is the end of the vision which we saw, which we were commanded to write while we were yet in the Spirit.
dc-testament/dc/76.114: But great and marvelous are the works of the Lord, and the mysteries of his kingdom which he showed unto us, which surpass all understanding in glory, and in might, and in dominion;
dc-testament/dc/76.115: Which he commanded us we should not write while we were yet in the Spirit, and are not lawful for man to utter;
dc-testament/dc/76.116: Neither is man capable to make them known, for they are only to be seen and understood by the power of the Holy Spirit, which God bestows on those who love him, and purify themselves before him;
dc-testament/dc/76.117: To whom he grants this privilege of seeing and knowing for themselves;
dc-testament/dc/76.118: That through the power and manifestation of the Spirit, while in the flesh, they may be able to bear his presence in the world of glory.
dc-testament/dc/76.119: And to God and the Lamb be glory, and honor, and dominion forever and ever. Amen.

dc-testament/dc/84.1: A revelation of Jesus Christ unto his servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and six elders, as they united their hearts and lifted their voices on high.
dc-testament/dc/84.2: Yea, the word of the Lord concerning his church, established in the last days for the restoration of his people, as he has spoken by the mouth of his prophets, and for the gathering of his saints to stand upon Mount Zion, which shall be the city of New Jerusalem.
dc-testament/dc/84.3: Which city shall be built, beginning at the temple lot, which is appointed by the finger of the Lord, in the western boundaries of the State of Missouri, and dedicated by the hand of Joseph Smith, Jun., and others with whom the Lord was well pleased.
dc-testament/dc/84.4: Verily this is the word of the Lord, that the city New Jerusalem shall be built by the gathering of the saints, beginning at this place, even the place of the temple, which temple shall be reared in this generation.
dc-testament/dc/84.5: For verily this generation shall not all pass away until an house shall be built unto the Lord, and a cloud shall rest upon it, which cloud shall be even the glory of the Lord, which shall fill the house.
dc-testament/dc/84.6: And the sons of Moses, according to the Holy Priesthood which he received under the hand of his father-in-law, Jethro;
dc-testament/dc/84.7: And Jethro received it under the hand of Caleb;
dc-testament/dc/84.8: And Caleb received it under the hand of Elihu;
dc-testament/dc/84.9: And Elihu under the hand of Jeremy;
dc-testament/dc/84.10: And Jeremy under the hand of Gad;
dc-testament/dc/84.11: And Gad under the hand of Esaias;
dc-testament/dc/84.12: And Esaias received it under the hand of God.
dc-testament/dc/84.13: Esaias also lived in the days of Abraham, and was blessed of him—
dc-testament/dc/84.14: Which Abraham received the priesthood from Melchizedek, who received it through the lineage of his fathers, even till Noah;
dc-testament/dc/84.15: And from Noah till Enoch, through the lineage of their fathers;
dc-testament/dc/84.16: And from Enoch to Abel, who was slain by the conspiracy of his brother, who received the priesthood by the commandments of God, by the hand of his father Adam, who was the first man—
dc-testament/dc/84.17: Which priesthood continueth in the church of God in all generations, and is without beginning of days or end of years.
dc-testament/dc/84.18: And the Lord confirmed a priesthood also upon Aaron and his seed, throughout all their generations, which priesthood also continueth and abideth forever with the priesthood which is after the holiest order of God.
dc-testament/dc/84.19: And this greater priesthood administereth the gospel and holdeth the key of the mysteries of the kingdom, even the key of the knowledge of God.
dc-testament/dc/84.20: Therefore, in the ordinances thereof, the power of godliness is manifest.
dc-testament/dc/84.21: And without the ordinances thereof, and the authority of the priesthood, the power of godliness is not manifest unto men in the flesh;
dc-testament/dc/84.22: For without this no man can see the face of God, even the Father, and live.
dc-testament/dc/84.23: Now this Moses plainly taught to the children of Israel in the wilderness, and sought diligently to sanctify his people that they might behold the face of God;
dc-testament/dc/84.24: But they hardened their hearts and could not endure his presence; therefore, the Lord in his wrath, for his anger was kindled against them, swore that they should not enter into his rest while in the wilderness, which rest is the fulness of his glory.
dc-testament/dc/84.25: Therefore, he took Moses out of their midst, and the Holy Priesthood also;
dc-testament/dc/84.26: And the lesser priesthood continued, which priesthood holdeth the key of the ministering of angels and the preparatory gospel;
dc-testament/dc/84.27: Which gospel is the gospel of repentance and of baptism, and the remission of sins, and the law of carnal commandments, which the Lord in his wrath caused to continue with the house of Aaron among the children of Israel until John, whom God raised up, being filled with the Holy Ghost from his mother’s womb.
dc-testament/dc/84.28: For he was baptized while he was yet in his childhood, and was ordained by the angel of God at the time he was eight days old unto this power, to overthrow the kingdom of the Jews, and to make straight the way of the Lord before the face of his people, to prepare them for the coming of the Lord, in whose hand is given all power.
dc-testament/dc/84.29: And again, the offices of elder and bishop are necessary appendages belonging unto the high priesthood.
dc-testament/dc/84.30: And again, the offices of teacher and deacon are necessary appendages belonging to the lesser priesthood, which priesthood was confirmed upon Aaron and his sons.
dc-testament/dc/84.31: Therefore, as I said concerning the sons of Moses—for the sons of Moses and also the sons of Aaron shall offer an acceptable offering and sacrifice in the house of the Lord, which house shall be built unto the Lord in this generation, upon the consecrated spot as I have appointed—
dc-testament/dc/84.32: And the sons of Moses and of Aaron shall be filled with the glory of the Lord, upon Mount Zion in the Lord’s house, whose sons are ye; and also many whom I have called and sent forth to build up my church.
dc-testament/dc/84.33: For whoso is faithful unto the obtaining these two priesthoods of which I have spoken, and the magnifying their calling, are sanctified by the Spirit unto the renewing of their bodies.
dc-testament/dc/84.34: They become the sons of Moses and of Aaron and the seed of Abraham, and the church and kingdom, and the elect of God.
dc-testament/dc/84.35: And also all they who receive this priesthood receive me, saith the Lord;
dc-testament/dc/84.36: For he that receiveth my servants receiveth me;
dc-testament/dc/84.37: And he that receiveth me receiveth my Father;
dc-testament/dc/84.38: And he that receiveth my Father receiveth my Father’s kingdom; therefore all that my Father hath shall be given unto him.
dc-testament/dc/84.39: And this is according to the oath and covenant which belongeth to the priesthood.
dc-testament/dc/84.40: Therefore, all those who receive the priesthood, receive this oath and covenant of my Father, which he cannot break, neither can it be moved.
dc-testament/dc/84.41: But whoso breaketh this covenant after he hath received it, and altogether turneth therefrom, shall not have forgiveness of sins in this world nor in the world to come.
dc-testament/dc/84.42: And wo unto all those who come not unto this priesthood which ye have received, which I now confirm upon you who are present this day, by mine own voice out of the heavens; and even I have given the heavenly hosts and mine angels charge concerning you.
dc-testament/dc/84.43: And I now give unto you a commandment to beware concerning yourselves, to give diligent heed to the words of eternal life.
dc-testament/dc/84.44: For you shall live by every word that proceedeth forth from the mouth of God.
dc-testament/dc/84.45: For the word of the Lord is truth, and whatsoever is truth is light, and whatsoever is light is Spirit, even the Spirit of Jesus Christ.
dc-testament/dc/84.46: And the Spirit giveth light to every man that cometh into the world; and the Spirit enlighteneth every man through the world, that hearkeneth to the voice of the Spirit.
dc-testament/dc/84.47: And every one that hearkeneth to the voice of the Spirit cometh unto God, even the Father.
dc-testament/dc/84.48: And the Father teacheth him of the covenant which he has renewed and confirmed upon you, which is confirmed upon you for your sakes, and not for your sakes only, but for the sake of the whole world.
dc-testament/dc/84.49: And the whole world lieth in sin, and groaneth under darkness and under the bondage of sin.
dc-testament/dc/84.50: And by this you may know they are under the bondage of sin, because they come not unto me.
dc-testament/dc/84.51: For whoso cometh not unto me is under the bondage of sin.
dc-testament/dc/84.52: And whoso receiveth not my voice is not acquainted with my voice, and is not of me.
dc-testament/dc/84.53: And by this you may know the righteous from the wicked, and that the whole world groaneth under sin and darkness even now.
dc-testament/dc/84.54: And your minds in times past have been darkened because of unbelief, and because you have treated lightly the things you have received—
dc-testament/dc/84.55: Which vanity and unbelief have brought the whole church under condemnation.
dc-testament/dc/84.56: And this condemnation resteth upon the children of Zion, even all.
dc-testament/dc/84.57: And they shall remain under this condemnation until they repent and remember the new covenant, even the Book of Mormon and the former commandments which I have given them, not only to say, but to do according to that which I have written—
dc-testament/dc/84.58: That they may bring forth fruit meet for their Father’s kingdom; otherwise there remaineth a scourge and judgment to be poured out upon the children of Zion.
dc-testament/dc/84.59: For shall the children of the kingdom pollute my holy land? Verily, I say unto you, Nay.
dc-testament/dc/84.60: Verily, verily, I say unto you who now hear my words, which are my voice, blessed are ye inasmuch as you receive these things;
dc-testament/dc/84.61: For I will forgive you of your sins with this commandment—that you remain steadfast in your minds in solemnity and the spirit of prayer, in bearing testimony to all the world of those things which are communicated unto you.
dc-testament/dc/84.62: Therefore, go ye into all the world; and unto whatsoever place ye cannot go ye shall send, that the testimony may go from you into all the world unto every creature.
dc-testament/dc/84.63: And as I said unto mine apostles, even so I say unto you, for you are mine apostles, even God’s high priests; ye are they whom my Father hath given me; ye are my friends;
dc-testament/dc/84.64: Therefore, as I said unto mine apostles I say unto you again, that every soul who believeth on your words, and is baptized by water for the remission of sins, shall receive the Holy Ghost.
dc-testament/dc/84.65: And these signs shall follow them that believe—
dc-testament/dc/84.66: In my name they shall do many wonderful works;
dc-testament/dc/84.67: In my name they shall cast out devils;
dc-testament/dc/84.68: In my name they shall heal the sick;
dc-testament/dc/84.69: In my name they shall open the eyes of the blind, and unstop the ears of the deaf;
dc-testament/dc/84.70: And the tongue of the dumb shall speak;
dc-testament/dc/84.71: And if any man shall administer poison unto them it shall not hurt them;
dc-testament/dc/84.72: And the poison of a serpent shall not have power to harm them.
dc-testament/dc/84.73: But a commandment I give unto them, that they shall not boast themselves of these things, neither speak them before the world; for these things are given unto you for your profit and for salvation.
dc-testament/dc/84.74: Verily, verily, I say unto you, they who believe not on your words, and are not baptized in water in my name, for the remission of their sins, that they may receive the Holy Ghost, shall be damned, and shall not come into my Father’s kingdom where my Father and I am.
dc-testament/dc/84.75: And this revelation unto you, and commandment, is in force from this very hour upon all the world, and the gospel is unto all who have not received it.
dc-testament/dc/84.76: But, verily I say unto all those to whom the kingdom has been given—from you it must be preached unto them, that they shall repent of their former evil works; for they are to be upbraided for their evil hearts of unbelief, and your brethren in Zion for their rebellion against you at the time I sent you.
dc-testament/dc/84.77: And again I say unto you, my friends, for from henceforth I shall call you friends, it is expedient that I give unto you this commandment, that ye become even as my friends in days when I was with them, traveling to preach the gospel in my power;
dc-testament/dc/84.78: For I suffered them not to have purse or scrip, neither two coats.
dc-testament/dc/84.79: Behold, I send you out to prove the world, and the laborer is worthy of his hire.
dc-testament/dc/84.80: And any man that shall go and preach this gospel of the kingdom, and fail not to continue faithful in all things, shall not be weary in mind, neither darkened, neither in body, limb, nor joint; and a hair of his head shall not fall to the ground unnoticed. And they shall not go hungry, neither athirst.
dc-testament/dc/84.81: Therefore, take ye no thought for the morrow, for what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink, or wherewithal ye shall be clothed.
dc-testament/dc/84.82: For, consider the lilies of the field, how they grow, they toil not, neither do they spin; and the kingdoms of the world, in all their glory, are not arrayed like one of these.
dc-testament/dc/84.83: For your Father, who is in heaven, knoweth that you have need of all these things.
dc-testament/dc/84.84: Therefore, let the morrow take thought for the things of itself.
dc-testament/dc/84.85: Neither take ye thought beforehand what ye shall say; but treasure up in your minds continually the words of life, and it shall be given you in the very hour that portion that shall be meted unto every man.
dc-testament/dc/84.86: Therefore, let no man among you, for this commandment is unto all the faithful who are called of God in the church unto the ministry, from this hour take purse or scrip, that goeth forth to proclaim this gospel of the kingdom.
dc-testament/dc/84.87: Behold, I send you out to reprove the world of all their unrighteous deeds, and to teach them of a judgment which is to come.
dc-testament/dc/84.88: And whoso receiveth you, there I will be also, for I will go before your face. I will be on your right hand and on your left, and my Spirit shall be in your hearts, and mine angels round about you, to bear you up.
dc-testament/dc/84.89: Whoso receiveth you receiveth me; and the same will feed you, and clothe you, and give you money.
dc-testament/dc/84.90: And he who feeds you, or clothes you, or gives you money, shall in nowise lose his reward.
dc-testament/dc/84.91: And he that doeth not these things is not my disciple; by this you may know my disciples.
dc-testament/dc/84.92: He that receiveth you not, go away from him alone by yourselves, and cleanse your feet even with water, pure water, whether in heat or in cold, and bear testimony of it unto your Father which is in heaven, and return not again unto that man.
dc-testament/dc/84.93: And in whatsoever village or city ye enter, do likewise.
dc-testament/dc/84.94: Nevertheless, search diligently and spare not; and wo unto that house, or that village or city that rejecteth you, or your words, or your testimony concerning me.
dc-testament/dc/84.95: Wo, I say again, unto that house, or that village or city that rejecteth you, or your words, or your testimony of me;
dc-testament/dc/84.96: For I, the Almighty, have laid my hands upon the nations, to scourge them for their wickedness.
dc-testament/dc/84.97: And plagues shall go forth, and they shall not be taken from the earth until I have completed my work, which shall be cut short in righteousness—
dc-testament/dc/84.98: Until all shall know me, who remain, even from the least unto the greatest, and shall be filled with the knowledge of the Lord, and shall see eye to eye, and shall lift up their voice, and with the voice together sing this new song, saying:
dc-testament/dc/84.99: The Lord hath brought again Zion;The Lord hath redeemed his people, Israel,According to the election of grace,Which was brought to pass by the faithAnd covenant of their fathers.
dc-testament/dc/84.100: The Lord hath redeemed his people;And Satan is bound and time is no longer.The Lord hath gathered all things in one.The Lord hath brought down Zion from above.The Lord hath brought up Zion from beneath.
dc-testament/dc/84.101: The earth hath travailed and brought forth her strength;And truth is established in her bowels;And the heavens have smiled upon her;And she is clothed with the glory of her God;For he stands in the midst of his people.
dc-testament/dc/84.102: Glory, and honor, and power, and might,Be ascribed to our God; for he is full of mercy,Justice, grace and truth, and peace,Forever and ever, Amen.
dc-testament/dc/84.103: And again, verily, verily, I say unto you, it is expedient that every man who goes forth to proclaim mine everlasting gospel, that inasmuch as they have families, and receive money by gift, that they should send it unto them or make use of it for their benefit, as the Lord shall direct them, for thus it seemeth me good.
dc-testament/dc/84.104: And let all those who have not families, who receive money, send it up unto the bishop in Zion, or unto the bishop in Ohio, that it may be consecrated for the bringing forth of the revelations and the printing thereof, and for establishing Zion.
dc-testament/dc/84.105: And if any man shall give unto any of you a coat, or a suit, take the old and cast it unto the poor, and go on your way rejoicing.
dc-testament/dc/84.106: And if any man among you be strong in the Spirit, let him take with him him that is weak, that he may be edified in all meekness, that he may become strong also.
dc-testament/dc/84.107: Therefore, take with you those who are ordained unto the lesser priesthood, and send them before you to make appointments, and to prepare the way, and to fill appointments that you yourselves are not able to fill.
dc-testament/dc/84.108: Behold, this is the way that mine apostles, in ancient days, built up my church unto me.
dc-testament/dc/84.109: Therefore, let every man stand in his own office, and labor in his own calling; and let not the head say unto the feet it hath no need of the feet; for without the feet how shall the body be able to stand?
dc-testament/dc/84.110: Also the body hath need of every member, that all may be edified together, that the system may be kept perfect.
dc-testament/dc/84.111: And behold, the high priests should travel, and also the elders, and also the lesser priests; but the deacons and teachers should be appointed to watch over the church, to be standing ministers unto the church.
dc-testament/dc/84.112: And the bishop, Newel K. Whitney, also should travel round about and among all the churches, searching after the poor to administer to their wants by humbling the rich and the proud.
dc-testament/dc/84.113: He should also employ an agent to take charge and to do his secular business as he shall direct.
dc-testament/dc/84.114: Nevertheless, let the bishop go unto the city of New York, also to the city of Albany, and also to the city of Boston, and warn the people of those cities with the sound of the gospel, with a loud voice, of the desolation and utter abolishment which await them if they do reject these things.
dc-testament/dc/84.115: For if they do reject these things the hour of their judgment is nigh, and their house shall be left unto them desolate.
dc-testament/dc/84.116: Let him trust in me and he shall not be confounded; and a hair of his head shall not fall to the ground unnoticed.
dc-testament/dc/84.117: And verily I say unto you, the rest of my servants, go ye forth as your circumstances shall permit, in your several callings, unto the great and notable cities and villages, reproving the world in righteousness of all their unrighteous and ungodly deeds, setting forth clearly and understandingly the desolation of abomination in the last days.
dc-testament/dc/84.118: For, with you saith the Lord Almighty, I will rend their kingdoms; I will not only shake the earth, but the starry heavens shall tremble.
dc-testament/dc/84.119: For I, the Lord, have put forth my hand to exert the powers of heaven; ye cannot see it now, yet a little while and ye shall see it, and know that I am, and that I will come and reign with my people.
dc-testament/dc/84.120: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. Amen.

dc-testament/dc/88.1: Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you who have assembled yourselves together to receive his will concerning you:
dc-testament/dc/88.2: Behold, this is pleasing unto your Lord, and the angels rejoice over you; the alms of your prayers have come up into the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth, and are recorded in the book of the names of the sanctified, even them of the celestial world.
dc-testament/dc/88.3: Wherefore, I now send upon you another Comforter, even upon you my friends, that it may abide in your hearts, even the Holy Spirit of promise; which other Comforter is the same that I promised unto my disciples, as is recorded in the testimony of John.
dc-testament/dc/88.4: This Comforter is the promise which I give unto you of eternal life, even the glory of the celestial kingdom;
dc-testament/dc/88.5: Which glory is that of the church of the Firstborn, even of God, the holiest of all, through Jesus Christ his Son—
dc-testament/dc/88.6: He that ascended up on high, as also he descended below all things, in that he comprehended all things, that he might be in all and through all things, the light of truth;
dc-testament/dc/88.7: Which truth shineth. This is the light of Christ. As also he is in the sun, and the light of the sun, and the power thereof by which it was made.
dc-testament/dc/88.8: As also he is in the moon, and is the light of the moon, and the power thereof by which it was made;
dc-testament/dc/88.9: As also the light of the stars, and the power thereof by which they were made;
dc-testament/dc/88.10: And the earth also, and the power thereof, even the earth upon which you stand.
dc-testament/dc/88.11: And the light which shineth, which giveth you light, is through him who enlighteneth your eyes, which is the same light that quickeneth your understandings;
dc-testament/dc/88.12: Which light proceedeth forth from the presence of God to fill the immensity of space—
dc-testament/dc/88.13: The light which is in all things, which giveth life to all things, which is the law by which all things are governed, even the power of God who sitteth upon his throne, who is in the bosom of eternity, who is in the midst of all things.
dc-testament/dc/88.14: Now, verily I say unto you, that through the redemption which is made for you is brought to pass the resurrection from the dead.
dc-testament/dc/88.15: And the spirit and the body are the soul of man.
dc-testament/dc/88.16: And the resurrection from the dead is the redemption of the soul.
dc-testament/dc/88.17: And the redemption of the soul is through him that quickeneth all things, in whose bosom it is decreed that the poor and the meek of the earth shall inherit it.
dc-testament/dc/88.18: Therefore, it must needs be sanctified from all unrighteousness, that it may be prepared for the celestial glory;
dc-testament/dc/88.19: For after it hath filled the measure of its creation, it shall be crowned with glory, even with the presence of God the Father;
dc-testament/dc/88.20: That bodies who are of the celestial kingdom may possess it forever and ever; for, for this intent was it made and created, and for this intent are they sanctified.
dc-testament/dc/88.21: And they who are not sanctified through the law which I have given unto you, even the law of Christ, must inherit another kingdom, even that of a terrestrial kingdom, or that of a telestial kingdom.
dc-testament/dc/88.22: For he who is not able to abide the law of a celestial kingdom cannot abide a celestial glory.
dc-testament/dc/88.23: And he who cannot abide the law of a terrestrial kingdom cannot abide a terrestrial glory.
dc-testament/dc/88.24: And he who cannot abide the law of a telestial kingdom cannot abide a telestial glory; therefore he is not meet for a kingdom of glory. Therefore he must abide a kingdom which is not a kingdom of glory.
dc-testament/dc/88.25: And again, verily I say unto you, the earth abideth the law of a celestial kingdom, for it filleth the measure of its creation, and transgresseth not the law—
dc-testament/dc/88.26: Wherefore, it shall be sanctified; yea, notwithstanding it shall die, it shall be quickened again, and shall abide the power by which it is quickened, and the righteous shall inherit it.
dc-testament/dc/88.27: For notwithstanding they die, they also shall rise again, a spiritual body.
dc-testament/dc/88.28: They who are of a celestial spirit shall receive the same body which was a natural body; even ye shall receive your bodies, and your glory shall be that glory by which your bodies are quickened.
dc-testament/dc/88.29: Ye who are quickened by a portion of the celestial glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.
dc-testament/dc/88.30: And they who are quickened by a portion of the terrestrial glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.
dc-testament/dc/88.31: And also they who are quickened by a portion of the telestial glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.
dc-testament/dc/88.32: And they who remain shall also be quickened; nevertheless, they shall return again to their own place, to enjoy that which they are willing to receive, because they were not willing to enjoy that which they might have received.
dc-testament/dc/88.33: For what doth it profit a man if a gift is bestowed upon him, and he receive not the gift? Behold, he rejoices not in that which is given unto him, neither rejoices in him who is the giver of the gift.
dc-testament/dc/88.34: And again, verily I say unto you, that which is governed by law is also preserved by law and perfected and sanctified by the same.
dc-testament/dc/88.35: That which breaketh a law, and abideth not by law, but seeketh to become a law unto itself, and willeth to abide in sin, and altogether abideth in sin, cannot be sanctified by law, neither by mercy, justice, nor judgment. Therefore, they must remain filthy still.
dc-testament/dc/88.36: All kingdoms have a law given;
dc-testament/dc/88.37: And there are many kingdoms; for there is no space in the which there is no kingdom; and there is no kingdom in which there is no space, either a greater or a lesser kingdom.
dc-testament/dc/88.38: And unto every kingdom is given a law; and unto every law there are certain bounds also and conditions.
dc-testament/dc/88.39: All beings who abide not in those conditions are not justified.
dc-testament/dc/88.40: For intelligence cleaveth unto intelligence; wisdom receiveth wisdom; truth embraceth truth; virtue loveth virtue; light cleaveth unto light; mercy hath compassion on mercy and claimeth her own; justice continueth its course and claimeth its own; judgment goeth before the face of him who sitteth upon the throne and governeth and executeth all things.
dc-testament/dc/88.41: He comprehendeth all things, and all things are before him, and all things are round about him; and he is above all things, and in all things, and is through all things, and is round about all things; and all things are by him, and of him, even God, forever and ever.
dc-testament/dc/88.42: And again, verily I say unto you, he hath given a law unto all things, by which they move in their times and their seasons;
dc-testament/dc/88.43: And their courses are fixed, even the courses of the heavens and the earth, which comprehend the earth and all the planets.
dc-testament/dc/88.44: And they give light to each other in their times and in their seasons, in their minutes, in their hours, in their days, in their weeks, in their months, in their years—all these are one year with God, but not with man.
dc-testament/dc/88.45: The earth rolls upon her wings, and the sun giveth his light by day, and the moon giveth her light by night, and the stars also give their light, as they roll upon their wings in their glory, in the midst of the power of God.
dc-testament/dc/88.46: Unto what shall I liken these kingdoms, that ye may understand?
dc-testament/dc/88.47: Behold, all these are kingdoms, and any man who hath seen any or the least of these hath seen God moving in his majesty and power.
dc-testament/dc/88.48: I say unto you, he hath seen him; nevertheless, he who came unto his own was not comprehended.
dc-testament/dc/88.49: The light shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehendeth it not; nevertheless, the day shall come when you shall comprehend even God, being quickened in him and by him.
dc-testament/dc/88.50: Then shall ye know that ye have seen me, that I am, and that I am the true light that is in you, and that you are in me; otherwise ye could not abound.
dc-testament/dc/88.51: Behold, I will liken these kingdoms unto a man having a field, and he sent forth his servants into the field to dig in the field.
dc-testament/dc/88.52: And he said unto the first: Go ye and labor in the field, and in the first hour I will come unto you, and ye shall behold the joy of my countenance.
dc-testament/dc/88.53: And he said unto the second: Go ye also into the field, and in the second hour I will visit you with the joy of my countenance.
dc-testament/dc/88.54: And also unto the third, saying: I will visit you;
dc-testament/dc/88.55: And unto the fourth, and so on unto the twelfth.
dc-testament/dc/88.56: And the lord of the field went unto the first in the first hour, and tarried with him all that hour, and he was made glad with the light of the countenance of his lord.
dc-testament/dc/88.57: And then he withdrew from the first that he might visit the second also, and the third, and the fourth, and so on unto the twelfth.
dc-testament/dc/88.58: And thus they all received the light of the countenance of their lord, every man in his hour, and in his time, and in his season—
dc-testament/dc/88.59: Beginning at the first, and so on unto the last, and from the last unto the first, and from the first unto the last;
dc-testament/dc/88.60: Every man in his own order, until his hour was finished, even according as his lord had commanded him, that his lord might be glorified in him, and he in his lord, that they all might be glorified.
dc-testament/dc/88.61: Therefore, unto this parable I will liken all these kingdoms, and the inhabitants thereof—every kingdom in its hour, and in its time, and in its season, even according to the decree which God hath made.
dc-testament/dc/88.62: And again, verily I say unto you, my friends, I leave these sayings with you to ponder in your hearts, with this commandment which I give unto you, that ye shall call upon me while I am near—
dc-testament/dc/88.63: Draw near unto me and I will draw near unto you; seek me diligently and ye shall find me; ask, and ye shall receive; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
dc-testament/dc/88.64: Whatsoever ye ask the Father in my name it shall be given unto you, that is expedient for you;
dc-testament/dc/88.65: And if ye ask anything that is not expedient for you, it shall turn unto your condemnation.
dc-testament/dc/88.66: Behold, that which you hear is as the voice of one crying in the wilderness—in the wilderness, because you cannot see him—my voice, because my voice is Spirit; my Spirit is truth; truth abideth and hath no end; and if it be in you it shall abound.
dc-testament/dc/88.67: And if your eye be single to my glory, your whole bodies shall be filled with light, and there shall be no darkness in you; and that body which is filled with light comprehendeth all things.
dc-testament/dc/88.68: Therefore, sanctify yourselves that your minds become single to God, and the days will come that you shall see him; for he will unveil his face unto you, and it shall be in his own time, and in his own way, and according to his own will.
dc-testament/dc/88.69: Remember the great and last promise which I have made unto you; cast away your idle thoughts and your excess of laughter far from you.
dc-testament/dc/88.70: Tarry ye, tarry ye in this place, and call a solemn assembly, even of those who are the first laborers in this last kingdom.
dc-testament/dc/88.71: And let those whom they have warned in their traveling call on the Lord, and ponder the warning in their hearts which they have received, for a little season.
dc-testament/dc/88.72: Behold, and lo, I will take care of your flocks, and will raise up elders and send unto them.
dc-testament/dc/88.73: Behold, I will hasten my work in its time.
dc-testament/dc/88.74: And I give unto you, who are the first laborers in this last kingdom, a commandment that you assemble yourselves together, and organize yourselves, and prepare yourselves, and sanctify yourselves; yea, purify your hearts, and cleanse your hands and your feet before me, that I may make you clean;
dc-testament/dc/88.75: That I may testify unto your Father, and your God, and my God, that you are clean from the blood of this wicked generation; that I may fulfil this promise, this great and last promise, which I have made unto you, when I will.
dc-testament/dc/88.76: Also, I give unto you a commandment that ye shall continue in prayer and fasting from this time forth.
dc-testament/dc/88.77: And I give unto you a commandment that you shall teach one another the doctrine of the kingdom.
dc-testament/dc/88.78: Teach ye diligently and my grace shall attend you, that you may be instructed more perfectly in theory, in principle, in doctrine, in the law of the gospel, in all things that pertain unto the kingdom of God, that are expedient for you to understand;
dc-testament/dc/88.79: Of things both in heaven and in the earth, and under the earth; things which have been, things which are, things which must shortly come to pass; things which are at home, things which are abroad; the wars and the perplexities of the nations, and the judgments which are on the land; and a knowledge also of countries and of kingdoms—
dc-testament/dc/88.80: That ye may be prepared in all things when I shall send you again to magnify the calling whereunto I have called you, and the mission with which I have commissioned you.
dc-testament/dc/88.81: Behold, I sent you out to testify and warn the people, and it becometh every man who hath been warned to warn his neighbor.
dc-testament/dc/88.82: Therefore, they are left without excuse, and their sins are upon their own heads.
dc-testament/dc/88.83: He that seeketh me early shall find me, and shall not be forsaken.
dc-testament/dc/88.84: Therefore, tarry ye, and labor diligently, that you may be perfected in your ministry to go forth among the Gentiles for the last time, as many as the mouth of the Lord shall name, to bind up the law and seal up the testimony, and to prepare the saints for the hour of judgment which is to come;
dc-testament/dc/88.85: That their souls may escape the wrath of God, the desolation of abomination which awaits the wicked, both in this world and in the world to come. Verily, I say unto you, let those who are not the first elders continue in the vineyard until the mouth of the Lord shall call them, for their time is not yet come; their garments are not clean from the blood of this generation.
dc-testament/dc/88.86: Abide ye in the liberty wherewith ye are made free; entangle not yourselves in sin, but let your hands be clean, until the Lord comes.
dc-testament/dc/88.87: For not many days hence and the earth shall tremble and reel to and fro as a drunken man; and the sun shall hide his face, and shall refuse to give light; and the moon shall be bathed in blood; and the stars shall become exceedingly angry, and shall cast themselves down as a fig that falleth from off a fig tree.
dc-testament/dc/88.88: And after your testimony cometh wrath and indignation upon the people.
dc-testament/dc/88.89: For after your testimony cometh the testimony of earthquakes, that shall cause groanings in the midst of her, and men shall fall upon the ground and shall not be able to stand.
dc-testament/dc/88.90: And also cometh the testimony of the voice of thunderings, and the voice of lightnings, and the voice of tempests, and the voice of the waves of the sea heaving themselves beyond their bounds.
dc-testament/dc/88.91: And all things shall be in commotion; and surely, men’s hearts shall fail them; for fear shall come upon all people.
dc-testament/dc/88.92: And angels shall fly through the midst of heaven, crying with a loud voice, sounding the trump of God, saying: Prepare ye, prepare ye, O inhabitants of the earth; for the judgment of our God is come. Behold, and lo, the Bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.
dc-testament/dc/88.93: And immediately there shall appear a great sign in heaven, and all people shall see it together.
dc-testament/dc/88.94: And another angel shall sound his trump, saying: That great church, the mother of abominations, that made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication, that persecuteth the saints of God, that shed their blood—she who sitteth upon many waters, and upon the islands of the sea—behold, she is the tares of the earth; she is bound in bundles; her bands are made strong, no man can loose them; therefore, she is ready to be burned. And he shall sound his trump both long and loud, and all nations shall hear it.
dc-testament/dc/88.95: And there shall be silence in heaven for the space of half an hour; and immediately after shall the curtain of heaven be unfolded, as a scroll is unfolded after it is rolled up, and the face of the Lord shall be unveiled;
dc-testament/dc/88.96: And the saints that are upon the earth, who are alive, shall be quickened and be caught up to meet him.
dc-testament/dc/88.97: And they who have slept in their graves shall come forth, for their graves shall be opened; and they also shall be caught up to meet him in the midst of the pillar of heaven—
dc-testament/dc/88.98: They are Christ’s, the first fruits, they who shall descend with him first, and they who are on the earth and in their graves, who are first caught up to meet him; and all this by the voice of the sounding of the trump of the angel of God.
dc-testament/dc/88.99: And after this another angel shall sound, which is the second trump; and then cometh the redemption of those who are Christ’s at his coming; who have received their part in that prison which is prepared for them, that they might receive the gospel, and be judged according to men in the flesh.
dc-testament/dc/88.100: And again, another trump shall sound, which is the third trump; and then come the spirits of men who are to be judged, and are found under condemnation;
dc-testament/dc/88.101: And these are the rest of the dead; and they live not again until the thousand years are ended, neither again, until the end of the earth.
dc-testament/dc/88.102: And another trump shall sound, which is the fourth trump, saying: There are found among those who are to remain until that great and last day, even the end, who shall remain filthy still.
dc-testament/dc/88.103: And another trump shall sound, which is the fifth trump, which is the fifth angel who committeth the everlasting gospel—flying through the midst of heaven, unto all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people;
dc-testament/dc/88.104: And this shall be the sound of his trump, saying to all people, both in heaven and in earth, and that are under the earth—for every ear shall hear it, and every knee shall bow, and every tongue shall confess, while they hear the sound of the trump, saying: Fear God, and give glory to him who sitteth upon the throne, forever and ever; for the hour of his judgment is come.
dc-testament/dc/88.105: And again, another angel shall sound his trump, which is the sixth angel, saying: She is fallen who made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication; she is fallen, is fallen!
dc-testament/dc/88.106: And again, another angel shall sound his trump, which is the seventh angel, saying: It is finished; it is finished! The Lamb of God hath overcome and trodden the wine-press alone, even the wine-press of the fierceness of the wrath of Almighty God.
dc-testament/dc/88.107: And then shall the angels be crowned with the glory of his might, and the saints shall be filled with his glory, and receive their inheritance and be made equal with him.
dc-testament/dc/88.108: And then shall the first angel again sound his trump in the ears of all living, and reveal the secret acts of men, and the mighty works of God in the first thousand years.
dc-testament/dc/88.109: And then shall the second angel sound his trump, and reveal the secret acts of men, and the thoughts and intents of their hearts, and the mighty works of God in the second thousand years—
dc-testament/dc/88.110: And so on, until the seventh angel shall sound his trump; and he shall stand forth upon the land and upon the sea, and swear in the name of him who sitteth upon the throne, that there shall be time no longer; and Satan shall be bound, that old serpent, who is called the devil, and shall not be loosed for the space of a thousand years.
dc-testament/dc/88.111: And then he shall be loosed for a little season, that he may gather together his armies.
dc-testament/dc/88.112: And Michael, the seventh angel, even the archangel, shall gather together his armies, even the hosts of heaven.
dc-testament/dc/88.113: And the devil shall gather together his armies; even the hosts of hell, and shall come up to battle against Michael and his armies.
dc-testament/dc/88.114: And then cometh the battle of the great God; and the devil and his armies shall be cast away into their own place, that they shall not have power over the saints any more at all.
dc-testament/dc/88.115: For Michael shall fight their battles, and shall overcome him who seeketh the throne of him who sitteth upon the throne, even the Lamb.
dc-testament/dc/88.116: This is the glory of God, and the sanctified; and they shall not any more see death.
dc-testament/dc/88.117: Therefore, verily I say unto you, my friends, call your solemn assembly, as I have commanded you.
dc-testament/dc/88.118: And as all have not faith, seek ye diligently and teach one another words of wisdom; yea, seek ye out of the best books words of wisdom; seek learning, even by study and also by faith.
dc-testament/dc/88.119: Organize yourselves; prepare every needful thing; and establish a house, even a house of prayer, a house of fasting, a house of faith, a house of learning, a house of glory, a house of order, a house of God;
dc-testament/dc/88.120: That your incomings may be in the name of the Lord; that your outgoings may be in the name of the Lord; that all your salutations may be in the name of the Lord, with uplifted hands unto the Most High.
dc-testament/dc/88.121: Therefore, cease from all your light speeches, from all laughter, from all your lustful desires, from all your pride and light-mindedness, and from all your wicked doings.
dc-testament/dc/88.122: Appoint among yourselves a teacher, and let not all be spokesmen at once; but let one speak at a time and let all listen unto his sayings, that when all have spoken that all may be edified of all, and that every man may have an equal privilege.
dc-testament/dc/88.123: See that ye love one another; cease to be covetous; learn to impart one to another as the gospel requires.
dc-testament/dc/88.124: Cease to be idle; cease to be unclean; cease to find fault one with another; cease to sleep longer than is needful; retire to thy bed early, that ye may not be weary; arise early, that your bodies and your minds may be invigorated.
dc-testament/dc/88.125: And above all things, clothe yourselves with the bond of charity, as with a mantle, which is the bond of perfectness and peace.
dc-testament/dc/88.126: Pray always, that ye may not faint, until I come. Behold, and lo, I will come quickly, and receive you unto myself. Amen.
dc-testament/dc/88.127: And again, the order of the house prepared for the presidency of the school of the prophets, established for their instruction in all things that are expedient for them, even for all the officers of the church, or in other words, those who are called to the ministry in the church, beginning at the high priests, even down to the deacons—
dc-testament/dc/88.128: And this shall be the order of the house of the presidency of the school: He that is appointed to be president, or teacher, shall be found standing in his place, in the house which shall be prepared for him.
dc-testament/dc/88.129: Therefore, he shall be first in the house of God, in a place that the congregation in the house may hear his words carefully and distinctly, not with loud speech.
dc-testament/dc/88.130: And when he cometh into the house of God, for he should be first in the house—behold, this is beautiful, that he may be an example—
dc-testament/dc/88.131: Let him offer himself in prayer upon his knees before God, in token or remembrance of the everlasting covenant.
dc-testament/dc/88.132: And when any shall come in after him, let the teacher arise, and, with uplifted hands to heaven, yea, even directly, salute his brother or brethren with these words:
dc-testament/dc/88.133: Art thou a brother or brethren? I salute you in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, in token or remembrance of the everlasting covenant, in which covenant I receive you to fellowship, in a determination that is fixed, immovable, and unchangeable, to be your friend and brother through the grace of God in the bonds of love, to walk in all the commandments of God blameless, in thanksgiving, forever and ever. Amen.
dc-testament/dc/88.134: And he that is found unworthy of this salutation shall not have place among you; for ye shall not suffer that mine house shall be polluted by him.
dc-testament/dc/88.135: And he that cometh in and is faithful before me, and is a brother, or if they be brethren, they shall salute the president or teacher with uplifted hands to heaven, with this same prayer and covenant, or by saying Amen, in token of the same.
dc-testament/dc/88.136: Behold, verily, I say unto you, this is an ensample unto you for a salutation to one another in the house of God, in the school of the prophets.
dc-testament/dc/88.137: And ye are called to do this by prayer and thanksgiving, as the Spirit shall give utterance in all your doings in the house of the Lord, in the school of the prophets, that it may become a sanctuary, a tabernacle of the Holy Spirit to your edification.
dc-testament/dc/88.138: And ye shall not receive any among you into this school save he is clean from the blood of this generation;
dc-testament/dc/88.139: And he shall be received by the ordinance of the washing of feet, for unto this end was the ordinance of the washing of feet instituted.
dc-testament/dc/88.140: And again, the ordinance of washing feet is to be administered by the president, or presiding elder of the church.
dc-testament/dc/88.141: It is to be commenced with prayer; and after partaking of bread and wine, he is to gird himself according to the pattern given in the thirteenth chapter of John’s testimony concerning me. Amen.

dc-testament/dc/93.1: Verily, thus saith the Lord: It shall come to pass that every soul who forsaketh his sins and cometh unto me, and calleth on my name, and obeyeth my voice, and keepeth my commandments, shall see my face and know that I am;
dc-testament/dc/93.2: And that I am the true light that lighteth every man that cometh into the world;
dc-testament/dc/93.3: And that I am in the Father, and the Father in me, and the Father and I are one—
dc-testament/dc/93.4: The Father because he gave me of his fulness, and the Son because I was in the world and made flesh my tabernacle, and dwelt among the sons of men.
dc-testament/dc/93.5: I was in the world and received of my Father, and the works of him were plainly manifest.
dc-testament/dc/93.6: And John saw and bore record of the fulness of my glory, and the fulness of John’s record is hereafter to be revealed.
dc-testament/dc/93.7: And he bore record, saying: I saw his glory, that he was in the beginning, before the world was;
dc-testament/dc/93.8: Therefore, in the beginning the Word was, for he was the Word, even the messenger of salvation—
dc-testament/dc/93.9: The light and the Redeemer of the world; the Spirit of truth, who came into the world, because the world was made by him, and in him was the life of men and the light of men.
dc-testament/dc/93.10: The worlds were made by him; men were made by him; all things were made by him, and through him, and of him.
dc-testament/dc/93.11: And I, John, bear record that I beheld his glory, as the glory of the Only Begotten of the Father, full of grace and truth, even the Spirit of truth, which came and dwelt in the flesh, and dwelt among us.
dc-testament/dc/93.12: And I, John, saw that he received not of the fulness at the first, but received grace for grace;
dc-testament/dc/93.13: And he received not of the fulness at first, but continued from grace to grace, until he received a fulness;
dc-testament/dc/93.14: And thus he was called the Son of God, because he received not of the fulness at the first.
dc-testament/dc/93.15: And I, John, bear record, and lo, the heavens were opened, and the Holy Ghost descended upon him in the form of a dove, and sat upon him, and there came a voice out of heaven saying: This is my beloved Son.
dc-testament/dc/93.16: And I, John, bear record that he received a fulness of the glory of the Father;
dc-testament/dc/93.17: And he received all power, both in heaven and on earth, and the glory of the Father was with him, for he dwelt in him.
dc-testament/dc/93.18: And it shall come to pass, that if you are faithful you shall receive the fulness of the record of John.
dc-testament/dc/93.19: I give unto you these sayings that you may understand and know how to worship, and know what you worship, that you may come unto the Father in my name, and in due time receive of his fulness.
dc-testament/dc/93.20: For if you keep my commandments you shall receive of his fulness, and be glorified in me as I am in the Father; therefore, I say unto you, you shall receive grace for grace.
dc-testament/dc/93.21: And now, verily I say unto you, I was in the beginning with the Father, and am the Firstborn;
dc-testament/dc/93.22: And all those who are begotten through me are partakers of the glory of the same, and are the church of the Firstborn.
dc-testament/dc/93.23: Ye were also in the beginning with the Father; that which is Spirit, even the Spirit of truth;
dc-testament/dc/93.24: And truth is knowledge of things as they are, and as they were, and as they are to come;
dc-testament/dc/93.25: And whatsoever is more or less than this is the spirit of that wicked one who was a liar from the beginning.
dc-testament/dc/93.26: The Spirit of truth is of God. I am the Spirit of truth, and John bore record of me, saying: He received a fulness of truth, yea, even of all truth;
dc-testament/dc/93.27: And no man receiveth a fulness unless he keepeth his commandments.
dc-testament/dc/93.28: He that keepeth his commandments receiveth truth and light, until he is glorified in truth and knoweth all things.
dc-testament/dc/93.29: Man was also in the beginning with God. Intelligence, or the light of truth, was not created or made, neither indeed can be.
dc-testament/dc/93.30: All truth is independent in that sphere in which God has placed it, to act for itself, as all intelligence also; otherwise there is no existence.
dc-testament/dc/93.31: Behold, here is the agency of man, and here is the condemnation of man; because that which was from the beginning is plainly manifest unto them, and they receive not the light.
dc-testament/dc/93.32: And every man whose spirit receiveth not the light is under condemnation.
dc-testament/dc/93.33: For man is spirit. The elements are eternal, and spirit and element, inseparably connected, receive a fulness of joy;
dc-testament/dc/93.34: And when separated, man cannot receive a fulness of joy.
dc-testament/dc/93.35: The elements are the tabernacle of God; yea, man is the tabernacle of God, even temples; and whatsoever temple is defiled, God shall destroy that temple.
dc-testament/dc/93.36: The glory of God is intelligence, or, in other words, light and truth.
dc-testament/dc/93.37: Light and truth forsake that evil one.
dc-testament/dc/93.38: Every spirit of man was innocent in the beginning; and God having redeemed man from the fall, men became again, in their infant state, innocent before God.
dc-testament/dc/93.39: And that wicked one cometh and taketh away light and truth, through disobedience, from the children of men, and because of the tradition of their fathers.
dc-testament/dc/93.40: But I have commanded you to bring up your children in light and truth.
dc-testament/dc/93.41: But verily I say unto you, my servant Frederick G. Williams, you have continued under this condemnation;
dc-testament/dc/93.42: You have not taught your children light and truth, according to the commandments; and that wicked one hath power, as yet, over you, and this is the cause of your affliction.
dc-testament/dc/93.43: And now a commandment I give unto you—if you will be delivered you shall set in order your own house, for there are many things that are not right in your house.
dc-testament/dc/93.44: Verily, I say unto my servant Sidney Rigdon, that in some things he hath not kept the commandments concerning his children; therefore, first set in order thy house.
dc-testament/dc/93.45: Verily, I say unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., or in other words, I will call you friends, for you are my friends, and ye shall have an inheritance with me—
dc-testament/dc/93.46: I called you servants for the world’s sake, and ye are their servants for my sake—
dc-testament/dc/93.47: And now, verily I say unto Joseph Smith, Jun.—You have not kept the commandments, and must needs stand rebuked before the Lord;
dc-testament/dc/93.48: Your family must needs repent and forsake some things, and give more earnest heed unto your sayings, or be removed out of their place.
dc-testament/dc/93.49: What I say unto one I say unto all; pray always lest that wicked one have power in you, and remove you out of your place.
dc-testament/dc/93.50: My servant Newel K. Whitney also, a bishop of my church, hath need to be chastened, and set in order his family, and see that they are more diligent and concerned at home, and pray always, or they shall be removed out of their place.
dc-testament/dc/93.51: Now, I say unto you, my friends, let my servant Sidney Rigdon go on his journey, and make haste, and also proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord, and the gospel of salvation, as I shall give him utterance; and by your prayer of faith with one consent I will uphold him.
dc-testament/dc/93.52: And let my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Frederick G. Williams make haste also, and it shall be given them even according to the prayer of faith; and inasmuch as you keep my sayings you shall not be confounded in this world, nor in the world to come.
dc-testament/dc/93.53: And, verily I say unto you, that it is my will that you should hasten to translate my scriptures, and to obtain a knowledge of history, and of countries, and of kingdoms, of laws of God and man, and all this for the salvation of Zion. Amen.

nt/1-cor/10.1: Moreover, brethren, I would not that ye should be ignorant, how that all our fathers were under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;
nt/1-cor/10.2: And were all baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
nt/1-cor/10.3: And did all eat the same spiritual meat;
nt/1-cor/10.4: And did all drink the same spiritual drink: for they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them: and that Rock was Christ.
nt/1-cor/10.5: But with many of them God was not well pleased: for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
nt/1-cor/10.6: Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.
nt/1-cor/10.7: Neither be ye idolaters, as [italics]were[/italics] some of them; as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.
nt/1-cor/10.8: Neither let us commit fornication, as some of them committed, and fell in one day three and twenty thousand.
nt/1-cor/10.9: Neither let us tempt Christ, as some of them also tempted, and were destroyed of serpents.
nt/1-cor/10.10: Neither murmur ye, as some of them also murmured, and were destroyed of the destroyer.
nt/1-cor/10.11: Now all these things happened unto them for ensamples: and they are written for our admonition, upon whom the ends of the world are come.
nt/1-cor/10.12: Wherefore let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.
nt/1-cor/10.13: There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God [italics]is[/italics] faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able; but will with the temptation also make a way to escape, that ye may be able to bear [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/1-cor/10.14: Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from idolatry.
nt/1-cor/10.15: I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
nt/1-cor/10.16: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
nt/1-cor/10.17: For we [italics]being[/italics] many are one bread, [italics]and[/italics] one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
nt/1-cor/10.18: Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
nt/1-cor/10.19: What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
nt/1-cor/10.20: But [italics]I say,[/italics] that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.
nt/1-cor/10.21: Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord’s table, and of the table of devils.
nt/1-cor/10.22: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?
nt/1-cor/10.23: All things are lawful for me, but all things are not expedient: all things are lawful for me, but all things edify not.
nt/1-cor/10.24: Let no man seek his own, but every man another’s [italics]wealth[/italics][italics].[/italics]
nt/1-cor/10.25: Whatsoever is sold in the shambles, [italics]that[/italics] eat, asking no question for conscience sake:
nt/1-cor/10.26: For the earth [italics]is[/italics] the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof.
nt/1-cor/10.27: If any of them that believe not bid you [italics]to a [/italics][italics]feast[/italics][italics],[/italics] and ye be disposed to go; whatsoever is set before you, eat, asking no question for conscience sake.
nt/1-cor/10.28: But if any man say unto you, This is offered in sacrifice unto idols, eat not for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience sake: for the earth [italics]is[/italics] the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof:
nt/1-cor/10.29: Conscience, I say, not thine own, but of the other: for why is my liberty judged of another [italics]man’s[/italics] conscience?
nt/1-cor/10.30: For if I by grace be a partaker, why am I evil spoken of for that for which I give thanks?
nt/1-cor/10.31: Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do all to the glory of God.
nt/1-cor/10.32: Give none offence, neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the church of God:
nt/1-cor/10.33: Even as I please all [italics]men[/italics] in all [italics]things,[/italics] not seeking mine own profit, but the [italics]profit[/italics] of many, that they may be saved.

nt/1-cor/11.1: Be ye followers of me, even as I also [italics]am[/italics] of Christ.
nt/1-cor/11.2: Now I praise you, brethren, that ye remember me in all things, and keep the ordinances, as I delivered [italics]them[/italics] to you.
nt/1-cor/11.3: But I would have you know, that the head of every man is Christ; and the head of the woman [italics]is[/italics] the man; and the head of Christ [italics]is[/italics] God.
nt/1-cor/11.4: Every man praying or prophesying, having [italics]his[/italics] head covered, dishonoureth his head.
nt/1-cor/11.5: But every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with [italics]her[/italics] head uncovered dishonoureth her head: for that is even all one as if she were shaven.
nt/1-cor/11.6: For if the woman be not covered, let her also be shorn: but if it be a shame for a woman to be shorn or shaven, let her be covered.
nt/1-cor/11.7: For a man indeed ought not to cover [italics]his[/italics] head, forasmuch as he is the image and glory of God: but the woman is the glory of the man.
nt/1-cor/11.8: For the man is not of the woman; but the woman of the man.
nt/1-cor/11.9: Neither was the man created for the woman; but the woman for the man.
nt/1-cor/11.10: For this cause ought the woman to have power on [italics]her[/italics] head because of the angels.
nt/1-cor/11.11: Nevertheless neither is the man without the woman, neither the woman without the man, in the Lord.
nt/1-cor/11.12: For as the woman [italics]is[/italics] of the man, even so [italics]is[/italics] the man also by the woman; but all things of God.
nt/1-cor/11.13: Judge in yourselves: is it comely that a woman pray unto God uncovered?
nt/1-cor/11.14: Doth not even nature itself teach you, that, if a man have long hair, it is a shame unto him?
nt/1-cor/11.15: But if a woman have long hair, it is a glory to her: for [italics]her[/italics] hair is given her for a covering.
nt/1-cor/11.16: But if any man seem to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither the churches of God.
nt/1-cor/11.17: Now in this that I declare [italics]unto you[/italics] I praise [italics]you[/italics] not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.
nt/1-cor/11.18: For first of all, when ye come together in the church, I hear that there be divisions among you; and I partly believe it.
nt/1-cor/11.19: For there must be also heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.
nt/1-cor/11.20: When ye come together therefore into one place, [italics]this[/italics] is not to eat the Lord’s supper.
nt/1-cor/11.21: For in eating every one taketh before [italics]other[/italics] his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.
nt/1-cor/11.22: What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God, and shame them that have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise [italics]you[/italics] not.
nt/1-cor/11.23: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the [italics]same[/italics] night in which he was betrayed took bread:
nt/1-cor/11.24: And when he had given thanks, he brake [italics]it,[/italics] and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.
nt/1-cor/11.25: After the same manner also [italics]he took[/italics] the cup, when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink [italics]it,[/italics] in remembrance of me.
nt/1-cor/11.26: For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord’s death till he come.
nt/1-cor/11.27: Wherefore whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink [italics]this[/italics] cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
nt/1-cor/11.28: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of [italics]that[/italics] bread, and drink of [italics]that[/italics] cup.
nt/1-cor/11.29: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.
nt/1-cor/11.30: For this cause many [italics]are[/italics] weak and sickly among you, and many sleep.
nt/1-cor/11.31: For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
nt/1-cor/11.32: But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
nt/1-cor/11.33: Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.
nt/1-cor/11.34: And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.

nt/1-cor/12.1: Now concerning spiritual [italics]gifts,[/italics] brethren, I would not have you ignorant.
nt/1-cor/12.2: Ye know that ye were Gentiles, carried away unto these dumb idols, even as ye were led.
nt/1-cor/12.3: Wherefore I give you to understand, that no man speaking by the Spirit of God calleth Jesus accursed: and [italics]that[/italics] no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.
nt/1-cor/12.4: Now there are diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit.
nt/1-cor/12.5: And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.
nt/1-cor/12.6: And there are diversities of operations, but it is the same God which worketh all in all.
nt/1-cor/12.7: But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to every man to profit withal.
nt/1-cor/12.8: For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom; to another the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
nt/1-cor/12.9: To another faith by the same Spirit; to another the gifts of healing by the same Spirit;
nt/1-cor/12.10: To another the working of miracles; to another prophecy; to another discerning of spirits; to another [italics]divers[/italics] kinds of tongues; to another the interpretation of tongues:
nt/1-cor/12.11: But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.
nt/1-cor/12.12: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also [italics]is[/italics] Christ.
nt/1-cor/12.13: For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body, whether [italics]we be[/italics] Jews or Gentiles, whether [italics]we be[/italics] bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.
nt/1-cor/12.14: For the body is not one member, but many.
nt/1-cor/12.15: If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
nt/1-cor/12.16: And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
nt/1-cor/12.17: If the whole body [italics]were[/italics] an eye, where [italics]were[/italics] the hearing? If the whole [italics]were[/italics] hearing, where [italics]were[/italics] the smelling?
nt/1-cor/12.18: But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
nt/1-cor/12.19: And if they were all one member, where [italics]were[/italics] the body?
nt/1-cor/12.20: But now [italics]are they[/italics] many members, yet but one body.
nt/1-cor/12.21: And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.
nt/1-cor/12.22: Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be more feeble, are necessary:
nt/1-cor/12.23: And those [italics]members[/italics] of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely [italics]parts[/italics] have more abundant comeliness.
nt/1-cor/12.24: For our comely [italics]parts[/italics] have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that [italics]part[/italics] which lacked:
nt/1-cor/12.25: That there should be no schism in the body; but [italics]that[/italics] the members should have the same care one for another.
nt/1-cor/12.26: And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.
nt/1-cor/12.27: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
nt/1-cor/12.28: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.
nt/1-cor/12.29: [italics]Are[/italics] all apostles? [italics]are[/italics] all prophets? [italics]are[/italics] all teachers? [italics]are[/italics] all workers of miracles?
nt/1-cor/12.30: Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
nt/1-cor/12.31: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.

nt/1-cor/15.1: Moreover, brethren, I declare unto you the gospel which I preached unto you, which also ye have received, and wherein ye stand;
nt/1-cor/15.2: By which also ye are saved, if ye keep in memory what I preached unto you, unless ye have believed in vain.
nt/1-cor/15.3: For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received, how that Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures;
nt/1-cor/15.4: And that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day according to the scriptures:
nt/1-cor/15.5: And that he was seen of Cephas, then of the twelve:
nt/1-cor/15.6: After that, he was seen of above five hundred brethren at once; of whom the greater part remain unto this present, but some are fallen asleep.
nt/1-cor/15.7: After that, he was seen of James; then of all the apostles.
nt/1-cor/15.8: And last of all he was seen of me also, as of one born out of due time.
nt/1-cor/15.9: For I am the least of the apostles, that am not meet to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
nt/1-cor/15.10: But by the grace of God I am what I am: and his grace which [italics]was bestowed[/italics] upon me was not in vain; but I laboured more abundantly than they all: yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
nt/1-cor/15.11: Therefore whether [italics]it were[/italics] I or they, so we preach, and so ye believed.
nt/1-cor/15.12: Now if Christ be preached that he rose from the dead, how say some among you that there is no resurrection of the dead?
nt/1-cor/15.13: But if there be no resurrection of the dead, then is Christ not risen:
nt/1-cor/15.14: And if Christ be not risen, then [italics]is[/italics] our preaching vain, and your faith [italics]is[/italics] also vain.
