nt/1-cor/15.15: Yea, and we are found false witnesses of God; because we have testified of God that he raised up Christ: whom he raised not up, if so be that the dead rise not.
nt/1-cor/15.16: For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ raised:
nt/1-cor/15.17: And if Christ be not raised, your faith [italics]is[/italics] vain; ye are yet in your sins.
nt/1-cor/15.18: Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are perished.
nt/1-cor/15.19: If in this life only we have hope in Christ, we are of all men most miserable.
nt/1-cor/15.20: But now is Christ risen from the dead, [italics]and[/italics] become the firstfruits of them that slept.
nt/1-cor/15.21: For since by man [italics]came[/italics] death, by man [italics]came[/italics] also the resurrection of the dead.
nt/1-cor/15.22: For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.
nt/1-cor/15.23: But every man in his own order: Christ the firstfruits; afterward they that are Christ’s at his coming.
nt/1-cor/15.24: Then [italics]cometh[/italics] the end, when he shall have delivered up the kingdom to God, even the Father; when he shall have put down all rule and all authority and power.
nt/1-cor/15.25: For he must reign, till he hath put all enemies under his feet.
nt/1-cor/15.26: The last enemy [italics]that[/italics] shall be destroyed [italics]is[/italics] death.
nt/1-cor/15.27: For he hath put all things under his feet. But when he saith all things are put under [italics]him, it is[/italics] manifest that he is excepted, which did put all things under him.
nt/1-cor/15.28: And when all things shall be subdued unto him, then shall the Son also himself be subject unto him that put all things under him, that God may be all in all.
nt/1-cor/15.29: Else what shall they do which are baptized for the dead, if the dead rise not at all? why are they then baptized for the dead?
nt/1-cor/15.30: And why stand we in jeopardy every hour?
nt/1-cor/15.31: I protest by your rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.
nt/1-cor/15.32: If after the manner of men I have fought with beasts at Ephesus, what advantageth it me, if the dead rise not? let us eat and drink; for to morrow we die.
nt/1-cor/15.33: Be not deceived: evil communications corrupt good manners.
nt/1-cor/15.34: Awake to righteousness, and sin not; for some have not the knowledge of God: I speak [italics]this[/italics] to your shame.
nt/1-cor/15.35: But some [italics]man[/italics] will say, How are the dead raised up? and with what body do they come?
nt/1-cor/15.36: [italics]Thou[/italics] fool, that which thou sowest is not quickened, except it die:
nt/1-cor/15.37: And that which thou sowest, thou sowest not that body that shall be, but bare grain, it may chance of wheat, or of some other [italics]grain:[/italics]
nt/1-cor/15.38: But God giveth it a body as it hath pleased him, and to every seed his own body.
nt/1-cor/15.39: All flesh [italics]is[/italics] not the same flesh: but [italics]there is[/italics] one [italics]kind of[/italics] flesh of men, another flesh of beasts, another of fishes, [italics]and[/italics] another of birds.
nt/1-cor/15.40: [italics]There[/italics][italics] are[/italics] also celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial: but the glory of the celestial [italics]is[/italics] one, and the [italics]glory[/italics] of the terrestrial [italics]is[/italics] another.
nt/1-cor/15.41: [italics]There is[/italics] one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars: for [italics]one[/italics] star differeth from [italics]another[/italics] star in glory.
nt/1-cor/15.42: So also [italics]is[/italics] the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
nt/1-cor/15.43: It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
nt/1-cor/15.44: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.
nt/1-cor/15.45: And so it is written, The first man Adam was made a living soul; the last Adam [italics]was made[/italics] a quickening spirit.
nt/1-cor/15.46: Howbeit that [italics]was[/italics] not first which is spiritual, but that which is natural; and afterward that which is spiritual.
nt/1-cor/15.47: The first man [italics]is[/italics] of the earth, earthy: the second man [italics]is[/italics] the Lord from heaven.
nt/1-cor/15.48: As [italics]is[/italics] the earthy, such [italics]are[/italics] they also that are earthy: and as [italics]is[/italics] the heavenly, such [italics]are[/italics] they also that are heavenly.
nt/1-cor/15.49: And as we have borne the image of the earthy, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly.
nt/1-cor/15.50: Now this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; neither doth corruption inherit incorruption.
nt/1-cor/15.51: Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
nt/1-cor/15.52: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.
nt/1-cor/15.53: For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal [italics]must[/italics] put on immortality.
nt/1-cor/15.54: So when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.
nt/1-cor/15.55: O death, where [italics]is[/italics] thy sting? O grave, where [italics]is[/italics] thy victory?
nt/1-cor/15.56: The sting of death [italics]is[/italics] sin; and the strength of sin [italics]is[/italics] the law.
nt/1-cor/15.57: But thanks [italics]be[/italics] to God, which giveth us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
nt/1-cor/15.58: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye steadfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.

nt/1-cor/2.1: And I, brethren, when I came to you, came not with excellency of speech or of wisdom, declaring unto you the testimony of God.
nt/1-cor/2.2: For I determined not to know any thing among you, save Jesus Christ, and him crucified.
nt/1-cor/2.3: And I was with you in weakness, and in fear, and in much trembling.
nt/1-cor/2.4: And my speech and my preaching [italics]was[/italics] not with enticing words of man’s wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power:
nt/1-cor/2.5: That your faith should not stand in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God.
nt/1-cor/2.6: Howbeit we speak wisdom among them that are perfect: yet not the wisdom of this world, nor of the princes of this world, that come to nought:
nt/1-cor/2.7: But we speak the wisdom of God in a mystery, [italics]even[/italics] the hidden [italics]wisdom[/italics][italics],[/italics] which God ordained before the world unto our glory:
nt/1-cor/2.8: Which none of the princes of this world knew: for had they known [italics]it,[/italics] they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.
nt/1-cor/2.9: But as it is written, Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love him.
nt/1-cor/2.10: But God hath revealed [italics]them[/italics] unto us by his Spirit: for the Spirit searcheth all things, yea, the deep things of God.
nt/1-cor/2.11: For what man knoweth the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him? even so the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God.
nt/1-cor/2.12: Now we have received, not the spirit of the world, but the spirit which is of God; that we might know the things that are freely given to us of God.
nt/1-cor/2.13: Which things also we speak, not in the words which man’s wisdom teacheth, but which the Holy Ghost teacheth; comparing spiritual things with spiritual.
nt/1-cor/2.14: But the natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God: for they are foolishness unto him: neither can he know [italics]them,[/italics] because they are spiritually discerned.
nt/1-cor/2.15: But he that is spiritual judgeth all things, yet he himself is judged of no man.
nt/1-cor/2.16: For who hath known the mind of the Lord, that he may instruct him? But we have the mind of Christ.

nt/1-cor/5.1: It is reported commonly [italics]that there is[/italics] fornication among you, and such fornication as is not so much as named among the Gentiles, that one should have his father’s wife.
nt/1-cor/5.2: And ye are puffed up, and have not rather mourned, that he that hath done this deed might be taken away from among you.
nt/1-cor/5.3: For I verily, as absent in body, but present in spirit, have judged already, as though I were present, [italics]concerning[/italics] him that hath so done this deed,
nt/1-cor/5.4: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when ye are gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,
nt/1-cor/5.5: To deliver such an one unto Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.
nt/1-cor/5.6: Your glorying [italics]is[/italics] not good. Know ye not that a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump?
nt/1-cor/5.7: Purge out therefore the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump, as ye are unleavened. For even Christ our passover is sacrificed for us:
nt/1-cor/5.8: Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old leaven, neither with the leaven of malice and wickedness; but with the unleavened [italics]bread[/italics] of sincerity and truth.
nt/1-cor/5.9: I wrote unto you in an epistle not to company with fornicators:
nt/1-cor/5.10: Yet not altogether with the fornicators of this world, or with the covetous, or extortioners, or with idolaters; for then must ye needs go out of the world.
nt/1-cor/5.11: But now I have written unto you not to keep company, if any man that is called a brother be a fornicator, or covetous, or an idolater, or a railer, or a drunkard, or an extortioner; with such an one no not to eat.
nt/1-cor/5.12: For what have I to do to judge them also that are without? do not ye judge them that are within?
nt/1-cor/5.13: But them that are without God judgeth. Therefore put away from among yourselves that wicked person.

nt/1-jn/1.1: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled, of the Word of life;
nt/1-jn/1.2: (For the life was manifested, and we have seen [italics]it,[/italics] and bear witness, and shew unto you that eternal life, which was with the Father, and was manifested unto us;)
nt/1-jn/1.3: That which we have seen and heard declare we unto you, that ye also may have fellowship with us: and truly our fellowship [italics]is[/italics] with the Father, and with his Son Jesus Christ.
nt/1-jn/1.4: And these things write we unto you, that your joy may be full.
nt/1-jn/1.5: This then is the message which we have heard of him, and declare unto you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.
nt/1-jn/1.6: If we say that we have fellowship with him, and walk in darkness, we lie, and do not the truth:
nt/1-jn/1.7: But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship one with another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin.
nt/1-jn/1.8: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
nt/1-jn/1.9: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us [italics]our[/italics] sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
nt/1-jn/1.10: If we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.

nt/1-jn/2.1: My little children, these things write I unto you, that ye sin not. And if any man sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous:
nt/1-jn/2.2: And he is the propitiation for our sins: and not for ours only, but also for [italics]the sins of[/italics] the whole world.
nt/1-jn/2.3: And hereby we do know that we know him, if we keep his commandments.
nt/1-jn/2.4: He that saith, I know him, and keepeth not his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
nt/1-jn/2.5: But whoso keepeth his word, in him verily is the love of God perfected: hereby know we that we are in him.
nt/1-jn/2.6: He that saith he abideth in him ought himself also so to walk, even as he walked.
nt/1-jn/2.7: Brethren, I write no new commandment unto you, but an old commandment which ye had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which ye have heard from the beginning.
nt/1-jn/2.8: Again, a new commandment I write unto you, which thing is true in him and in you: because the darkness is past, and the true light now shineth.
nt/1-jn/2.9: He that saith he is in the light, and hateth his brother, is in darkness even until now.
nt/1-jn/2.10: He that loveth his brother abideth in the light, and there is none occasion of stumbling in him.
nt/1-jn/2.11: But he that hateth his brother is in darkness, and walketh in darkness, and knoweth not whither he goeth, because that darkness hath blinded his eyes.
nt/1-jn/2.12: I write unto you, little children, because your sins are forgiven you for his name’s sake.
nt/1-jn/2.13: I write unto you, fathers, because ye have known him [italics]that is[/italics] from the beginning. I write unto you, young men, because ye have overcome the wicked one. I write unto you, little children, because ye have known the Father.
nt/1-jn/2.14: I have written unto you, fathers, because ye have known him [italics]that is[/italics] from the beginning. I have written unto you, young men, because ye are strong, and the word of God abideth in you, and ye have overcome the wicked one.
nt/1-jn/2.15: Love not the world, neither the things [italics]that are[/italics] in the world. If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
nt/1-jn/2.16: For all that [italics]is[/italics] in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world.
nt/1-jn/2.17: And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever.
nt/1-jn/2.18: Little children, it is the last time: and as ye have heard that antichrist shall come, even now are there many antichrists; whereby we know that it is the last time.
nt/1-jn/2.19: They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would [italics]no doubt[/italics] have continued with us: but [italics]they went out,[/italics] that they might be made manifest that they were not all of us.
nt/1-jn/2.20: But ye have an unction from the Holy One, and ye know all things.
nt/1-jn/2.21: I have not written unto you because ye know not the truth, but because ye know it, and that no lie is of the truth.
nt/1-jn/2.22: Who is a liar but he that denieth that Jesus is the Christ? He is antichrist, that denieth the Father and the Son.
nt/1-jn/2.23: Whosoever denieth the Son, the same hath not the Father: [[italics]but[/italics]] [italics]he that [/italics][italics]acknowledgeth[/italics][italics] the Son hath the Father also.[/italics]
nt/1-jn/2.24: Let that therefore abide in you, which ye have heard from the beginning. If that which ye have heard from the beginning shall remain in you, ye also shall continue in the Son, and in the Father.
nt/1-jn/2.25: And this is the promise that he hath promised us, [italics]even[/italics] eternal life.
nt/1-jn/2.26: These [italics]things[/italics] have I written unto you concerning them that seduce you.
nt/1-jn/2.27: But the anointing which ye have received of him abideth in you, and ye need not that any man teach you: but as the same anointing teacheth you of all things, and is truth, and is no lie, and even as it hath taught you, ye shall abide in him.
nt/1-jn/2.28: And now, little children, abide in him; that, when he shall appear, we may have confidence, and not be ashamed before him at his coming.
nt/1-jn/2.29: If ye know that he is righteous, ye know that every one that doeth righteousness is born of him.

nt/1-jn/3.1: Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the sons of God: therefore the world knoweth us not, because it knew him not.
nt/1-jn/3.2: Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is.
nt/1-jn/3.3: And every man that hath this hope in him purifieth himself, even as he is pure.
nt/1-jn/3.4: Whosoever committeth sin transgresseth also the law: for sin is the transgression of the law.
nt/1-jn/3.5: And ye know that he was manifested to take away our sins; and in him is no sin.
nt/1-jn/3.6: Whosoever abideth in him sinneth not: whosoever sinneth hath not seen him, neither known him.
nt/1-jn/3.7: Little children, let no man deceive you: he that doeth righteousness is righteous, even as he is righteous.
nt/1-jn/3.8: He that committeth sin is of the devil; for the devil sinneth from the beginning. For this purpose the Son of God was manifested, that he might destroy the works of the devil.
nt/1-jn/3.9: Whosoever is born of God doth not commit sin; for his seed remaineth in him: and he cannot sin, because he is born of God.
nt/1-jn/3.10: In this the children of God are manifest, and the children of the devil: whosoever doeth not righteousness is not of God, neither he that loveth not his brother.
nt/1-jn/3.11: For this is the message that ye heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.
nt/1-jn/3.12: Not as Cain, [italics]who[/italics] was of that wicked one, and slew his brother. And wherefore slew he him? Because his own works were evil, and his brother’s righteous.
nt/1-jn/3.13: Marvel not, my brethren, if the world hate you.
nt/1-jn/3.14: We know that we have passed from death unto life, because we love the brethren. He that loveth not [italics]his[/italics] brother abideth in death.
nt/1-jn/3.15: Whosoever hateth his brother is a murderer: and ye know that no murderer hath eternal life abiding in him.
nt/1-jn/3.16: Hereby perceive we the love [italics]of [/italics][italics]God[/italics][italics],[/italics] because he laid down his life for us: and we ought to lay down [italics]our[/italics] lives for the brethren.
nt/1-jn/3.17: But whoso hath this world’s good, and seeth his brother have need, and shutteth up his bowels [italics]of [/italics][italics]compassion[/italics] from him, how dwelleth the love of God in him?
nt/1-jn/3.18: My little children, let us not love in word, neither in tongue; but in deed and in truth.
nt/1-jn/3.19: And hereby we know that we are of the truth, and shall assure our hearts before him.
nt/1-jn/3.20: For if our heart condemn us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.
nt/1-jn/3.21: Beloved, if our heart condemn us not, [italics]then[/italics] have we confidence toward God.
nt/1-jn/3.22: And whatsoever we ask, we receive of him, because we keep his commandments, and do those things that are pleasing in his sight.
nt/1-jn/3.23: And this is his commandment, That we should believe on the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, as he gave us commandment.
nt/1-jn/3.24: And he that keepeth his commandments dwelleth in him, and he in him. And hereby we know that he abideth in us, by the Spirit which he hath given us.

nt/1-jn/4.1: Beloved, believe not every spirit, but try the spirits whether they are of God: because many false prophets are gone out into the world.
nt/1-jn/4.2: Hereby know ye the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God:
nt/1-jn/4.3: And every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God: and this is that [italics]spirit[/italics] of antichrist, whereof ye have heard that it should come; and even now already is it in the world.
nt/1-jn/4.4: Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world.
nt/1-jn/4.5: They are of the world: therefore speak they of the world, and the world heareth them.
nt/1-jn/4.6: We are of God: he that knoweth God heareth us; he that is not of God heareth not us. Hereby know we the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.
nt/1-jn/4.7: Beloved, let us love one another: for love is of God; and every one that loveth is born of God, and knoweth God.
nt/1-jn/4.8: He that loveth not knoweth not God; for God is love.
nt/1-jn/4.9: In this was manifested the love of God toward us, because that God sent his only begotten Son into the world, that we might live through him.
nt/1-jn/4.10: Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son [italics]to be[/italics] the propitiation for our sins.
nt/1-jn/4.11: Beloved, if God so loved us, we ought also to love one another.
nt/1-jn/4.12: No man hath seen God at any time. If we love one another, God dwelleth in us, and his love is perfected in us.
nt/1-jn/4.13: Hereby know we that we dwell in him, and he in us, because he hath given us of his Spirit.
nt/1-jn/4.14: And we have seen and do testify that the Father sent the Son [italics]to be[/italics] the Saviour of the world.
nt/1-jn/4.15: Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in God.
nt/1-jn/4.16: And we have known and believed the love that God hath to us. God is love; and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God in him.
nt/1-jn/4.17: Herein is our love made perfect, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment: because as he is, so are we in this world.
nt/1-jn/4.18: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear: because fear hath torment. He that feareth is not made perfect in love.
nt/1-jn/4.19: We love him, because he first loved us.
nt/1-jn/4.20: If a man say, I love God, and hateth his brother, he is a liar: for he that loveth not his brother whom he hath seen, how can he love God whom he hath not seen?
nt/1-jn/4.21: And this commandment have we from him, That he who loveth God love his brother also.

nt/1-jn/5.1: Whosoever believeth that Jesus is the Christ is born of God: and every one that loveth him that begat loveth him also that is begotten of him.
nt/1-jn/5.2: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God, and keep his commandments.
nt/1-jn/5.3: For this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments: and his commandments are not grievous.
nt/1-jn/5.4: For whatsoever is born of God overcometh the world: and this is the victory that overcometh the world, [italics]even[/italics] our faith.
nt/1-jn/5.5: Who is he that overcometh the world, but he that believeth that Jesus is the Son of God?
nt/1-jn/5.6: This is he that came by water and blood, [italics]even[/italics] Jesus Christ; not by water only, but by water and blood. And it is the Spirit that beareth witness, because the Spirit is truth.
nt/1-jn/5.7: For there are three that bear record in heaven, the Father, the Word, and the Holy Ghost: and these three are one.
nt/1-jn/5.8: And there are three that bear witness in earth, the Spirit, and the water, and the blood: and these three agree in one.
nt/1-jn/5.9: If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater: for this is the witness of God which he hath testified of his Son.
nt/1-jn/5.10: He that believeth on the Son of God hath the witness in himself: he that believeth not God hath made him a liar; because he believeth not the record that God gave of his Son.
nt/1-jn/5.11: And this is the record, that God hath given to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
nt/1-jn/5.12: He that hath the Son hath life; [italics]and[/italics] he that hath not the Son of God hath not life.
nt/1-jn/5.13: These things have I written unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life, and that ye may believe on the name of the Son of God.
nt/1-jn/5.14: And this is the confidence that we have in him, that, if we ask any thing according to his will, he heareth us:
nt/1-jn/5.15: And if we know that he hear us, whatsoever we ask, we know that we have the petitions that we desired of him.
nt/1-jn/5.16: If any man see his brother sin a sin [italics]which is[/italics] not unto death, he shall ask, and he shall give him life for them that sin not unto death. There is a sin unto death: I do not say that he shall pray for it.
nt/1-jn/5.17: All unrighteousness is sin: and there is a sin not unto death.
nt/1-jn/5.18: We know that whosoever is born of God sinneth not; but he that is begotten of God keepeth himself, and that wicked one toucheth him not.
nt/1-jn/5.19: [italics]And[/italics] we know that we are of God, and the whole world lieth in wickedness.
nt/1-jn/5.20: And we know that the Son of God is come, and hath given us an understanding, that we may know him that is true, and we are in him that is true, [italics]even[/italics] in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and eternal life.
nt/1-jn/5.21: Little children, keep yourselves from idols. Amen.

nt/1-pet/1.1: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the strangers scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
nt/1-pet/1.2: Elect according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, through sanctification of the Spirit, unto obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ: Grace unto you, and peace, be multiplied.
nt/1-pet/1.3: Blessed [italics]be[/italics] the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy hath begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
nt/1-pet/1.4: To an inheritance incorruptible, and undefiled, and that fadeth not away, reserved in heaven for you,
nt/1-pet/1.5: Who are kept by the power of God through faith unto salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
nt/1-pet/1.6: Wherein ye greatly rejoice, though now for a season, if need be, ye are in heaviness through manifold temptations:
nt/1-pet/1.7: That the trial of your faith, being much more precious than of gold that perisheth, though it be tried with fire, might be found unto praise and honour and glory at the appearing of Jesus Christ:
nt/1-pet/1.8: Whom having not seen, ye love; in whom, though now ye see [italics]him[/italics] not, yet believing, ye rejoice with joy unspeakable and full of glory:
nt/1-pet/1.9: Receiving the end of your faith, [italics]even[/italics] the salvation of [italics]your[/italics] souls.
nt/1-pet/1.10: Of which salvation the prophets have inquired and searched diligently, who prophesied of the grace [italics]that should come[/italics] unto you:
nt/1-pet/1.11: Searching what, or what manner of time the Spirit of Christ which was in them did signify, when it testified beforehand the sufferings of Christ, and the glory that should follow.
nt/1-pet/1.12: Unto whom it was revealed, that not unto themselves, but unto us they did minister the things, which are now reported unto you by them that have preached the gospel unto you with the Holy Ghost sent down from heaven; which things the angels desire to look into.
nt/1-pet/1.13: Wherefore gird up the loins of your mind, be sober, and hope to the end for the grace that is to be brought unto you at the revelation of Jesus Christ;
nt/1-pet/1.14: As obedient children, not fashioning yourselves according to the former lusts in your ignorance:
nt/1-pet/1.15: But as he which hath called you is holy, so be ye holy in all manner of conversation;
nt/1-pet/1.16: Because it is written, Be ye holy; for I am holy.
nt/1-pet/1.17: And if ye call on the Father, who without respect of persons judgeth according to every man’s work, pass the time of your sojourning [italics]here[/italics] in fear:
nt/1-pet/1.18: Forasmuch as ye know that ye were not redeemed with corruptible things, [italics]as[/italics] silver and gold, from your vain conversation [italics]received[/italics] by tradition from your fathers;
nt/1-pet/1.19: But with the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot:
nt/1-pet/1.20: Who verily was foreordained before the foundation of the world, but was manifest in these last times for you,
nt/1-pet/1.21: Who by him do believe in God, that raised him up from the dead, and gave him glory; that your faith and hope might be in God.
nt/1-pet/1.22: Seeing ye have purified your souls in obeying the truth through the Spirit unto unfeigned love of the brethren, [italics]see that ye[/italics] love one another with a pure heart fervently:
nt/1-pet/1.23: Being born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever.
nt/1-pet/1.24: For all flesh [italics]is[/italics] as grass, and all the glory of man as the flower of grass. The grass withereth, and the flower thereof falleth away:
nt/1-pet/1.25: But the word of the Lord endureth for ever. And this is the word which by the gospel is preached unto you.

nt/1-pet/2.1: Wherefore laying aside all malice, and all guile, and hypocrisies, and envies, and all evil speakings,
nt/1-pet/2.2: As newborn babes, desire the sincere milk of the word, that ye may grow thereby:
nt/1-pet/2.3: If so be ye have tasted that the Lord [italics]is[/italics] gracious.
nt/1-pet/2.4: To whom coming, [italics]as unto[/italics] a living stone, disallowed indeed of men, but chosen of God, [italics]and[/italics] precious,
nt/1-pet/2.5: Ye also, as lively stones, are built up a spiritual house, an holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God by Jesus Christ.
nt/1-pet/2.6: Wherefore also it is contained in the scripture, Behold, I lay in Sion a chief corner stone, elect, precious: and he that believeth on him shall not be confounded.
nt/1-pet/2.7: Unto you therefore which believe [italics]he is[/italics] precious: but unto them which be disobedient, the stone which the builders disallowed, the same is made the head of the corner,
nt/1-pet/2.8: And a stone of stumbling, and a rock of offence, [italics]even to them[/italics] which stumble at the word, being disobedient: whereunto also they were appointed.
nt/1-pet/2.9: But ye [italics]are[/italics] a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people; that ye should shew forth the praises of him who hath called you out of darkness into his marvellous light:
nt/1-pet/2.10: Which in time past [italics]were[/italics] not a people, but [italics]are[/italics] now the people of God: which had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.
nt/1-pet/2.11: Dearly beloved, I beseech [italics]you[/italics] as strangers and pilgrims, abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul;
nt/1-pet/2.12: Having your conversation honest among the Gentiles: that, whereas they speak against you as evildoers, they may by [italics]your[/italics] good works, which they shall behold, glorify God in the day of visitation.
nt/1-pet/2.13: Submit yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake: whether it be to the king, as supreme;
nt/1-pet/2.14: Or unto governors, as unto them that are sent by him for the punishment of evildoers, and for the praise of them that do well.
nt/1-pet/2.15: For so is the will of God, that with well doing ye may put to silence the ignorance of foolish men:
nt/1-pet/2.16: As free, and not using [italics]your[/italics] liberty for a cloak of maliciousness, but as the servants of God.
nt/1-pet/2.17: Honour all [italics]men.[/italics] Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honour the king.
nt/1-pet/2.18: Servants, [italics]be[/italics] subject to [italics]your[/italics] masters with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the froward.
nt/1-pet/2.19: For this [italics]is[/italics] thankworthy, if a man for conscience toward God endure grief, suffering wrongfully.
nt/1-pet/2.20: For what glory [italics]is it,[/italics] if, when ye be buffeted for your faults, ye shall take it patiently? but if, when ye do well, and suffer [italics]for it,[/italics] ye take it patiently, this [italics]is[/italics] acceptable with God.
nt/1-pet/2.21: For even hereunto were ye called: because Christ also suffered for us, leaving us an example, that ye should follow his steps:
nt/1-pet/2.22: Who did no sin, neither was guile found in his mouth:
nt/1-pet/2.23: Who, when he was reviled, reviled not again; when he suffered, he threatened not; but committed [italics]himself[/italics] to him that judgeth righteously:
nt/1-pet/2.24: Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness: by whose stripes ye were healed.
nt/1-pet/2.25: For ye were as sheep going astray; but are now returned unto the Shepherd and Bishop of your souls.

nt/1-pet/3.1: Likewise, ye wives, [italics]be[/italics] in subjection to your own husbands; that, if any obey not the word, they also may without the word be won by the conversation of the wives;
nt/1-pet/3.2: While they behold your chaste conversation [italics]coupled[/italics] with fear.
nt/1-pet/3.3: Whose adorning let it not be that outward [italics]adorning[/italics] of plaiting the hair, and of wearing of gold, or of putting on of apparel;
nt/1-pet/3.4: But [italics]let it be[/italics] the hidden man of the heart, in that which is not corruptible, [italics]even the [/italics][italics]ornament[/italics] of a meek and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price.
nt/1-pet/3.5: For after this manner in the old time the holy women also, who trusted in God, adorned themselves, being in subjection unto their own husbands:
nt/1-pet/3.6: Even as Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord: whose daughters ye are, as long as ye do well, and are not afraid with any amazement.
nt/1-pet/3.7: Likewise, ye husbands, dwell with [italics]them[/italics] according to knowledge, giving honour unto the wife, as unto the weaker vessel, and as being heirs together of the grace of life; that your prayers be not hindered.
nt/1-pet/3.8: Finally, [italics]be ye[/italics] all of one mind, having compassion one of another, love as brethren, [italics]be[/italics] pitiful, [italics]be[/italics] courteous:
nt/1-pet/3.9: Not rendering evil for evil, or railing for railing: but contrariwise blessing; knowing that ye are thereunto called, that ye should inherit a blessing.
nt/1-pet/3.10: For he that will love life, and see good days, let him refrain his tongue from evil, and his lips that they speak no guile:
nt/1-pet/3.11: Let him eschew evil, and do good; let him seek peace, and ensue it.
nt/1-pet/3.12: For the eyes of the Lord [italics]are[/italics] over the righteous, and his ears [italics]are open[/italics] unto their prayers: but the face of the Lord [italics]is[/italics] against them that do evil.
nt/1-pet/3.13: And who [italics]is[/italics] he that will harm you, if ye be followers of that which is good?
nt/1-pet/3.14: But and if ye suffer for righteousness’ sake, happy [italics]are ye:[/italics] and be not afraid of their terror, neither be troubled;
nt/1-pet/3.15: But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and [italics]be[/italics] ready always to [italics]give[/italics] an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you with meekness and fear:
nt/1-pet/3.16: Having a good conscience; that, whereas they speak evil of you, as of evildoers, they may be ashamed that falsely accuse your good conversation in Christ.
nt/1-pet/3.17: For [italics]it is[/italics] better, if the will of God be so, that ye suffer for well doing, than for evil doing.
nt/1-pet/3.18: For Christ also hath once suffered for sins, the just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh, but quickened by the Spirit:
nt/1-pet/3.19: By which also he went and preached unto the spirits in prison;
nt/1-pet/3.20: Which sometime were disobedient, when once the longsuffering of God waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was a preparing, wherein few, that is, eight souls were saved by water.
nt/1-pet/3.21: The like figure whereunto [italics]even[/italics] baptism doth also now save us (not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God,) by the resurrection of Jesus Christ:
nt/1-pet/3.22: Who is gone into heaven, and is on the right hand of God; angels and authorities and powers being made subject unto him.

nt/1-pet/5.1: The elders which are among you I exhort, who am also an elder, and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and also a partaker of the glory that shall be revealed:
nt/1-pet/5.2: Feed the flock of God which is among you, taking the oversight [italics]thereof,[/italics] not by constraint, but willingly; not for filthy lucre, but of a ready mind;
nt/1-pet/5.3: Neither as being lords over [italics]God’s[/italics] heritage, but being ensamples to the flock.
nt/1-pet/5.4: And when the chief Shepherd shall appear, ye shall receive a crown of glory that fadeth not away.
nt/1-pet/5.5: Likewise, ye younger, submit yourselves unto the elder. Yea, all [italics]of you[/italics] be subject one to another, and be clothed with humility: for God resisteth the proud, and giveth grace to the humble.
nt/1-pet/5.6: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time:
nt/1-pet/5.7: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
nt/1-pet/5.8: Be sober, be vigilant; because your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour:
nt/1-pet/5.9: Whom resist steadfast in the faith, knowing that the same afflictions are accomplished in your brethren that are in the world.
nt/1-pet/5.10: But the God of all grace, who hath called us unto his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after that ye have suffered a while, make you perfect, stablish, strengthen, settle [italics]you.[/italics]
nt/1-pet/5.11: To him [italics]be[/italics] glory and dominion for ever and ever. Amen.
nt/1-pet/5.12: By Silvanus, a faithful brother unto you, as I suppose, I have written briefly, exhorting, and testifying that this is the true grace of God wherein ye stand.
nt/1-pet/5.13: The [italics]church that is[/italics] at Babylon, elected together with [italics]you,[/italics] saluteth you; and [italics]so doth[/italics] Marcus my son.
nt/1-pet/5.14: Greet ye one another with a kiss of charity. Peace [italics]be[/italics] with you all that are in Christ Jesus. Amen.

nt/1-thes/4.1: Furthermore then we beseech you, brethren, and exhort [italics]you[/italics] by the Lord Jesus, that as ye have received of us how ye ought to walk and to please God, [italics]so[/italics] ye would abound more and more.
nt/1-thes/4.2: For ye know what commandments we gave you by the Lord Jesus.
nt/1-thes/4.3: For this is the will of God, [italics]even[/italics] your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication:
nt/1-thes/4.4: That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour;
nt/1-thes/4.5: Not in the lust of concupiscence, even as the Gentiles which know not God:
nt/1-thes/4.6: That no [italics]man[/italics] go beyond and defraud his brother in [italics]any[/italics] matter: because that the Lord [italics]is[/italics] the avenger of all such, as we also have forewarned you and testified.
nt/1-thes/4.7: For God hath not called us unto uncleanness, but unto holiness.
nt/1-thes/4.8: He therefore that despiseth, despiseth not man, but God, who hath also given unto us his holy Spirit.
nt/1-thes/4.9: But as touching brotherly love ye need not that I write unto you: for ye yourselves are taught of God to love one another.
nt/1-thes/4.10: And indeed ye do it toward all the brethren which are in all Macedonia: but we beseech you, brethren, that ye increase more and more;
nt/1-thes/4.11: And that ye study to be quiet, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you;
nt/1-thes/4.12: That ye may walk honestly toward them that are without, and [italics]that[/italics] ye may have lack of nothing.
nt/1-thes/4.13: But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep, that ye sorrow not, even as others which have no hope.
nt/1-thes/4.14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so them also which sleep in Jesus will God bring with him.
nt/1-thes/4.15: For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive [italics]and[/italics] remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not prevent them which are asleep.
nt/1-thes/4.16: For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:
nt/1-thes/4.17: Then we which are alive [italics]and[/italics] remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord.
nt/1-thes/4.18: Wherefore comfort one another with these words.

nt/1-tim/2.1: I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, [italics]and[/italics] giving of thanks, be made for all men;
nt/1-tim/2.2: For kings, and [italics]for[/italics] all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty.
nt/1-tim/2.3: For this [italics]is[/italics] good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;
nt/1-tim/2.4: Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth.
nt/1-tim/2.5: For [italics]there is[/italics] one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;
nt/1-tim/2.6: Who gave himself a ransom for all, to be testified in due time.
nt/1-tim/2.7: Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an apostle, (I speak the truth in Christ, [italics]and[/italics] lie not;) a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and verity.
nt/1-tim/2.8: I will therefore that men pray every where, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting.
nt/1-tim/2.9: In like manner also, that women adorn themselves in modest apparel, with shamefacedness and sobriety; not with broided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array;
nt/1-tim/2.10: But (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works.
nt/1-tim/2.11: Let the woman learn in silence with all subjection.
nt/1-tim/2.12: But I suffer not a woman to teach, nor to usurp authority over the man, but to be in silence.
nt/1-tim/2.13: For Adam was first formed, then Eve.
nt/1-tim/2.14: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived was in the transgression.
nt/1-tim/2.15: Notwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety.

nt/1-tim/3.1: This [italics]is[/italics] a true saying, If a man desire the office of a bishop, he desireth a good work.
nt/1-tim/3.2: A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, vigilant, sober, of good behaviour, given to hospitality, apt to teach;
nt/1-tim/3.3: Not given to wine, no striker, not greedy of filthy lucre; but patient, not a brawler, not covetous;
nt/1-tim/3.4: One that ruleth well his own house, having his children in subjection with all gravity;
nt/1-tim/3.5: (For if a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?)
nt/1-tim/3.6: Not a novice, lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil.
nt/1-tim/3.7: Moreover he must have a good report of them which are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
nt/1-tim/3.8: Likewise [italics]must[/italics] the deacons [italics]be[/italics] grave, not doubletongued, not given to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre;
nt/1-tim/3.9: Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.
nt/1-tim/3.10: And let these also first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon, being [italics]found[/italics] blameless.
nt/1-tim/3.11: Even so [italics]must their[/italics] wives [italics]be[/italics] grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things.
nt/1-tim/3.12: Let the deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.
nt/1-tim/3.13: For they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
nt/1-tim/3.14: These things write I unto thee, hoping to come unto thee shortly:
nt/1-tim/3.15: But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.
nt/1-tim/3.16: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory.

nt/1-tim/6.1: Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and [italics]his[/italics] doctrine be not blasphemed.
nt/1-tim/6.2: And they that have believing masters, let them not despise [italics]them,[/italics] because they are brethren; but rather do [italics]them[/italics] service, because they are faithful and beloved, partakers of the benefit. These things teach and exhort.
nt/1-tim/6.3: If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to wholesome words, [italics]even[/italics] the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness;
nt/1-tim/6.4: He is proud, knowing nothing, but doting about questions and strifes of words, whereof cometh envy, strife, railings, evil surmisings,
nt/1-tim/6.5: Perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds, and destitute of the truth, supposing that gain is godliness: from such withdraw thyself.
nt/1-tim/6.6: But godliness with contentment is great gain.
nt/1-tim/6.7: For we brought nothing into [italics]this[/italics] world, [italics]and it is[/italics] certain we can carry nothing out.
nt/1-tim/6.8: And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.
nt/1-tim/6.9: But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and [italics]into[/italics] many foolish and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition.
nt/1-tim/6.10: For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.
nt/1-tim/6.11: But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.
nt/1-tim/6.12: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, whereunto thou art also called, and hast professed a good profession before many witnesses.
nt/1-tim/6.13: I give thee charge in the sight of God, who quickeneth all things, and [italics]before[/italics] Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate witnessed a good confession;
nt/1-tim/6.14: That thou keep [italics]this[/italics] commandment without spot, unrebukeable, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ:
nt/1-tim/6.15: Which in his times he shall shew, [italics]who is[/italics] the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords;
nt/1-tim/6.16: Who only hath immortality, dwelling in the light which no man can approach unto; whom no man hath seen, nor can see: to whom [italics]be[/italics] honour and power everlasting. Amen.
nt/1-tim/6.17: Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;
nt/1-tim/6.18: That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate;
nt/1-tim/6.19: Laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold on eternal life.
nt/1-tim/6.20: O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy trust, avoiding profane [italics]and[/italics] vain babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called:
nt/1-tim/6.21: Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace [italics]be[/italics] with thee. Amen.

nt/2-cor/1.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy [italics]our[/italics] brother, unto the church of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints which are in all Achaia:
nt/2-cor/1.2: Grace [italics]be[/italics] to you and peace from God our Father, and [italics]from[/italics] the Lord Jesus Christ.
nt/2-cor/1.3: Blessed [italics]be[/italics] God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort;
nt/2-cor/1.4: Who comforteth us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.
nt/2-cor/1.5: For as the sufferings of Christ abound in us, so our consolation also aboundeth by Christ.
nt/2-cor/1.6: And whether we be afflicted, [italics]it is[/italics] for your consolation and salvation, which is effectual in the enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer: or whether we be comforted, [italics]it is[/italics] for your consolation and salvation.
nt/2-cor/1.7: And our hope of you [italics]is[/italics] steadfast, knowing, that as ye are partakers of the sufferings, so [italics]shall ye be[/italics] also of the consolation.
nt/2-cor/1.8: For we would not, brethren, have you ignorant of our trouble which came to us in Asia, that we were pressed out of measure, above strength, insomuch that we despaired even of life:
nt/2-cor/1.9: But we had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God which raiseth the dead:
nt/2-cor/1.10: Who delivered us from so great a death, and doth deliver: in whom we trust that he will yet deliver [italics]us;[/italics]
nt/2-cor/1.11: Ye also helping together by prayer for us, that for the gift [italics]bestowed[/italics] upon us by the means of many persons thanks may be given by many on our behalf.
nt/2-cor/1.12: For our rejoicing is this, the testimony of our conscience, that in simplicity and godly sincerity, not with fleshly wisdom, but by the grace of God, we have had our conversation in the world, and more abundantly to you-ward.
nt/2-cor/1.13: For we write none other things unto you, than what ye read or acknowledge; and I trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end;
nt/2-cor/1.14: As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also [italics]are[/italics] ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.
nt/2-cor/1.15: And in this confidence I was minded to come unto you before, that ye might have a second benefit;
nt/2-cor/1.16: And to pass by you into Macedonia, and to come again out of Macedonia unto you, and of you to be brought on my way toward Judæa.
nt/2-cor/1.17: When I therefore was thus minded, did I use lightness? or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be yea yea, and nay nay?
nt/2-cor/1.18: But [italics]as[/italics] God [italics]is[/italics] true, our word toward you was not yea and nay.
nt/2-cor/1.19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, [italics]even[/italics] by me and Silvanus and Timotheus, was not yea and nay, but in him was yea.
nt/2-cor/1.20: For all the promises of God in him [italics]are[/italics] yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us.
nt/2-cor/1.21: Now he which stablisheth us with you in Christ, and hath anointed us, [italics]is[/italics] God;
nt/2-cor/1.22: Who hath also sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit in our hearts.
nt/2-cor/1.23: Moreover I call God for a record upon my soul, that to spare you I came not as yet unto Corinth.
nt/2-cor/1.24: Not for that we have dominion over your faith, but are helpers of your joy: for by faith ye stand.

nt/2-cor/12.1: It is not expedient for me doubtless to glory. I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.
nt/2-cor/12.2: I knew a man in Christ above fourteen years ago, (whether in the body, I cannot tell; or whether out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;) such an one caught up to the third heaven.
nt/2-cor/12.3: And I knew such a man, (whether in the body, or out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;)
nt/2-cor/12.4: How that he was caught up into paradise, and heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.
nt/2-cor/12.5: Of such an one will I glory: yet of myself I will not glory, but in mine infirmities.
nt/2-cor/12.6: For though I would desire to glory, I shall not be a fool; for I will say the truth: but [italics]now[/italics] I forbear, lest any man should think of me above that which he seeth me [italics]to be,[/italics] or [italics]that[/italics] he heareth of me.
nt/2-cor/12.7: And lest I should be exalted above measure through the abundance of the revelations, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan to buffet me, lest I should be exalted above measure.
nt/2-cor/12.8: For this thing I besought the Lord thrice, that it might depart from me.
nt/2-cor/12.9: And he said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
nt/2-cor/12.10: Therefore I take pleasure in infirmities, in reproaches, in necessities, in persecutions, in distresses for Christ’s sake: for when I am weak, then am I strong.
nt/2-cor/12.11: I am become a fool in glorying; ye have compelled me: for I ought to have been commended of you: for in nothing am I behind the very chiefest apostles, though I be nothing.
nt/2-cor/12.12: Truly the signs of an apostle were wrought among you in all patience, in signs, and wonders, and mighty deeds.
nt/2-cor/12.13: For what is it wherein ye were inferior to other churches, except [italics]it be[/italics] that I myself was not burdensome to you? forgive me this wrong.
nt/2-cor/12.14: Behold, the third time I am ready to come to you; and I will not be burdensome to you: for I seek not yours, but you: for the children ought not to lay up for the parents, but the parents for the children.
nt/2-cor/12.15: And I will very gladly spend and be spent for you; though the more abundantly I love you, the less I be loved.
nt/2-cor/12.16: But be it so, I did not burden you: nevertheless, being crafty, I caught you with guile.
nt/2-cor/12.17: Did I make a gain of you by any of them whom I sent unto you?
nt/2-cor/12.18: I desired Titus, and with [italics]him[/italics] I sent a brother. Did Titus make a gain of you? walked we not in the same spirit? [italics]walked we[/italics] not in the same steps?
nt/2-cor/12.19: Again, think ye that we excuse ourselves unto you? we speak before God in Christ: but [italics]we do[/italics] all things, dearly beloved, for your edifying.
nt/2-cor/12.20: For I fear, lest, when I come, I shall not find you such as I would, and [italics]that[/italics] I shall be found unto you such as ye would not: lest [italics]there be[/italics] debates, envyings, wraths, strifes, backbitings, whisperings, swellings, tumults:
nt/2-cor/12.21: [italics]And[/italics] lest, when I come again, my God will humble me among you, and [italics]that[/italics] I shall bewail many which have sinned already, and have not repented of the uncleanness and fornication and lasciviousness which they have committed.

nt/2-cor/3.1: Do we begin again to commend ourselves? or need we, as some [italics]others,[/italics] epistles of commendation to you, or [italics]letters[/italics] of commendation from you?
nt/2-cor/3.2: Ye are our epistle written in our hearts, known and read of all men:
nt/2-cor/3.3: [italics]Forasmuch as ye are[/italics] manifestly declared to be the epistle of Christ ministered by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tables of stone, but in fleshy tables of the heart.
nt/2-cor/3.4: And such trust have we through Christ to God-ward:
nt/2-cor/3.5: Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves; but our sufficiency [italics]is[/italics] of God;
nt/2-cor/3.6: Who also hath made us able ministers of the new testament; not of the letter, but of the spirit: for the letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life.
nt/2-cor/3.7: But if the ministration of death, written [italics]and[/italics] engraven in stones, was glorious, so that the children of Israel could not steadfastly behold the face of Moses for the glory of his countenance; which [italics]glory[/italics] was to be done away:
nt/2-cor/3.8: How shall not the ministration of the spirit be rather glorious?
nt/2-cor/3.9: For if the ministration of condemnation [italics]be[/italics] glory, much more doth the ministration of righteousness exceed in glory.
nt/2-cor/3.10: For even that which was made glorious had no glory in this respect, by reason of the glory that excelleth.
nt/2-cor/3.11: For if that which is done away [italics]was[/italics] glorious, much more that which remaineth [italics]is[/italics] glorious.
nt/2-cor/3.12: Seeing then that we have such hope, we use great plainness of speech:
nt/2-cor/3.13: And not as Moses, [italics]which[/italics] put a veil over his face, that the children of Israel could not steadfastly look to the end of that which is abolished:
nt/2-cor/3.14: But their minds were blinded: for until this day remaineth the same veil untaken away in the reading of the old testament; which [italics]veil[/italics] is done away in Christ.
nt/2-cor/3.15: But even unto this day, when Moses is read, the veil is upon their heart.
nt/2-cor/3.16: Nevertheless when it shall turn to the Lord, the veil shall be taken away.
nt/2-cor/3.17: Now the Lord is that Spirit: and where the Spirit of the Lord [italics]is,[/italics] there [italics]is[/italics] liberty.
nt/2-cor/3.18: But we all, with open face beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image from glory to glory, [italics]even[/italics] as by the Spirit of the Lord.

nt/2-cor/4.1: Therefore seeing we have this ministry, as we have received mercy, we faint not;
nt/2-cor/4.2: But have renounced the hidden things of dishonesty, not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God deceitfully; but by manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every man’s conscience in the sight of God.
nt/2-cor/4.3: But if our gospel be hid, it is hid to them that are lost:
nt/2-cor/4.4: In whom the god of this world hath blinded the minds of them which believe not, lest the light of the glorious gospel of Christ, who is the image of God, should shine unto them.
nt/2-cor/4.5: For we preach not ourselves, but Christ Jesus the Lord; and ourselves your servants for Jesus’ sake.
nt/2-cor/4.6: For God, who commanded the light to shine out of darkness, hath shined in our hearts, to [italics]give[/italics] the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.
nt/2-cor/4.7: But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us.
nt/2-cor/4.8: [italics]We are[/italics] troubled on every side, yet not distressed; [italics]we are[/italics] perplexed, but not in despair;
nt/2-cor/4.9: Persecuted, but not forsaken; cast down, but not destroyed;
nt/2-cor/4.10: Always bearing about in the body the dying of the Lord Jesus, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our body.
nt/2-cor/4.11: For we which live are alway delivered unto death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our mortal flesh.
nt/2-cor/4.12: So then death worketh in us, but life in you.
nt/2-cor/4.13: We having the same spirit of faith, according as it is written, I believed, and therefore have I spoken; we also believe, and therefore speak;
nt/2-cor/4.14: Knowing that he which raised up the Lord Jesus shall raise up us also by Jesus, and shall present [italics]us[/italics] with you.
nt/2-cor/4.15: For all things [italics]are[/italics] for your sakes, that the abundant grace might through the thanksgiving of many redound to the glory of God.
nt/2-cor/4.16: For which cause we faint not; but though our outward man perish, yet the inward [italics]man[/italics] is renewed day by day.
nt/2-cor/4.17: For our light affliction, which is but for a moment, worketh for us a far more exceeding [italics]and[/italics] eternal weight of glory;
nt/2-cor/4.18: While we look not at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen: for the things which are seen [italics]are[/italics] temporal; but the things which are not seen [italics]are[/italics] eternal.

nt/2-cor/5.1: For we know that if our earthly house of [italics]this[/italics] tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
nt/2-cor/5.2: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:
nt/2-cor/5.3: If so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.
nt/2-cor/5.4: For we that are in [italics]this[/italics] tabernacle do groan, being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that mortality might be swallowed up of life.
nt/2-cor/5.5: Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing [italics]is[/italics] God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.
nt/2-cor/5.6: Therefore [italics]we are[/italics] always confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:
nt/2-cor/5.7: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)
nt/2-cor/5.8: We are confident, [italics]I say,[/italics] and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
nt/2-cor/5.9: Wherefore we labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.
nt/2-cor/5.10: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things [italics]done[/italics] in [italics]his[/italics] body, according to that he hath done, whether [italics]it be[/italics] good or bad.
nt/2-cor/5.11: Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are made manifest unto God; and I trust also are made manifest in your consciences.
nt/2-cor/5.12: For we commend not ourselves again unto you, but give you occasion to glory on our behalf, that ye may have somewhat to [italics]answer[/italics] them which glory in appearance, and not in heart.
nt/2-cor/5.13: For whether we be beside ourselves, [italics]it is[/italics] to God: or whether we be sober, [italics]it is[/italics] for your cause.
nt/2-cor/5.14: For the love of Christ constraineth us; because we thus judge, that if one died for all, then were all dead:
nt/2-cor/5.15: And [italics]that[/italics] he died for all, that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves, but unto him which died for them, and rose again.
nt/2-cor/5.16: Wherefore henceforth know we no man after the flesh: yea, though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we [italics]him[/italics] no more.
nt/2-cor/5.17: Therefore if any man [italics]be[/italics] in Christ, [italics]he is[/italics] a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.
nt/2-cor/5.18: And all things [italics]are[/italics] of God, who hath reconciled us to himself by Jesus Christ, and hath given to us the ministry of reconciliation;
nt/2-cor/5.19: To wit, that God was in Christ, reconciling the world unto himself, not imputing their trespasses unto them; and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation.
nt/2-cor/5.20: Now then we are ambassadors for Christ, as though God did beseech [italics]you[/italics] by us: we pray [italics]you[/italics] in Christ’s stead, be ye reconciled to God.
nt/2-cor/5.21: For he hath made him [italics]to be[/italics] sin for us, who knew no sin; that we might be made the righteousness of God in him.

nt/2-pet/3.1: This second epistle, beloved, I now write unto you; in [italics]both[/italics] which I stir up your pure minds by way of remembrance:
nt/2-pet/3.2: That ye may be mindful of the words which were spoken before by the holy prophets, and of the commandment of us the apostles of the Lord and Saviour:
nt/2-pet/3.3: Knowing this first, that there shall come in the last days scoffers, walking after their own lusts,
nt/2-pet/3.4: And saying, Where is the promise of his coming? for since the fathers fell asleep, all things continue as [italics]they were[/italics] from the beginning of the creation.
nt/2-pet/3.5: For this they willingly are ignorant of, that by the word of God the heavens were of old, and the earth standing out of the water and in the water:
nt/2-pet/3.6: Whereby the world that then was, being overflowed with water, perished:
nt/2-pet/3.7: But the heavens and the earth, which are now, by the same word are kept in store, reserved unto fire against the day of judgment and perdition of ungodly men.
nt/2-pet/3.8: But, beloved, be not ignorant of this one thing, that one day [italics]is[/italics] with the Lord as a thousand years, and a thousand years as one day.
nt/2-pet/3.9: The Lord is not slack concerning his promise, as some men count slackness; but is longsuffering to us-ward, not willing that any should perish, but that all should come to repentance.
nt/2-pet/3.10: But the day of the Lord will come as a thief in the night; in the which the heavens shall pass away with a great noise, and the elements shall melt with fervent heat, the earth also and the works that are therein shall be burned up.
nt/2-pet/3.11: [italics]Seeing[/italics] then [italics]that[/italics] all these things shall be dissolved, what manner [italics]of persons[/italics] ought ye to be in [italics]all[/italics] holy conversation and godliness,
nt/2-pet/3.12: Looking for and hasting unto the coming of the day of God, wherein the heavens being on fire shall be dissolved, and the elements shall melt with fervent heat?
nt/2-pet/3.13: Nevertheless we, according to his promise, look for new heavens and a new earth, wherein dwelleth righteousness.
nt/2-pet/3.14: Wherefore, beloved, seeing that ye look for such things, be diligent that ye may be found of him in peace, without spot, and blameless.
nt/2-pet/3.15: And account [italics]that[/italics] the longsuffering of our Lord [italics]is[/italics] salvation; even as our beloved brother Paul also according to the wisdom given unto him hath written unto you;
nt/2-pet/3.16: As also in all [italics]his[/italics] epistles, speaking in them of these things; in which are some things hard to be understood, which they that are unlearned and unstable wrest, as [italics]they do[/italics] also the other scriptures, unto their own destruction.
nt/2-pet/3.17: Ye therefore, beloved, seeing ye know [italics]these things[/italics] before, beware lest ye also, being led away with the error of the wicked, fall from your own steadfastness.
nt/2-pet/3.18: But grow in grace, and [italics]in[/italics] the knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. To him [italics]be[/italics] glory both now and for ever. Amen.

nt/2-tim/1.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of life which is in Christ Jesus,
nt/2-tim/1.2: To Timothy, [italics]my[/italics] dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, [italics]and[/italics] peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
nt/2-tim/1.3: I thank God, whom I serve from [italics]my[/italics] forefathers with pure conscience, that without ceasing I have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day;
nt/2-tim/1.4: Greatly desiring to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy;
nt/2-tim/1.5: When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice; and I am persuaded that in thee also.
nt/2-tim/1.6: Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands.
nt/2-tim/1.7: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
nt/2-tim/1.8: Be not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;
nt/2-tim/1.9: Who hath saved us, and called [italics]us[/italics] with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began,
nt/2-tim/1.10: But is now made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel:
nt/2-tim/1.11: Whereunto I am appointed a preacher, and an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.
nt/2-tim/1.12: For the which cause I also suffer these things: nevertheless I am not ashamed: for I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed unto him against that day.
nt/2-tim/1.13: Hold fast the form of sound words, which thou hast heard of me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
nt/2-tim/1.14: That good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the Holy Ghost which dwelleth in us.
nt/2-tim/1.15: This thou knowest, that all they which are in Asia be turned away from me; of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.
nt/2-tim/1.16: The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain:
nt/2-tim/1.17: But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found [italics]me.[/italics]
nt/2-tim/1.18: The Lord grant unto him that he may find mercy of the Lord in that day: and in how many things he ministered unto me at Ephesus, thou knowest very well.

nt/2-tim/2.1: Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
nt/2-tim/2.2: And the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses, the same commit thou to faithful men, who shall be able to teach others also.
nt/2-tim/2.3: Thou therefore endure hardness, as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.
nt/2-tim/2.4: No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of [italics]this[/italics] life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier.
nt/2-tim/2.5: And if a man also strive for masteries, [italics]yet[/italics] is he not crowned, except he strive lawfully.
nt/2-tim/2.6: The husbandman that laboureth must be first partaker of the fruits.
nt/2-tim/2.7: Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.
nt/2-tim/2.8: Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my gospel:
nt/2-tim/2.9: Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, [italics]even[/italics] unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.
nt/2-tim/2.10: Therefore I endure all things for the elect’s sakes, that they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
nt/2-tim/2.11: [italics]It is[/italics] a faithful saying: For if we be dead with [italics]him,[/italics] we shall also live with [italics]him:[/italics]
nt/2-tim/2.12: If we suffer, we shall also reign with [italics]him:[/italics] if we deny [italics]him,[/italics] he also will deny us:
nt/2-tim/2.13: If we believe not, [italics]yet[/italics] he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.
nt/2-tim/2.14: Of these things put [italics]them[/italics] in remembrance, charging [italics]them[/italics] before the Lord that they strive not about words to no profit, [italics]but[/italics] to the subverting of the hearers.
nt/2-tim/2.15: Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.
nt/2-tim/2.16: But shun profane [italics]and[/italics] vain babblings: for they will increase unto more ungodliness.
nt/2-tim/2.17: And their word will eat as doth a canker: of whom is Hymenæus and Philetus;
nt/2-tim/2.18: Who concerning the truth have erred, saying that the resurrection is past already; and overthrow the faith of some.
nt/2-tim/2.19: Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity.
nt/2-tim/2.20: But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of earth; and some to honour, and some to dishonour.
nt/2-tim/2.21: If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified, and meet for the master’s use, [italics]and[/italics] prepared unto every good work.
nt/2-tim/2.22: Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace, with them that call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
nt/2-tim/2.23: But foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they do gender strifes.
nt/2-tim/2.24: And the servant of the Lord must not strive; but be gentle unto all [italics]men,[/italics] apt to teach, patient,
nt/2-tim/2.25: In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;
nt/2-tim/2.26: And [italics]that[/italics] they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil, who are taken captive by him at his will.

nt/2-tim/4.1: I charge [italics]thee[/italics] therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;
nt/2-tim/4.2: Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine.
nt/2-tim/4.3: For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears;
nt/2-tim/4.4: And they shall turn away [italics]their[/italics] ears from the truth, and shall be turned unto fables.
nt/2-tim/4.5: But watch thou in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, make full proof of thy ministry.
nt/2-tim/4.6: For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.
nt/2-tim/4.7: I have fought a good fight, I have finished [italics]my[/italics] course, I have kept the faith:
nt/2-tim/4.8: Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.
nt/2-tim/4.9: Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:
nt/2-tim/4.10: For Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world, and is departed unto Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia.
nt/2-tim/4.11: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.
nt/2-tim/4.12: And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.
nt/2-tim/4.13: The cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring [italics]with thee,[/italics] and the books, [italics]but[/italics] especially the parchments.
nt/2-tim/4.14: Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his works:
nt/2-tim/4.15: Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly withstood our words.
nt/2-tim/4.16: At my first answer no man stood with me, but all [italics]men[/italics] forsook me: [italics]I pray God[/italics] that it may not be laid to their charge.
nt/2-tim/4.17: Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known, and [italics]that[/italics] all the Gentiles might hear: and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
nt/2-tim/4.18: And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve [italics]me[/italics] unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom [italics]be[/italics] glory for ever and ever. Amen.
nt/2-tim/4.19: Salute Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
nt/2-tim/4.20: Erastus abode at Corinth: but Trophimus have I left at Miletum sick.
nt/2-tim/4.21: Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.
nt/2-tim/4.22: The Lord Jesus Christ [italics]be[/italics] with thy spirit. Grace [italics]be[/italics] with you. Amen.

nt/acts/1.1: The former treatise have I made, O Theophilus, of all that Jesus began both to do and teach,
nt/acts/1.2: Until the day in which he was taken up, after that he through the Holy Ghost had given commandments unto the apostles whom he had chosen:
nt/acts/1.3: To whom also he shewed himself alive after his passion by many infallible proofs, being seen of them forty days, and speaking of the things pertaining to the kingdom of God:
nt/acts/1.4: And, being assembled together with [italics]them,[/italics] commanded them that they should not depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which, [italics]saith he,[/italics] ye have heard of me.
nt/acts/1.5: For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence.
nt/acts/1.6: When they therefore were come together, they asked of him, saying, Lord, wilt thou at this time restore again the kingdom to Israel?
nt/acts/1.7: And he said unto them, It is not for you to know the times or the seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power.
nt/acts/1.8: But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judæa, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.
nt/acts/1.9: And when he had spoken these things, while they beheld, he was taken up; and a cloud received him out of their sight.
nt/acts/1.10: And while they looked steadfastly toward heaven as he went up, behold, two men stood by them in white apparel;
nt/acts/1.11: Which also said, Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into heaven? this same Jesus, which is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go into heaven.
nt/acts/1.12: Then returned they unto Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is from Jerusalem a sabbath day’s journey.
nt/acts/1.13: And when they were come in, they went up into an upper room, where abode both Peter, and James, and John, and Andrew, Philip, and Thomas, Bartholomew, and Matthew, James [italics]the son[/italics] of Alphæus, and Simon Zelotes, and Judas [italics]the brother[/italics] of James.
nt/acts/1.14: These all continued with one accord in prayer and supplication, with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brethren.
nt/acts/1.15: ¶ And in those days Peter stood up in the midst of the disciples, and said, (the number of names together were about an hundred and twenty,)
nt/acts/1.16: Men [italics]and[/italics] brethren, this scripture must needs have been fulfilled, which the Holy Ghost by the mouth of David spake before concerning Judas, which was guide to them that took Jesus.
nt/acts/1.17: For he was numbered with us, and had obtained part of this ministry.
nt/acts/1.18: Now this man purchased a field with the reward of iniquity; and falling headlong, he burst asunder in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.
nt/acts/1.19: And it was known unto all the dwellers at Jerusalem; insomuch as that field is called in their proper tongue, Aceldama, that is to say, The field of blood.
nt/acts/1.20: For it is written in the book of Psalms, Let his habitation be desolate, and let no man dwell therein: and his bishoprick let another take.
nt/acts/1.21: Wherefore of these men which have companied with us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,
nt/acts/1.22: Beginning from the baptism of John, unto that same day that he was taken up from us, must one be ordained to be a witness with us of his resurrection.
nt/acts/1.23: And they appointed two, Joseph called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.
nt/acts/1.24: And they prayed, and said, Thou, Lord, which knowest the hearts of all [italics]men,[/italics] shew whether of these two thou hast chosen,
nt/acts/1.25: That he may take part of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas by transgression fell, that he might go to his own place.
nt/acts/1.26: And they gave forth their lots; and the lot fell upon Matthias; and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.
