nt/acts/10.1: There was a certain man in Cæsarea called Cornelius, a centurion of the band called the Italian [italics]band,[/italics]
nt/acts/10.2: [italics]A[/italics] devout [italics]man,[/italics] and one that feared God with all his house, which gave much alms to the people, and prayed to God alway.
nt/acts/10.3: He saw in a vision evidently about the ninth hour of the day an angel of God coming in to him, and saying unto him, Cornelius.
nt/acts/10.4: And when he looked on him, he was afraid, and said, What is it, Lord? And he said unto him, Thy prayers and thine alms are come up for a memorial before God.
nt/acts/10.5: And now send men to Joppa, and call for [italics]one[/italics] Simon, whose surname is Peter:
nt/acts/10.6: He lodgeth with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea side: he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to do.
nt/acts/10.7: And when the angel which spake unto Cornelius was departed, he called two of his household servants, and a devout soldier of them that waited on him continually;
nt/acts/10.8: And when he had declared all [italics]these[/italics] things unto them, he sent them to Joppa.
nt/acts/10.9: ¶ On the morrow, as they went on their journey, and drew nigh unto the city, Peter went up upon the housetop to pray about the sixth hour:
nt/acts/10.10: And he became very hungry, and would have eaten: but while they made ready, he fell into a trance,
nt/acts/10.11: And saw heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending unto him, as it had been a great sheet knit at the four corners, and let down to the earth:
nt/acts/10.12: Wherein were all manner of fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
nt/acts/10.13: And there came a voice to him, Rise, Peter; kill, and eat.
nt/acts/10.14: But Peter said, Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten any thing that is common or unclean.
nt/acts/10.15: And the voice [italics]spake[/italics] unto him again the second time, What God hath cleansed, [italics]that[/italics] call not thou common.
nt/acts/10.16: This was done thrice: and the vessel was received up again into heaven.
nt/acts/10.17: Now while Peter doubted in himself what this vision which he had seen should mean, behold, the men which were sent from Cornelius had made inquiry for Simon’s house, and stood before the gate,
nt/acts/10.18: And called, and asked whether Simon, which was surnamed Peter, were lodged there.
nt/acts/10.19: ¶ While Peter thought on the vision, the Spirit said unto him, Behold, three men seek thee.
nt/acts/10.20: Arise therefore, and get thee down, and go with them, doubting nothing: for I have sent them.
nt/acts/10.21: Then Peter went down to the men which were sent unto him from Cornelius; and said, Behold, I am he whom ye seek: what [italics]is[/italics] the cause wherefore ye are come?
nt/acts/10.22: And they said, Cornelius the centurion, a just man, and one that feareth God, and of good report among all the nation of the Jews, was warned from God by an holy angel to send for thee into his house, and to hear words of thee.
nt/acts/10.23: Then called he them in, and lodged [italics]them.[/italics] And on the morrow Peter went away with them, and certain brethren from Joppa accompanied him.
nt/acts/10.24: And the morrow after they entered into Cæsarea. And Cornelius waited for them, and had called together his kinsmen and near friends.
nt/acts/10.25: And as Peter was coming in, Cornelius met him, and fell down at his feet, and worshipped [italics]him.[/italics]
nt/acts/10.26: But Peter took him up, saying, Stand up; I myself also am a man.
nt/acts/10.27: And as he talked with him, he went in, and found many that were come together.
nt/acts/10.28: And he said unto them, Ye know how that it is an unlawful thing for a man that is a Jew to keep company, or come unto one of another nation; but God hath shewed me that I should not call any man common or unclean.
nt/acts/10.29: Therefore came I [italics]unto you[/italics] without gainsaying, as soon as I was sent for: I ask therefore for what intent ye have sent for me?
nt/acts/10.30: And Cornelius said, Four days ago I was fasting until this hour; and at the ninth hour I prayed in my house, and, behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,
nt/acts/10.31: And said, Cornelius, thy prayer is heard, and thine alms are had in remembrance in the sight of God.
nt/acts/10.32: Send therefore to Joppa, and call hither Simon, whose surname is Peter; he is lodged in the house of [italics]one[/italics] Simon a tanner by the sea side: who, when he cometh, shall speak unto thee.
nt/acts/10.33: Immediately therefore I sent to thee; and thou hast well done that thou art come. Now therefore are we all here present before God, to hear all things that are commanded thee of God.
nt/acts/10.34: ¶ Then Peter opened [italics]his[/italics] mouth, and said, Of a truth I perceive that God is no respecter of persons:
nt/acts/10.35: But in every nation he that feareth him, and worketh righteousness, is accepted with him.
nt/acts/10.36: The word which [italics]God[/italics] sent unto the children of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ: (he is Lord of all:)
nt/acts/10.37: That word, [italics]I say,[/italics] ye know, which was published throughout all Judæa, and began from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;
nt/acts/10.38: How God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Ghost and with power: who went about doing good, and healing all that were oppressed of the devil; for God was with him.
nt/acts/10.39: And we are witnesses of all things which he did both in the land of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they slew and hanged on a tree:
nt/acts/10.40: Him God raised up the third day, and shewed him openly;
nt/acts/10.41: Not to all the people, but unto witnesses chosen before of God, [italics]even[/italics] to us, who did eat and drink with him after he rose from the dead.
nt/acts/10.42: And he commanded us to preach unto the people, and to testify that it is he which was ordained of God [italics]to be[/italics] the Judge of quick and dead.
nt/acts/10.43: To him give all the prophets witness, that through his name whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins.
nt/acts/10.44: ¶ While Peter yet spake these words, the Holy Ghost fell on all them which heard the word.
nt/acts/10.45: And they of the circumcision which believed were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because that on the Gentiles also was poured out the gift of the Holy Ghost.
nt/acts/10.46: For they heard them speak with tongues, and magnify God. Then answered Peter,
nt/acts/10.47: Can any man forbid water, that these should not be baptized, which have received the Holy Ghost as well as we?
nt/acts/10.48: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of the Lord. Then prayed they him to tarry certain days.

nt/acts/11.1: And the apostles and brethren that were in Judæa heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.
nt/acts/11.2: And when Peter was come up to Jerusalem, they that were of the circumcision contended with him,
nt/acts/11.3: Saying, Thou wentest in to men uncircumcised, and didst eat with them.
nt/acts/11.4: But Peter rehearsed [italics]the matter[/italics] from the beginning, and expounded [italics]it[/italics] by order unto them, saying,
nt/acts/11.5: I was in the city of Joppa praying: and in a trance I saw a vision, A certain vessel descend, as it had been a great sheet, let down from heaven by four corners; and it came even to me:
nt/acts/11.6: Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
nt/acts/11.7: And I heard a voice saying unto me, Arise, Peter; slay and eat.
nt/acts/11.8: But I said, Not so, Lord: for nothing common or unclean hath at any time entered into my mouth.
nt/acts/11.9: But the voice answered me again from heaven, What God hath cleansed, [italics]that[/italics] call not thou common.
nt/acts/11.10: And this was done three times: and all were drawn up again into heaven.
nt/acts/11.11: And, behold, immediately there were three men already come unto the house where I was, sent from Cæsarea unto me.
nt/acts/11.12: And the Spirit bade me go with them, nothing doubting. Moreover these six brethren accompanied me, and we entered into the man’s house:
nt/acts/11.13: And he shewed us how he had seen an angel in his house, which stood and said unto him, Send men to Joppa, and call for Simon, whose surname is Peter;
nt/acts/11.14: Who shall tell thee words, whereby thou and all thy house shall be saved.
nt/acts/11.15: And as I began to speak, the Holy Ghost fell on them, as on us at the beginning.
nt/acts/11.16: Then remembered I the word of the Lord, how that he said, John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.
nt/acts/11.17: Forasmuch then as God gave them the like gift as [italics]he did[/italics] unto us, who believed on the Lord Jesus Christ; what was I, that I could withstand God?
nt/acts/11.18: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, Then hath God also to the Gentiles granted repentance unto life.
nt/acts/11.19: ¶ Now they which were scattered abroad upon the persecution that arose about Stephen travelled as far as Phenice, and Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to none but unto the Jews only.
nt/acts/11.20: And some of them were men of Cyprus and Cyrene, which, when they were come to Antioch, spake unto the Grecians, preaching the Lord Jesus.
nt/acts/11.21: And the hand of the Lord was with them: and a great number believed, and turned unto the Lord.
nt/acts/11.22: ¶ Then tidings of these things came unto the ears of the church which was in Jerusalem: and they sent forth Barnabas, that he should go as far as Antioch.
nt/acts/11.23: Who, when he came, and had seen the grace of God, was glad, and exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the Lord.
nt/acts/11.24: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Ghost and of faith: and much people was added unto the Lord.
nt/acts/11.25: Then departed Barnabas to Tarsus, for to seek Saul:
nt/acts/11.26: And when he had found him, he brought him unto Antioch. And it came to pass, that a whole year they assembled themselves with the church, and taught much people. And the disciples were called Christians first in Antioch.
nt/acts/11.27: ¶ And in these days came prophets from Jerusalem unto Antioch.
nt/acts/11.28: And there stood up one of them named Agabus, and signified by the Spirit that there should be great dearth throughout all the world: which came to pass in the days of Claudius Cæsar.
nt/acts/11.29: Then the disciples, every man according to his ability, determined to send relief unto the brethren which dwelt in Judæa:
nt/acts/11.30: Which also they did, and sent it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

nt/acts/13.1: Now there were in the church that was at Antioch certain prophets and teachers; as Barnabas, and Simeon that was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen, which had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
nt/acts/13.2: As they ministered to the Lord, and fasted, the Holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have called them.
nt/acts/13.3: And when they had fasted and prayed, and laid [italics]their[/italics] hands on them, they sent [italics]them[/italics] away.
nt/acts/13.4: ¶ So they, being sent forth by the Holy Ghost, departed unto Seleucia; and from thence they sailed to Cyprus.
nt/acts/13.5: And when they were at Salamis, they preached the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews: and they had also John to [italics]their[/italics] minister.
nt/acts/13.6: And when they had gone through the isle unto Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name [italics]was[/italics] Bar-jesus:
nt/acts/13.7: Which was with the deputy of the country, Sergius Paulus, a prudent man; who called for Barnabas and Saul, and desired to hear the word of God.
nt/acts/13.8: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn away the deputy from the faith.
nt/acts/13.9: Then Saul, (who also [italics]is called[/italics] Paul,) filled with the Holy Ghost, set his eyes on him,
nt/acts/13.10: And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, [italics]thou[/italics] child of the devil, [italics]thou[/italics] enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?
nt/acts/13.11: And now, behold, the hand of the Lord [italics]is[/italics] upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun for a season. And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness; and he went about seeking some to lead him by the hand.
nt/acts/13.12: Then the deputy, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the doctrine of the Lord.
nt/acts/13.13: Now when Paul and his company loosed from Paphos, they came to Perga in Pamphylia: and John departing from them returned to Jerusalem.
nt/acts/13.14: ¶ But when they departed from Perga, they came to Antioch in Pisidia, and went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and sat down.
nt/acts/13.15: And after the reading of the law and the prophets the rulers of the synagogue sent unto them, saying, [italics]Ye[/italics] men [italics]and[/italics] brethren, if ye have any word of exhortation for the people, say on.
nt/acts/13.16: Then Paul stood up, and beckoning with [italics]his[/italics] hand said, Men of Israel, and ye that fear God, give audience.
nt/acts/13.17: The God of this people of Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they dwelt as strangers in the land of Egypt, and with an high arm brought he them out of it.
nt/acts/13.18: And about the time of forty years suffered he their manners in the wilderness.
nt/acts/13.19: And when he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Chanaan, he divided their land to them by lot.
nt/acts/13.20: And after that he gave [italics]unto them[/italics] judges about the space of four hundred and fifty years, until Samuel the prophet.
nt/acts/13.21: And afterward they desired a king: and God gave unto them Saul the son of Cis, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, by the space of forty years.
nt/acts/13.22: And when he had removed him, he raised up unto them David to be their king; to whom also he gave testimony, and said, I have found David the [italics]son[/italics] of Jesse, a man after mine own heart, which shall fulfil all my will.
nt/acts/13.23: Of this man’s seed hath God according to [italics]his[/italics] promise raised unto Israel a Saviour, Jesus:
nt/acts/13.24: When John had first preached before his coming the baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
nt/acts/13.25: And as John fulfilled his course, he said, Whom think ye that I am? I am not [italics]he.[/italics] But, behold, there cometh one after me, whose shoes of [italics]his[/italics] feet I am not worthy to loose.
nt/acts/13.26: Men [italics]and[/italics] brethren, children of the stock of Abraham, and whosoever among you feareth God, to you is the word of this salvation sent.
nt/acts/13.27: For they that dwell at Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they knew him not, nor yet the voices of the prophets which are read every sabbath day, they have fulfilled [italics]them[/italics] in condemning [italics]him.[/italics]
nt/acts/13.28: And though they found no cause of death [italics]in him,[/italics] yet desired they Pilate that he should be slain.
nt/acts/13.29: And when they had fulfilled all that was written of him, they took [italics]him[/italics] down from the tree, and laid [italics]him[/italics] in a sepulchre.
nt/acts/13.30: But God raised him from the dead:
nt/acts/13.31: And he was seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses unto the people.
nt/acts/13.32: And we declare unto you glad tidings, how that the promise which was made unto the fathers,
nt/acts/13.33: God hath fulfilled the same unto us their children, in that he hath raised up Jesus again; as it is also written in the second psalm, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee.
nt/acts/13.34: And as concerning that he raised him up from the dead, [italics]now[/italics] no more to return to corruption, he said on this wise, I will give you the sure mercies of David.
nt/acts/13.35: Wherefore he saith also in another [italics]psalm,[/italics] Thou shalt not suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
nt/acts/13.36: For David, after he had served his own generation by the will of God, fell on sleep, and was laid unto his fathers, and saw corruption:
nt/acts/13.37: But he, whom God raised again, saw no corruption.
nt/acts/13.38: ¶ Be it known unto you therefore, men [italics]and[/italics] brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins:
nt/acts/13.39: And by him all that believe are justified from all things, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses.
nt/acts/13.40: Beware therefore, lest that come upon you, which is spoken of in the prophets;
nt/acts/13.41: Behold, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish: for I work a work in your days, a work which ye shall in no wise believe, though a man declare it unto you.
nt/acts/13.42: And when the Jews were gone out of the synagogue, the Gentiles besought that these words might be preached to them the next sabbath.
nt/acts/13.43: Now when the congregation was broken up, many of the Jews and religious proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas: who, speaking to them, persuaded them to continue in the grace of God.
nt/acts/13.44: ¶ And the next sabbath day came almost the whole city together to hear the word of God.
nt/acts/13.45: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with envy, and spake against those things which were spoken by Paul, contradicting and blaspheming.
nt/acts/13.46: Then Paul and Barnabas waxed bold, and said, It was necessary that the word of God should first have been spoken to you: but seeing ye put it from you, and judge yourselves unworthy of everlasting life, lo, we turn to the Gentiles.
nt/acts/13.47: For so hath the Lord commanded us, [italics]saying,[/italics] I have set thee to be a light of the Gentiles, that thou shouldest be for salvation unto the ends of the earth.
nt/acts/13.48: And when the Gentiles heard this, they were glad, and glorified the word of the Lord: and as many as were ordained to eternal life believed.
nt/acts/13.49: And the word of the Lord was published throughout all the region.
nt/acts/13.50: But the Jews stirred up the devout and honourable women, and the chief men of the city, and raised persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them out of their coasts.
nt/acts/13.51: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came unto Iconium.
nt/acts/13.52: And the disciples were filled with joy, and with the Holy Ghost.

nt/acts/19.1: And it came to pass, that, while Apollos was at Corinth, Paul having passed through the upper coasts came to Ephesus: and finding certain disciples,
nt/acts/19.2: He said unto them, Have ye received the Holy Ghost since ye believed? And they said unto him, We have not so much as heard whether there be any Holy Ghost.
nt/acts/19.3: And he said unto them, Unto what then were ye baptized? And they said, Unto John’s baptism.
nt/acts/19.4: Then said Paul, John verily baptized with the baptism of repentance, saying unto the people, that they should believe on him which should come after him, that is, on Christ Jesus.
nt/acts/19.5: When they heard [italics]this,[/italics] they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
nt/acts/19.6: And when Paul had laid [italics]his[/italics] hands upon them, the Holy Ghost came on them; and they spake with tongues, and prophesied.
nt/acts/19.7: And all the men were about twelve.
nt/acts/19.8: And he went into the synagogue, and spake boldly for the space of three months, disputing and persuading the things concerning the kingdom of God.
nt/acts/19.9: But when divers were hardened, and believed not, but spake evil of that way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, disputing daily in the school of one Tyrannus.
nt/acts/19.10: And this continued by the space of two years; so that all they which dwelt in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks.
nt/acts/19.11: And God wrought special miracles by the hands of Paul:
nt/acts/19.12: So that from his body were brought unto the sick handkerchiefs or aprons, and the diseases departed from them, and the evil spirits went out of them.
nt/acts/19.13: ¶ Then certain of the vagabond Jews, exorcists, took upon them to call over them which had evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preacheth.
nt/acts/19.14: And there were seven sons of [italics]one[/italics] Sceva, a Jew, [italics]and[/italics] chief of the priests, which did so.
nt/acts/19.15: And the evil spirit answered and said, Jesus I know, and Paul I know; but who are ye?
nt/acts/19.16: And the man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them, and overcame them, and prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.
nt/acts/19.17: And this was known to all the Jews and Greeks also dwelling at Ephesus; and fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.
nt/acts/19.18: And many that believed came, and confessed, and shewed their deeds.
nt/acts/19.19: Many of them also which used curious arts brought their books together, and burned them before all [italics]men:[/italics] and they counted the price of them, and found [italics]it[/italics] fifty thousand [italics]pieces[/italics] of silver.
nt/acts/19.20: So mightily grew the word of God and prevailed.
nt/acts/19.21: ¶ After these things were ended, Paul purposed in the spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying, After I have been there, I must also see Rome.
nt/acts/19.22: So he sent into Macedonia two of them that ministered unto him, Timotheus and Erastus; but he himself stayed in Asia for a season.
nt/acts/19.23: And the same time there arose no small stir about that way.
nt/acts/19.24: For a certain [italics]man[/italics] named Demetrius, a silversmith, which made silver shrines for Diana, brought no small gain unto the craftsmen;
nt/acts/19.25: Whom he called together with the workmen of like occupation, and said, Sirs, ye know that by this craft we have our wealth.
nt/acts/19.26: Moreover ye see and hear, that not alone at Ephesus, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul hath persuaded and turned away much people, saying that they be no gods, which are made with hands:
nt/acts/19.27: So that not only this our craft is in danger to be set at nought; but also that the temple of the great goddess Diana should be despised, and her magnificence should be destroyed, whom all Asia and the world worshippeth.
nt/acts/19.28: And when they heard [italics]these sayings,[/italics] they were full of wrath, and cried out, saying, Great [italics]is[/italics] Diana of the Ephesians.
nt/acts/19.29: And the whole city was filled with confusion: and having caught Gaius and Aristarchus, men of Macedonia, Paul’s companions in travel, they rushed with one accord into the theatre.
nt/acts/19.30: And when Paul would have entered in unto the people, the disciples suffered him not.
nt/acts/19.31: And certain of the chief of Asia, which were his friends, sent unto him, desiring [italics]him[/italics] that he would not adventure himself into the theatre.
nt/acts/19.32: Some therefore cried one thing, and some another: for the assembly was confused; and the more part knew not wherefore they were come together.
nt/acts/19.33: And they drew Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. And Alexander beckoned with the hand, and would have made his defence unto the people.
nt/acts/19.34: But when they knew that he was a Jew, all with one voice about the space of two hours cried out, Great [italics]is[/italics] Diana of the Ephesians.
nt/acts/19.35: And when the townclerk had appeased the people, he said, [italics]Ye[/italics] men of Ephesus, what man is there that knoweth not how that the city of the Ephesians is a worshipper of the great goddess Diana, and of the [italics]image[/italics] which fell down from Jupiter?
nt/acts/19.36: Seeing then that these things cannot be spoken against, ye ought to be quiet, and to do nothing rashly.
nt/acts/19.37: For ye have brought hither these men, which are neither robbers of churches, nor yet blasphemers of your goddess.
nt/acts/19.38: Wherefore if Demetrius, and the craftsmen which are with him, have a matter against any man, the law is open, and there are deputies: let them implead one another.
nt/acts/19.39: But if ye inquire any thing concerning other matters, it shall be determined in a lawful assembly.
nt/acts/19.40: For we are in danger to be called in question for this day’s uproar, there being no cause whereby we may give an account of this concourse.
nt/acts/19.41: And when he had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.

nt/acts/2.1: And when the day of Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place.
nt/acts/2.2: And suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.
nt/acts/2.3: And there appeared unto them cloven tongues like as of fire, and it sat upon each of them.
nt/acts/2.4: And they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.
nt/acts/2.5: And there were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, devout men, out of every nation under heaven.
nt/acts/2.6: Now when this was noised abroad, the multitude came together, and were confounded, because that every man heard them speak in his own language.
nt/acts/2.7: And they were all amazed and marvelled, saying one to another, Behold, are not all these which speak Galilæans?
nt/acts/2.8: And how hear we every man in our own tongue, wherein we were born?
nt/acts/2.9: Parthians, and Medes, and Elamites, and the dwellers in Mesopotamia, and in Judæa, and Cappadocia, in Pontus, and Asia,
nt/acts/2.10: Phrygia, and Pamphylia, in Egypt, and in the parts of Libya about Cyrene, and strangers of Rome, Jews and proselytes,
nt/acts/2.11: Cretes and Arabians, we do hear them speak in our tongues the wonderful works of God.
nt/acts/2.12: And they were all amazed, and were in doubt, saying one to another, What meaneth this?
nt/acts/2.13: Others mocking said, These men are full of new wine.
nt/acts/2.14: ¶ But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and said unto them, Ye men of Judæa, and all [italics]ye[/italics] that dwell at Jerusalem, be this known unto you, and hearken to my words:
nt/acts/2.15: For these are not drunken, as ye suppose, seeing it is [italics]but[/italics] the third hour of the day.
nt/acts/2.16: But this is that which was spoken by the prophet Joel;
nt/acts/2.17: And it shall come to pass in the last days, saith God, I will pour out of my Spirit upon all flesh: and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams:
nt/acts/2.18: And on my servants and on my handmaidens I will pour out in those days of my Spirit; and they shall prophesy:
nt/acts/2.19: And I will shew wonders in heaven above, and signs in the earth beneath; blood, and fire, and vapour of smoke:
nt/acts/2.20: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before that great and notable day of the Lord come:
nt/acts/2.21: And it shall come to pass, [italics]that[/italics] whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.
nt/acts/2.22: Ye men of Israel, hear these words; Jesus of Nazareth, a man approved of God among you by miracles and wonders and signs, which God did by him in the midst of you, as ye yourselves also know:
nt/acts/2.23: Him, being delivered by the determinate counsel and foreknowledge of God, ye have taken, and by wicked hands have crucified and slain:
nt/acts/2.24: Whom God hath raised up, having loosed the pains of death: because it was not possible that he should be holden of it.
nt/acts/2.25: For David speaketh concerning him, I foresaw the Lord always before my face, for he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved:
nt/acts/2.26: Therefore did my heart rejoice, and my tongue was glad; moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope:
nt/acts/2.27: Because thou wilt not leave my soul in hell, neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
nt/acts/2.28: Thou hast made known to me the ways of life; thou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance.
nt/acts/2.29: Men [italics]and[/italics] brethren, let me freely speak unto you of the patriarch David, that he is both dead and buried, and his sepulchre is with us unto this day.
nt/acts/2.30: Therefore being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him, that of the fruit of his loins, according to the flesh, he would raise up Christ to sit on his throne;
nt/acts/2.31: He seeing this before spake of the resurrection of Christ, that his soul was not left in hell, neither his flesh did see corruption.
nt/acts/2.32: This Jesus hath God raised up, whereof we all are witnesses.
nt/acts/2.33: Therefore being by the right hand of God exalted, and having received of the Father the promise of the Holy Ghost, he hath shed forth this, which ye now see and hear.
nt/acts/2.34: For David is not ascended into the heavens: but he saith himself, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
nt/acts/2.35: Until I make thy foes thy footstool.
nt/acts/2.36: Therefore let all the house of Israel know assuredly, that God hath made that same Jesus, whom ye have crucified, both Lord and Christ.
nt/acts/2.37: ¶ Now when they heard [italics]this,[/italics] they were pricked in their heart, and said unto Peter and to the rest of the apostles, Men [italics]and[/italics] brethren, what shall we do?
nt/acts/2.38: Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost.
nt/acts/2.39: For the promise is unto you, and to your children, and to all that are afar off, [italics]even[/italics] as many as the Lord our God shall call.
nt/acts/2.40: And with many other words did he testify and exhort, saying, Save yourselves from this untoward generation.
nt/acts/2.41: ¶ Then they that gladly received his word were baptized: and the same day there were added [italics]unto them[/italics] about three thousand souls.
nt/acts/2.42: And they continued steadfastly in the apostles’ doctrine and fellowship, and in breaking of bread, and in prayers.
nt/acts/2.43: And fear came upon every soul: and many wonders and signs were done by the apostles.
nt/acts/2.44: And all that believed were together, and had all things common;
nt/acts/2.45: And sold their possessions and goods, and parted them to all [italics]men,[/italics] as every man had need.
nt/acts/2.46: And they, continuing daily with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread from house to house, did eat their meat with gladness and singleness of heart,
nt/acts/2.47: Praising God, and having favour with all the people. And the Lord added to the church daily such as should be saved.

nt/acts/3.1: Now Peter and John went up together into the temple at the hour of prayer, [italics]being[/italics] the ninth [italics]hour.[/italics]
nt/acts/3.2: And a certain man lame from his mother’s womb was carried, whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask alms of them that entered into the temple;
nt/acts/3.3: Who seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple asked an alms.
nt/acts/3.4: And Peter, fastening his eyes upon him with John, said, Look on us.
nt/acts/3.5: And he gave heed unto them, expecting to receive something of them.
nt/acts/3.6: Then Peter said, Silver and gold have I none; but such as I have give I thee: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.
nt/acts/3.7: And he took him by the right hand, and lifted [italics]him[/italics] up: and immediately his feet and ankle bones received strength.
nt/acts/3.8: And he leaping up stood, and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking, and leaping, and praising God.
nt/acts/3.9: And all the people saw him walking and praising God:
nt/acts/3.10: And they knew that it was he which sat for alms at the Beautiful gate of the temple: and they were filled with wonder and amazement at that which had happened unto him.
nt/acts/3.11: And as the lame man which was healed held Peter and John, all the people ran together unto them in the porch that is called Solomon’s, greatly wondering.
nt/acts/3.12: ¶ And when Peter saw [italics]it,[/italics] he answered unto the people, Ye men of Israel, why marvel ye at this? or why look ye so earnestly on us, as though by our own power or holiness we had made this man to walk?
nt/acts/3.13: The God of Abraham, and of Isaac, and of Jacob, the God of our fathers, hath glorified his Son Jesus; whom ye delivered up, and denied him in the presence of Pilate, when he was determined to let [italics]him[/italics] go.
nt/acts/3.14: But ye denied the Holy One and the Just, and desired a murderer to be granted unto you;
nt/acts/3.15: And killed the Prince of life, whom God hath raised from the dead; whereof we are witnesses.
nt/acts/3.16: And his name through faith in his name hath made this man strong, whom ye see and know: yea, the faith which is by him hath given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.
nt/acts/3.17: And now, brethren, I wot that through ignorance ye did [italics]it,[/italics] as [italics]did[/italics] also your rulers.
nt/acts/3.18: But those things, which God before had shewed by the mouth of all his prophets, that Christ should suffer, he hath so fulfilled.
nt/acts/3.19: ¶ Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out, when the times of refreshing shall come from the presence of the Lord;
nt/acts/3.20: And he shall send Jesus Christ, which before was preached unto you:
nt/acts/3.21: Whom the heaven must receive until the times of restitution of all things, which God hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began.
nt/acts/3.22: For Moses truly said unto the fathers, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you.
nt/acts/3.23: And it shall come to pass, [italics]that[/italics] every soul, which will not hear that prophet, shall be destroyed from among the people.
nt/acts/3.24: Yea, and all the prophets from Samuel and those that follow after, as many as have spoken, have likewise foretold of these days.
nt/acts/3.25: Ye are the children of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying unto Abraham, And in thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.
nt/acts/3.26: Unto you first God, having raised up his Son Jesus, sent him to bless you, in turning away every one of you from his iniquities.

nt/acts/4.1: And as they spake unto the people, the priests, and the captain of the temple, and the Sadducees, came upon them,
nt/acts/4.2: Being grieved that they taught the people, and preached through Jesus the resurrection from the dead.
nt/acts/4.3: And they laid hands on them, and put [italics]them[/italics] in hold unto the next day: for it was now eventide.
nt/acts/4.4: Howbeit many of them which heard the word believed; and the number of the men was about five thousand.
nt/acts/4.5: ¶ And it came to pass on the morrow, that their rulers, and elders, and scribes,
nt/acts/4.6: And Annas the high priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the kindred of the high priest, were gathered together at Jerusalem.
nt/acts/4.7: And when they had set them in the midst, they asked, By what power, or by what name, have ye done this?
nt/acts/4.8: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Ghost, said unto them, Ye rulers of the people, and elders of Israel,
nt/acts/4.9: If we this day be examined of the good deed done to the impotent man, by what means he is made whole;
nt/acts/4.10: Be it known unto you all, and to all the people of Israel, that by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom ye crucified, whom God raised from the dead, [italics]even[/italics] by him doth this man stand here before you whole.
nt/acts/4.11: This is the stone which was set at nought of you builders, which is become the head of the corner.
nt/acts/4.12: Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved.
nt/acts/4.13: ¶ Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marvelled; and they took knowledge of them, that they had been with Jesus.
nt/acts/4.14: And beholding the man which was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it.
nt/acts/4.15: But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves,
nt/acts/4.16: Saying, What shall we do to these men? for that indeed a notable miracle hath been done by them [italics]is[/italics] manifest to all them that dwell in Jerusalem; and we cannot deny [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/acts/4.17: But that it spread no further among the people, let us straitly threaten them, that they speak henceforth to no man in this name.
nt/acts/4.18: And they called them, and commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.
nt/acts/4.19: But Peter and John answered and said unto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God to hearken unto you more than unto God, judge ye.
nt/acts/4.20: For we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.
nt/acts/4.21: So when they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people: for all [italics]men[/italics] glorified God for that which was done.
nt/acts/4.22: For the man was above forty years old, on whom this miracle of healing was shewed.
nt/acts/4.23: ¶ And being let go, they went to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said unto them.
nt/acts/4.24: And when they heard that, they lifted up their voice to God with one accord, and said, Lord, thou [italics]art[/italics] God, which hast made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all that in them is:
nt/acts/4.25: Who by the mouth of thy servant David hast said, Why did the heathen rage, and the people imagine vain things?
nt/acts/4.26: The kings of the earth stood up, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord, and against his Christ.
nt/acts/4.27: For of a truth against thy holy child Jesus, whom thou hast anointed, both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles, and the people of Israel, were gathered together,
nt/acts/4.28: For to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done.
nt/acts/4.29: And now, Lord, behold their threatenings: and grant unto thy servants, that with all boldness they may speak thy word,
nt/acts/4.30: By stretching forth thine hand to heal; and that signs and wonders may be done by the name of thy holy child Jesus.
nt/acts/4.31: ¶ And when they had prayed, the place was shaken where they were assembled together; and they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with boldness.
nt/acts/4.32: And the multitude of them that believed were of one heart and of one soul: neither said any [italics]of them[/italics] that ought of the things which he possessed was his own; but they had all things common.
nt/acts/4.33: And with great power gave the apostles witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus: and great grace was upon them all.
nt/acts/4.34: Neither was there any among them that lacked: for as many as were possessors of lands or houses sold them, and brought the prices of the things that were sold,
nt/acts/4.35: And laid [italics]them[/italics] down at the apostles’ feet: and distribution was made unto every man according as he had need.
nt/acts/4.36: And Joses, who by the apostles was surnamed Barnabas, (which is, being interpreted, The son of consolation,) a Levite, [italics]and[/italics] of the country of Cyprus,
nt/acts/4.37: Having land, sold [italics]it,[/italics] and brought the money, and laid [italics]it[/italics] at the apostles’ feet.

nt/acts/5.1: But a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession,
nt/acts/5.2: And kept back [italics]part[/italics] of the price, his wife also being privy [italics]to it,[/italics] and brought a certain part, and laid [italics]it[/italics] at the apostles’ feet.
nt/acts/5.3: But Peter said, Ananias, why hath Satan filled thine heart to lie to the Holy Ghost, and to keep back [italics]part[/italics] of the price of the land?
nt/acts/5.4: Whiles it remained, was it not thine own? and after it was sold, was it not in thine own power? why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart? thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.
nt/acts/5.5: And Ananias hearing these words fell down, and gave up the ghost: and great fear came on all them that heard these things.
nt/acts/5.6: And the young men arose, wound him up, and carried [italics]him[/italics] out, and buried [italics]him.[/italics]
nt/acts/5.7: And it was about the space of three hours after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in.
nt/acts/5.8: And Peter answered unto her, Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much? And she said, Yea, for so much.
nt/acts/5.9: Then Peter said unto her, How is it that ye have agreed together to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? behold, the feet of them which have buried thy husband [italics]are[/italics] at the door, and shall carry thee out.
nt/acts/5.10: Then fell she down straightway at his feet, and yielded up the ghost: and the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying [italics]her[/italics] forth, buried [italics]her[/italics] by her husband.
nt/acts/5.11: And great fear came upon all the church, and upon as many as heard these things.
nt/acts/5.12: ¶ And by the hands of the apostles were many signs and wonders wrought among the people; (and they were all with one accord in Solomon’s porch.
nt/acts/5.13: And of the rest durst no man join himself to them: but the people magnified them.
nt/acts/5.14: And believers were the more added to the Lord, multitudes both of men and women.)
nt/acts/5.15: Insomuch that they brought forth the sick into the streets, and laid [italics]them[/italics] on beds and couches, that at the least the shadow of Peter passing by might overshadow some of them.
nt/acts/5.16: There came also a multitude [italics]out[/italics] of the cities round about unto Jerusalem, bringing sick folks, and them which were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed every one.
nt/acts/5.17: ¶ Then the high priest rose up, and all they that were with him, (which is the sect of the Sadducees,) and were filled with indignation,
nt/acts/5.18: And laid their hands on the apostles, and put them in the common prison.
nt/acts/5.19: But the angel of the Lord by night opened the prison doors, and brought them forth, and said,
nt/acts/5.20: Go, stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.
nt/acts/5.21: And when they heard [italics]that,[/italics] they entered into the temple early in the morning, and taught. But the high priest came, and they that were with him, and called the council together, and all the senate of the children of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
nt/acts/5.22: But when the officers came, and found them not in the prison, they returned, and told,
nt/acts/5.23: Saying, The prison truly found we shut with all safety, and the keepers standing without before the doors: but when we had opened, we found no man within.
nt/acts/5.24: Now when the high priest and the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these things, they doubted of them whereunto this would grow.
nt/acts/5.25: Then came one and told them, saying, Behold, the men whom ye put in prison are standing in the temple, and teaching the people.
nt/acts/5.26: Then went the captain with the officers, and brought them without violence: for they feared the people, lest they should have been stoned.
nt/acts/5.27: And when they had brought them, they set [italics]them[/italics] before the council: and the high priest asked them,
nt/acts/5.28: Saying, Did not we straitly command you that ye should not teach in this name? and, behold, ye have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and intend to bring this man’s blood upon us.
nt/acts/5.29: ¶ Then Peter and the [italics]other[/italics] apostles answered and said, We ought to obey God rather than men.
nt/acts/5.30: The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom ye slew and hanged on a tree.
nt/acts/5.31: Him hath God exalted with his right hand [italics]to be[/italics] a Prince and a Saviour, for to give repentance to Israel, and forgiveness of sins.
nt/acts/5.32: And we are his witnesses of these things; and [italics]so is[/italics] also the Holy Ghost, whom God hath given to them that obey him.
nt/acts/5.33: ¶ When they heard [italics]that,[/italics] they were cut [italics]to the heart,[/italics] and took counsel to slay them.
nt/acts/5.34: Then stood there up one in the council, a Pharisee, named Gamaliel, a doctor of the law, had in reputation among all the people, and commanded to put the apostles forth a little space;
nt/acts/5.35: And said unto them, Ye men of Israel, take heed to yourselves what ye intend to do as touching these men.
nt/acts/5.36: For before these days rose up Theudas, boasting himself to be somebody; to whom a number of men, about four hundred, joined themselves: who was slain; and all, as many as obeyed him, were scattered, and brought to nought.
nt/acts/5.37: After this man rose up Judas of Galilee in the days of the taxing, and drew away much people after him: he also perished; and all, [italics]even[/italics] as many as obeyed him, were dispersed.
nt/acts/5.38: And now I say unto you, Refrain from these men, and let them alone: for if this counsel or this work be of men, it will come to nought:
nt/acts/5.39: But if it be of God, ye cannot overthrow it; lest haply ye be found even to fight against God.
nt/acts/5.40: And to him they agreed: and when they had called the apostles, and beaten [italics]them,[/italics] they commanded that they should not speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
nt/acts/5.41: ¶ And they departed from the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for his name.
nt/acts/5.42: And daily in the temple, and in every house, they ceased not to teach and preach Jesus Christ.

nt/acts/7.1: Then said the high priest, Are these things so?
nt/acts/7.2: And he said, Men, brethren, and fathers, hearken; The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran,
nt/acts/7.3: And said unto him, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.
nt/acts/7.4: Then came he out of the land of the Chaldæans, and dwelt in Charran: and from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, wherein ye now dwell.
nt/acts/7.5: And he gave him none inheritance in it, no, not [italics]so much as[/italics] to set his foot on: yet he promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when [italics]as yet[/italics] he had no child.
nt/acts/7.6: And God spake on this wise, That his seed should sojourn in a strange land; and that they should bring them into bondage, and entreat [italics]them[/italics] evil four hundred years.
nt/acts/7.7: And the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will I judge, said God: and after that shall they come forth, and serve me in this place.
nt/acts/7.8: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision: and so [italics]Abraham[/italics] begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac [italics]begat[/italics] Jacob; and Jacob [italics]begat[/italics] the twelve patriarchs.
nt/acts/7.9: And the patriarchs, moved with envy, sold Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
nt/acts/7.10: And delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.
nt/acts/7.11: Now there came a dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.
nt/acts/7.12: But when Jacob heard that there was corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.
nt/acts/7.13: And at the second [italics]time[/italics] Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph’s kindred was made known unto Pharaoh.
nt/acts/7.14: Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to [italics]him,[/italics] and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.
nt/acts/7.15: So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,
nt/acts/7.16: And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor [italics]the father[/italics] of Sychem.
nt/acts/7.17: But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
nt/acts/7.18: Till another king arose, which knew not Joseph.
nt/acts/7.19: The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.
nt/acts/7.20: In which time Moses was born, and was exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father’s house three months:
nt/acts/7.21: And when he was cast out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.
nt/acts/7.22: And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.
nt/acts/7.23: And when he was full forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.
nt/acts/7.24: And seeing one [italics]of them[/italics] suffer wrong, he defended [italics]him,[/italics] and avenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:
nt/acts/7.25: For he supposed his brethren would have understood how that God by his hand would deliver them: but they understood not.
nt/acts/7.26: And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye wrong one to another?
nt/acts/7.27: But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us?
nt/acts/7.28: Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?
nt/acts/7.29: Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.
nt/acts/7.30: And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.
nt/acts/7.31: When Moses saw [italics]it,[/italics] he wondered at the sight: and as he drew near to behold [italics]it,[/italics] the voice of the Lord came unto him,
nt/acts/7.32: [italics]Saying,[/italics] I [italics]am[/italics] the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.
nt/acts/7.33: Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.
nt/acts/7.34: I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.
nt/acts/7.35: This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send [italics]to be[/italics] a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.
nt/acts/7.36: He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.
nt/acts/7.37: ¶ This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.
nt/acts/7.38: This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and [italics]with[/italics] our fathers: who received the lively oracles to give unto us:
nt/acts/7.39: To whom our fathers would not obey, but thrust [italics]him[/italics] from them, and in their hearts turned back again into Egypt,
nt/acts/7.40: Saying unto Aaron, Make us gods to go before us: for [italics]as for[/italics] this Moses, which brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
nt/acts/7.41: And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.
nt/acts/7.42: Then God turned, and gave them up to worship the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, O ye house of Israel, have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices [italics]by the space of[/italics] forty years in the wilderness?
nt/acts/7.43: Yea, ye took up the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Remphan, figures which ye made to worship them: and I will carry you away beyond Babylon.
nt/acts/7.44: Our fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness, as he had appointed, speaking unto Moses, that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seen.
nt/acts/7.45: Which also our fathers that came after brought in with Jesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God drave out before the face of our fathers, unto the days of David;
nt/acts/7.46: Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
nt/acts/7.47: But Solomon built him an house.
nt/acts/7.48: Howbeit the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands; as saith the prophet,
nt/acts/7.49: Heaven [italics]is[/italics] my throne, and earth [italics]is[/italics] my footstool: what house will ye build me? saith the Lord: or what [italics]is[/italics] the place of my rest?
nt/acts/7.50: Hath not my hand made all these things?
nt/acts/7.51: ¶ Ye stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Ghost: as your fathers [italics]did,[/italics] so [italics]do[/italics] ye.
nt/acts/7.52: Which of the prophets have not your fathers persecuted? and they have slain them which shewed before of the coming of the Just One; of whom ye have been now the betrayers and murderers:
nt/acts/7.53: Who have received the law by the disposition of angels, and have not kept [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/acts/7.54: ¶ When they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with [italics]their[/italics] teeth.
nt/acts/7.55: But he, being full of the Holy Ghost, looked up steadfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,
nt/acts/7.56: And said, Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.
nt/acts/7.57: Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and ran upon him with one accord,
nt/acts/7.58: And cast [italics]him[/italics] out of the city, and stoned [italics]him:[/italics] and the witnesses laid down their clothes at a young man’s feet, whose name was Saul.
nt/acts/7.59: And they stoned Stephen, calling upon [italics]God[/italics][italics],[/italics] and saying, Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.
nt/acts/7.60: And he kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, Lord, lay not this sin to their charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleep.

nt/acts/8.1: And Saul was consenting unto his death. And at that time there was a great persecution against the church which was at Jerusalem; and they were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judæa and Samaria, except the apostles.
nt/acts/8.2: And devout men carried Stephen [italics]to his burial,[/italics] and made great lamentation over him.
nt/acts/8.3: As for Saul, he made havoc of the church, entering into every house, and haling men and women committed [italics]them[/italics] to prison.
nt/acts/8.4: Therefore they that were scattered abroad went every where preaching the word.
nt/acts/8.5: Then Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and preached Christ unto them.
nt/acts/8.6: And the people with one accord gave heed unto those things which Philip spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.
nt/acts/8.7: For unclean spirits, crying with loud voice, came out of many that were possessed [italics]with them:[/italics] and many taken with palsies, and that were lame, were healed.
nt/acts/8.8: And there was great joy in that city.
nt/acts/8.9: But there was a certain man, called Simon, which beforetime in the same city used sorcery, and bewitched the people of Samaria, giving out that himself was some great one:
nt/acts/8.10: To whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.
nt/acts/8.11: And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.
nt/acts/8.12: But when they believed Philip preaching the things concerning the kingdom of God, and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
nt/acts/8.13: Then Simon himself believed also: and when he was baptized, he continued with Philip, and wondered, beholding the miracles and signs which were done.
nt/acts/8.14: Now when the apostles which were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent unto them Peter and John:
nt/acts/8.15: Who, when they were come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Ghost:
nt/acts/8.16: (For as yet he was fallen upon none of them: only they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)
nt/acts/8.17: Then laid they [italics]their[/italics] hands on them, and they received the Holy Ghost.
nt/acts/8.18: And when Simon saw that through laying on of the apostles’ hands the Holy Ghost was given, he offered them money,
nt/acts/8.19: Saying, Give me also this power, that on whomsoever I lay hands, he may receive the Holy Ghost.
nt/acts/8.20: But Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.
nt/acts/8.21: Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter: for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.
nt/acts/8.22: Repent therefore of this thy wickedness, and pray God, if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiven thee.
nt/acts/8.23: For I perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness, and [italics]in[/italics] the bond of iniquity.
nt/acts/8.24: Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for me, that none of these things which ye have spoken come upon me.
nt/acts/8.25: And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
nt/acts/8.26: And the angel of the Lord spake unto Philip, saying, Arise, and go toward the south unto the way that goeth down from Jerusalem unto Gaza, which is desert.
nt/acts/8.27: And he arose and went: and, behold, a man of Ethiopia, an eunuch of great authority under Candace queen of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Jerusalem for to worship,
nt/acts/8.28: Was returning, and sitting in his chariot read Esaias the prophet.
nt/acts/8.29: Then the Spirit said unto Philip, Go near, and join thyself to this chariot.
nt/acts/8.30: And Philip ran thither to [italics]him,[/italics] and heard him read the prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?
nt/acts/8.31: And he said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip that he would come up and sit with him.
nt/acts/8.32: The place of the scripture which he read was this, He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; and like a lamb dumb before his shearer, so opened he not his mouth:
nt/acts/8.33: In his humiliation his judgment was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.
nt/acts/8.34: And the eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the prophet this? of himself, or of some other man?
nt/acts/8.35: Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same scripture, and preached unto him Jesus.
nt/acts/8.36: And as they went on [italics]their[/italics] way, they came unto a certain water: and the eunuch said, See, [italics]here is[/italics] water; what doth hinder me to be baptized?
nt/acts/8.37: And Philip said, If thou believest with all thine heart, thou mayest. And he answered and said, I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.
nt/acts/8.38: And he commanded the chariot to stand still: and they went down both into the water, both Philip and the eunuch; and he baptized him.
nt/acts/8.39: And when they were come up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the eunuch saw him no more: and he went on his way rejoicing.
nt/acts/8.40: But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing through he preached in all the cities, till he came to Cæsarea.

nt/col/1.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timotheus [italics]our[/italics] brother,
nt/col/1.2: To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace [italics]be[/italics] unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
nt/col/1.3: We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,
nt/col/1.4: Since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love [italics]which ye have[/italics] to all the saints,
nt/col/1.5: For the hope which is laid up for you in heaven, whereof ye heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel;
nt/col/1.6: Which is come unto you, as [italics]it is[/italics] in all the world; and bringeth forth fruit, as [italics]it doth[/italics] also in you, since the day ye heard [italics]of it,[/italics] and knew the grace of God in truth:
nt/col/1.7: As ye also learned of Epaphras our dear fellowservant, who is for you a faithful minister of Christ;
nt/col/1.8: Who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.
nt/col/1.9: For this cause we also, since the day we heard [italics]it,[/italics] do not cease to pray for you, and to desire that ye might be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding;
nt/col/1.10: That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;
nt/col/1.11: Strengthened with all might, according to his glorious power, unto all patience and longsuffering with joyfulness;
nt/col/1.12: Giving thanks unto the Father, which hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light:
nt/col/1.13: Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness, and hath translated [italics]us[/italics] into the kingdom of his dear Son:
nt/col/1.14: In whom we have redemption through his blood, [italics]even[/italics] the forgiveness of sins:
nt/col/1.15: Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature:
nt/col/1.16: For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether [italics]they be[/italics] thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers: all things were created by him, and for him:
nt/col/1.17: And he is before all things, and by him all things consist.
nt/col/1.18: And he is the head of the body, the church: who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all [italics]things[/italics] he might have the preeminence.
nt/col/1.19: For it pleased [italics]the Father[/italics] that in him should all fulness dwell;
nt/col/1.20: And, having made peace through the blood of his cross, by him to reconcile all things unto himself; by him, [italics]I say,[/italics] whether [italics]they be[/italics] things in earth, or things in heaven.
nt/col/1.21: And you, that were sometime alienated and enemies in [italics]your[/italics] mind by wicked works, yet now hath he reconciled
nt/col/1.22: In the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and unblameable and unreproveable in his sight:
nt/col/1.23: If ye continue in the faith grounded and settled, and [italics]be[/italics] not moved away from the hope of the gospel, which ye have heard, [italics]and[/italics] which was preached to every creature which is under heaven; whereof I Paul am made a minister;
nt/col/1.24: Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body’s sake, which is the church:
nt/col/1.25: Whereof I am made a minister, according to the dispensation of God which is given to me for you, to fulfil the word of God;
nt/col/1.26: [italics]Even[/italics] the mystery which hath been hid from ages and from generations, but now is made manifest to his saints:
nt/col/1.27: To whom God would make known what [italics]is[/italics] the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you, the hope of glory:
nt/col/1.28: Whom we preach, warning every man, and teaching every man in all wisdom; that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus:
nt/col/1.29: Whereunto I also labour, striving according to his working, which worketh in me mightily.

nt/col/3.1: If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.
nt/col/3.2: Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.
nt/col/3.3: For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.
nt/col/3.4: When Christ, [italics]who is[/italics] our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.
nt/col/3.5: Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:
nt/col/3.6: For which things’ sake the wrath of God cometh on the children of disobedience:
nt/col/3.7: In the which ye also walked some time, when ye lived in them.
nt/col/3.8: But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.
nt/col/3.9: Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds;
nt/col/3.10: And have put on the new [italics]man[/italics][italics],[/italics] which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him:
nt/col/3.11: Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond [italics]nor[/italics] free: but Christ [italics]is[/italics] all, and in all.
nt/col/3.12: Put on therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering;
nt/col/3.13: Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also [italics]do[/italics] ye.
nt/col/3.14: And above all these things [italics]put on[/italics] charity, which is the bond of perfectness.
nt/col/3.15: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye thankful.
nt/col/3.16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.
nt/col/3.17: And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, [italics]do[/italics] all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.
nt/col/3.18: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.
nt/col/3.19: Husbands, love [italics]your[/italics] wives, and be not bitter against them.
nt/col/3.20: Children, obey [italics]your[/italics] parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord.
nt/col/3.21: Fathers, provoke not your children [italics]to [/italics][italics]anger[/italics][italics],[/italics] lest they be discouraged.
nt/col/3.22: Servants, obey in all things [italics]your[/italics] masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in singleness of heart, fearing God:
nt/col/3.23: And whatsoever ye do, do [italics]it[/italics] heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men;
nt/col/3.24: Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.
nt/col/3.25: But he that doeth wrong shall receive for the wrong which he hath done: and there is no respect of persons.

nt/eph/1.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, to the saints which are at Ephesus, and to the faithful in Christ Jesus:
nt/eph/1.2: Grace [italics]be[/italics] to you, and peace, from God our Father, and [italics]from[/italics] the Lord Jesus Christ.
nt/eph/1.3: Blessed [italics]be[/italics] the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heavenly [italics]places[/italics] in Christ:
nt/eph/1.4: According as he hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before him in love:
nt/eph/1.5: Having predestinated us unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,
nt/eph/1.6: To the praise of the glory of his grace, wherein he hath made us accepted in the beloved.
nt/eph/1.7: In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, according to the riches of his grace;
nt/eph/1.8: Wherein he hath abounded toward us in all wisdom and prudence;
nt/eph/1.9: Having made known unto us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he hath purposed in himself:
nt/eph/1.10: That in the dispensation of the fulness of times he might gather together in one all things in Christ, both which are in heaven, and which are on earth; [italics]even[/italics] in him:
nt/eph/1.11: In whom also we have obtained an inheritance, being predestinated according to the purpose of him who worketh all things after the counsel of his own will:
nt/eph/1.12: That we should be to the praise of his glory, who first trusted in Christ.
nt/eph/1.13: In whom ye also [italics]trusted,[/italics] after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise,
nt/eph/1.14: Which is the earnest of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession, unto the praise of his glory.
nt/eph/1.15: Wherefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,
nt/eph/1.16: Cease not to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers;
nt/eph/1.17: That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:
nt/eph/1.18: The eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,
nt/eph/1.19: And what [italics]is[/italics] the exceeding greatness of his power to us-ward who believe, according to the working of his mighty power,
nt/eph/1.20: Which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and set [italics]him[/italics] at his own right hand in the heavenly [italics]places,[/italics]
nt/eph/1.21: Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come:
nt/eph/1.22: And hath put all [italics]things[/italics] under his feet, and gave him [italics]to be[/italics] the head over all [italics]things[/italics] to the church,
nt/eph/1.23: Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all.

nt/eph/3.1: For this cause I Paul, the prisoner of Jesus Christ for you Gentiles,
nt/eph/3.2: If ye have heard of the dispensation of the grace of God which is given me to you-ward:
nt/eph/3.3: How that by revelation he made known unto me the mystery; (as I wrote afore in few words,
nt/eph/3.4: Whereby, when ye read, ye may understand my knowledge in the mystery of Christ)
nt/eph/3.5: Which in other ages was not made known unto the sons of men, as it is now revealed unto his holy apostles and prophets by the Spirit;
nt/eph/3.6: That the Gentiles should be fellowheirs, and of the same body, and partakers of his promise in Christ by the gospel:
nt/eph/3.7: Whereof I was made a minister, according to the gift of the grace of God given unto me by the effectual working of his power.
nt/eph/3.8: Unto me, who am less than the least of all saints, is this grace given, that I should preach among the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ;
nt/eph/3.9: And to make all [italics]men[/italics] see what [italics]is[/italics] the fellowship of the mystery, which from the beginning of the world hath been hid in God, who created all things by Jesus Christ:
nt/eph/3.10: To the intent that now unto the principalities and powers in heavenly [italics]places[/italics] might be known by the church the manifold wisdom of God,
nt/eph/3.11: According to the eternal purpose which he purposed in Christ Jesus our Lord:
nt/eph/3.12: In whom we have boldness and access with confidence by the faith of him.
nt/eph/3.13: Wherefore I desire that ye faint not at my tribulations for you, which is your glory.
nt/eph/3.14: For this cause I bow my knees unto the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ,
nt/eph/3.15: Of whom the whole family in heaven and earth is named,
nt/eph/3.16: That he would grant you, according to the riches of his glory, to be strengthened with might by his Spirit in the inner man;
nt/eph/3.17: That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith; that ye, being rooted and grounded in love,
nt/eph/3.18: May be able to comprehend with all saints what [italics]is[/italics] the breadth, and length, and depth, and height;
nt/eph/3.19: And to know the love of Christ, which passeth knowledge, that ye might be filled with all the fulness of God.
nt/eph/3.20: Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us,
nt/eph/3.21: Unto him [italics]be[/italics] glory in the church by Christ Jesus throughout all ages, world without end. Amen.

nt/eph/4.1: I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called,
nt/eph/4.2: With all lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering, forbearing one another in love;
nt/eph/4.3: Endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
nt/eph/4.4: [italics]There is[/italics] one body, and one Spirit, even as ye are called in one hope of your calling;
nt/eph/4.5: One Lord, one faith, one baptism,
nt/eph/4.6: One God and Father of all, who [italics]is[/italics] above all, and through all, and in you all.
nt/eph/4.7: But unto every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ.
nt/eph/4.8: Wherefore he saith, When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men.
nt/eph/4.9: (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth?
nt/eph/4.10: He that descended is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things.)
nt/eph/4.11: And he gave some, apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers;
nt/eph/4.12: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:
nt/eph/4.13: Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:
nt/eph/4.14: That we [italics]henceforth[/italics] be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the sleight of men, [italics]and[/italics] cunning craftiness, whereby they lie in wait to deceive;
nt/eph/4.15: But speaking the truth in love, may grow up into him in all things, which is the head, [italics]even[/italics] Christ:
nt/eph/4.16: From whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth, according to the effectual working in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love.
nt/eph/4.17: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,
nt/eph/4.18: Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:
nt/eph/4.19: Who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.
nt/eph/4.20: But ye have not so learned Christ;
nt/eph/4.21: If so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is in Jesus:
nt/eph/4.22: That ye put off concerning the former conversation the old man, which is corrupt according to the deceitful lusts;
nt/eph/4.23: And be renewed in the spirit of your mind;
nt/eph/4.24: And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.
nt/eph/4.25: Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.
nt/eph/4.26: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath:
nt/eph/4.27: Neither give place to the devil.
nt/eph/4.28: Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with [italics]his[/italics] hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.
nt/eph/4.29: Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.
nt/eph/4.30: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.
nt/eph/4.31: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
nt/eph/4.32: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ’s sake hath forgiven you.

nt/eph/5.1: Be ye therefore followers of God, as dear children;
nt/eph/5.2: And walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour.
nt/eph/5.3: But fornication, and all uncleanness, or covetousness, let it not be once named among you, as becometh saints;
nt/eph/5.4: Neither filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not convenient: but rather giving of thanks.
nt/eph/5.5: For this ye know, that no whoremonger, nor unclean person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, hath any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God.
nt/eph/5.6: Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things cometh the wrath of God upon the children of disobedience.
nt/eph/5.7: Be not ye therefore partakers with them.
nt/eph/5.8: For ye were sometimes darkness, but now [italics]are ye[/italics] light in the Lord: walk as children of light:
nt/eph/5.9: (For the fruit of the Spirit [italics]is[/italics] in all goodness and righteousness and truth;)
nt/eph/5.10: Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord.
nt/eph/5.11: And have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove [italics]them.[/italics]
nt/eph/5.12: For it is a shame even to speak of those things which are done of them in secret.
nt/eph/5.13: But all things that are reproved are made manifest by the light: for whatsoever doth make manifest is light.
nt/eph/5.14: Wherefore he saith, Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.
