nt/eph/5.15: See then that ye walk circumspectly, not as fools, but as wise,
nt/eph/5.16: Redeeming the time, because the days are evil.
nt/eph/5.17: Wherefore be ye not unwise, but understanding what the will of the Lord [italics]is.[/italics]
nt/eph/5.18: And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit;
nt/eph/5.19: Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;
nt/eph/5.20: Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ;
nt/eph/5.21: Submitting yourselves one to another in the fear of God.
nt/eph/5.22: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.
nt/eph/5.23: For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.
nt/eph/5.24: Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so [italics]let[/italics] the wives [italics]be[/italics] to their own husbands in every thing.
nt/eph/5.25: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;
nt/eph/5.26: That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,
nt/eph/5.27: That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.
nt/eph/5.28: So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.
nt/eph/5.29: For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:
nt/eph/5.30: For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.
nt/eph/5.31: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.
nt/eph/5.32: This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.
nt/eph/5.33: Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife [italics]see[/italics] that she reverence [italics]her[/italics] husband.

nt/gal/2.1: Then fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and took Titus with [italics]me[/italics] also.
nt/gal/2.2: And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.
nt/gal/2.3: But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:
nt/gal/2.4: And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:
nt/gal/2.5: To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.
nt/gal/2.6: But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man’s person:) for they who seemed [italics]to be somewhat[/italics] in conference added nothing to me:
nt/gal/2.7: But contrariwise, when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as [italics]the gospel[/italics] of the circumcision [italics]was[/italics] unto Peter;
nt/gal/2.8: (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)
nt/gal/2.9: And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we [italics]should go[/italics] unto the heathen, and they unto the circumcision.
nt/gal/2.10: Only [italics]they would[/italics] that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.
nt/gal/2.11: But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.
nt/gal/2.12: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.
nt/gal/2.13: And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.
nt/gal/2.14: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before [italics]them[/italics] all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?
nt/gal/2.15: We [italics]who are[/italics] Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,
nt/gal/2.16: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.
nt/gal/2.17: But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, [italics]is[/italics] therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.
nt/gal/2.18: For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.
nt/gal/2.19: For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.
nt/gal/2.20: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.
nt/gal/2.21: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness [italics]come[/italics] by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.

nt/gal/3.1: O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?
nt/gal/3.2: This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
nt/gal/3.3: Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?
nt/gal/3.4: Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if [italics]it be[/italics] yet in vain.
nt/gal/3.5: He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, [italics]doeth he it[/italics] by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
nt/gal/3.6: Even as Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.
nt/gal/3.7: Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham.
nt/gal/3.8: And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham, [italics]saying,[/italics] In thee shall all nations be blessed.
nt/gal/3.9: So then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful Abraham.
nt/gal/3.10: For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, Cursed [italics]is[/italics] every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them.
nt/gal/3.11: But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, [italics]it is[/italics] evident: for, The just shall live by faith.
nt/gal/3.12: And the law is not of faith: but, The man that doeth them shall live in them.
nt/gal/3.13: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed [italics]is[/italics] every one that hangeth on a tree:
nt/gal/3.14: That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
nt/gal/3.15: Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though [italics]it be[/italics] but a man’s covenant, yet [italics]if it be[/italics] confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.
nt/gal/3.16: Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.
nt/gal/3.17: And this I say, [italics]that[/italics] the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the promise of none effect.
nt/gal/3.18: For if the inheritance [italics]be[/italics] of the law, [italics]it is[/italics] no more of promise: but God gave [italics]it[/italics] to Abraham by promise.
nt/gal/3.19: Wherefore then [italics]serveth[/italics] the law? It was added because of transgressions, till the seed should come to whom the promise was made; [italics]and it was[/italics] ordained by angels in the hand of a mediator.
nt/gal/3.20: Now a mediator is not [italics]a mediator[/italics] of one, but God is one.
nt/gal/3.21: [italics]Is[/italics] the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.
nt/gal/3.22: But the scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.
nt/gal/3.23: But before faith came, we were kept under the law, shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed.
nt/gal/3.24: Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster [italics]to bring us[/italics] unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith.
nt/gal/3.25: But after that faith is come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.
nt/gal/3.26: For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.
nt/gal/3.27: For as many of you as have been baptized into Christ have put on Christ.
nt/gal/3.28: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus.
nt/gal/3.29: And if ye [italics]be[/italics] Christ’s, then are ye Abraham’s seed, and heirs according to the promise.

nt/gal/4.1: Now I say, [italics]That[/italics] the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;
nt/gal/4.2: But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.
nt/gal/4.3: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world:
nt/gal/4.4: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law,
nt/gal/4.5: To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.
nt/gal/4.6: And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
nt/gal/4.7: Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
nt/gal/4.8: Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods.
nt/gal/4.9: But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?
nt/gal/4.10: Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.
nt/gal/4.11: I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.
nt/gal/4.12: Brethren, I beseech you, be as I [italics]am;[/italics] for I [italics]am[/italics] as ye [italics]are:[/italics] ye have not injured me at all.
nt/gal/4.13: Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.
nt/gal/4.14: And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, [italics]even[/italics] as Christ Jesus.
nt/gal/4.15: Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if [italics]it had been[/italics] possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.
nt/gal/4.16: Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the truth?
nt/gal/4.17: They zealously affect you, [italics]but[/italics] not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.
nt/gal/4.18: But [italics]it is[/italics] good to be zealously affected always in [italics]a[/italics] good [italics]thing,[/italics] and not only when I am present with you.
nt/gal/4.19: My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,
nt/gal/4.20: I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you.
nt/gal/4.21: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?
nt/gal/4.22: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.
nt/gal/4.23: But he [italics]who was[/italics] of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman [italics]was[/italics] by promise.
nt/gal/4.24: Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar.
nt/gal/4.25: For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.
nt/gal/4.26: But Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.
nt/gal/4.27: For it is written, Rejoice, [italics]thou[/italics] barren that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband.
nt/gal/4.28: Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of promise.
nt/gal/4.29: But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him [italics]that was born[/italics] after the Spirit, even so [italics]it is[/italics] now.
nt/gal/4.30: Nevertheless what saith the scripture? Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.
nt/gal/4.31: So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free.

nt/heb/1.1: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,
nt/heb/1.2: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by [italics]his[/italics] Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;
nt/heb/1.3: Who being the brightness of [italics]his[/italics] glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
nt/heb/1.4: Being made so much better than the angels, as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.
nt/heb/1.5: For unto which of the angels said he at any time, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee? And again, I will be to him a Father, and he shall be to me a Son?
nt/heb/1.6: And again, when he bringeth in the firstbegotten into the world, he saith, And let all the angels of God worship him.
nt/heb/1.7: And of the angels he saith, Who maketh his angels spirits, and his ministers a flame of fire.
nt/heb/1.8: But unto the Son [italics]he saith,[/italics] Thy throne, O God, [italics]is[/italics] for ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness [italics]is[/italics] the sceptre of thy kingdom.
nt/heb/1.9: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, [italics]even[/italics] thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
nt/heb/1.10: And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth; and the heavens are the works of thine hands:
nt/heb/1.11: They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall wax old as doth a garment;
nt/heb/1.12: And as a vesture shalt thou fold them up, and they shall be changed: but thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail.
nt/heb/1.13: But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool?
nt/heb/1.14: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth to minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation?

nt/heb/10.1: For the law having a shadow of good things to come, [italics]and[/italics] not the very image of the things, can never with those sacrifices which they offered year by year continually make the comers thereunto perfect.
nt/heb/10.2: For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once purged should have had no more conscience of sins.
nt/heb/10.3: But in those [italics]sacrifices[/italics][italics] there is[/italics] a remembrance again [italics]made[/italics] of sins every year.
nt/heb/10.4: For [italics]it is[/italics] not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.
nt/heb/10.5: Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me:
nt/heb/10.6: In burnt offerings and [italics]sacrifices[/italics] for sin thou hast had no pleasure.
nt/heb/10.7: Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.
nt/heb/10.8: Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and [italics]offering[/italics] for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure [italics]therein;[/italics] which are offered by the law;
nt/heb/10.9: Then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second.
nt/heb/10.10: By the which will we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once [italics]for all.[/italics]
nt/heb/10.11: And every priest standeth daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins:
nt/heb/10.12: But this man, after he had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;
nt/heb/10.13: From henceforth expecting till his enemies be made his footstool.
nt/heb/10.14: For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.
nt/heb/10.15: [italics]Whereof[/italics] the Holy Ghost also is a witness to us: for after that he had said before,
nt/heb/10.16: This [italics]is[/italics] the covenant that I will make with them after those days, saith the Lord, I will put my laws into their hearts, and in their minds will I write them;
nt/heb/10.17: And their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.
nt/heb/10.18: Now where remission of these [italics]is, there is[/italics] no more offering for sin.
nt/heb/10.19: Having therefore, brethren, boldness to enter into the holiest by the blood of Jesus,
nt/heb/10.20: By a new and living way, which he hath consecrated for us, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh;
nt/heb/10.21: And [italics]having[/italics] an high priest over the house of God;
nt/heb/10.22: Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.
nt/heb/10.23: Let us hold fast the profession of [italics]our[/italics] faith without wavering; (for he [italics]is[/italics] faithful that promised;)
nt/heb/10.24: And let us consider one another to provoke unto love and to good works:
nt/heb/10.25: Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some [italics]is;[/italics] but exhorting [italics]one another:[/italics] and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.
nt/heb/10.26: For if we sin wilfully after that we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remaineth no more sacrifice for sins,
nt/heb/10.27: But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery indignation, which shall devour the adversaries.
nt/heb/10.28: He that despised Moses’ law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:
nt/heb/10.29: Of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?
nt/heb/10.30: For we know him that hath said, Vengeance [italics]belongeth[/italics] unto me, I will recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall judge his people.
nt/heb/10.31: [italics]It is[/italics] a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
nt/heb/10.32: But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great fight of afflictions;
nt/heb/10.33: Partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.
nt/heb/10.34: For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring substance.
nt/heb/10.35: Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.
nt/heb/10.36: For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.
nt/heb/10.37: For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.
nt/heb/10.38: Now the just shall live by faith: but if [italics]any man[/italics] draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him.
nt/heb/10.39: But we are not of them who draw back unto perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.

nt/heb/11.1: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
nt/heb/11.2: For by it the elders obtained a good report.
nt/heb/11.3: Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.
nt/heb/11.4: By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.
nt/heb/11.5: By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this testimony, that he pleased God.
nt/heb/11.6: But without faith [italics]it is[/italics] impossible to please [italics]him:[/italics] for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and [italics]that[/italics] he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.
nt/heb/11.7: By faith Noah, being warned of God of things not seen as yet, moved with fear, prepared an ark to the saving of his house; by the which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is by faith.
nt/heb/11.8: By faith Abraham, when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an inheritance, obeyed; and he went out, not knowing whither he went.
nt/heb/11.9: By faith he sojourned in the land of promise, as [italics]in[/italics] a strange country, dwelling in tabernacles with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise:
nt/heb/11.10: For he looked for a city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker [italics]is[/italics] God.
nt/heb/11.11: Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed, and was delivered of a child when she was past age, because she judged him faithful who had promised.
nt/heb/11.12: Therefore sprang there even of one, and him as good as dead, [italics]so many[/italics] as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as the sand which is by the sea shore innumerable.
nt/heb/11.13: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them afar off, and were persuaded of [italics]them,[/italics] and embraced [italics]them,[/italics] and confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.
nt/heb/11.14: For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a country.
nt/heb/11.15: And truly, if they had been mindful of that [italics]country[/italics] from whence they came out, they might have had opportunity to have returned.
nt/heb/11.16: But now they desire a better [italics]country[/italics][italics],[/italics] that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.
nt/heb/11.17: By faith Abraham, when he was tried, offered up Isaac: and he that had received the promises offered up his only begotten [italics]son,[/italics]
nt/heb/11.18: Of whom it was said, That in Isaac shall thy seed be called:
nt/heb/11.19: Accounting that God [italics]was[/italics] able to raise [italics]him[/italics] up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.
nt/heb/11.20: By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.
nt/heb/11.21: By faith Jacob, when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph; and worshipped, [italics]leaning[/italics] upon the top of his staff.
nt/heb/11.22: By faith Joseph, when he died, made mention of the departing of the children of Israel; and gave commandment concerning his bones.
nt/heb/11.23: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw [italics]he was[/italics] a proper child; and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.
nt/heb/11.24: By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter;
nt/heb/11.25: Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;
nt/heb/11.26: Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.
nt/heb/11.27: By faith he forsook Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.
nt/heb/11.28: Through faith he kept the passover, and the sprinkling of blood, lest he that destroyed the firstborn should touch them.
nt/heb/11.29: By faith they passed through the Red sea as by dry [italics]land:[/italics] which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.
nt/heb/11.30: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed about seven days.
nt/heb/11.31: By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.
nt/heb/11.32: And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and [italics]of[/italics] Barak, and [italics]of[/italics] Samson, and [italics]of[/italics] Jephthae; [italics]of[/italics] David also, and Samuel, and [italics]of[/italics] the prophets:
nt/heb/11.33: Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
nt/heb/11.34: Quenched the violence of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakness were made strong, waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.
nt/heb/11.35: Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting deliverance; that they might obtain a better resurrection:
nt/heb/11.36: And others had trial of [italics]cruel[/italics] mockings and scourgings, yea, moreover of bonds and imprisonment:
nt/heb/11.37: They were stoned, they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented;
nt/heb/11.38: (Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts, and [italics]in[/italics] mountains, and [italics]in[/italics] dens and caves of the earth.
nt/heb/11.39: And these all, having obtained a good report through faith, received not the promise:
nt/heb/11.40: God having provided some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made perfect.

nt/heb/12.1: Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin which doth so easily beset [italics]us,[/italics] and let us run with patience the race that is set before us,
nt/heb/12.2: Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of [italics]our[/italics] faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.
nt/heb/12.3: For consider him that endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, lest ye be wearied and faint in your minds.
nt/heb/12.4: Ye have not yet resisted unto blood, striving against sin.
nt/heb/12.5: And ye have forgotten the exhortation which speaketh unto you as unto children, My son, despise not thou the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art rebuked of him:
nt/heb/12.6: For whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth.
nt/heb/12.7: If ye endure chastening, God dealeth with you as with sons; for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?
nt/heb/12.8: But if ye be without chastisement, whereof all are partakers, then are ye bastards, and not sons.
nt/heb/12.9: Furthermore we have had fathers of our flesh which corrected [italics]us,[/italics] and we gave [italics]them[/italics] reverence: shall we not much rather be in subjection unto the Father of spirits, and live?
nt/heb/12.10: For they verily for a few days chastened [italics]us[/italics] after their own pleasure; but he for [italics]our[/italics] profit, that [italics]we[/italics] might be partakers of his holiness.
nt/heb/12.11: Now no chastening for the present seemeth to be joyous, but grievous: nevertheless afterward it yieldeth the peaceable fruit of righteousness unto them which are exercised thereby.
nt/heb/12.12: Wherefore lift up the hands which hang down, and the feeble knees;
nt/heb/12.13: And make straight paths for your feet, lest that which is lame be turned out of the way; but let it rather be healed.
nt/heb/12.14: Follow peace with all [italics]men,[/italics] and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord:
nt/heb/12.15: Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble [italics]you,[/italics] and thereby many be defiled;
nt/heb/12.16: Lest there [italics]be[/italics] any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsel of meat sold his birthright.
nt/heb/12.17: For ye know how that afterward, when he would have inherited the blessing, he was rejected: for he found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with tears.
nt/heb/12.18: For ye are not come unto the mount that might be touched, and that burned with fire, nor unto blackness, and darkness, and tempest,
nt/heb/12.19: And the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which [italics]voice[/italics] they that heard entreated that the word should not be spoken to them any more:
nt/heb/12.20: (For they could not endure that which was commanded, And if so much as a beast touch the mountain, it shall be stoned, or thrust through with a dart:
nt/heb/12.21: And so terrible was the sight, [italics]that[/italics] Moses said, I exceedingly fear and quake:)
nt/heb/12.22: But ye are come unto mount Sion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to an innumerable company of angels,
nt/heb/12.23: To the general assembly and church of the firstborn, which are written in heaven, and to God the Judge of all, and to the spirits of just men made perfect,
nt/heb/12.24: And to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling, that speaketh better things than [italics]that of[/italics] Abel.
nt/heb/12.25: See that ye refuse not him that speaketh. For if they escaped not who refused him that spake on earth, much more [italics]shall not[/italics] we [italics]escape,[/italics] if we turn away from him that [italics]speaketh[/italics] from heaven:
nt/heb/12.26: Whose voice then shook the earth: but now he hath promised, saying, Yet once more I shake not the earth only, but also heaven.
nt/heb/12.27: And this [italics]word,[/italics] Yet once more, signifieth the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remain.
nt/heb/12.28: Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, whereby we may serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear:
nt/heb/12.29: For our God [italics]is[/italics] a consuming fire.

nt/heb/13.2: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.
nt/heb/13.3: Remember them that are in bonds, as bound with them; [italics]and[/italics] them which suffer adversity, as being yourselves also in the body.
nt/heb/13.4: Marriage [italics]is[/italics] honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.
nt/heb/13.5: [italics]Let your[/italics] conversation [italics]be[/italics] without covetousness; [italics]and be[/italics] content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
nt/heb/13.6: So that we may boldly say, The Lord [italics]is[/italics] my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.
nt/heb/13.7: Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of [italics]their[/italics] conversation.
nt/heb/13.8: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.
nt/heb/13.9: Be not carried about with divers and strange doctrines. For [italics]it is[/italics] a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.
nt/heb/13.10: We have an altar, whereof they have no right to eat which serve the tabernacle.
nt/heb/13.11: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.
nt/heb/13.12: Wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate.
nt/heb/13.13: Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach.
nt/heb/13.14: For here have we no continuing city, but we seek one to come.
nt/heb/13.15: By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of [italics]our[/italics] lips giving thanks to his name.
nt/heb/13.16: But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.
nt/heb/13.17: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that [italics]is[/italics] unprofitable for you.
nt/heb/13.18: Pray for us: for we trust we have a good conscience, in all things willing to live honestly.
nt/heb/13.19: But I beseech [italics]you[/italics] the rather to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.
nt/heb/13.20: Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the everlasting covenant,
nt/heb/13.21: Make you perfect in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom [italics]be[/italics] glory for ever and ever. Amen.
nt/heb/13.22: And I beseech you, brethren, suffer the word of exhortation: for I have written a letter unto you in few words.
nt/heb/13.23: Know ye that [italics]our[/italics] brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.
nt/heb/13.24: Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.

nt/heb/2.1: Therefore we ought to give the more earnest heed to the things which we have heard, lest at any time we should let [italics]them[/italics] slip.
nt/heb/2.2: For if the word spoken by angels was steadfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just recompence of reward;
nt/heb/2.3: How shall we escape, if we neglect so great salvation; which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed unto us by them that heard [italics]him;[/italics]
nt/heb/2.4: God also bearing [italics]them[/italics] witness, both with signs and wonders, and with divers miracles, and gifts of the Holy Ghost, according to his own will?
nt/heb/2.5: For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.
nt/heb/2.6: But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is man, that thou art mindful of him? or the son of man, that thou visitest him?
nt/heb/2.7: Thou madest him a little lower than the angels; thou crownedst him with glory and honour, and didst set him over the works of thy hands:
nt/heb/2.8: Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet. For in that he put all in subjection under him, he left nothing [italics]that is[/italics] not put under him. But now we see not yet all things put under him.
nt/heb/2.9: But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; that he by the grace of God should taste death for every man.
nt/heb/2.10: For it became him, for whom [italics]are[/italics] all things, and by whom [italics]are[/italics] all things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.
nt/heb/2.11: For both he that sanctifieth and they who are sanctified [italics]are[/italics] all of one: for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brethren,
nt/heb/2.12: Saying, I will declare thy name unto my brethren, in the midst of the church will I sing praise unto thee.
nt/heb/2.13: And again, I will put my trust in him. And again, Behold I and the children which God hath given me.
nt/heb/2.14: Forasmuch then as the children are partakers of flesh and blood, he also himself likewise took part of the same; that through death he might destroy him that had the power of death, that is, the devil;
nt/heb/2.15: And deliver them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.
nt/heb/2.16: For verily he took not on [italics]him the nature of[/italics] angels; but he took on [italics]him[/italics] the seed of Abraham.
nt/heb/2.17: Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto [italics]his[/italics] brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things [italics]pertaining[/italics] to God, to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.
nt/heb/2.18: For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succour them that are tempted.

nt/heb/4.1: Let us therefore fear, lest, a promise being left [italics]us[/italics] of entering into his rest, any of you should seem to come short of it.
nt/heb/4.2: For unto us was the gospel preached, as well as unto them: but the word preached did not profit them, not being mixed with faith in them that heard [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/heb/4.3: For we which have believed do enter into rest, as he said, As I have sworn in my wrath, if they shall enter into my rest: although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.
nt/heb/4.4: For he spake in a certain place of the seventh [italics]day[/italics] on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all his works.
nt/heb/4.5: And in this [italics]place[/italics] again, If they shall enter into my rest.
nt/heb/4.6: Seeing therefore it remaineth that some must enter therein, and they to whom it was first preached entered not in because of unbelief:
nt/heb/4.7: Again, he limiteth a certain day, saying in David, To day, after so long a time; as it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts.
nt/heb/4.8: For if Jesus had given them rest, then would he not afterward have spoken of another day.
nt/heb/4.9: There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.
nt/heb/4.10: For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God [italics]did[/italics] from his.
nt/heb/4.11: Let us labour therefore to enter into that rest, lest any man fall after the same example of unbelief.
nt/heb/4.12: For the word of God [italics]is[/italics] quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and [italics]is[/italics] a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.
nt/heb/4.13: Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things [italics]are[/italics] naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do.
nt/heb/4.14: Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast [italics]our[/italics] profession.
nt/heb/4.15: For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as [italics]we are, yet[/italics] without sin.
nt/heb/4.16: Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need.

nt/heb/5.1: For every high priest taken from among men is ordained for men in things [italics]pertaining[/italics] to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins:
nt/heb/5.2: Who can have compassion on the ignorant, and on them that are out of the way; for that he himself also is compassed with infirmity.
nt/heb/5.3: And by reason hereof he ought, as for the people, so also for himself, to offer for sins.
nt/heb/5.4: And no man taketh this honour unto himself, but he that is called of God, as [italics]was[/italics] Aaron.
nt/heb/5.5: So also Christ glorified not himself to be made an high priest; but he that said unto him, Thou art my Son, to day have I begotten thee.
nt/heb/5.6: As he saith also in another [italics]place,[/italics] Thou [italics]art[/italics] a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
nt/heb/5.7: Who in the days of his flesh, when he had offered up prayers and supplications with strong crying and tears unto him that was able to save him from death, and was heard in that he feared;
nt/heb/5.8: Though he were a Son, yet learned he obedience by the things which he suffered;
nt/heb/5.9: And being made perfect, he became the author of eternal salvation unto all them that obey him;
nt/heb/5.10: Called of God an high priest after the order of Melchisedec.
nt/heb/5.11: Of whom we have many things to say, and hard to be uttered, seeing ye are dull of hearing.
nt/heb/5.12: For when for the time ye ought to be teachers, ye have need that one teach you again which [italics]be[/italics] the first principles of the oracles of God; and are become such as have need of milk, and not of strong meat.
nt/heb/5.13: For every one that useth milk [italics]is[/italics] unskilful in the word of righteousness: for he is a babe.
nt/heb/5.14: But strong meat belongeth to them that are of full age, [italics]even[/italics] those who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern both good and evil.

nt/heb/7.1: For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;
nt/heb/7.2: To whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all; first being by interpretation King of righteousness, and after that also King of Salem, which is, King of peace;
nt/heb/7.3: Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a priest continually.
nt/heb/7.4: Now consider how great this man [italics]was,[/italics] unto whom even the patriarch Abraham gave the tenth of the spoils.
nt/heb/7.5: And verily they that are of the sons of Levi, who receive the office of the priesthood, have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brethren, though they come out of the loins of Abraham:
nt/heb/7.6: But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.
nt/heb/7.7: And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.
nt/heb/7.8: And here men that die receive tithes; but there he [italics]receiveth them,[/italics] of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.
nt/heb/7.9: And as I may so say, Levi also, who receiveth tithes, payed tithes in Abraham.
nt/heb/7.10: For he was yet in the loins of his father, when Melchisedec met him.
nt/heb/7.11: If therefore perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, (for under it the people received the law,) what further need [italics]was there[/italics] that another priest should rise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?
nt/heb/7.12: For the priesthood being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.
nt/heb/7.13: For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.
nt/heb/7.14: For [italics]it is[/italics] evident that our Lord sprang out of Juda; of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.
nt/heb/7.15: And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,
nt/heb/7.16: Who is made, not after the law of a carnal commandment, but after the power of an endless life.
nt/heb/7.17: For he testifieth, Thou [italics]art[/italics] a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
nt/heb/7.18: For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.
nt/heb/7.19: For the law made nothing perfect, but the bringing in of a better hope [italics]did;[/italics] by the which we draw nigh unto God.
nt/heb/7.20: And inasmuch as not without an oath [italics]he was made priest:[/italics]
nt/heb/7.21: (For those priests were made without an oath; but this with an oath by him that said unto him, The Lord sware and will not repent, Thou [italics]art[/italics] a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec:)
nt/heb/7.22: By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better testament.
nt/heb/7.23: And they truly were many priests, because they were not suffered to continue by reason of death:
nt/heb/7.24: But this [italics]man,[/italics] because he continueth ever, hath an unchangeable priesthood.
nt/heb/7.25: Wherefore he is able also to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make intercession for them.
nt/heb/7.26: For such an high priest became us, [italics]who is[/italics] holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;
nt/heb/7.27: Who needeth not daily, as those high priests, to offer up sacrifice, first for his own sins, and then for the people’s: for this he did once, when he offered up himself.
nt/heb/7.28: For the law maketh men high priests which have infirmity; but the word of the oath, which was since the law, [italics]maketh[/italics] the Son, who is consecrated for evermore.

nt/heb/8.1: Now of the things which we have spoken [italics]this is[/italics] the sum: We have such an high priest, who is set on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens;
nt/heb/8.2: A minister of the sanctuary, and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, and not man.
nt/heb/8.3: For every high priest is ordained to offer gifts and sacrifices: wherefore [italics]it is[/italics] of necessity that this man have somewhat also to offer.
nt/heb/8.4: For if he were on earth, he should not be a priest, seeing that there are priests that offer gifts according to the law:
nt/heb/8.5: Who serve unto the example and shadow of heavenly things, as Moses was admonished of God when he was about to make the tabernacle: for, See, saith he, [italics]that[/italics] thou make all things according to the pattern shewed to thee in the mount.
nt/heb/8.6: But now hath he obtained a more excellent ministry, by how much also he is the mediator of a better covenant, which was established upon better promises.
nt/heb/8.7: For if that first [italics]covenant[/italics] had been faultless, then should no place have been sought for the second.
nt/heb/8.8: For finding fault with them, he saith, Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah:
nt/heb/8.9: Not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they continued not in my covenant, and I regarded them not, saith the Lord.
nt/heb/8.10: For this [italics]is[/italics] the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, saith the Lord; I will put my laws into their mind, and write them in their hearts: and I will be to them a God, and they shall be to me a people:
nt/heb/8.11: And they shall not teach every man his neighbour, and every man his brother, saying, Know the Lord: for all shall know me, from the least to the greatest.
nt/heb/8.12: For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness, and their sins and their iniquities will I remember no more.
nt/heb/8.13: In that he saith, A new [italics]covenant[/italics][italics],[/italics] he hath made the first old. Now that which decayeth and waxeth old [italics]is[/italics] ready to vanish away.

nt/heb/9.1: Then verily the first [italics]covenant[/italics] had also ordinances of divine service, and a worldly sanctuary.
nt/heb/9.2: For there was a tabernacle made; the first, wherein [italics]was[/italics] the candlestick, and the table, and the shewbread; which is called the sanctuary.
nt/heb/9.3: And after the second veil, the tabernacle which is called the Holiest of all;
nt/heb/9.4: Which had the golden censer, and the ark of the covenant overlaid round about with gold, wherein [italics]was[/italics] the golden pot that had manna, and Aaron’s rod that budded, and the tables of the covenant;
nt/heb/9.5: And over it the cherubims of glory shadowing the mercyseat; of which we cannot now speak particularly.
nt/heb/9.6: Now when these things were thus ordained, the priests went always into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the service [italics]of God.[/italics]
nt/heb/9.7: But into the second [italics]went[/italics] the high priest alone once every year, not without blood, which he offered for himself, and [italics]for[/italics] the errors of the people:
nt/heb/9.8: The Holy Ghost this signifying, that the way into the holiest of all was not yet made manifest, while as the first tabernacle was yet standing:
nt/heb/9.9: Which [italics]was[/italics] a figure for the time then present, in which were offered both gifts and sacrifices, that could not make him that did the service perfect, as pertaining to the conscience;
nt/heb/9.10: [italics]Which stood[/italics] only in meats and drinks, and divers washings, and carnal ordinances, imposed [italics]on them[/italics] until the time of reformation.
nt/heb/9.11: But Christ being come an high priest of good things to come, by a greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this building;
nt/heb/9.12: Neither by the blood of goats and calves, but by his own blood he entered in once into the holy place, having obtained eternal redemption [italics]for us.[/italics]
nt/heb/9.13: For if the blood of bulls and of goats, and the ashes of an heifer sprinkling the unclean, sanctifieth to the purifying of the flesh:
nt/heb/9.14: How much more shall the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without spot to God, purge your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?
nt/heb/9.15: And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament, that by means of death, for the redemption of the transgressions [italics]that were[/italics] under the first testament, they which are called might receive the promise of eternal inheritance.
nt/heb/9.16: For where a testament [italics]is,[/italics] there must also of necessity be the death of the testator.
nt/heb/9.17: For a testament [italics]is[/italics] of force after men are dead: otherwise it is of no strength at all while the testator liveth.
nt/heb/9.18: Whereupon neither the first [italics]testament[/italics] was dedicated without blood.
nt/heb/9.19: For when Moses had spoken every precept to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of calves and of goats, with water, and scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book, and all the people,
nt/heb/9.20: Saying, This [italics]is[/italics] the blood of the testament which God hath enjoined unto you.
nt/heb/9.21: Moreover he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle, and all the vessels of the ministry.
nt/heb/9.22: And almost all things are by the law purged with blood; and without shedding of blood is no remission.
nt/heb/9.23: [italics]It was[/italics] therefore necessary that the patterns of things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.
nt/heb/9.24: For Christ is not entered into the holy places made with hands, [italics]which are[/italics] the figures of the true; but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us:
nt/heb/9.25: Nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest entereth into the holy place every year with blood of others;
nt/heb/9.26: For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once in the end of the world hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
nt/heb/9.27: And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:
nt/heb/9.28: So Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many; and unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time without sin unto salvation.

nt/james/1.1: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are scattered abroad, greeting.
nt/james/1.2: My brethren, count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations;
nt/james/1.3: Knowing [italics]this,[/italics] that the trying of your faith worketh patience.
nt/james/1.4: But let patience have [italics]her[/italics] perfect work, that ye may be perfect and entire, wanting nothing.
nt/james/1.5: If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all [italics]men[/italics] liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him.
nt/james/1.6: But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.
nt/james/1.7: For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord.
nt/james/1.8: A double minded man [italics]is[/italics] unstable in all his ways.
nt/james/1.9: Let the brother of low degree rejoice in that he is exalted:
nt/james/1.10: But the rich, in that he is made low: because as the flower of the grass he shall pass away.
nt/james/1.11: For the sun is no sooner risen with a burning heat, but it withereth the grass, and the flower thereof falleth, and the grace of the fashion of it perisheth: so also shall the rich man fade away in his ways.
nt/james/1.12: Blessed [italics]is[/italics] the man that endureth temptation: for when he is tried, he shall receive the crown of life, which the Lord hath promised to them that love him.
nt/james/1.13: Let no man say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God: for God cannot be tempted with evil, neither tempteth he any man:
nt/james/1.14: But every man is tempted, when he is drawn away of his own lust, and enticed.
nt/james/1.15: Then when lust hath conceived, it bringeth forth sin: and sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death.
nt/james/1.16: Do not err, my beloved brethren.
nt/james/1.17: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, and cometh down from the Father of lights, with whom is no variableness, neither shadow of turning.
nt/james/1.18: Of his own will begat he us with the word of truth, that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures.
nt/james/1.19: Wherefore, my beloved brethren, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath:
nt/james/1.20: For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.
nt/james/1.21: Wherefore lay apart all filthiness and superfluity of naughtiness, and receive with meekness the engrafted word, which is able to save your souls.
nt/james/1.22: But be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.
nt/james/1.23: For if any be a hearer of the word, and not a doer, he is like unto a man beholding his natural face in a glass:
nt/james/1.24: For he beholdeth himself, and goeth his way, and straightway forgetteth what manner of man he was.
nt/james/1.25: But whoso looketh into the perfect law of liberty, and continueth [italics]therein,[/italics] he being not a forgetful hearer, but a doer of the work, this man shall be blessed in his deed.
nt/james/1.26: If any man among you seem to be religious, and bridleth not his tongue, but deceiveth his own heart, this man’s religion [italics]is[/italics] vain.
nt/james/1.27: Pure religion and undefiled before God and the Father is this, To visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, [italics]and[/italics] to keep himself unspotted from the world.

nt/john/1.1: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
nt/john/1.2: The same was in the beginning with God.
nt/john/1.3: All things were made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.
nt/john/1.4: In him was life; and the life was the light of men.
nt/john/1.5: And the light shineth in darkness; and the darkness comprehended it not.
nt/john/1.6: ¶ There was a man sent from God, whose name [italics]was[/italics] John.
nt/john/1.7: The same came for a witness, to bear witness of the Light, that all [italics]men[/italics] through him might believe.
nt/john/1.8: He was not that Light, but [italics]was sent[/italics] to bear witness of that Light.
nt/john/1.9: [italics]That[/italics] was the true Light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.
nt/john/1.10: He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.
nt/john/1.11: He came unto his own, and his own received him not.
nt/john/1.12: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the sons of God, [italics]even[/italics] to them that believe on his name:
nt/john/1.13: Which were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
nt/john/1.14: And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth.
nt/john/1.15: ¶ John bare witness of him, and cried, saying, This was he of whom I spake, He that cometh after me is preferred before me: for he was before me.
nt/john/1.16: And of his fulness have all we received, and grace for grace.
nt/john/1.17: For the law was given by Moses, [italics]but[/italics] grace and truth came by Jesus Christ.
nt/john/1.18: No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared [italics]him.[/italics]
nt/john/1.19: ¶ And this is the record of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, Who art thou?
nt/john/1.20: And he confessed, and denied not; but confessed, I am not the Christ.
nt/john/1.21: And they asked him, What then? Art thou Elias? And he saith, I am not. Art thou that prophet? And he answered, No.
nt/john/1.22: Then said they unto him, Who art thou? that we may give an answer to them that sent us. What sayest thou of thyself?
nt/john/1.23: He said, I [italics]am[/italics] the voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make straight the way of the Lord, as said the prophet Esaias.
nt/john/1.24: And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.
nt/john/1.25: And they asked him, and said unto him, Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not that Christ, nor Elias, neither that prophet?
nt/john/1.26: John answered them, saying, I baptize with water: but there standeth one among you, whom ye know not;
nt/john/1.27: He it is, who coming after me is preferred before me, whose shoe’s latchet I am not worthy to unloose.
nt/john/1.28: These things were done in Bethabara beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing.
nt/john/1.29: ¶ The next day John seeth Jesus coming unto him, and saith, Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.
nt/john/1.30: This is he of whom I said, After me cometh a man which is preferred before me: for he was before me.
nt/john/1.31: And I knew him not: but that he should be made manifest to Israel, therefore am I come baptizing with water.
nt/john/1.32: And John bare record, saying, I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it abode upon him.
nt/john/1.33: And I knew him not: but he that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.
nt/john/1.34: And I saw, and bare record that this is the Son of God.
nt/john/1.35: ¶ Again the next day after John stood, and two of his disciples;
nt/john/1.36: And looking upon Jesus as he walked, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God!
nt/john/1.37: And the two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
nt/john/1.38: Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and saith unto them, What seek ye? They said unto him, Rabbi, (which is to say, being interpreted, Master,) where dwellest thou?
nt/john/1.39: He saith unto them, Come and see. They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.
nt/john/1.40: One of the two which heard John [italics]speak,[/italics] and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
nt/john/1.41: He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.
nt/john/1.42: And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone.
nt/john/1.43: ¶ The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee, and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, Follow me.
nt/john/1.44: Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
nt/john/1.45: Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.
nt/john/1.46: And Nathanael said unto him, Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth? Philip saith unto him, Come and see.
nt/john/1.47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and saith of him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile!
nt/john/1.48: Nathanael saith unto him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said unto him, Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.
nt/john/1.49: Nathanael answered and saith unto him, Rabbi, thou art the Son of God; thou art the King of Israel.
nt/john/1.50: Jesus answered and said unto him, Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.
nt/john/1.51: And he saith unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.

nt/john/10.1: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.
nt/john/10.2: But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
nt/john/10.3: To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out.
nt/john/10.4: And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice.
nt/john/10.5: And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.
nt/john/10.6: This parable spake Jesus unto them: but they understood not what things they were which he spake unto them.
nt/john/10.7: Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.
nt/john/10.8: All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.
nt/john/10.9: I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.
nt/john/10.10: The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have [italics]it[/italics] more abundantly.
nt/john/10.11: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.
nt/john/10.12: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.
nt/john/10.13: The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.
nt/john/10.14: I am the good shepherd, and know my [italics]sheep[/italics][italics],[/italics] and am known of mine.
nt/john/10.15: As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep.
nt/john/10.16: And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, [italics]and[/italics] one shepherd.
nt/john/10.17: Therefore doth my Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I might take it again.
nt/john/10.18: No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. This commandment have I received of my Father.
nt/john/10.19: ¶ There was a division therefore again among the Jews for these sayings.
nt/john/10.20: And many of them said, He hath a devil, and is mad; why hear ye him?
nt/john/10.21: Others said, These are not the words of him that hath a devil. Can a devil open the eyes of the blind?
nt/john/10.22: ¶ And it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, and it was winter.
nt/john/10.23: And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon’s porch.
nt/john/10.24: Then came the Jews round about him, and said unto him, How long dost thou make us to doubt? If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.
nt/john/10.25: Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, they bear witness of me.
nt/john/10.26: But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.
nt/john/10.27: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:
nt/john/10.28: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any [italics]man[/italics] pluck them out of my hand.
nt/john/10.29: My Father, which gave [italics]them[/italics] me, is greater than all; and no [italics]man[/italics] is able to pluck [italics]them[/italics] out of my Father’s hand.
nt/john/10.31: Then the Jews took up stones again to stone him.
nt/john/10.32: Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?
nt/john/10.33: The Jews answered him, saying, For a good work we stone thee not; but for blasphemy; and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God.
nt/john/10.34: Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?
nt/john/10.35: If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;
nt/john/10.36: Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?
nt/john/10.37: If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.
nt/john/10.38: But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father [italics]is[/italics] in me, and I in him.
nt/john/10.39: Therefore they sought again to take him: but he escaped out of their hand,
nt/john/10.40: And went away again beyond Jordan into the place where John at first baptized; and there he abode.
nt/john/10.41: And many resorted unto him, and said, John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true.
nt/john/10.42: And many believed on him there.

nt/john/12.1: Then Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany, where Lazarus was which had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.
nt/john/12.2: There they made him a supper; and Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.
nt/john/12.3: Then took Mary a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment.
nt/john/12.4: Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, Simon’s [italics]son,[/italics] which should betray him,
nt/john/12.5: Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence, and given to the poor?
nt/john/12.6: This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein.
nt/john/12.7: Then said Jesus, Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this.
nt/john/12.8: For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.
nt/john/12.9: Much people of the Jews therefore knew that he was there: and they came not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.
nt/john/12.10: ¶ But the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death;
nt/john/12.11: Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
nt/john/12.12: ¶ On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
nt/john/12.13: Took branches of palm trees, and went forth to meet him, and cried, Hosanna: Blessed [italics]is[/italics] the King of Israel that cometh in the name of the Lord.
nt/john/12.14: And Jesus, when he had found a young ass, sat thereon; as it is written,
nt/john/12.15: Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass’s colt.
nt/john/12.16: These things understood not his disciples at the first: but when Jesus was glorified, then remembered they that these things were written of him, and [italics]that[/italics] they had done these things unto him.
nt/john/12.17: The people therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of his grave, and raised him from the dead, bare record.
nt/john/12.18: For this cause the people also met him, for that they heard that he had done this miracle.
nt/john/12.19: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, Perceive ye how ye prevail nothing? behold, the world is gone after him.
nt/john/12.20: ¶ And there were certain Greeks among them that came up to worship at the feast:
nt/john/12.21: The same came therefore to Philip, which was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying, Sir, we would see Jesus.
nt/john/12.22: Philip cometh and telleth Andrew: and again Andrew and Philip tell Jesus.
nt/john/12.23: ¶ And Jesus answered them, saying, The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.
nt/john/12.24: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.
nt/john/12.25: He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.
nt/john/12.26: If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will [italics]my[/italics] Father honour.
nt/john/12.27: Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.
nt/john/12.28: Father, glorify thy name. Then came there a voice from heaven, [italics]saying,[/italics] I have both glorified [italics]it,[/italics] and will glorify [italics]it[/italics] again.
nt/john/12.29: The people therefore, that stood by, and heard [italics]it,[/italics] said that it thundered: others said, An angel spake to him.
nt/john/12.30: Jesus answered and said, This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.
nt/john/12.31: Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.
nt/john/12.32: And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all [italics]men[/italics] unto me.
nt/john/12.33: This he said, signifying what death he should die.
nt/john/12.34: The people answered him, We have heard out of the law that Christ abideth for ever: and how sayest thou, The Son of man must be lifted up? who is this Son of man?
nt/john/12.35: Then Jesus said unto them, Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.
nt/john/12.36: While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light. These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from them.
nt/john/12.37: ¶ But though he had done so many miracles before them, yet they believed not on him:
nt/john/12.38: That the saying of Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spake, Lord, who hath believed our report? and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?
nt/john/12.39: Therefore they could not believe, because that Esaias said again,
nt/john/12.40: He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart; that they should not see with [italics]their[/italics] eyes, nor understand with [italics]their[/italics] heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.
nt/john/12.41: These things said Esaias, when he saw his glory, and spake of him.
nt/john/12.42: ¶ Nevertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess [italics]him,[/italics] lest they should be put out of the synagogue:
nt/john/12.43: For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.
nt/john/12.44: ¶ Jesus cried and said, He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.
nt/john/12.45: And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.
nt/john/12.46: I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.
nt/john/12.47: And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.
nt/john/12.48: He that rejecteth me, and receiveth not my words, hath one that judgeth him: the word that I have spoken, the same shall judge him in the last day.
nt/john/12.49: For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.
nt/john/12.50: And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.

nt/john/13.1: Now before the feast of the passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he loved them unto the end.
nt/john/13.2: And supper being ended, the devil having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s [italics]son,[/italics] to betray him;
nt/john/13.3: Jesus knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he was come from God, and went to God;
nt/john/13.4: He riseth from supper, and laid aside his garments; and took a towel, and girded himself.
nt/john/13.5: After that he poureth water into a basin, and began to wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe [italics]them[/italics] with the towel wherewith he was girded.
nt/john/13.6: Then cometh he to Simon Peter: and Peter saith unto him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?
nt/john/13.7: Jesus answered and said unto him, What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.
nt/john/13.8: Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.
nt/john/13.9: Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, not my feet only, but also [italics]my[/italics] hands and [italics]my[/italics] head.
nt/john/13.10: Jesus saith to him, He that is washed needeth not save to wash [italics]his[/italics] feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.
nt/john/13.11: For he knew who should betray him; therefore said he, Ye are not all clean.
nt/john/13.12: So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?
nt/john/13.13: Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for [italics]so[/italics] I am.
nt/john/13.14: If I then, [italics]your[/italics] Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another’s feet.
nt/john/13.15: For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.
nt/john/13.16: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
nt/john/13.17: If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.
nt/john/13.18: ¶ I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.
nt/john/13.19: Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am [italics]he[/italics][italics].[/italics]
nt/john/13.20: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
nt/john/13.21: When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
nt/john/13.22: Then the disciples looked one on another, doubting of whom he spake.
nt/john/13.23: Now there was leaning on Jesus’ bosom one of his disciples, whom Jesus loved.
nt/john/13.24: Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, that he should ask who it should be of whom he spake.
nt/john/13.25: He then lying on Jesus’ breast saith unto him, Lord, who is it?
nt/john/13.26: Jesus answered, He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped [italics]it.[/italics] And when he had dipped the sop, he gave [italics]it[/italics] to Judas Iscariot, [italics]the son[/italics] of Simon.
nt/john/13.27: And after the sop Satan entered into him. Then said Jesus unto him, That thou doest, do quickly.
nt/john/13.28: Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.
nt/john/13.29: For some [italics]of them[/italics] thought, because Judas had the bag, that Jesus had said unto him, Buy [italics]those things[/italics] that we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor.
nt/john/13.30: He then having received the sop went immediately out: and it was night.
nt/john/13.31: ¶ Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
nt/john/13.32: If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.
nt/john/13.33: Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.
nt/john/13.34: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.
nt/john/13.35: By this shall all [italics]men[/italics] know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.
nt/john/13.36: ¶ Simon Peter said unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered him, Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.
nt/john/13.37: Peter said unto him, Lord, why cannot I follow thee now? I will lay down my life for thy sake.
nt/john/13.38: Jesus answered him, Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.

nt/john/14.1: Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
nt/john/14.2: In my Father’s house are many mansions: if [italics]it were[/italics] not [italics]so,[/italics] I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.
nt/john/14.3: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, [italics]there[/italics] ye may be also.
nt/john/14.4: And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.
nt/john/14.5: Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?
nt/john/14.6: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
nt/john/14.7: If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.
nt/john/14.8: Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.
nt/john/14.9: Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou [italics]then,[/italics] Shew us the Father?
nt/john/14.10: Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.
nt/john/14.11: Believe me that I [italics]am[/italics] in the Father, and the Father in me: or else believe me for the very works’ sake.
