nt/john/9.31: Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.
nt/john/9.32: Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.
nt/john/9.33: If this man were not of God, he could do nothing.
nt/john/9.34: They answered and said unto him, Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us? And they cast him out.
nt/john/9.35: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?
nt/john/9.36: He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?
nt/john/9.37: And Jesus said unto him, Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.
nt/john/9.38: And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.
nt/john/9.39: ¶ And Jesus said, For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.
nt/john/9.40: And [italics]some[/italics] of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also?
nt/john/9.41: Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth.

nt/luke/1.1: Forasmuch as many have taken in hand to set forth in order a declaration of those things which are most surely believed among us,
nt/luke/1.2: Even as they delivered them unto us, which from the beginning were eyewitnesses, and ministers of the word;
nt/luke/1.3: It seemed good to me also, having had perfect understanding of all things from the very first, to write unto thee in order, most excellent Theophilus,
nt/luke/1.4: That thou mightest know the certainty of those things, wherein thou hast been instructed.
nt/luke/1.5: ¶ There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judæa, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the course of Abia: and his wife [italics]was[/italics] of the daughters of Aaron, and her name [italics]was[/italics] Elisabeth.
nt/luke/1.6: And they were both righteous before God, walking in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blameless.
nt/luke/1.7: And they had no child, because that Elisabeth was barren, and they both were [italics]now[/italics] well stricken in years.
nt/luke/1.8: And it came to pass, that while he executed the priest’s office before God in the order of his course,
nt/luke/1.9: According to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to burn incense when he went into the temple of the Lord.
nt/luke/1.10: And the whole multitude of the people were praying without at the time of incense.
nt/luke/1.11: And there appeared unto him an angel of the Lord standing on the right side of the altar of incense.
nt/luke/1.12: And when Zacharias saw [italics]him,[/italics] he was troubled, and fear fell upon him.
nt/luke/1.13: But the angel said unto him, Fear not, Zacharias: for thy prayer is heard; and thy wife Elisabeth shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name John.
nt/luke/1.14: And thou shalt have joy and gladness; and many shall rejoice at his birth.
nt/luke/1.15: For he shall be great in the sight of the Lord, and shall drink neither wine nor strong drink; and he shall be filled with the Holy Ghost, even from his mother’s womb.
nt/luke/1.16: And many of the children of Israel shall he turn to the Lord their God.
nt/luke/1.17: And he shall go before him in the spirit and power of Elias, to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
nt/luke/1.18: And Zacharias said unto the angel, Whereby shall I know this? for I am an old man, and my wife well stricken in years.
nt/luke/1.19: And the angel answering said unto him, I am Gabriel, that stand in the presence of God; and am sent to speak unto thee, and to shew thee these glad tidings.
nt/luke/1.20: And, behold, thou shalt be dumb, and not able to speak, until the day that these things shall be performed, because thou believest not my words, which shall be fulfilled in their season.
nt/luke/1.21: And the people waited for Zacharias, and marvelled that he tarried so long in the temple.
nt/luke/1.22: And when he came out, he could not speak unto them: and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple: for he beckoned unto them, and remained speechless.
nt/luke/1.23: And it came to pass, that, as soon as the days of his ministration were accomplished, he departed to his own house.
nt/luke/1.24: And after those days his wife Elisabeth conceived, and hid herself five months, saying,
nt/luke/1.25: Thus hath the Lord dealt with me in the days wherein he looked on [italics]me,[/italics] to take away my reproach among men.
nt/luke/1.26: And in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God unto a city of Galilee, named Nazareth,
nt/luke/1.27: To a virgin espoused to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin’s name [italics]was[/italics] Mary.
nt/luke/1.28: And the angel came in unto her, and said, Hail, [italics]thou that art[/italics] highly favoured, the Lord [italics]is[/italics] with thee: blessed [italics]art[/italics] thou among women.
nt/luke/1.29: And when she saw [italics]him,[/italics] she was troubled at his saying, and cast in her mind what manner of salutation this should be.
nt/luke/1.30: And the angel said unto her, Fear not, Mary: for thou hast found favour with God.
nt/luke/1.31: And, behold, thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a son, and shalt call his name Jesus.
nt/luke/1.32: He shall be great, and shall be called the Son of the Highest: and the Lord God shall give unto him the throne of his father David:
nt/luke/1.33: And he shall reign over the house of Jacob for ever; and of his kingdom there shall be no end.
nt/luke/1.34: Then said Mary unto the angel, How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?
nt/luke/1.35: And the angel answered and said unto her, The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee: therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be called the Son of God.
nt/luke/1.36: And, behold, thy cousin Elisabeth, she hath also conceived a son in her old age: and this is the sixth month with her, who was called barren.
nt/luke/1.37: For with God nothing shall be impossible.
nt/luke/1.38: And Mary said, Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it unto me according to thy word. And the angel departed from her.
nt/luke/1.39: And Mary arose in those days, and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Juda;
nt/luke/1.40: And entered into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elisabeth.
nt/luke/1.41: And it came to pass, that, when Elisabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leaped in her womb; and Elisabeth was filled with the Holy Ghost:
nt/luke/1.42: And she spake out with a loud voice, and said, Blessed [italics]art[/italics] thou among women, and blessed [italics]is[/italics] the fruit of thy womb.
nt/luke/1.43: And whence [italics]is[/italics] this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
nt/luke/1.44: For, lo, as soon as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine ears, the babe leaped in my womb for joy.
nt/luke/1.45: And blessed [italics]is[/italics] she that believed: for there shall be a performance of those things which were told her from the Lord.
nt/luke/1.46: And Mary said, My soul doth magnify the Lord,
nt/luke/1.47: And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.
nt/luke/1.48: For he hath regarded the low estate of his handmaiden: for, behold, from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed.
nt/luke/1.49: For he that is mighty hath done to me great things; and holy [italics]is[/italics] his name.
nt/luke/1.50: And his mercy [italics]is[/italics] on them that fear him from generation to generation.
nt/luke/1.51: He hath shewed strength with his arm; he hath scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.
nt/luke/1.52: He hath put down the mighty from [italics]their[/italics] seats, and exalted them of low degree.
nt/luke/1.53: He hath filled the hungry with good things; and the rich he hath sent empty away.
nt/luke/1.54: He hath holpen his servant Israel, in remembrance of [italics]his[/italics] mercy;
nt/luke/1.55: As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham, and to his seed for ever.
nt/luke/1.56: And Mary abode with her about three months, and returned to her own house.
nt/luke/1.57: Now Elisabeth’s full time came that she should be delivered; and she brought forth a son.
nt/luke/1.58: And her neighbours and her cousins heard how the Lord had shewed great mercy upon her; and they rejoiced with her.
nt/luke/1.59: And it came to pass, that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child; and they called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.
nt/luke/1.60: And his mother answered and said, Not [italics]so;[/italics] but he shall be called John.
nt/luke/1.61: And they said unto her, There is none of thy kindred that is called by this name.
nt/luke/1.62: And they made signs to his father, how he would have him called.
nt/luke/1.63: And he asked for a writing table, and wrote, saying, His name is John. And they marvelled all.
nt/luke/1.64: And his mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue [italics]loosed,[/italics] and he spake, and praised God.
nt/luke/1.65: And fear came on all that dwelt round about them: and all these sayings were noised abroad throughout all the hill country of Judæa.
nt/luke/1.66: And all they that heard [italics]them[/italics] laid [italics]them[/italics] up in their hearts, saying, What manner of child shall this be! And the hand of the Lord was with him.
nt/luke/1.67: And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Ghost, and prophesied, saying,
nt/luke/1.68: Blessed [italics]be[/italics] the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people,
nt/luke/1.69: And hath raised up an horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David;
nt/luke/1.70: As he spake by the mouth of his holy prophets, which have been since the world began:
nt/luke/1.71: That we should be saved from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us;
nt/luke/1.72: To perform the mercy [italics]promised[/italics] to our fathers, and to remember his holy covenant;
nt/luke/1.73: The oath which he sware to our father Abraham,
nt/luke/1.74: That he would grant unto us, that we being delivered out of the hand of our enemies might serve him without fear,
nt/luke/1.75: In holiness and righteousness before him, all the days of our life.
nt/luke/1.76: And thou, child, shalt be called the prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways;
nt/luke/1.77: To give knowledge of salvation unto his people by the remission of their sins,
nt/luke/1.78: Through the tender mercy of our God; whereby the dayspring from on high hath visited us,
nt/luke/1.79: To give light to them that sit in darkness and [italics]in[/italics] the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
nt/luke/1.80: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, and was in the deserts till the day of his shewing unto Israel.

nt/luke/15.1: Then drew near unto him all the publicans and sinners for to hear him.
nt/luke/15.2: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
nt/luke/15.3: ¶ And he spake this parable unto them, saying,
nt/luke/15.4: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?
nt/luke/15.5: And when he hath found [italics]it,[/italics] he layeth [italics]it[/italics] on his shoulders, rejoicing.
nt/luke/15.6: And when he cometh home, he calleth together [italics]his[/italics] friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.
nt/luke/15.7: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.
nt/luke/15.8: ¶ Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find [italics]it?[/italics]
nt/luke/15.9: And when she hath found [italics]it,[/italics] she calleth [italics]her[/italics] friends and [italics]her[/italics] neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.
nt/luke/15.10: Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.
nt/luke/15.11: ¶ And he said, A certain man had two sons:
nt/luke/15.12: And the younger of them said to [italics]his[/italics] father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth [italics]to me.[/italics] And he divided unto them [italics]his[/italics] living.
nt/luke/15.13: And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living.
nt/luke/15.14: And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.
nt/luke/15.15: And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
nt/luke/15.16: And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.
nt/luke/15.17: And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!
nt/luke/15.18: I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee,
nt/luke/15.19: And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.
nt/luke/15.20: And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.
nt/luke/15.21: And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.
nt/luke/15.22: But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put [italics]it[/italics] on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on [italics]his[/italics] feet:
nt/luke/15.23: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill [italics]it;[/italics] and let us eat, and be merry:
nt/luke/15.24: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.
nt/luke/15.25: Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing.
nt/luke/15.26: And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.
nt/luke/15.27: And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.
nt/luke/15.28: And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and entreated him.
nt/luke/15.29: And he answering said to [italics]his[/italics] father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:
nt/luke/15.30: But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.
nt/luke/15.31: And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.
nt/luke/15.32: It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.

nt/luke/19.1: And [italics]Jesus[/italics] entered and passed through Jericho.
nt/luke/19.2: And, behold, [italics]there was[/italics] a man named Zacchæus, which was the chief among the publicans, and he was rich.
nt/luke/19.3: And he sought to see Jesus who he was; and could not for the press, because he was little of stature.
nt/luke/19.4: And he ran before, and climbed up into a sycomore tree to see him: for he was to pass that [italics]way.[/italics]
nt/luke/19.5: And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and saw him, and said unto him, Zacchæus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.
nt/luke/19.6: And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.
nt/luke/19.7: And when they saw [italics]it,[/italics] they all murmured, saying, That he was gone to be guest with a man that is a sinner.
nt/luke/19.8: And Zacchæus stood, and said unto the Lord; Behold, Lord, the half of my goods I give to the poor; and if I have taken any thing from any man by false accusation, I restore [italics]him[/italics] fourfold.
nt/luke/19.9: And Jesus said unto him, This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.
nt/luke/19.10: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.
nt/luke/19.11: And as they heard these things, he added and spake a parable, because he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they thought that the kingdom of God should immediately appear.
nt/luke/19.12: He said therefore, A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.
nt/luke/19.13: And he called his ten servants, and delivered them ten pounds, and said unto them, Occupy till I come.
nt/luke/19.14: But his citizens hated him, and sent a message after him, saying, We will not have this [italics]man[/italics] to reign over us.
nt/luke/19.15: And it came to pass, that when he was returned, having received the kingdom, then he commanded these servants to be called unto him, to whom he had given the money, that he might know how much every man had gained by trading.
nt/luke/19.16: Then came the first, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained ten pounds.
nt/luke/19.17: And he said unto him, Well, thou good servant: because thou hast been faithful in a very little, have thou authority over ten cities.
nt/luke/19.18: And the second came, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained five pounds.
nt/luke/19.19: And he said likewise to him, Be thou also over five cities.
nt/luke/19.20: And another came, saying, Lord, behold, [italics]here is[/italics] thy pound, which I have kept laid up in a napkin:
nt/luke/19.21: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou didst not sow.
nt/luke/19.22: And he saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, [italics]thou[/italics] wicked servant. Thou knewest that I was an austere man, taking up that I laid not down, and reaping that I did not sow:
nt/luke/19.23: Wherefore then gavest not thou my money into the bank, that at my coming I might have required mine own with usury?
nt/luke/19.24: And he said unto them that stood by, Take from him the pound, and give [italics]it[/italics] to him that hath ten pounds.
nt/luke/19.25: (And they said unto him, Lord, he hath ten pounds.)
nt/luke/19.26: For I say unto you, That unto every one which hath shall be given; and from him that hath not, even that he hath shall be taken away from him.
nt/luke/19.27: But those mine enemies, which would not that I should reign over them, bring hither, and slay [italics]them[/italics] before me.
nt/luke/19.28: ¶ And when he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending up to Jerusalem.
nt/luke/19.29: And it came to pass, when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount called [italics]the mount[/italics] of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,
nt/luke/19.30: Saying, Go ye into the village over against [italics]you;[/italics] in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied, whereon yet never man sat: loose him, and bring [italics]him hither.[/italics]
nt/luke/19.31: And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose [italics]him?[/italics] thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath need of him.
nt/luke/19.32: And they that were sent went their way, and found even as he had said unto them.
nt/luke/19.33: And as they were loosing the colt, the owners thereof said unto them, Why loose ye the colt?
nt/luke/19.34: And they said, The Lord hath need of him.
nt/luke/19.35: And they brought him to Jesus: and they cast their garments upon the colt, and they set Jesus thereon.
nt/luke/19.36: And as he went, they spread their clothes in the way.
nt/luke/19.37: And when he was come nigh, even now at the descent of the mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen;
nt/luke/19.38: Saying, Blessed [italics]be[/italics] the King that cometh in the name of the Lord: peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.
nt/luke/19.39: And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.
nt/luke/19.40: And he answered and said unto them, I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.
nt/luke/19.41: ¶ And when he was come near, he beheld the city, and wept over it,
nt/luke/19.42: Saying, If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things [italics]which belong[/italics] unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.
nt/luke/19.43: For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side,
nt/luke/19.44: And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.
nt/luke/19.45: And he went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold therein, and them that bought;
nt/luke/19.46: Saying unto them, It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.
nt/luke/19.47: And he taught daily in the temple. But the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to destroy him,
nt/luke/19.48: And could not find what they might do: for all the people were very attentive to hear him.

nt/luke/2.1: And it came to pass in those days, that there went out a decree from Cæsar Augustus, that all the world should be taxed.
nt/luke/2.2: ([italics]And[/italics] this taxing was first made when Cyrenius was governor of Syria.)
nt/luke/2.3: And all went to be taxed, every one into his own city.
nt/luke/2.4: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judæa, unto the city of David, which is called Bethlehem; (because he was of the house and lineage of David:)
nt/luke/2.5: To be taxed with Mary his espoused wife, being great with child.
nt/luke/2.6: And so it was, that, while they were there, the days were accomplished that she should be delivered.
nt/luke/2.7: And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger; because there was no room for them in the inn.
nt/luke/2.8: And there were in the same country shepherds abiding in the field, keeping watch over their flock by night.
nt/luke/2.9: And, lo, the angel of the Lord came upon them, and the glory of the Lord shone round about them: and they were sore afraid.
nt/luke/2.10: And the angel said unto them, Fear not: for, behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy, which shall be to all people.
nt/luke/2.11: For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Saviour, which is Christ the Lord.
nt/luke/2.12: And this [italics]shall be[/italics] a sign unto you; Ye shall find the babe wrapped in swaddling clothes, lying in a manger.
nt/luke/2.13: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God, and saying,
nt/luke/2.14: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men.
nt/luke/2.15: And it came to pass, as the angels were gone away from them into heaven, the shepherds said one to another, Let us now go even unto Bethlehem, and see this thing which is come to pass, which the Lord hath made known unto us.
nt/luke/2.16: And they came with haste, and found Mary, and Joseph, and the babe lying in a manger.
nt/luke/2.17: And when they had seen [italics]it,[/italics] they made known abroad the saying which was told them concerning this child.
nt/luke/2.18: And all they that heard [italics]it[/italics] wondered at those things which were told them by the shepherds.
nt/luke/2.19: But Mary kept all these things, and pondered [italics]them[/italics] in her heart.
nt/luke/2.20: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, as it was told unto them.
nt/luke/2.21: And when eight days were accomplished for the circumcising of the child, his name was called Jesus, which was so named of the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
nt/luke/2.22: And when the days of her purification according to the law of Moses were accomplished, they brought him to Jerusalem, to present [italics]him[/italics] to the Lord;
nt/luke/2.23: (As it is written in the law of the Lord, Every male that openeth the womb shall be called holy to the Lord;)
nt/luke/2.24: And to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.
nt/luke/2.25: And, behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name [italics]was[/italics] Simeon; and the same man [italics]was[/italics] just and devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.
nt/luke/2.26: And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
nt/luke/2.27: And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him after the custom of the law,
nt/luke/2.28: Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God, and said,
nt/luke/2.29: Lord, now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:
nt/luke/2.30: For mine eyes have seen thy salvation,
nt/luke/2.31: Which thou hast prepared before the face of all people;
nt/luke/2.32: A light to lighten the Gentiles, and the glory of thy people Israel.
nt/luke/2.33: And Joseph and his mother marvelled at those things which were spoken of him.
nt/luke/2.34: And Simeon blessed them, and said unto Mary his mother, Behold, this [italics]child[/italics] is set for the fall and rising again of many in Israel; and for a sign which shall be spoken against;
nt/luke/2.35: (Yea, a sword shall pierce through thy own soul also,) that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.
nt/luke/2.36: And there was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser: she was of a great age, and had lived with an husband seven years from her virginity;
nt/luke/2.37: And she [italics]was[/italics] a widow of about fourscore and four years, which departed not from the temple, but served [italics]God[/italics] with fastings and prayers night and day.
nt/luke/2.38: And she coming in that instant gave thanks likewise unto the Lord, and spake of him to all them that looked for redemption in Jerusalem.
nt/luke/2.39: And when they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city Nazareth.
nt/luke/2.40: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit, filled with wisdom: and the grace of God was upon him.
nt/luke/2.41: Now his parents went to Jerusalem every year at the feast of the passover.
nt/luke/2.42: And when he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem after the custom of the feast.
nt/luke/2.43: And when they had fulfilled the days, as they returned, the child Jesus tarried behind in Jerusalem; and Joseph and his mother knew not [italics]of it.[/italics]
nt/luke/2.44: But they, supposing him to have been in the company, went a day’s journey; and they sought him among [italics]their[/italics] kinsfolk and acquaintance.
nt/luke/2.45: And when they found him not, they turned back again to Jerusalem, seeking him.
nt/luke/2.46: And it came to pass, that after three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the doctors, both hearing them, and asking them questions.
nt/luke/2.47: And all that heard him were astonished at his understanding and answers.
nt/luke/2.48: And when they saw him, they were amazed: and his mother said unto him, Son, why hast thou thus dealt with us? behold, thy father and I have sought thee sorrowing.
nt/luke/2.49: And he said unto them, How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father’s business?
nt/luke/2.50: And they understood not the saying which he spake unto them.
nt/luke/2.51: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and was subject unto them: but his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.
nt/luke/2.52: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favour with God and man.

nt/luke/22.1: Now the feast of unleavened bread drew nigh, which is called the Passover.
nt/luke/22.2: And the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.
nt/luke/22.3: ¶ Then entered Satan into Judas surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.
nt/luke/22.4: And he went his way, and communed with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray him unto them.
nt/luke/22.5: And they were glad, and covenanted to give him money.
nt/luke/22.6: And he promised, and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude.
nt/luke/22.7: ¶ Then came the day of unleavened bread, when the passover must be killed.
nt/luke/22.8: And he sent Peter and John, saying, Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat.
nt/luke/22.9: And they said unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare?
nt/luke/22.10: And he said unto them, Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in.
nt/luke/22.11: And ye shall say unto the goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
nt/luke/22.12: And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.
nt/luke/22.13: And they went, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
nt/luke/22.14: And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve apostles with him.
nt/luke/22.15: And he said unto them, With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer:
nt/luke/22.16: For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
nt/luke/22.17: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, Take this, and divide [italics]it[/italics] among yourselves:
nt/luke/22.18: For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.
nt/luke/22.19: ¶ And he took bread, and gave thanks, and brake [italics]it,[/italics] and gave unto them, saying, This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me.
nt/luke/22.20: Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup [italics]is[/italics] the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you.
nt/luke/22.21: ¶ But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me [italics]is[/italics] with me on the table.
nt/luke/22.22: And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!
nt/luke/22.23: And they began to inquire among themselves, which of them it was that should do this thing.
nt/luke/22.24: ¶ And there was also a strife among them, which of them should be accounted the greatest.
nt/luke/22.25: And he said unto them, The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors.
nt/luke/22.26: But ye [italics]shall[/italics] not [italics]be[/italics] so: but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and he that is chief, as he that doth serve.
nt/luke/22.27: For whether [italics]is[/italics] greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? [italics]is[/italics] not he that sitteth at meat? but I am among you as he that serveth.
nt/luke/22.28: Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations.
nt/luke/22.29: And I appoint unto you a kingdom, as my Father hath appointed unto me;
nt/luke/22.30: That ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
nt/luke/22.31: ¶ And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, Satan hath desired [italics]to [/italics][italics]have[/italics] you, that he may sift [italics]you[/italics] as wheat:
nt/luke/22.32: But I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not: and when thou art converted, strengthen thy brethren.
nt/luke/22.33: And he said unto him, Lord, I am ready to go with thee, both into prison, and to death.
nt/luke/22.34: And he said, I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me.
nt/luke/22.35: And he said unto them, When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing? And they said, Nothing.
nt/luke/22.36: Then said he unto them, But now, he that hath a purse, let him take [italics]it,[/italics] and likewise [italics]his[/italics] scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.
nt/luke/22.37: For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end.
nt/luke/22.38: And they said, Lord, behold, here [italics]are[/italics] two swords. And he said unto them, It is enough.
nt/luke/22.39: ¶ And he came out, and went, as he was wont, to the mount of Olives; and his disciples also followed him.
nt/luke/22.40: And when he was at the place, he said unto them, Pray that ye enter not into temptation.
nt/luke/22.41: And he was withdrawn from them about a stone’s cast, and kneeled down, and prayed,
nt/luke/22.42: Saying, Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup from me: nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.
nt/luke/22.43: And there appeared an angel unto him from heaven, strengthening him.
nt/luke/22.44: And being in an agony he prayed more earnestly: and his sweat was as it were great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
nt/luke/22.45: And when he rose up from prayer, and was come to his disciples, he found them sleeping for sorrow,
nt/luke/22.46: And said unto them, Why sleep ye? rise and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.
nt/luke/22.47: ¶ And while he yet spake, behold a multitude, and he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, went before them, and drew near unto Jesus to kiss him.
nt/luke/22.48: But Jesus said unto him, Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
nt/luke/22.49: When they which were about him saw what would follow, they said unto him, Lord, shall we smite with the sword?
nt/luke/22.50: ¶ And one of them smote the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.
nt/luke/22.51: And Jesus answered and said, Suffer ye thus far. And he touched his ear, and healed him.
nt/luke/22.52: Then Jesus said unto the chief priests, and captains of the temple, and the elders, which were come to him, Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves?
nt/luke/22.53: When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.
nt/luke/22.54: ¶ Then took they him, and led [italics]him,[/italics] and brought him into the high priest’s house. And Peter followed afar off.
nt/luke/22.55: And when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the hall, and were set down together, Peter sat down among them.
nt/luke/22.56: But a certain maid beheld him as he sat by the fire, and earnestly looked upon him, and said, This man was also with him.
nt/luke/22.57: And he denied him, saying, Woman, I know him not.
nt/luke/22.58: And after a little while another saw him, and said, Thou art also of them. And Peter said, Man, I am not.
nt/luke/22.59: And about the space of one hour after another confidently affirmed, saying, Of a truth this [italics]fellow[/italics] also was with him: for he is a Galilæan.
nt/luke/22.60: And Peter said, Man, I know not what thou sayest. And immediately, while he yet spake, the cock crew.
nt/luke/22.61: And the Lord turned, and looked upon Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
nt/luke/22.62: And Peter went out, and wept bitterly.
nt/luke/22.63: ¶ And the men that held Jesus mocked him, and smote [italics]him.[/italics]
nt/luke/22.64: And when they had blindfolded him, they struck him on the face, and asked him, saying, Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?
nt/luke/22.65: And many other things blasphemously spake they against him.
nt/luke/22.66: ¶ And as soon as it was day, the elders of the people and the chief priests and the scribes came together, and led him into their council, saying,
nt/luke/22.67: Art thou the Christ? tell us. And he said unto them, If I tell you, ye will not believe:
nt/luke/22.68: And if I also ask [italics]you,[/italics] ye will not answer me, nor let [italics]me[/italics] go.
nt/luke/22.69: Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.
nt/luke/22.70: Then said they all, Art thou then the Son of God? And he said unto them, Ye say that I am.
nt/luke/22.71: And they said, What need we any further witness? for we ourselves have heard of his own mouth.

nt/luke/23.1: And the whole multitude of them arose, and led him unto Pilate.
nt/luke/23.2: And they began to accuse him, saying, We found this [italics]fellow[/italics] perverting the nation, and forbidding to give tribute to Cæsar, saying that he himself is Christ a King.
nt/luke/23.3: And Pilate asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answered him and said, Thou sayest [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/luke/23.4: Then said Pilate to the chief priests and [italics]to[/italics] the people, I find no fault in this man.
nt/luke/23.5: And they were the more fierce, saying, He stirreth up the people, teaching throughout all Jewry, beginning from Galilee to this place.
nt/luke/23.6: When Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked whether the man were a Galilæan.
nt/luke/23.7: And as soon as he knew that he belonged unto Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who himself also was at Jerusalem at that time.
nt/luke/23.8: ¶ And when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceeding glad: for he was desirous to see him of a long [italics]season,[/italics] because he had heard many things of him; and he hoped to have seen some miracle done by him.
nt/luke/23.9: Then he questioned with him in many words; but he answered him nothing.
nt/luke/23.10: And the chief priests and scribes stood and vehemently accused him.
nt/luke/23.11: And Herod with his men of war set him at nought, and mocked [italics]him,[/italics] and arrayed him in a gorgeous robe, and sent him again to Pilate.
nt/luke/23.12: ¶ And the same day Pilate and Herod were made friends together: for before they were at enmity between themselves.
nt/luke/23.13: ¶ And Pilate, when he had called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
nt/luke/23.14: Said unto them, Ye have brought this man unto me, as one that perverteth the people: and, behold, I, having examined [italics]him[/italics] before you, have found no fault in this man touching those things whereof ye accuse him:
nt/luke/23.15: No, nor yet Herod: for I sent you to him; and, lo, nothing worthy of death is done unto him.
nt/luke/23.16: I will therefore chastise him, and release [italics]him.[/italics]
nt/luke/23.17: (For of necessity he must release one unto them at the feast.)
nt/luke/23.18: And they cried out all at once, saying, Away with this [italics]man,[/italics] and release unto us Barabbas:
nt/luke/23.19: (Who for a certain sedition made in the city, and for murder, was cast into prison.)
nt/luke/23.20: Pilate therefore, willing to release Jesus, spake again to them.
nt/luke/23.21: But they cried, saying, Crucify [italics]him,[/italics] crucify him.
nt/luke/23.22: And he said unto them the third time, Why, what evil hath he done? I have found no cause of death in him: I will therefore chastise him, and let [italics]him[/italics] go.
nt/luke/23.23: And they were instant with loud voices, requiring that he might be crucified. And the voices of them and of the chief priests prevailed.
nt/luke/23.24: And Pilate gave sentence that it should be as they required.
nt/luke/23.25: And he released unto them him that for sedition and murder was cast into prison, whom they had desired; but he delivered Jesus to their will.
nt/luke/23.26: And as they led him away, they laid hold upon one Simon, a Cyrenian, coming out of the country, and on him they laid the cross, that he might bear [italics]it[/italics] after Jesus.
nt/luke/23.27: ¶ And there followed him a great company of people, and of women, which also bewailed and lamented him.
nt/luke/23.28: But Jesus turning unto them said, Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children.
nt/luke/23.29: For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed [italics]are[/italics] the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck.
nt/luke/23.30: Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.
nt/luke/23.31: For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?
nt/luke/23.32: And there were also two other, malefactors, led with him to be put to death.
nt/luke/23.33: And when they were come to the place, which is called Calvary, there they crucified him, and the malefactors, one on the right hand, and the other on the left.
nt/luke/23.34: ¶ Then said Jesus, Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do. And they parted his raiment, and cast lots.
nt/luke/23.35: And the people stood beholding. And the rulers also with them derided [italics]him,[/italics] saying, He saved others; let him save himself, if he be Christ, the chosen of God.
nt/luke/23.36: And the soldiers also mocked him, coming to him, and offering him vinegar,
nt/luke/23.37: And saying, If thou be the king of the Jews, save thyself.
nt/luke/23.38: And a superscription also was written over him in letters of Greek, and Latin, and Hebrew, This Is the King of the Jews.
nt/luke/23.39: ¶ And one of the malefactors which were hanged railed on him, saying, If thou be Christ, save thyself and us.
nt/luke/23.40: But the other answering rebuked him, saying, Dost not thou fear God, seeing thou art in the same condemnation?
nt/luke/23.41: And we indeed justly; for we receive the due reward of our deeds: but this man hath done nothing amiss.
nt/luke/23.42: And he said unto Jesus, Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom.
nt/luke/23.43: And Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.
nt/luke/23.44: And it was about the sixth hour, and there was a darkness over all the earth until the ninth hour.
nt/luke/23.45: And the sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was rent in the midst.
nt/luke/23.46: ¶ And when Jesus had cried with a loud voice, he said, Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit: and having said thus, he gave up the ghost.
nt/luke/23.47: Now when the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, Certainly this was a righteous man.
nt/luke/23.48: And all the people that came together to that sight, beholding the things which were done, smote their breasts, and returned.
nt/luke/23.49: And all his acquaintance, and the women that followed him from Galilee, stood afar off, beholding these things.
nt/luke/23.50: ¶ And, behold, [italics]there was[/italics] a man named Joseph, a counsellor; [italics]and he was[/italics] a good man, and a just:
nt/luke/23.51: (The same had not consented to the counsel and deed of them;) [italics]he was[/italics] of Arimathæa, a city of the Jews: who also himself waited for the kingdom of God.
nt/luke/23.52: This [italics]man[/italics] went unto Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus.
nt/luke/23.53: And he took it down, and wrapped it in linen, and laid it in a sepulchre that was hewn in stone, wherein never man before was laid.
nt/luke/23.54: And that day was the preparation, and the sabbath drew on.
nt/luke/23.55: And the women also, which came with him from Galilee, followed after, and beheld the sepulchre, and how his body was laid.
nt/luke/23.56: And they returned, and prepared spices and ointments; and rested the sabbath day according to the commandment.

nt/luke/24.1: Now upon the first [italics]day[/italics] of the week, very early in the morning, they came unto the sepulchre, bringing the spices which they had prepared, and certain [italics]others[/italics] with them.
nt/luke/24.2: And they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre.
nt/luke/24.3: And they entered in, and found not the body of the Lord Jesus.
nt/luke/24.4: And it came to pass, as they were much perplexed thereabout, behold, two men stood by them in shining garments:
nt/luke/24.5: And as they were afraid, and bowed down [italics]their[/italics] faces to the earth, they said unto them, Why seek ye the living among the dead?
nt/luke/24.6: He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
nt/luke/24.7: Saying, The Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.
nt/luke/24.9: And returned from the sepulchre, and told all these things unto the eleven, and to all the rest.
nt/luke/24.10: It was Mary Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary [italics]the mother[/italics] of James, and other [italics]women that were[/italics] with them, which told these things unto the apostles.
nt/luke/24.11: And their words seemed to them as idle tales, and they believed them not.
nt/luke/24.12: Then arose Peter, and ran unto the sepulchre; and stooping down, he beheld the linen clothes laid by themselves, and departed, wondering in himself at that which was come to pass.
nt/luke/24.13: ¶ And, behold, two of them went that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was from Jerusalem [italics]about[/italics] threescore furlongs.
nt/luke/24.14: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
nt/luke/24.15: And it came to pass, that, while they communed [italics]together[/italics] and reasoned, Jesus himself drew near, and went with them.
nt/luke/24.16: But their eyes were holden that they should not know him.
nt/luke/24.17: And he said unto them, What manner of communications [italics]are[/italics] these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
nt/luke/24.18: And the one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answering said unto him, Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things which are come to pass there in these days?
nt/luke/24.19: And he said unto them, What things? And they said unto him, Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
nt/luke/24.20: And how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death, and have crucified him.
nt/luke/24.21: But we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed Israel: and beside all this, to day is the third day since these things were done.
nt/luke/24.22: Yea, and certain women also of our company made us astonished, which were early at the sepulchre;
nt/luke/24.23: And when they found not his body, they came, saying, that they had also seen a vision of angels, which said that he was alive.
nt/luke/24.24: And certain of them which were with us went to the sepulchre, and found [italics]it[/italics] even so as the women had said: but him they saw not.
nt/luke/24.25: Then he said unto them, O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken:
nt/luke/24.26: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?
nt/luke/24.27: And beginning at Moses and all the prophets, he expounded unto them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself.
nt/luke/24.28: And they drew nigh unto the village, whither they went: and he made as though he would have gone further.
nt/luke/24.29: But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
nt/luke/24.30: And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed [italics]it,[/italics] and brake, and gave to them.
nt/luke/24.31: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him; and he vanished out of their sight.
nt/luke/24.32: And they said one to another, Did not our heart burn within us, while he talked with us by the way, and while he opened to us the scriptures?
nt/luke/24.33: And they rose up the same hour, and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and them that were with them,
nt/luke/24.34: Saying, The Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.
nt/luke/24.35: And they told what things [italics]were done[/italics] in the way, and how he was known of them in breaking of bread.
nt/luke/24.36: ¶ And as they thus spake, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, Peace [italics]be[/italics] unto you.
nt/luke/24.37: But they were terrified and affrighted, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.
nt/luke/24.38: And he said unto them, Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?
nt/luke/24.39: Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.
nt/luke/24.40: And when he had thus spoken, he shewed them [italics]his[/italics] hands and [italics]his[/italics] feet.
nt/luke/24.41: And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
nt/luke/24.42: And they gave him a piece of a broiled fish, and of an honeycomb.
nt/luke/24.43: And he took [italics]it,[/italics] and did eat before them.
nt/luke/24.44: And he said unto them, These [italics]are[/italics] the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you, that all things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and [italics]in[/italics] the prophets, and [italics]in[/italics] the psalms, concerning me.
nt/luke/24.45: Then opened he their understanding, that they might understand the scriptures,
nt/luke/24.46: And said unto them, Thus it is written, and thus it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day:
nt/luke/24.47: And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
nt/luke/24.48: And ye are witnesses of these things.
nt/luke/24.49: ¶ And, behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be endued with power from on high.
nt/luke/24.50: ¶ And he led them out as far as to Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.
nt/luke/24.51: And it came to pass, while he blessed them, he was parted from them, and carried up into heaven.
nt/luke/24.52: And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy:
nt/luke/24.53: And were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.

nt/luke/3.1: Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Cæsar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judæa, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of Ituræa and of the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias the tetrarch of Abilene,
nt/luke/3.2: Annas and Caiaphas being the high priests, the word of God came unto John the son of Zacharias in the wilderness.
nt/luke/3.3: And he came into all the country about Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins;
nt/luke/3.4: As it is written in the book of the words of Esaias the prophet, saying, The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
nt/luke/3.5: Every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be brought low; and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough ways [italics]shall be[/italics] made smooth;
nt/luke/3.6: And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.
nt/luke/3.7: Then said he to the multitude that came forth to be baptized of him, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
nt/luke/3.8: Bring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance, and begin not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham to [italics]our[/italics] father: for I say unto you, That God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.
nt/luke/3.9: And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: every tree therefore which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
nt/luke/3.10: And the people asked him, saying, What shall we do then?
nt/luke/3.11: He answereth and saith unto them, He that hath two coats, let him impart to him that hath none; and he that hath meat, let him do likewise.
nt/luke/3.12: Then came also publicans to be baptized, and said unto him, Master, what shall we do?
nt/luke/3.13: And he said unto them, Exact no more than that which is appointed you.
nt/luke/3.14: And the soldiers likewise demanded of him, saying, And what shall we do? And he said unto them, Do violence to no man, neither accuse [italics]any[/italics] falsely; and be content with your wages.
nt/luke/3.15: And as the people were in expectation, and all men mused in their hearts of John, whether he were the Christ, or not;
nt/luke/3.16: John answered, saying unto [italics]them[/italics] all, I indeed baptize you with water; but one mightier than I cometh, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to unloose: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire:
nt/luke/3.17: Whose fan [italics]is[/italics] in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and will gather the wheat into his garner; but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.
nt/luke/3.18: And many other things in his exhortation preached he unto the people.
nt/luke/3.19: But Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him for Herodias his brother Philip’s wife, and for all the evils which Herod had done,
nt/luke/3.20: Added yet this above all, that he shut up John in prison.
nt/luke/3.21: Now when all the people were baptized, it came to pass, that Jesus also being baptized, and praying, the heaven was opened,
nt/luke/3.22: And the Holy Ghost descended in a bodily shape like a dove upon him, and a voice came from heaven, which said, Thou art my beloved Son; in thee I am well pleased.
nt/luke/3.23: And Jesus himself began to be about thirty years of age, being (as was supposed) the son of Joseph, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Heli,
nt/luke/3.24: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Matthat, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Levi, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Melchi, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Janna, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Joseph,
nt/luke/3.25: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Mattathias, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Amos, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Naum, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Esli, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Nagge,
nt/luke/3.26: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Maath, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Mattathias, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Semei, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Joseph, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Juda,
nt/luke/3.27: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Joanna, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Rhesa, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Zorobabel, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Salathiel, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Neri,
nt/luke/3.28: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Melchi, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Addi, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Cosam, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Elmodam, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Er,
nt/luke/3.29: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Jose, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Eliezer, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Jorim, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Matthat, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Levi,
nt/luke/3.30: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Simeon, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Juda, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Joseph, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Jonan, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Eliakim,
nt/luke/3.31: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Melea, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Menan, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Mattatha, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Nathan, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of David,
nt/luke/3.32: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Jesse, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Obed, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Booz, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Salmon, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Naasson,
nt/luke/3.33: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Aminadab, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Aram, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Esrom, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Phares, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Juda,
nt/luke/3.34: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Jacob, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Isaac, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Abraham, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Thara, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Nachor,
nt/luke/3.35: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Saruch, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Ragau, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Phalec, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Heber, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Sala,
nt/luke/3.36: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Cainan, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Arphaxad, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Sem, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Noe, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Lamech,
nt/luke/3.37: Which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Mathusala, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Enoch, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Jared, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Maleleel, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Cainan,
nt/luke/3.38: Which was [italics]the [/italics][italics]son[/italics] of Enos, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Seth, which was [italics]the son[/italics] of Adam, which was [italics]the [/italics][italics]son[/italics] of God.

nt/luke/4.1: And Jesus being full of the Holy Ghost returned from Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,
nt/luke/4.2: Being forty days tempted of the devil. And in those days he did eat nothing: and when they were ended, he afterward hungered.
nt/luke/4.3: And the devil said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, command this stone that it be made bread.
nt/luke/4.4: And Jesus answered him, saying, It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.
nt/luke/4.5: And the devil, taking him up into an high mountain, shewed unto him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
nt/luke/4.6: And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and to whomsoever I will I give it.
nt/luke/4.7: If thou therefore wilt worship me, all shall be thine.
nt/luke/4.8: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.
nt/luke/4.9: And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence:
nt/luke/4.10: For it is written, He shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee:
nt/luke/4.11: And in [italics]their[/italics] hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
nt/luke/4.12: And Jesus answering said unto him, It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
nt/luke/4.13: And when the devil had ended all the temptation, he departed from him for a season.
nt/luke/4.14: ¶ And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee: and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about.
nt/luke/4.15: And he taught in their synagogues, being glorified of all.
nt/luke/4.16: ¶ And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up: and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up for to read.
nt/luke/4.17: And there was delivered unto him the book of the prophet Esaias. And when he had opened the book, he found the place where it was written,
nt/luke/4.18: The Spirit of the Lord [italics]is[/italics] upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised,
nt/luke/4.19: To preach the acceptable year of the Lord.
nt/luke/4.20: And he closed the book, and he gave [italics]it[/italics] again to the minister, and sat down. And the eyes of all them that were in the synagogue were fastened on him.
nt/luke/4.21: And he began to say unto them, This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.
nt/luke/4.22: And all bare him witness, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this Joseph’s son?
nt/luke/4.23: And he said unto them, Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country.
nt/luke/4.24: And he said, Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country.
nt/luke/4.25: But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land;
nt/luke/4.26: But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, [italics]a city[/italics] of Sidon, unto a woman [italics]that was[/italics] a widow.
nt/luke/4.27: And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.
nt/luke/4.28: And all they in the synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,
nt/luke/4.29: And rose up, and thrust him out of the city, and led him unto the brow of the hill whereon their city was built, that they might cast him down headlong.
nt/luke/4.30: But he passing through the midst of them went his way,
nt/luke/4.31: And came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and taught them on the sabbath days.
nt/luke/4.32: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for his word was with power.
nt/luke/4.33: ¶ And in the synagogue there was a man, which had a spirit of an unclean devil, and cried out with a loud voice,
nt/luke/4.34: Saying, Let [italics]us[/italics] alone; what have we to do with thee, [italics]thou[/italics] Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art; the Holy One of God.
nt/luke/4.35: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him. And when the devil had thrown him in the midst, he came out of him, and hurt him not.
nt/luke/4.36: And they were all amazed, and spake among themselves, saying, What a word [italics]is[/italics] this! for with authority and power he commandeth the unclean spirits, and they come out.
nt/luke/4.37: And the fame of him went out into every place of the country round about.
nt/luke/4.38: ¶ And he arose out of the synagogue, and entered into Simon’s house. And Simon’s wife’s mother was taken with a great fever; and they besought him for her.
nt/luke/4.39: And he stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her: and immediately she arose and ministered unto them.
nt/luke/4.40: ¶ Now when the sun was setting, all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.
nt/luke/4.41: And devils also came out of many, crying out, and saying, Thou art Christ the Son of God. And he rebuking [italics]them[/italics] suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was Christ.
nt/luke/4.42: And when it was day, he departed and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came unto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.
nt/luke/4.43: And he said unto them, I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.
nt/luke/4.44: And he preached in the synagogues of Galilee.

nt/luke/5.1: And it came to pass, that, as the people pressed upon him to hear the word of God, he stood by the lake of Gennesaret,
nt/luke/5.2: And saw two ships standing by the lake: but the fishermen were gone out of them, and were washing [italics]their[/italics] nets.
nt/luke/5.3: And he entered into one of the ships, which was Simon’s, and prayed him that he would thrust out a little from the land. And he sat down, and taught the people out of the ship.
nt/luke/5.4: Now when he had left speaking, he said unto Simon, Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.
nt/luke/5.5: And Simon answering said unto him, Master, we have toiled all the night, and have taken nothing: nevertheless at thy word I will let down the net.
nt/luke/5.6: And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes: and their net brake.
nt/luke/5.7: And they beckoned unto [italics]their[/italics] partners, which were in the other ship, that they should come and help them. And they came, and filled both the ships, so that they began to sink.
nt/luke/5.8: When Simon Peter saw [italics]it,[/italics] he fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O Lord.
nt/luke/5.9: For he was astonished, and all that were with him, at the draught of the fishes which they had taken:
nt/luke/5.10: And so [italics]was[/italics] also James, and John, the sons of Zebedee, which were partners with Simon. And Jesus said unto Simon, Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.
nt/luke/5.11: And when they had brought their ships to land, they forsook all, and followed him.
nt/luke/5.12: ¶ And it came to pass, when he was in a certain city, behold a man full of leprosy: who seeing Jesus fell on [italics]his[/italics] face, and besought him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
nt/luke/5.13: And he put forth [italics]his[/italics] hand, and touched him, saying, I will: be thou clean. And immediately the leprosy departed from him.
nt/luke/5.14: And he charged him to tell no man: but go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
nt/luke/5.15: But so much the more went there a fame abroad of him: and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.
nt/luke/5.16: ¶ And he withdrew himself into the wilderness, and prayed.
nt/luke/5.17: And it came to pass on a certain day, as he was teaching, that there were Pharisees and doctors of the law sitting by, which were come out of every town of Galilee, and Judæa, and Jerusalem: and the power of the Lord was [italics]present[/italics] to heal them.
nt/luke/5.18: ¶ And, behold, men brought in a bed a man which was taken with a palsy: and they sought [italics]means[/italics] to bring him in, and to lay [italics]him[/italics] before him.
nt/luke/5.19: And when they could not find by what [italics]way[/italics] they might bring him in because of the multitude, they went upon the housetop, and let him down through the tiling with [italics]his[/italics] couch into the midst before Jesus.
nt/luke/5.20: And when he saw their faith, he said unto him, Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.
nt/luke/5.21: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, Who is this which speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?
nt/luke/5.22: But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answering said unto them, What reason ye in your hearts?
nt/luke/5.23: Whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Rise up and walk?
nt/luke/5.24: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power upon earth to forgive sins, (he said unto the sick of the palsy,) I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy couch, and go into thine house.
nt/luke/5.25: And immediately he rose up before them, and took up that whereon he lay, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.
nt/luke/5.26: And they were all amazed, and they glorified God, and were filled with fear, saying, We have seen strange things to day.
nt/luke/5.27: ¶ And after these things he went forth, and saw a publican, named Levi, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he said unto him, Follow me.
nt/luke/5.28: And he left all, rose up, and followed him.
nt/luke/5.29: And Levi made him a great feast in his own house: and there was a great company of publicans and of others that sat down with them.
nt/luke/5.30: But their scribes and Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, Why do ye eat and drink with publicans and sinners?
nt/luke/5.31: And Jesus answering said unto them, They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick.
nt/luke/5.32: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
nt/luke/5.33: ¶ And they said unto him, Why do the disciples of John fast often, and make prayers, and likewise [italics]the disciples[/italics] of the Pharisees; but thine eat and drink?
nt/luke/5.34: And he said unto them, Can ye make the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them?
nt/luke/5.35: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
nt/luke/5.36: ¶ And he spake also a parable unto them; No man putteth a piece of a new garment upon an old; if otherwise, then both the new maketh a rent, and the piece that was [italics]taken[/italics] out of the new agreeth not with the old.
nt/luke/5.37: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles; else the new wine will burst the bottles, and be spilled, and the bottles shall perish.
nt/luke/5.38: But new wine must be put into new bottles; and both are preserved.
nt/luke/5.39: No man also having drunk old [italics]wine[/italics] straightway desireth new: for he saith, The old is better.

nt/mark/1.1: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;
nt/mark/1.2: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
nt/mark/1.3: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
nt/mark/1.4: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
nt/mark/1.5: And there went out unto him all the land of Judæa, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.
nt/mark/1.6: And John was clothed with camel’s hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;
nt/mark/1.7: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.
nt/mark/1.8: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
nt/mark/1.9: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.
nt/mark/1.10: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:
nt/mark/1.11: And there came a voice from heaven, [italics]saying,[/italics] Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
nt/mark/1.12: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.
nt/mark/1.13: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.
nt/mark/1.14: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
nt/mark/1.15: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.
nt/mark/1.16: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
nt/mark/1.17: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
nt/mark/1.18: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.
nt/mark/1.19: And when he had gone a little further thence, he saw James the [italics]son[/italics] of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
nt/mark/1.20: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.
nt/mark/1.21: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.
nt/mark/1.22: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.
nt/mark/1.23: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
nt/mark/1.24: Saying, Let [italics]us[/italics] alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.
nt/mark/1.25: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
nt/mark/1.26: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.
nt/mark/1.27: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine [italics]is[/italics] this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.
nt/mark/1.28: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.
nt/mark/1.29: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
nt/mark/1.30: But Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.
nt/mark/1.31: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.
nt/mark/1.32: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.
nt/mark/1.33: And all the city was gathered together at the door.
nt/mark/1.34: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.
nt/mark/1.35: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.
nt/mark/1.36: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.
nt/mark/1.37: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All [italics]men[/italics] seek for thee.
nt/mark/1.38: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
nt/mark/1.39: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.
nt/mark/1.40: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
nt/mark/1.41: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth [italics]his[/italics] hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
nt/mark/1.42: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.
nt/mark/1.43: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;
nt/mark/1.44: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
nt/mark/1.45: But he went out, and began to publish [italics]it[/italics] much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.

nt/mark/10.1: And he arose from thence, and cometh into the coasts of Judæa by the farther side of Jordan: and the people resort unto him again; and, as he was wont, he taught them again.
nt/mark/10.2: ¶ And the Pharisees came to him, and asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away [italics]his[/italics] wife? tempting him.
nt/mark/10.3: And he answered and said unto them, What did Moses command you?
nt/mark/10.4: And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement, and to put [italics]her[/italics] away.
nt/mark/10.5: And Jesus answered and said unto them, For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.
nt/mark/10.6: But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.
nt/mark/10.7: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;
nt/mark/10.8: And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.
nt/mark/10.9: What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.
nt/mark/10.10: And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same [italics]matter.[/italics]
nt/mark/10.11: And he saith unto them, Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.
nt/mark/10.12: And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.
nt/mark/10.13: ¶ And they brought young children to him, that he should touch them: and [italics]his[/italics] disciples rebuked those that brought [italics]them.[/italics]
nt/mark/10.14: But when Jesus saw [italics]it,[/italics] he was much displeased, and said unto them, Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
nt/mark/10.15: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter therein.
nt/mark/10.16: And he took them up in his arms, put [italics]his[/italics] hands upon them, and blessed them.
nt/mark/10.17: ¶ And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled to him, and asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?
nt/mark/10.18: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? [italics]there is[/italics] none good but one, [italics]that is,[/italics] God.
nt/mark/10.19: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.
nt/mark/10.20: And he answered and said unto him, Master, all these have I observed from my youth.
nt/mark/10.21: Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.
nt/mark/10.22: And he was sad at that saying, and went away grieved: for he had great possessions.
nt/mark/10.23: ¶ And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!
nt/mark/10.24: And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God!
nt/mark/10.25: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
nt/mark/10.26: And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselves, Who then can be saved?
nt/mark/10.27: And Jesus looking upon them saith, With men [italics]it is[/italics] impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.
nt/mark/10.28: ¶ Then Peter began to say unto him, Lo, we have left all, and have followed thee.
nt/mark/10.29: And Jesus answered and said, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel’s,
