nt/mark/10.30: But he shall receive an hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.
nt/mark/10.31: But many [italics]that are[/italics] first shall be last; and the last first.
nt/mark/10.32: ¶ And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus went before them: and they were amazed; and as they followed, they were afraid. And he took again the twelve, and began to tell them what things should happen unto him,
nt/mark/10.33: [italics]Saying,[/italics] Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:
nt/mark/10.34: And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.
nt/mark/10.35: ¶ And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, come unto him, saying, Master, we would that thou shouldest do for us whatsoever we shall desire.
nt/mark/10.36: And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?
nt/mark/10.37: They said unto him, Grant unto us that we may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.
nt/mark/10.38: But Jesus said unto them, Ye know not what ye ask: can ye drink of the cup that I drink of? and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?
nt/mark/10.39: And they said unto him, We can. And Jesus said unto them, Ye shall indeed drink of the cup that I drink of; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized:
nt/mark/10.40: But to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but [italics]it shall be given to them[/italics] for whom it is prepared.
nt/mark/10.41: And when the ten heard [italics]it,[/italics] they began to be much displeased with James and John.
nt/mark/10.42: But Jesus called them [italics]to him,[/italics] and saith unto them, Ye know that they which are accounted to rule over the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and their great ones exercise authority upon them.
nt/mark/10.43: But so shall it not be among you: but whosoever will be great among you, shall be your minister:
nt/mark/10.44: And whosoever of you will be the chiefest, shall be servant of all.
nt/mark/10.45: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.
nt/mark/10.46: ¶ And they came to Jericho: and as he went out of Jericho with his disciples and a great number of people, blind Bartimæus, the son of Timæus, sat by the highway side begging.
nt/mark/10.47: And when he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, and say, Jesus, [italics]thou[/italics] Son of David, have mercy on me.
nt/mark/10.48: And many charged him that he should hold his peace: but he cried the more a great deal, [italics]Thou[/italics] Son of David, have mercy on me.
nt/mark/10.49: And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be called. And they call the blind man, saying unto him, Be of good comfort, rise; he calleth thee.
nt/mark/10.50: And he, casting away his garment, rose, and came to Jesus.
nt/mark/10.51: And Jesus answered and said unto him, What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.
nt/mark/10.52: And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.

nt/mark/11.1: And when they came nigh to Jerusalem, unto Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount of Olives, he sendeth forth two of his disciples,
nt/mark/11.2: And saith unto them, Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring [italics]him.[/italics]
nt/mark/11.3: And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.
nt/mark/11.4: And they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door without in a place where two ways met; and they loose him.
nt/mark/11.5: And certain of them that stood there said unto them, What do ye, loosing the colt?
nt/mark/11.6: And they said unto them even as Jesus had commanded: and they let them go.
nt/mark/11.7: And they brought the colt to Jesus, and cast their garments on him; and he sat upon him.
nt/mark/11.8: And many spread their garments in the way: and others cut down branches off the trees, and strawed [italics]them[/italics] in the way.
nt/mark/11.9: And they that went before, and they that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna; Blessed [italics]is[/italics] he that cometh in the name of the Lord:
nt/mark/11.10: Blessed [italics]be[/italics] the kingdom of our father David, that cometh in the name of the Lord: Hosanna in the highest.
nt/mark/11.11: And Jesus entered into Jerusalem, and into the temple: and when he had looked round about upon all things, and now the eventide was come, he went out unto Bethany with the twelve.
nt/mark/11.12: ¶ And on the morrow, when they were come from Bethany, he was hungry:
nt/mark/11.13: And seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came, if haply he might find any thing thereon: and when he came to it, he found nothing but leaves; for the time of figs was not [italics]yet.[/italics]
nt/mark/11.14: And Jesus answered and said unto it, No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever. And his disciples heard [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/mark/11.15: ¶ And they come to Jerusalem: and Jesus went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves;
nt/mark/11.16: And would not suffer that any man should carry [italics]any[/italics] vessel through the temple.
nt/mark/11.17: And he taught, saying unto them, Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.
nt/mark/11.18: And the scribes and chief priests heard [italics]it,[/italics] and sought how they might destroy him: for they feared him, because all the people was astonished at his doctrine.
nt/mark/11.19: And when even was come, he went out of the city.
nt/mark/11.20: ¶ And in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots.
nt/mark/11.21: And Peter calling to remembrance saith unto him, Master, behold, the fig tree which thou cursedst is withered away.
nt/mark/11.22: And Jesus answering saith unto them, Have faith in God.
nt/mark/11.23: For verily I say unto you, That whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall believe that those things which he saith shall come to pass; he shall have whatsoever he saith.
nt/mark/11.24: Therefore I say unto you, What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive [italics]them,[/italics] and ye shall have [italics]them.[/italics]
nt/mark/11.25: And when ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.
nt/mark/11.26: But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses.
nt/mark/11.27: ¶ And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders,
nt/mark/11.28: And say unto him, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority to do these things?
nt/mark/11.29: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
nt/mark/11.30: The baptism of John, was [italics]it[/italics] from heaven, or of men? answer me.
nt/mark/11.31: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then did ye not believe him?
nt/mark/11.32: But if we shall say, Of men; they feared the people: for all [italics]men[/italics] counted John, that he was a prophet indeed.
nt/mark/11.33: And they answered and said unto Jesus, We cannot tell. And Jesus answering saith unto them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

nt/mark/14.1: After two days was [italics]the feast of[/italics] the passover, and of unleavened bread: and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take him by craft, and put [italics]him[/italics] to death.
nt/mark/14.2: But they said, Not on the feast [italics]day,[/italics] lest there be an uproar of the people.
nt/mark/14.3: ¶ And being in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment of spikenard very precious; and she brake the box, and poured [italics]it[/italics] on his head.
nt/mark/14.4: And there were some that had indignation within themselves, and said, Why was this waste of the ointment made?
nt/mark/14.5: For it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and have been given to the poor. And they murmured against her.
nt/mark/14.6: And Jesus said, Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.
nt/mark/14.7: For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.
nt/mark/14.8: She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.
nt/mark/14.9: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, [italics]this[/italics] also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.
nt/mark/14.10: ¶ And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests, to betray him unto them.
nt/mark/14.11: And when they heard [italics]it,[/italics] they were glad, and promised to give him money. And he sought how he might conveniently betray him.
nt/mark/14.12: ¶ And the first day of unleavened bread, when they killed the passover, his disciples said unto him, Where wilt thou that we go and prepare that thou mayest eat the passover?
nt/mark/14.13: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them, Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.
nt/mark/14.14: And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
nt/mark/14.15: And he will shew you a large upper room furnished [italics]and[/italics] prepared: there make ready for us.
nt/mark/14.16: And his disciples went forth, and came into the city, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
nt/mark/14.17: And in the evening he cometh with the twelve.
nt/mark/14.18: And as they sat and did eat, Jesus said, Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.
nt/mark/14.19: And they began to be sorrowful, and to say unto him one by one, [italics]Is[/italics] it I? and another [italics]said, Is[/italics] it I?
nt/mark/14.20: And he answered and said unto them, [italics]It is[/italics] one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish.
nt/mark/14.21: The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.
nt/mark/14.22: ¶ And as they did eat, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake [italics]it,[/italics] and gave to them, and said, Take, eat: this is my body.
nt/mark/14.23: And he took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave [italics]it[/italics] to them: and they all drank of it.
nt/mark/14.24: And he said unto them, This is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.
nt/mark/14.25: Verily I say unto you, I will drink no more of the fruit of the vine, until that day that I drink it new in the kingdom of God.
nt/mark/14.26: ¶ And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.
nt/mark/14.27: And Jesus saith unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.
nt/mark/14.28: But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.
nt/mark/14.29: But Peter said unto him, Although all shall be offended, yet [italics]will[/italics] not I.
nt/mark/14.30: And Jesus saith unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this day, [italics]even[/italics] in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.
nt/mark/14.31: But he spake the more vehemently, If I should die with thee, I will not deny thee in any wise. Likewise also said they all.
nt/mark/14.32: And they came to a place which was named Gethsemane: and he saith to his disciples, Sit ye here, while I shall pray.
nt/mark/14.33: And he taketh with him Peter and James and John, and began to be sore amazed, and to be very heavy;
nt/mark/14.34: And saith unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful unto death: tarry ye here, and watch.
nt/mark/14.35: And he went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
nt/mark/14.36: And he said, Abba, Father, all things [italics]are[/italics] possible unto thee; take away this cup from me: nevertheless not what I will, but what thou wilt.
nt/mark/14.37: And he cometh, and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter, Simon, sleepest thou? couldest not thou watch one hour?
nt/mark/14.38: Watch ye and pray, lest ye enter into temptation. The spirit truly [italics]is[/italics] ready, but the flesh [italics]is[/italics] weak.
nt/mark/14.39: And again he went away, and prayed, and spake the same words.
nt/mark/14.40: And when he returned, he found them asleep again, (for their eyes were heavy,) neither wist they what to answer him.
nt/mark/14.41: And he cometh the third time, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take [italics]your[/italics] rest: it is enough, the hour is come; behold, the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
nt/mark/14.42: Rise up, let us go; lo, he that betrayeth me is at hand.
nt/mark/14.43: ¶ And immediately, while he yet spake, cometh Judas, one of the twelve, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
nt/mark/14.44: And he that betrayed him had given them a token, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he; take him, and lead [italics]him[/italics] away safely.
nt/mark/14.45: And as soon as he was come, he goeth straightway to him, and saith, Master, master; and kissed him.
nt/mark/14.46: ¶ And they laid their hands on him, and took him.
nt/mark/14.47: And one of them that stood by drew a sword, and smote a servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
nt/mark/14.48: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and [italics]with[/italics] staves to take me?
nt/mark/14.49: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.
nt/mark/14.50: And they all forsook him, and fled.
nt/mark/14.51: And there followed him a certain young man, having a linen cloth cast about [italics]his[/italics] naked [italics]body;[/italics] and the young men laid hold on him:
nt/mark/14.52: And he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.
nt/mark/14.53: ¶ And they led Jesus away to the high priest: and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.
nt/mark/14.54: And Peter followed him afar off, even into the palace of the high priest: and he sat with the servants, and warmed himself at the fire.
nt/mark/14.55: And the chief priests and all the council sought for witness against Jesus to put him to death; and found none.
nt/mark/14.56: For many bare false witness against him, but their witness agreed not together.
nt/mark/14.57: And there arose certain, and bare false witness against him, saying,
nt/mark/14.58: We heard him say, I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and within three days I will build another made without hands.
nt/mark/14.59: But neither so did their witness agree together.
nt/mark/14.60: And the high priest stood up in the midst, and asked Jesus, saying, Answerest thou nothing? what [italics]is it which[/italics] these witness against thee?
nt/mark/14.61: But he held his peace, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, and said unto him, Art thou the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?
nt/mark/14.62: And Jesus said, I am: and ye shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.
nt/mark/14.63: Then the high priest rent his clothes, and saith, What need we any further witnesses?
nt/mark/14.64: Ye have heard the blasphemy: what think ye? And they all condemned him to be guilty of death.
nt/mark/14.65: And some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to buffet him, and to say unto him, Prophesy: and the servants did strike him with the palms of their hands.
nt/mark/14.66: ¶ And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there cometh one of the maids of the high priest:
nt/mark/14.67: And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked upon him, and said, And thou also wast with Jesus of Nazareth.
nt/mark/14.68: But he denied, saying, I know not, neither understand I what thou sayest. And he went out into the porch; and the cock crew.
nt/mark/14.69: And a maid saw him again, and began to say to them that stood by, This is [italics]one[/italics] of them.
nt/mark/14.70: And he denied it again. And a little after, they that stood by said again to Peter, Surely thou art [italics]one[/italics] of them: for thou art a Galilæan, and thy speech agreeth [italics]thereto.[/italics]
nt/mark/14.71: But he began to curse and to swear, [italics]saying,[/italics] I know not this man of whom ye speak.
nt/mark/14.72: And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice. And when he thought thereon, he wept.

nt/mark/15.1: And straightway in the morning the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council, and bound Jesus, and carried [italics]him[/italics] away, and delivered [italics]him[/italics] to Pilate.
nt/mark/15.2: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, Thou sayest [italics]it[/italics][italics].[/italics]
nt/mark/15.3: And the chief priests accused him of many things: but he answered nothing.
nt/mark/15.4: And Pilate asked him again, saying, Answerest thou nothing? behold how many things they witness against thee.
nt/mark/15.5: But Jesus yet answered nothing; so that Pilate marvelled.
nt/mark/15.6: Now at [italics]that[/italics] feast he released unto them one prisoner, whomsoever they desired.
nt/mark/15.7: And there was [italics]one[/italics] named Barabbas, [italics]which lay[/italics] bound with them that had made insurrection with him, who had committed murder in the insurrection.
nt/mark/15.8: And the multitude crying aloud began to desire [italics]him to do[/italics] as he had ever done unto them.
nt/mark/15.9: But Pilate answered them, saying, Will ye that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
nt/mark/15.10: For he knew that the chief priests had delivered him for envy.
nt/mark/15.11: But the chief priests moved the people, that he should rather release Barabbas unto them.
nt/mark/15.12: And Pilate answered and said again unto them, What will ye then that I shall do [italics]unto him[/italics] whom ye call the King of the Jews?
nt/mark/15.13: And they cried out again, Crucify him.
nt/mark/15.14: Then Pilate said unto them, Why, what evil hath he done? And they cried out the more exceedingly, Crucify him.
nt/mark/15.15: ¶ And [italics]so[/italics] Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas unto them, and delivered Jesus, when he had scourged [italics]him,[/italics] to be crucified.
nt/mark/15.16: And the soldiers led him away into the hall, called Prætorium; and they call together the whole band.
nt/mark/15.17: And they clothed him with purple, and plaited a crown of thorns, and put it about his [italics]head,[/italics]
nt/mark/15.18: And began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews!
nt/mark/15.19: And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit upon him, and bowing [italics]their[/italics] knees worshipped him.
nt/mark/15.20: And when they had mocked him, they took off the purple from him, and put his own clothes on him, and led him out to crucify him.
nt/mark/15.21: And they compel one Simon a Cyrenian, who passed by, coming out of the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to bear his cross.
nt/mark/15.22: And they bring him unto the place Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, The place of a skull.
nt/mark/15.23: And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh: but he received [italics]it[/italics] not.
nt/mark/15.24: And when they had crucified him, they parted his garments, casting lots upon them, what every man should take.
nt/mark/15.25: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.
nt/mark/15.26: And the superscription of his accusation was written over, The King of the Jews.
nt/mark/15.27: And with him they crucify two thieves; the one on his right hand, and the other on his left.
nt/mark/15.28: And the scripture was fulfilled, which saith, And he was numbered with the transgressors.
nt/mark/15.29: And they that passed by railed on him, wagging their heads, and saying, Ah, thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest [italics]it[/italics] in three days,
nt/mark/15.30: Save thyself, and come down from the cross.
nt/mark/15.31: Likewise also the chief priests mocking said among themselves with the scribes, He saved others; himself he cannot save.
nt/mark/15.32: Let Christ the King of Israel descend now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him reviled him.
nt/mark/15.33: And when the sixth hour was come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.
nt/mark/15.34: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani? which is, being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
nt/mark/15.35: And some of them that stood by, when they heard [italics]it,[/italics] said, Behold, he calleth Elias.
nt/mark/15.36: And one ran and filled a sponge full of vinegar, and put [italics]it[/italics] on a reed, and gave him to drink, saying, Let alone; let us see whether Elias will come to take him down.
nt/mark/15.37: And Jesus cried with a loud voice, and gave up the ghost.
nt/mark/15.38: And the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom.
nt/mark/15.39: ¶ And when the centurion, which stood over against him, saw that he so cried out, and gave up the ghost, he said, Truly this man was the Son of God.
nt/mark/15.40: There were also women looking on afar off: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;
nt/mark/15.41: (Who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and ministered unto him;) and many other women which came up with him unto Jerusalem.
nt/mark/15.42: ¶ And now when the even was come, because it was the preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,
nt/mark/15.43: Joseph of Arimathæa, an honourable counsellor, which also waited for the kingdom of God, came, and went in boldly unto Pilate, and craved the body of Jesus.
nt/mark/15.44: And Pilate marvelled if he were already dead: and calling [italics]unto him[/italics] the centurion, he asked him whether he had been any while dead.
nt/mark/15.45: And when he knew [italics]it[/italics] of the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.
nt/mark/15.46: And he bought fine linen, and took him down, and wrapped him in the linen, and laid him in a sepulchre which was hewn out of a rock, and rolled a stone unto the door of the sepulchre.
nt/mark/15.47: And Mary Magdalene and Mary [italics]the mother[/italics] of Joses beheld where he was laid.

nt/mark/16.1: And when the sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the [italics]mother[/italics] of James, and Salome, had bought sweet spices, that they might come and anoint him.
nt/mark/16.2: And very early in the morning the first [italics]day[/italics] of the week, they came unto the sepulchre at the rising of the sun.
nt/mark/16.3: And they said among themselves, Who shall roll us away the stone from the door of the sepulchre?
nt/mark/16.4: And when they looked, they saw that the stone was rolled away: for it was very great.
nt/mark/16.5: And entering into the sepulchre, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a long white garment; and they were affrighted.
nt/mark/16.6: And he saith unto them, Be not affrighted: Ye seek Jesus of Nazareth, which was crucified: he is risen; he is not here: behold the place where they laid him.
nt/mark/16.7: But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.
nt/mark/16.8: And they went out quickly, and fled from the sepulchre; for they trembled and were amazed: neither said they any thing to any [italics]man;[/italics] for they were afraid.
nt/mark/16.9: ¶ Now when [italics]Jesus[/italics] was risen early the first [italics]day[/italics] of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had cast seven devils.
nt/mark/16.10: [italics]And[/italics] she went and told them that had been with him, as they mourned and wept.
nt/mark/16.11: And they, when they had heard that he was alive, and had been seen of her, believed not.
nt/mark/16.12: ¶ After that he appeared in another form unto two of them, as they walked, and went into the country.
nt/mark/16.13: And they went and told [italics]it[/italics] unto the residue: neither believed they them.
nt/mark/16.14: ¶ Afterward he appeared unto the eleven as they sat at meat, and upbraided them with their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they believed not them which had seen him after he was risen.
nt/mark/16.15: And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.
nt/mark/16.16: He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he that believeth not shall be damned.
nt/mark/16.17: And these signs shall follow them that believe; In my name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues;
nt/mark/16.18: They shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover.
nt/mark/16.19: ¶ So then after the Lord had spoken unto them, he was received up into heaven, and sat on the right hand of God.
nt/mark/16.20: And they went forth, and preached every where, the Lord working with [italics]them,[/italics] and confirming the word with signs following. Amen.

nt/mark/2.1: And again he entered into Capernaum after [italics]some[/italics] days; and it was noised that he was in the house.
nt/mark/2.2: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive [italics]them,[/italics] no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.
nt/mark/2.3: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.
nt/mark/2.4: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken [italics]it[/italics] up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
nt/mark/2.5: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.
nt/mark/2.6: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
nt/mark/2.7: Why doth this [italics]man[/italics] thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?
nt/mark/2.8: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
nt/mark/2.9: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, [italics]Thy[/italics] sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
nt/mark/2.10: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
nt/mark/2.11: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
nt/mark/2.12: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
nt/mark/2.13: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
nt/mark/2.14: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the [italics]son[/italics] of Alphæus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
nt/mark/2.15: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
nt/mark/2.16: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
nt/mark/2.17: When Jesus heard [italics]it,[/italics] he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
nt/mark/2.18: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
nt/mark/2.19: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
nt/mark/2.20: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
nt/mark/2.21: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.
nt/mark/2.22: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.
nt/mark/2.23: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.
nt/mark/2.24: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
nt/mark/2.25: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
nt/mark/2.26: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
nt/mark/2.27: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
nt/mark/2.28: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.

nt/mark/3.1: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.
nt/mark/3.2: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
nt/mark/3.3: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
nt/mark/3.4: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.
nt/mark/3.5: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched [italics]it[/italics] out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
nt/mark/3.6: And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
nt/mark/3.7: But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judæa,
nt/mark/3.8: And from Jerusalem, and from Idumæa, and [italics]from[/italics] beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.
nt/mark/3.9: And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
nt/mark/3.10: For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
nt/mark/3.11: And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
nt/mark/3.12: And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.
nt/mark/3.13: And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth [italics]unto him[/italics] whom he would: and they came unto him.
nt/mark/3.14: And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,
nt/mark/3.15: And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:
nt/mark/3.17: And James the [italics]son[/italics] of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:
nt/mark/3.18: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the [italics]son[/italics] of Alphæus, and Thaddæus, and Simon the Canaanite,
nt/mark/3.19: And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.
nt/mark/3.20: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
nt/mark/3.21: And when his friends heard [italics]of it,[/italics] they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.
nt/mark/3.22: ¶ And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.
nt/mark/3.23: And he called them [italics]unto him,[/italics] and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
nt/mark/3.24: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
nt/mark/3.25: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
nt/mark/3.26: And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
nt/mark/3.27: No man can enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.
nt/mark/3.28: Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
nt/mark/3.29: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:
nt/mark/3.30: Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
nt/mark/3.31: ¶ There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.
nt/mark/3.32: And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
nt/mark/3.33: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
nt/mark/3.34: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
nt/mark/3.35: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.

nt/mark/4.1: And he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.
nt/mark/4.2: And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,
nt/mark/4.3: Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:
nt/mark/4.4: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
nt/mark/4.5: And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:
nt/mark/4.6: But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
nt/mark/4.7: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
nt/mark/4.8: And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.
nt/mark/4.9: And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
nt/mark/4.10: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.
nt/mark/4.11: And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all [italics]these[/italics] things are done in parables:
nt/mark/4.12: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and [italics]their[/italics] sins should be forgiven them.
nt/mark/4.13: And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?
nt/mark/4.15: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.
nt/mark/4.16: And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;
nt/mark/4.17: And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word’s sake, immediately they are offended.
nt/mark/4.18: And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,
nt/mark/4.19: And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.
nt/mark/4.20: And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive [italics]it,[/italics] and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.
nt/mark/4.21: ¶ And he said unto them, Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?
nt/mark/4.22: For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.
nt/mark/4.23: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.
nt/mark/4.24: And he said unto them, Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you: and unto you that hear shall more be given.
nt/mark/4.25: For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath.
nt/mark/4.26: ¶ And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground;
nt/mark/4.27: And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how.
nt/mark/4.28: For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.
nt/mark/4.29: But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the harvest is come.
nt/mark/4.30: ¶ And he said, Whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?
nt/mark/4.31: [italics]It is[/italics] like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:
nt/mark/4.32: But when it is sown, it groweth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and shooteth out great branches; so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it.
nt/mark/4.33: And with many such parables spake he the word unto them, as they were able to hear [italics]it[/italics][italics].[/italics]
nt/mark/4.34: But without a parable spake he not unto them: and when they were alone, he expounded all things to his disciples.
nt/mark/4.35: And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, Let us pass over unto the other side.
nt/mark/4.36: And when they had sent away the multitude, they took him even as he was in the ship. And there were also with him other little ships.
nt/mark/4.37: And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the ship, so that it was now full.
nt/mark/4.38: And he was in the hinder part of the ship, asleep on a pillow: and they awake him, and say unto him, Master, carest thou not that we perish?
nt/mark/4.39: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
nt/mark/4.40: And he said unto them, Why are ye so fearful? how is it that ye have no faith?
nt/mark/4.41: And they feared exceedingly, and said one to another, What manner of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?

nt/mark/8.1: In those days the multitude being very great, and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples [italics]unto him,[/italics] and saith unto them,
nt/mark/8.2: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:
nt/mark/8.3: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.
nt/mark/8.4: And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these [italics]men[/italics] with bread here in the wilderness?
nt/mark/8.5: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.
nt/mark/8.6: And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before [italics]them;[/italics] and they did set [italics]them[/italics] before the people.
nt/mark/8.7: And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commanded to set them also before [italics]them.[/italics]
nt/mark/8.8: So they did eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken [italics]meat[/italics] that was left seven baskets.
nt/mark/8.9: And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he sent them away.
nt/mark/8.10: ¶ And straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.
nt/mark/8.11: And the Pharisees came forth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a sign from heaven, tempting him.
nt/mark/8.12: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.
nt/mark/8.13: And he left them, and entering into the ship again departed to the other side.
nt/mark/8.14: ¶ Now [italics]the disciples[/italics] had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf.
nt/mark/8.15: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and [italics]of[/italics] the leaven of Herod.
nt/mark/8.16: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, [italics]It is[/italics] because we have no bread.
nt/mark/8.17: And when Jesus knew [italics]it,[/italics] he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?
nt/mark/8.18: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
nt/mark/8.19: When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.
nt/mark/8.20: And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.
nt/mark/8.21: And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?
nt/mark/8.22: ¶ And he cometh to Bethsaida; and they bring a blind man unto him, and besought him to touch him.
nt/mark/8.23: And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon him, he asked him if he saw ought.
nt/mark/8.24: And he looked up, and said, I see men as trees, walking.
nt/mark/8.25: After that he put [italics]his[/italics] hands again upon his eyes, and made him look up: and he was restored, and saw every man clearly.
nt/mark/8.26: And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell [italics]it[/italics] to any in the town.
nt/mark/8.27: ¶ And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Cæsarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?
nt/mark/8.28: And they answered, John the Baptist: but some [italics]say,[/italics] Elias; and others, One of the prophets.
nt/mark/8.29: And he saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am? And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.
nt/mark/8.30: And he charged them that they should tell no man of him.
nt/mark/8.31: And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders, and [italics]of[/italics] the chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
nt/mark/8.32: And he spake that saying openly. And Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.
nt/mark/8.33: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.
nt/mark/8.34: ¶ And when he had called the people [italics]unto him[/italics] with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
nt/mark/8.35: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel’s, the same shall save it.
nt/mark/8.36: For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
nt/mark/8.37: Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
nt/mark/8.38: Whosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.

nt/mark/9.1: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That there be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power.
nt/mark/9.2: ¶ And after six days Jesus taketh [italics]with him[/italics] Peter, and James, and John, and leadeth them up into an high mountain apart by themselves: and he was transfigured before them.
nt/mark/9.3: And his raiment became shining, exceeding white as snow; so as no fuller on earth can white them.
nt/mark/9.4: And there appeared unto them Elias with Moses: and they were talking with Jesus.
nt/mark/9.5: And Peter answered and said to Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here: and let us make three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
nt/mark/9.6: For he wist not what to say; for they were sore afraid.
nt/mark/9.7: And there was a cloud that overshadowed them: and a voice came out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son: hear him.
nt/mark/9.8: And suddenly, when they had looked round about, they saw no man any more, save Jesus only with themselves.
nt/mark/9.9: And as they came down from the mountain, he charged them that they should tell no man what things they had seen, till the Son of man were risen from the dead.
nt/mark/9.10: And they kept that saying with themselves, questioning one with another what the rising from the dead should mean.
nt/mark/9.11: ¶ And they asked him, saying, Why say the scribes that Elias must first come?
nt/mark/9.12: And he answered and told them, Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.
nt/mark/9.13: But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.
nt/mark/9.14: ¶ And when he came to [italics]his[/italics] disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the scribes questioning with them.
nt/mark/9.15: And straightway all the people, when they beheld him, were greatly amazed, and running to [italics]him[/italics] saluted him.
nt/mark/9.16: And he asked the scribes, What question ye with them?
nt/mark/9.17: And one of the multitude answered and said, Master, I have brought unto thee my son, which hath a dumb spirit;
nt/mark/9.18: And wheresoever he taketh him, he teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.
nt/mark/9.19: He answereth him, and saith, O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.
nt/mark/9.20: And they brought him unto him: and when he saw him, straightway the spirit tare him; and he fell on the ground, and wallowed foaming.
nt/mark/9.21: And he asked his father, How long is it ago since this came unto him? And he said, Of a child.
nt/mark/9.22: And ofttimes it hath cast him into the fire, and into the waters, to destroy him: but if thou canst do any thing, have compassion on us, and help us.
nt/mark/9.23: Jesus said unto him, If thou canst believe, all things [italics]are[/italics] possible to him that believeth.
nt/mark/9.24: And straightway the father of the child cried out, and said with tears, Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.
nt/mark/9.25: When Jesus saw that the people came running together, he rebuked the foul spirit, saying unto him, [italics]Thou[/italics] dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.
nt/mark/9.26: And [italics]the spirit[/italics] cried, and rent him sore, and came out of him: and he was as one dead; insomuch that many said, He is dead.
nt/mark/9.27: But Jesus took him by the hand, and lifted him up; and he arose.
nt/mark/9.28: And when he was come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, Why could not we cast him out?
nt/mark/9.29: And he said unto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.
nt/mark/9.30: ¶ And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/mark/9.31: For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, The Son of man is delivered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and after that he is killed, he shall rise the third day.
nt/mark/9.32: But they understood not that saying, and were afraid to ask him.
nt/mark/9.33: ¶ And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?
nt/mark/9.34: But they held their peace: for by the way they had disputed among themselves, who [italics]should be[/italics] the greatest.
nt/mark/9.35: And he sat down, and called the twelve, and saith unto them, If any man desire to be first, [italics]the same[/italics] shall be last of all, and servant of all.
nt/mark/9.36: And he took a child, and set him in the midst of them: and when he had taken him in his arms, he said unto them,
nt/mark/9.37: Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.
nt/mark/9.38: ¶ And John answered him, saying, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name, and he followeth not us: and we forbad him, because he followeth not us.
nt/mark/9.39: But Jesus said, Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.
nt/mark/9.40: For he that is not against us is on our part.
nt/mark/9.41: For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.
nt/mark/9.42: And whosoever shall offend one of [italics]these[/italics] little ones that believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.
nt/mark/9.43: And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
nt/mark/9.44: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
nt/mark/9.45: And if thy foot offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
nt/mark/9.46: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
nt/mark/9.47: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire:
nt/mark/9.48: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
nt/mark/9.49: For every one shall be salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.
nt/mark/9.50: Salt [italics]is[/italics] good: but if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have peace one with another.

nt/matt/1.1: The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
nt/matt/1.2: Abraham begat Isaac; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat Judas and his brethren;
nt/matt/1.3: And Judas begat Phares and Zara of Thamar; and Phares begat Esrom; and Esrom begat Aram;
nt/matt/1.4: And Aram begat Aminadab; and Aminadab begat Naasson; and Naasson begat Salmon;
nt/matt/1.5: And Salmon begat Booz of Rachab; and Booz begat Obed of Ruth; and Obed begat Jesse;
nt/matt/1.6: And Jesse begat David the king; and David the king begat Solomon of her [italics]that had been the wife[/italics] of Urias;
nt/matt/1.7: And Solomon begat Roboam; and Roboam begat Abia; and Abia begat Asa;
nt/matt/1.8: And Asa begat Josaphat; and Josaphat begat Joram; and Joram begat Ozias;
nt/matt/1.9: And Ozias begat Joatham; and Joatham begat Achaz; and Achaz begat Ezekias;
nt/matt/1.10: And Ezekias begat Manasses; and Manasses begat Amon; and Amon begat Josias;
nt/matt/1.11: And Josias begat Jechonias and his brethren, about the time they were carried away to Babylon:
nt/matt/1.12: And after they were brought to Babylon, Jechonias begat Salathiel; and Salathiel begat Zorobabel;
nt/matt/1.13: And Zorobabel begat Abiud; and Abiud begat Eliakim; and Eliakim begat Azor;
nt/matt/1.14: And Azor begat Sadoc; and Sadoc begat Achim; and Achim begat Eliud;
nt/matt/1.15: And Eliud begat Eleazar; and Eleazar begat Matthan; and Matthan begat Jacob;
nt/matt/1.16: And Jacob begat Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.
nt/matt/1.17: So all the generations from Abraham to David [italics]are[/italics] fourteen generations; and from David until the carrying away into Babylon [italics]are[/italics] fourteen generations; and from the carrying away into Babylon unto Christ [italics]are[/italics] fourteen generations.
nt/matt/1.18: ¶ Now the birth of Jesus Christ was on this wise: When as his mother Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child of the Holy Ghost.
nt/matt/1.19: Then Joseph her husband, being a just [italics]man,[/italics] and not willing to make her a publick example, was minded to put her away privily.
nt/matt/1.20: But while he thought on these things, behold, the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in a dream, saying, Joseph, thou son of David, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife: for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
nt/matt/1.21: And she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name Jesus: for he shall save his people from their sins.
nt/matt/1.22: Now all this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the prophet, saying,
nt/matt/1.23: Behold, a virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which being interpreted is, God with us.
nt/matt/1.24: Then Joseph being raised from sleep did as the angel of the Lord had bidden him, and took unto him his wife:
nt/matt/1.25: And knew her not till she had brought forth her firstborn son: and he called his name Jesus.

nt/matt/11.1: And it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.
nt/matt/11.2: Now when John had heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples,
nt/matt/11.3: And said unto him, Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?
nt/matt/11.4: Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:
nt/matt/11.5: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
nt/matt/11.6: And blessed is [italics]he,[/italics] whosoever shall not be offended in me.
nt/matt/11.7: ¶ And as they departed, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
nt/matt/11.8: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? behold, they that wear soft [italics]clothing[/italics] are in kings’ houses.
nt/matt/11.9: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? yea, I say unto you, and more than a prophet.
nt/matt/11.10: For this is [italics]he,[/italics] of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
nt/matt/11.11: Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
nt/matt/11.12: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.
nt/matt/11.13: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
nt/matt/11.14: And if ye will receive [italics]it,[/italics] this is Elias, which was for to come.
nt/matt/11.15: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
nt/matt/11.16: ¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,
nt/matt/11.17: And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented.
nt/matt/11.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil.
nt/matt/11.19: The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.
nt/matt/11.20: ¶ Then began he to upbraid the cities wherein most of his mighty works were done, because they repented not:
nt/matt/11.21: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
nt/matt/11.22: But I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of judgment, than for you.
nt/matt/11.23: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to hell: for if the mighty works, which have been done in thee, had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
nt/matt/11.24: But I say unto you, That it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.
nt/matt/11.25: ¶ At that time Jesus answered and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.
nt/matt/11.26: Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.
nt/matt/11.27: All things are delivered unto me of my Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, and [italics]he[/italics] to whomsoever the Son will reveal [italics]him.[/italics]
nt/matt/11.28: ¶ Come unto me, all [italics]ye[/italics] that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
nt/matt/11.29: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.
nt/matt/11.30: For my yoke [italics]is[/italics] easy, and my burden is light.

nt/matt/12.1: At that time Jesus went on the sabbath day through the corn; and his disciples were an hungred, and began to pluck the ears of corn, and to eat.
nt/matt/12.2: But when the Pharisees saw [italics]it,[/italics] they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.
nt/matt/12.3: But he said unto them, Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him;
nt/matt/12.4: How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests?
nt/matt/12.5: Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?
nt/matt/12.6: But I say unto you, That in this place is [italics]one[/italics] greater than the temple.
nt/matt/12.7: But if ye had known what [italics]this[/italics] meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.
nt/matt/12.8: For the Son of man is Lord even of the sabbath day.
nt/matt/12.9: And when he was departed thence, he went into their synagogue:
nt/matt/12.10: ¶ And, behold, there was a man which had [italics]his[/italics] hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.
nt/matt/12.11: And he said unto them, What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift [italics]it[/italics] out?
nt/matt/12.12: How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.
nt/matt/12.13: Then saith he to the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched [italics]it[/italics] forth; and it was restored whole, like as the other.
nt/matt/12.14: ¶ Then the Pharisees went out, and held a council against him, how they might destroy him.
nt/matt/12.15: But when Jesus knew [italics]it,[/italics] he withdrew himself from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all;
nt/matt/12.16: And charged them that they should not make him known:
nt/matt/12.17: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,
nt/matt/12.18: Behold my servant, whom I have chosen; my beloved, in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my spirit upon him, and he shall shew judgment to the Gentiles.
nt/matt/12.19: He shall not strive, nor cry; neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.
nt/matt/12.20: A bruised reed shall he not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench, till he send forth judgment unto victory.
nt/matt/12.21: And in his name shall the Gentiles trust.
nt/matt/12.22: ¶ Then was brought unto him one possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb: and he healed him, insomuch that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.
nt/matt/12.23: And all the people were amazed, and said, Is not this the son of David?
nt/matt/12.24: But when the Pharisees heard [italics]it,[/italics] they said, This [italics]fellow[/italics] doth not cast out devils, but by Beelzebub the prince of the devils.
nt/matt/12.25: And Jesus knew their thoughts, and said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand:
nt/matt/12.26: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?
nt/matt/12.27: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast [italics]them[/italics] out? therefore they shall be your judges.
nt/matt/12.28: But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto you.
nt/matt/12.29: Or else how can one enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.
nt/matt/12.30: He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.
nt/matt/12.31: ¶ Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men: but the blasphemy [italics]against[/italics] the [italics]Holy[/italics] Ghost shall not be forgiven unto men.
nt/matt/12.32: And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the [italics]world[/italics] to come.
nt/matt/12.33: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by [italics]his[/italics] fruit.
nt/matt/12.34: O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.
nt/matt/12.35: A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.
nt/matt/12.36: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
nt/matt/12.37: For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
nt/matt/12.38: ¶ Then certain of the scribes and of the Pharisees answered, saying, Master, we would see a sign from thee.
nt/matt/12.39: But he answered and said unto them, An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:
nt/matt/12.40: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale’s belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
nt/matt/12.41: The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas [italics]is[/italics] here.
nt/matt/12.42: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon [italics]is[/italics] here.
nt/matt/12.43: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.
nt/matt/12.44: Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth [italics]it[/italics] empty, swept, and garnished.
nt/matt/12.45: Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last [italics]state[/italics] of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.
nt/matt/12.46: ¶ While he yet talked to the people, behold, [italics]his[/italics] mother and his brethren stood without, desiring to speak with him.
nt/matt/12.47: Then one said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to speak with thee.
nt/matt/12.48: But he answered and said unto him that told him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?
nt/matt/12.49: And he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
nt/matt/12.50: For whosoever shall do the will of my Father which is in heaven, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.

nt/matt/13.1: The same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.
nt/matt/13.2: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
nt/matt/13.3: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;
nt/matt/13.4: And when he sowed, some [italics]seeds[/italics] fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:
nt/matt/13.5: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:
nt/matt/13.6: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.
nt/matt/13.7: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:
nt/matt/13.8: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.
nt/matt/13.9: Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
nt/matt/13.10: And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?
nt/matt/13.11: He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.
nt/matt/13.12: For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.
nt/matt/13.13: Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.
nt/matt/13.14: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:
nt/matt/13.15: For this people’s heart is waxed gross, and [italics]their[/italics] ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with [italics]their[/italics] eyes, and hear with [italics]their[/italics] ears, and should understand with [italics]their[/italics] heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
nt/matt/13.16: But blessed [italics]are[/italics] your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.
nt/matt/13.17: For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous [italics]men[/italics] have desired to see [italics]those things[/italics] which ye see, and have not seen [italics]them;[/italics] and to hear [italics]those things[/italics] which ye hear, and have not heard [italics]them.[/italics]
nt/matt/13.18: ¶ Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.
nt/matt/13.19: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth [italics]it[/italics] not, then cometh the wicked [italics]one,[/italics] and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.
nt/matt/13.20: But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;
nt/matt/13.21: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.
nt/matt/13.22: He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.
nt/matt/13.23: But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth [italics]it;[/italics] which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
nt/matt/13.24: ¶ Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:
nt/matt/13.25: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.
nt/matt/13.26: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.
nt/matt/13.27: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?
nt/matt/13.28: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?
nt/matt/13.29: But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.
nt/matt/13.30: Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.
nt/matt/13.31: ¶ Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field:
nt/matt/13.32: Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.
nt/matt/13.33: ¶ Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.
nt/matt/13.34: All these things spake Jesus unto the multitude in parables; and without a parable spake he not unto them:
nt/matt/13.35: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things which have been kept secret from the foundation of the world.
nt/matt/13.36: Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the tares of the field.
nt/matt/13.37: He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;
nt/matt/13.38: The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked [italics]one;[/italics]
nt/matt/13.39: The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.
nt/matt/13.40: As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.
nt/matt/13.41: The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity;
nt/matt/13.42: And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
nt/matt/13.43: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
nt/matt/13.44: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.
nt/matt/13.45: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls:
nt/matt/13.46: Who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.
nt/matt/13.47: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind:
nt/matt/13.48: Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.
nt/matt/13.49: So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just,
nt/matt/13.50: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
nt/matt/13.51: Jesus saith unto them, Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.
nt/matt/13.52: Then said he unto them, Therefore every scribe [italics]which is[/italics] instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man [italics]that is[/italics] an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure [italics]things[/italics] new and old.
nt/matt/13.53: ¶ And it came to pass, [italics]that[/italics] when Jesus had finished these parables, he departed thence.
nt/matt/13.54: And when he was come into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, insomuch that they were astonished, and said, Whence hath this [italics]man[/italics] this wisdom, and [italics]these[/italics] mighty works?
nt/matt/13.55: Is not this the carpenter’s son? is not his mother called Mary? and his brethren, James, and Joses, and Simon, and Judas?
nt/matt/13.56: And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence then hath this [italics]man[/italics] all these things?
nt/matt/13.57: And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.
nt/matt/13.58: And he did not many mighty works there because of their unbelief.

nt/matt/16.1: The Pharisees also with the Sadducees came, and tempting desired him that he would shew them a sign from heaven.
nt/matt/16.2: He answered and said unto them, When it is evening, ye say, [italics]It will be[/italics] fair weather: for the sky is red.
nt/matt/16.3: And in the morning, [italics]It will be[/italics] foul weather to day: for the sky is red and lowring. O [italics]ye[/italics] hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not [italics]discern[/italics] the signs of the times?
nt/matt/16.4: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas. And he left them, and departed.
nt/matt/16.5: And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.
nt/matt/16.6: ¶ Then Jesus said unto them, Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
nt/matt/16.7: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, [italics]It is[/italics] because we have taken no bread.
nt/matt/16.8: [italics]Which[/italics] when Jesus perceived, he said unto them, O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?
nt/matt/16.9: Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
nt/matt/16.10: Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
nt/matt/16.11: How is it that ye do not understand that I spake [italics]it[/italics] not to you concerning bread, that ye should beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees?
nt/matt/16.12: Then understood they how that he bade [italics]them[/italics] not beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
nt/matt/16.13: ¶ When Jesus came into the coasts of Cæsarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
nt/matt/16.14: And they said, Some [italics]say that thou art[/italics] John the Baptist: some, Elias; and others, Jeremias, or one of the prophets.
nt/matt/16.15: He saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am?
nt/matt/16.16: And Simon Peter answered and said, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.
nt/matt/16.17: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed [italics]it[/italics] unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.
nt/matt/16.18: And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it.
nt/matt/16.19: And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.
nt/matt/16.20: Then charged he his disciples that they should tell no man that he was Jesus the Christ.
nt/matt/16.21: ¶ From that time forth began Jesus to shew unto his disciples, how that he must go unto Jerusalem, and suffer many things of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised again the third day.
nt/matt/16.22: Then Peter took him, and began to rebuke him, saying, Be it far from thee, Lord: this shall not be unto thee.
nt/matt/16.23: But he turned, and said unto Peter, Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.
nt/matt/16.24: ¶ Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any [italics]man[/italics] will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
nt/matt/16.25: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.
nt/matt/16.26: For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
nt/matt/16.27: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels; and then he shall reward every man according to his works.
nt/matt/16.28: Verily I say unto you, There be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.

nt/matt/17.1: And after six days Jesus taketh Peter, James, and John his brother, and bringeth them up into an high mountain apart,
