nt/matt/9.3: And, behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves, This [italics]man[/italics] blasphemeth.
nt/matt/9.4: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?
nt/matt/9.5: For whether is easier, to say, [italics]Thy[/italics] sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?
nt/matt/9.6: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.
nt/matt/9.7: And he arose, and departed to his house.
nt/matt/9.8: But when the multitudes saw [italics]it,[/italics] they marvelled, and glorified God, which had given such power unto men.
nt/matt/9.9: ¶ And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.
nt/matt/9.10: ¶ And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.
nt/matt/9.11: And when the Pharisees saw [italics]it,[/italics] they said unto his disciples, Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?
nt/matt/9.12: But when Jesus heard [italics]that,[/italics] he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.
nt/matt/9.13: But go ye and learn what [italics]that[/italics] meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
nt/matt/9.14: ¶ Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?
nt/matt/9.15: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.
nt/matt/9.16: No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.
nt/matt/9.17: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.
nt/matt/9.18: ¶ While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain ruler, and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.
nt/matt/9.19: And Jesus arose, and followed him, and [italics]so did[/italics] his disciples.
nt/matt/9.20: ¶ And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind [italics]him,[/italics] and touched the hem of his garment:
nt/matt/9.21: For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.
nt/matt/9.22: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.
nt/matt/9.23: And when Jesus came into the ruler’s house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,
nt/matt/9.24: He said unto them, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.
nt/matt/9.25: But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.
nt/matt/9.26: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.
nt/matt/9.27: ¶ And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, [italics]Thou[/italics] Son of David, have mercy on us.
nt/matt/9.28: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
nt/matt/9.29: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.
nt/matt/9.30: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, See [italics]that[/italics] no man know [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/matt/9.31: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that country.
nt/matt/9.32: ¶ As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil.
nt/matt/9.33: And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.
nt/matt/9.34: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.
nt/matt/9.35: And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.
nt/matt/9.36: ¶ But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.
nt/matt/9.37: Then saith he unto his disciples, The harvest truly [italics]is[/italics] plenteous, but the labourers [italics]are[/italics] few;
nt/matt/9.38: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.

nt/philip/2.1: If [italics]there be[/italics] therefore any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any bowels and mercies,
nt/philip/2.2: Fulfil ye my joy, that ye be likeminded, having the same love, [italics]being[/italics] of one accord, of one mind.
nt/philip/2.3: [italics]Let[/italics] nothing [italics]be done[/italics] through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves.
nt/philip/2.4: Look not every man on his own things, but every man also on the things of others.
nt/philip/2.5: Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus:
nt/philip/2.6: Who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God:
nt/philip/2.7: But made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a servant, and was made in the likeness of men:
nt/philip/2.8: And being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himself, and became obedient unto death, even the death of the cross.
nt/philip/2.9: Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name:
nt/philip/2.10: That at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of [italics]things[/italics] in heaven, and [italics]things[/italics] in earth, and [italics]things[/italics] under the earth;
nt/philip/2.11: And [italics]that[/italics] every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ [italics]is[/italics] Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
nt/philip/2.12: Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
nt/philip/2.13: For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of [italics]his[/italics] good pleasure.
nt/philip/2.14: Do all things without murmurings and disputings:
nt/philip/2.15: That ye may be blameless and harmless, the sons of God, without rebuke, in the midst of a crooked and perverse nation, among whom ye shine as lights in the world;
nt/philip/2.16: Holding forth the word of life; that I may rejoice in the day of Christ, that I have not run in vain, neither laboured in vain.
nt/philip/2.17: Yea, and if I be offered upon the sacrifice and service of your faith, I joy, and rejoice with you all.
nt/philip/2.18: For the same cause also do ye joy, and rejoice with me.
nt/philip/2.19: But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus shortly unto you, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know your state.
nt/philip/2.20: For I have no man likeminded, who will naturally care for your state.
nt/philip/2.21: For all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.
nt/philip/2.22: But ye know the proof of him, that, as a son with the father, he hath served with me in the gospel.
nt/philip/2.23: Him therefore I hope to send presently, so soon as I shall see how it will go with me.
nt/philip/2.24: But I trust in the Lord that I also myself shall come shortly.
nt/philip/2.25: Yet I supposed it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, and companion in labour, and fellowsoldier, but your messenger, and he that ministered to my wants.
nt/philip/2.26: For he longed after you all, and was full of heaviness, because that ye had heard that he had been sick.
nt/philip/2.27: For indeed he was sick nigh unto death: but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.
nt/philip/2.28: I sent him therefore the more carefully, that, when ye see him again, ye may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.
nt/philip/2.29: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all gladness; and hold such in reputation:
nt/philip/2.30: Because for the work of Christ he was nigh unto death, not regarding his life, to supply your lack of service toward me.

nt/philip/4.1: Therefore, my brethren dearly beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, so stand fast in the Lord, [italics]my[/italics] dearly beloved.
nt/philip/4.2: I beseech Euodias, and beseech Syntyche, that they be of the same mind in the Lord.
nt/philip/4.3: And I entreat thee also, true yokefellow, help those women which laboured with me in the gospel, with Clement also, and [italics]with[/italics] other my fellowlabourers, whose names [italics]are[/italics] in the book of life.
nt/philip/4.4: Rejoice in the Lord alway: [italics]and[/italics] again I say, Rejoice.
nt/philip/4.5: Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord [italics]is[/italics] at hand.
nt/philip/4.6: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
nt/philip/4.7: And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
nt/philip/4.8: Finally, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things [italics]are[/italics] honest, whatsoever things [italics]are[/italics] just, whatsoever things [italics]are[/italics] pure, whatsoever things [italics]are[/italics] lovely, whatsoever things [italics]are[/italics] of good report; if [italics]there be[/italics] any virtue, and if [italics]there be[/italics] any praise, think on these things.
nt/philip/4.9: Those things, which ye have both learned, and received, and heard, and seen in me, do: and the God of peace shall be with you.
nt/philip/4.10: But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly, that now at the last your care of me hath flourished again; wherein ye were also careful, but ye lacked opportunity.
nt/philip/4.11: Not that I speak in respect of want: for I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, [italics]therewith[/italics] to be content.
nt/philip/4.12: I know both how to be abased, and I know how to abound: every where and in all things I am instructed both to be full and to be hungry, both to abound and to suffer need.
nt/philip/4.13: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
nt/philip/4.14: Notwithstanding ye have well done, that ye did communicate with my affliction.
nt/philip/4.15: Now ye Philippians know also, that in the beginning of the gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no church communicated with me as concerning giving and receiving, but ye only.
nt/philip/4.16: For even in Thessalonica ye sent once and again unto my necessity.
nt/philip/4.17: Not because I desire a gift: but I desire fruit that may abound to your account.
nt/philip/4.18: But I have all, and abound: I am full, having received of Epaphroditus the things [italics]which were sent[/italics] from you, an odour of a sweet smell, a sacrifice acceptable, wellpleasing to God.
nt/philip/4.19: But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus.
nt/philip/4.20: Now unto God and our Father [italics]be[/italics] glory for ever and ever. Amen.
nt/philip/4.21: Salute every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren which are with me greet you.
nt/philip/4.22: All the saints salute you, chiefly they that are of Cæsar’s household.
nt/philip/4.23: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [italics]be[/italics] with you all. Amen.

nt/rev/1.1: The Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave unto him, to shew unto his servants things which must shortly come to pass; and he sent and signified [italics]it[/italics] by his angel unto his servant John:
nt/rev/1.2: Who bare record of the word of God, and of the testimony of Jesus Christ, and of all things that he saw.
nt/rev/1.3: Blessed [italics]is[/italics] he that readeth, and they that hear the words of this prophecy, and keep those things which are written therein: for the time [italics]is[/italics] at hand.
nt/rev/1.4: John to the seven churches which are in Asia: Grace [italics]be[/italics] unto you, and peace, from him which is, and which was, and which is to come; and from the seven Spirits which are before his throne;
nt/rev/1.5: And from Jesus Christ, [italics]who is[/italics] the faithful witness, [italics]and[/italics] the first begotten of the dead, and the prince of the kings of the earth. Unto him that loved us, and washed us from our sins in his own blood,
nt/rev/1.6: And hath made us kings and priests unto God and his Father; to him [italics]be[/italics] glory and dominion for ever and ever. Amen.
nt/rev/1.7: Behold, he cometh with clouds; and every eye shall see him, and they [italics]also[/italics] which pierced him: and all kindreds of the earth shall wail because of him. Even so, Amen.
nt/rev/1.8: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the ending, saith the Lord, which is, and which was, and which is to come, the Almighty.
nt/rev/1.9: I John, who also am your brother, and companion in tribulation, and in the kingdom and patience of Jesus Christ, was in the isle that is called Patmos, for the word of God, and for the testimony of Jesus Christ.
nt/rev/1.10: I was in the Spirit on the Lord’s day, and heard behind me a great voice, as of a trumpet,
nt/rev/1.11: Saying, I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last: and, What thou seest, write in a book, and send [italics]it[/italics] unto the seven churches which are in Asia; unto Ephesus, and unto Smyrna, and unto Pergamos, and unto Thyatira, and unto Sardis, and unto Philadelphia, and unto Laodicea.
nt/rev/1.12: And I turned to see the voice that spake with me. And being turned, I saw seven golden candlesticks;
nt/rev/1.13: And in the midst of the seven candlesticks [italics]one[/italics] like unto the Son of man, clothed with a garment down to the foot, and girt about the paps with a golden girdle.
nt/rev/1.14: His head and [italics]his[/italics] hairs [italics]were[/italics] white like wool, as white as snow; and his eyes [italics]were[/italics] as a flame of fire;
nt/rev/1.15: And his feet like unto fine brass, as if they burned in a furnace; and his voice as the sound of many waters.
nt/rev/1.16: And he had in his right hand seven stars: and out of his mouth went a sharp twoedged sword: and his countenance [italics]was[/italics] as the sun shineth in his strength.
nt/rev/1.17: And when I saw him, I fell at his feet as dead. And he laid his right hand upon me, saying unto me, Fear not; I am the first and the last:
nt/rev/1.18: [italics]I am[/italics] he that liveth, and was dead; and, behold, I am alive for evermore, Amen; and have the keys of hell and of death.
nt/rev/1.19: Write the things which thou hast seen, and the things which are, and the things which shall be hereafter;
nt/rev/1.20: The mystery of the seven stars which thou sawest in my right hand, and the seven golden candlesticks. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches: and the seven candlesticks which thou sawest are the seven churches.

nt/rev/12.1: And there appeared a great wonder in heaven; a woman clothed with the sun, and the moon under her feet, and upon her head a crown of twelve stars:
nt/rev/12.2: And she being with child cried, travailing in birth, and pained to be delivered.
nt/rev/12.3: And there appeared another wonder in heaven; and behold a great red dragon, having seven heads and ten horns, and seven crowns upon his heads.
nt/rev/12.4: And his tail drew the third part of the stars of heaven, and did cast them to the earth: and the dragon stood before the woman which was ready to be delivered, for to devour her child as soon as it was born.
nt/rev/12.5: And she brought forth a man child, who was to rule all nations with a rod of iron: and her child was caught up unto God, and [italics]to[/italics] his throne.
nt/rev/12.6: And the woman fled into the wilderness, where she hath a place prepared of God, that they should feed her there a thousand two hundred [italics]and[/italics] threescore days.
nt/rev/12.7: And there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought against the dragon; and the dragon fought and his angels,
nt/rev/12.8: And prevailed not; neither was their place found any more in heaven.
nt/rev/12.9: And the great dragon was cast out, that old serpent, called the Devil, and Satan, which deceiveth the whole world: he was cast out into the earth, and his angels were cast out with him.
nt/rev/12.10: And I heard a loud voice saying in heaven, Now is come salvation, and strength, and the kingdom of our God, and the power of his Christ: for the accuser of our brethren is cast down, which accused them before our God day and night.
nt/rev/12.11: And they overcame him by the blood of the Lamb, and by the word of their testimony; and they loved not their lives unto the death.
nt/rev/12.12: Therefore rejoice, [italics]ye[/italics] heavens, and ye that dwell in them. Woe to the inhabiters of the earth and of the sea! for the devil is come down unto you, having great wrath, because he knoweth that he hath but a short time.
nt/rev/12.13: And when the dragon saw that he was cast unto the earth, he persecuted the woman which brought forth the man [italics]child.[/italics]
nt/rev/12.14: And to the woman were given two wings of a great eagle, that she might fly into the wilderness, into her place, where she is nourished for a time, and times, and half a time, from the face of the serpent.
nt/rev/12.15: And the serpent cast out of his mouth water as a flood after the woman, that he might cause her to be carried away of the flood.
nt/rev/12.16: And the earth helped the woman, and the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed up the flood which the dragon cast out of his mouth.
nt/rev/12.17: And the dragon was wroth with the woman, and went to make war with the remnant of her seed, which keep the commandments of God, and have the testimony of Jesus Christ.

nt/rev/13.1: And I stood upon the sand of the sea, and saw a beast rise up out of the sea, having seven heads and ten horns, and upon his horns ten crowns, and upon his heads the name of blasphemy.
nt/rev/13.2: And the beast which I saw was like unto a leopard, and his feet were as [italics]the feet[/italics] of a bear, and his mouth as the mouth of a lion: and the dragon gave him his power, and his seat, and great authority.
nt/rev/13.3: And I saw one of his heads as it were wounded to death; and his deadly wound was healed: and all the world wondered after the beast.
nt/rev/13.4: And they worshipped the dragon which gave power unto the beast: and they worshipped the beast, saying, Who [italics]is[/italics] like unto the beast? who is able to make war with him?
nt/rev/13.5: And there was given unto him a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies; and power was given unto him to continue forty [italics]and[/italics] two months.
nt/rev/13.6: And he opened his mouth in blasphemy against God, to blaspheme his name, and his tabernacle, and them that dwell in heaven.
nt/rev/13.7: And it was given unto him to make war with the saints, and to overcome them: and power was given him over all kindreds, and tongues, and nations.
nt/rev/13.8: And all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him, whose names are not written in the book of life of the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world.
nt/rev/13.9: If any man have an ear, let him hear.
nt/rev/13.10: He that leadeth into captivity shall go into captivity: he that killeth with the sword must be killed with the sword. Here is the patience and the faith of the saints.
nt/rev/13.11: And I beheld another beast coming up out of the earth; and he had two horns like a lamb, and he spake as a dragon.
nt/rev/13.12: And he exerciseth all the power of the first beast before him, and causeth the earth and them which dwell therein to worship the first beast, whose deadly wound was healed.
nt/rev/13.13: And he doeth great wonders, so that he maketh fire come down from heaven on the earth in the sight of men,
nt/rev/13.14: And deceiveth them that dwell on the earth by [italics]the means of[/italics] those miracles which he had power to do in the sight of the beast; saying to them that dwell on the earth, that they should make an image to the beast, which had the wound by a sword, and did live.
nt/rev/13.15: And he had power to give life unto the image of the beast, that the image of the beast should both speak, and cause that as many as would not worship the image of the beast should be killed.
nt/rev/13.16: And he causeth all, both small and great, rich and poor, free and bond, to receive a mark in their right hand, or in their foreheads:
nt/rev/13.17: And that no man might buy or sell, save he that had the mark, or the name of the beast, or the number of his name.
nt/rev/13.18: Here is wisdom. Let him that hath understanding count the number of the beast: for it is the number of a man; and his number [italics]is[/italics] Six hundred threescore [italics]and[/italics] six.

nt/rev/17.1: And there came one of the seven angels which had the seven vials, and talked with me, saying unto me, Come hither; I will shew unto thee the judgment of the great whore that sitteth upon many waters:
nt/rev/17.2: With whom the kings of the earth have committed fornication, and the inhabitants of the earth have been made drunk with the wine of her fornication.
nt/rev/17.3: So he carried me away in the spirit into the wilderness: and I saw a woman sit upon a scarlet coloured beast, full of names of blasphemy, having seven heads and ten horns.
nt/rev/17.4: And the woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet colour, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls, having a golden cup in her hand full of abominations and filthiness of her fornication:
nt/rev/17.5: And upon her forehead [italics]was[/italics] a name written, Mystery, Babylon the Great, the Mother of Harlots and Abominations of the Earth.
nt/rev/17.6: And I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus: and when I saw her, I wondered with great admiration.
nt/rev/17.7: And the angel said unto me, Wherefore didst thou marvel? I will tell thee the mystery of the woman, and of the beast that carrieth her, which hath the seven heads and ten horns.
nt/rev/17.8: The beast that thou sawest was, and is not; and shall ascend out of the bottomless pit, and go into perdition: and they that dwell on the earth shall wonder, whose names were not written in the book of life from the foundation of the world, when they behold the beast that was, and is not, and yet is.
nt/rev/17.9: And here [italics]is[/italics] the mind which hath wisdom. The seven heads are seven mountains, on which the woman sitteth.
nt/rev/17.10: And there are seven kings: five are fallen, and one is, [italics]and[/italics] the other is not yet come; and when he cometh, he must continue a short space.
nt/rev/17.11: And the beast that was, and is not, even he is the eighth, and is of the seven, and goeth into perdition.
nt/rev/17.12: And the ten horns which thou sawest are ten kings, which have received no kingdom as yet; but receive power as kings one hour with the beast.
nt/rev/17.13: These have one mind, and shall give their power and strength unto the beast.
nt/rev/17.14: These shall make war with the Lamb, and the Lamb shall overcome them: for he is Lord of lords, and King of kings: and they that are with him [italics]are[/italics] called, and chosen, and faithful.
nt/rev/17.15: And he saith unto me, The waters which thou sawest, where the whore sitteth, are peoples, and multitudes, and nations, and tongues.
nt/rev/17.16: And the ten horns which thou sawest upon the beast, these shall hate the whore, and shall make her desolate and naked, and shall eat her flesh, and burn her with fire.
nt/rev/17.17: For God hath put in their hearts to fulfil his will, and to agree, and give their kingdom unto the beast, until the words of God shall be fulfilled.
nt/rev/17.18: And the woman which thou sawest is that great city, which reigneth over the kings of the earth.

nt/rev/19.1: And after these things I heard a great voice of much people in heaven, saying, Alleluia; Salvation, and glory, and honour, and power, unto the Lord our God:
nt/rev/19.2: For true and righteous [italics]are[/italics] his judgments: for he hath judged the great whore, which did corrupt the earth with her fornication, and hath avenged the blood of his servants at her hand.
nt/rev/19.3: And again they said, Alleluia. And her smoke rose up for ever and ever.
nt/rev/19.4: And the four and twenty elders and the four beasts fell down and worshipped God that sat on the throne, saying, Amen; Alleluia.
nt/rev/19.5: And a voice came out of the throne, saying, Praise our God, all ye his servants, and ye that fear him, both small and great.
nt/rev/19.6: And I heard as it were the voice of a great multitude, and as the voice of many waters, and as the voice of mighty thunderings, saying, Alleluia: for the Lord God omnipotent reigneth.
nt/rev/19.7: Let us be glad and rejoice, and give honour to him: for the marriage of the Lamb is come, and his wife hath made herself ready.
nt/rev/19.8: And to her was granted that she should be arrayed in fine linen, clean and white: for the fine linen is the righteousness of saints.
nt/rev/19.9: And he saith unto me, Write, Blessed [italics]are[/italics] they which are called unto the marriage supper of the Lamb. And he saith unto me, These are the true sayings of God.
nt/rev/19.10: And I fell at his feet to worship him. And he said unto me, See [italics]thou do it[/italics] not: I am thy fellowservant, and of thy brethren that have the testimony of Jesus: worship God: for the testimony of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
nt/rev/19.11: And I saw heaven opened, and behold a white horse; and he that sat upon him [italics]was[/italics] called Faithful and True, and in righteousness he doth judge and make war.
nt/rev/19.12: His eyes [italics]were[/italics] as a flame of fire, and on his head [italics]were[/italics] many crowns; and he had a name written, that no man knew, but he himself.
nt/rev/19.13: And he [italics]was[/italics] clothed with a vesture dipped in blood: and his name is called The Word of God.
nt/rev/19.14: And the armies [italics]which were[/italics] in heaven followed him upon white horses, clothed in fine linen, white and clean.
nt/rev/19.15: And out of his mouth goeth a sharp sword, that with it he should smite the nations: and he shall rule them with a rod of iron: and he treadeth the winepress of the fierceness and wrath of Almighty God.
nt/rev/19.16: And he hath on [italics]his[/italics] vesture and on his thigh a name written, King of Kings, and Lord of Lords.
nt/rev/19.17: And I saw an angel standing in the sun; and he cried with a loud voice, saying to all the fowls that fly in the midst of heaven, Come and gather yourselves together unto the supper of the great God;
nt/rev/19.18: That ye may eat the flesh of kings, and the flesh of captains, and the flesh of mighty men, and the flesh of horses, and of them that sit on them, and the flesh of all [italics]men[/italics][italics], both[/italics] free and bond, both small and great.
nt/rev/19.19: And I saw the beast, and the kings of the earth, and their armies, gathered together to make war against him that sat on the horse, and against his army.
nt/rev/19.20: And the beast was taken, and with him the false prophet that wrought miracles before him, with which he deceived them that had received the mark of the beast, and them that worshipped his image. These both were cast alive into a lake of fire burning with brimstone.
nt/rev/19.21: And the remnant were slain with the sword of him that sat upon the horse, which [italics]sword[/italics] proceeded out of his mouth: and all the fowls were filled with their flesh.

nt/rev/20.1: And I saw an angel come down from heaven, having the key of the bottomless pit and a great chain in his hand.
nt/rev/20.2: And he laid hold on the dragon, that old serpent, which is the Devil, and Satan, and bound him a thousand years,
nt/rev/20.3: And cast him into the bottomless pit, and shut him up, and set a seal upon him, that he should deceive the nations no more, till the thousand years should be fulfilled: and after that he must be loosed a little season.
nt/rev/20.4: And I saw thrones, and they sat upon them, and judgment was given unto them: and [italics]I saw[/italics] the souls of them that were beheaded for the witness of Jesus, and for the word of God, and which had not worshipped the beast, neither his image, neither had received [italics]his[/italics] mark upon their foreheads, or in their hands; and they lived and reigned with Christ a thousand years.
nt/rev/20.5: But the rest of the dead lived not again until the thousand years were finished. This [italics]is[/italics] the first resurrection.
nt/rev/20.6: Blessed and holy [italics]is[/italics] he that hath part in the first resurrection: on such the second death hath no power, but they shall be priests of God and of Christ, and shall reign with him a thousand years.
nt/rev/20.7: And when the thousand years are expired, Satan shall be loosed out of his prison,
nt/rev/20.8: And shall go out to deceive the nations which are in the four quarters of the earth, Gog and Magog, to gather them together to battle: the number of whom [italics]is[/italics] as the sand of the sea.
nt/rev/20.9: And they went up on the breadth of the earth, and compassed the camp of the saints about, and the beloved city: and fire came down from God out of heaven, and devoured them.
nt/rev/20.10: And the devil that deceived them was cast into the lake of fire and brimstone, where the beast and the false prophet [italics]are,[/italics] and shall be tormented day and night for ever and ever.
nt/rev/20.11: And I saw a great white throne, and him that sat on it, from whose face the earth and the heaven fled away; and there was found no place for them.
nt/rev/20.12: And I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened: and another book was opened, which is [italics]the[/italics] [italics]book[/italics] of life: and the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works.
nt/rev/20.13: And the sea gave up the dead which were in it; and death and hell delivered up the dead which were in them: and they were judged every man according to their works.
nt/rev/20.14: And death and hell were cast into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
nt/rev/20.15: And whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire.

nt/rev/21.1: And I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away; and there was no more sea.
nt/rev/21.2: And I John saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down from God out of heaven, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
nt/rev/21.3: And I heard a great voice out of heaven saying, Behold, the tabernacle of God [italics]is[/italics] with men, and he will dwell with them, and they shall be his people, and God himself shall be with them, [italics]and be[/italics] their God.
nt/rev/21.4: And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away.
nt/rev/21.5: And he that sat upon the throne said, Behold, I make all things new. And he said unto me, Write: for these words are true and faithful.
nt/rev/21.6: And he said unto me, It is done. I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will give unto him that is athirst of the fountain of the water of life freely.
nt/rev/21.7: He that overcometh shall inherit all things; and I will be his God, and he shall be my son.
nt/rev/21.8: But the fearful, and unbelieving, and the abominable, and murderers, and whoremongers, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all liars, shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone: which is the second death.
nt/rev/21.9: And there came unto me one of the seven angels which had the seven vials full of the seven last plagues, and talked with me, saying, Come hither, I will shew thee the bride, the Lamb’s wife.
nt/rev/21.10: And he carried me away in the spirit to a great and high mountain, and shewed me that great city, the holy Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God,
nt/rev/21.11: Having the glory of God: and her light [italics]was[/italics] like unto a stone most precious, even like a jasper stone, clear as crystal;
nt/rev/21.12: And had a wall great and high, [italics]and[/italics] had twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and names written thereon, which are [italics]the names[/italics] of the twelve tribes of the children of Israel:
nt/rev/21.13: On the east three gates; on the north three gates; on the south three gates; and on the west three gates.
nt/rev/21.14: And the wall of the city had twelve foundations, and in them the names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
nt/rev/21.15: And he that talked with me had a golden reed to measure the city, and the gates thereof, and the wall thereof.
nt/rev/21.16: And the city lieth foursquare, and the length is as large as the breadth: and he measured the city with the reed, twelve thousand furlongs. The length and the breadth and the height of it are equal.
nt/rev/21.17: And he measured the wall thereof, an hundred [italics]and[/italics] forty [italics]and[/italics] four cubits, [italics]according to[/italics] the measure of a man, that is, of the angel.
nt/rev/21.18: And the building of the wall of it was [italics]of[/italics] jasper: and the city [italics]was[/italics] pure gold, like unto clear glass.
nt/rev/21.19: And the foundations of the wall of the city [italics]were[/italics] garnished with all manner of precious stones. The first foundation [italics]was[/italics] jasper; the second, sapphire; the third, a chalcedony; the fourth, an emerald;
nt/rev/21.20: The fifth, sardonyx; the sixth, sardius; the seventh, chrysolite; the eighth, beryl; the ninth, a topaz; the tenth, a chrysoprasus; the eleventh, a jacinth; the twelfth, an amethyst.
nt/rev/21.21: And the twelve gates [italics]were[/italics] twelve pearls; every several gate was of one pearl: and the street of the city [italics]was[/italics] pure gold, as it were transparent glass.
nt/rev/21.22: And I saw no temple therein: for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are the temple of it.
nt/rev/21.23: And the city had no need of the sun, neither of the moon, to shine in it: for the glory of God did lighten it, and the Lamb [italics]is[/italics] the light thereof.
nt/rev/21.24: And the nations of them which are saved shall walk in the light of it: and the kings of the earth do bring their glory and honour into it.
nt/rev/21.25: And the gates of it shall not be shut at all by day: for there shall be no night there.
nt/rev/21.26: And they shall bring the glory and honour of the nations into it.
nt/rev/21.27: And there shall in no wise enter into it any thing that defileth, neither [italics]whatsoever[/italics] worketh abomination, or [italics]maketh[/italics] a lie: but they which are written in the Lamb’s book of life.

nt/rev/22.1: And he shewed me a pure river of water of life, clear as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb.
nt/rev/22.2: In the midst of the street of it, and on either side of the river, [italics]was there[/italics] the tree of life, which bare twelve [italics]manner of[/italics] fruits, [italics]and[/italics] yielded her fruit every month: and the leaves of the tree [italics]were[/italics] for the healing of the nations.
nt/rev/22.3: And there shall be no more curse: but the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be in it; and his servants shall serve him:
nt/rev/22.4: And they shall see his face; and his name [italics]shall be[/italics] in their foreheads.
nt/rev/22.5: And there shall be no night there; and they need no candle, neither light of the sun; for the Lord God giveth them light: and they shall reign for ever and ever.
nt/rev/22.6: And he said unto me, These sayings [italics]are[/italics] faithful and true: and the Lord God of the holy prophets sent his angel to shew unto his servants the things which must shortly be done.
nt/rev/22.7: Behold, I come quickly: blessed [italics]is[/italics] he that keepeth the sayings of the prophecy of this book.
nt/rev/22.8: And I John saw these things, and heard [italics]them.[/italics] And when I had heard and seen, I fell down to worship before the feet of the angel which shewed me these things.
nt/rev/22.9: Then saith he unto me, See [italics]thou do it[/italics] not: for I am thy fellowservant, and of thy brethren the prophets, and of them which keep the sayings of this book: worship God.
nt/rev/22.10: And he saith unto me, Seal not the sayings of the prophecy of this book: for the time is at hand.
nt/rev/22.11: He that is unjust, let him be unjust still: and he which is filthy, let him be filthy still: and he that is righteous, let him be righteous still: and he that is holy, let him be holy still.
nt/rev/22.12: And, behold, I come quickly; and my reward [italics]is[/italics] with me, to give every man according as his work shall be.
nt/rev/22.13: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last.
nt/rev/22.14: Blessed [italics]are[/italics] they that do his commandments, that they may have right to the tree of life, and may enter in through the gates into the city.
nt/rev/22.15: For without [italics]are[/italics] dogs, and sorcerers, and whoremongers, and murderers, and idolaters, and whosoever loveth and maketh a lie.
nt/rev/22.16: I Jesus have sent mine angel to testify unto you these things in the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, [italics]and[/italics] the bright and morning star.
nt/rev/22.17: And the Spirit and the bride say, Come. And let him that heareth say, Come. And let him that is athirst come. And whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely.
nt/rev/22.18: For I testify unto every man that heareth the words of the prophecy of this book, If any man shall add unto these things, God shall add unto him the plagues that are written in this book:
nt/rev/22.19: And if any man shall take away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God shall take away his part out of the book of life, and out of the holy city, and [italics]from[/italics] the things which are written in this book.
nt/rev/22.20: He which testifieth these things saith, Surely I come quickly. Amen. Even so, come, Lord Jesus.
nt/rev/22.21: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [italics]be[/italics] with you all. Amen.

nt/rev/3.1: And unto the angel of the church in Sardis write; These things saith he that hath the seven Spirits of God, and the seven stars; I know thy works, that thou hast a name that thou livest, and art dead.
nt/rev/3.2: Be watchful, and strengthen the things which remain, that are ready to die: for I have not found thy works perfect before God.
nt/rev/3.3: Remember therefore how thou hast received and heard, and hold fast, and repent. If therefore thou shalt not watch, I will come on thee as a thief, and thou shalt not know what hour I will come upon thee.
nt/rev/3.4: Thou hast a few names even in Sardis which have not defiled their garments; and they shall walk with me in white: for they are worthy.
nt/rev/3.5: He that overcometh, the same shall be clothed in white raiment; and I will not blot out his name out of the book of life, but I will confess his name before my Father, and before his angels.
nt/rev/3.6: He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches.
nt/rev/3.7: And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write; These things saith he that is holy, he that is true, he that hath the key of David, he that openeth, and no man shutteth; and shutteth, and no man openeth;
nt/rev/3.8: I know thy works: behold, I have set before thee an open door, and no man can shut it: for thou hast a little strength, and hast kept my word, and hast not denied my name.
nt/rev/3.9: Behold, I will make them of the synagogue of Satan, which say they are Jews, and are not, but do lie; behold, I will make them to come and worship before thy feet, and to know that I have loved thee.
nt/rev/3.10: Because thou hast kept the word of my patience, I also will keep thee from the hour of temptation, which shall come upon all the world, to try them that dwell upon the earth.
nt/rev/3.11: Behold, I come quickly: hold that fast which thou hast, that no man take thy crown.
nt/rev/3.12: Him that overcometh will I make a pillar in the temple of my God, and he shall go no more out: and I will write upon him the name of my God, and the name of the city of my God, [italics]which is[/italics] new Jerusalem, which cometh down out of heaven from my God: and [italics]I will write upon him[/italics] my new name.
nt/rev/3.13: He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches.
nt/rev/3.14: And unto the angel of the church of the Laodiceans write; These things saith the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of the creation of God;
nt/rev/3.15: I know thy works, that thou art neither cold nor hot: I would thou wert cold or hot.
nt/rev/3.16: So then because thou art lukewarm, and neither cold nor hot, I will spue thee out of my mouth.
nt/rev/3.17: Because thou sayest, I am rich, and increased with goods, and have need of nothing; and knowest not that thou art wretched, and miserable, and poor, and blind, and naked:
nt/rev/3.18: I counsel thee to buy of me gold tried in the fire, that thou mayest be rich; and white raiment, that thou mayest be clothed, and [italics]that[/italics] the shame of thy nakedness do not appear; and anoint thine eyes with eyesalve, that thou mayest see.
nt/rev/3.19: As many as I love, I rebuke and chasten: be zealous therefore, and repent.
nt/rev/3.20: Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me.
nt/rev/3.21: To him that overcometh will I grant to sit with me in my throne, even as I also overcame, and am set down with my Father in his throne.
nt/rev/3.22: He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches.

nt/rev/5.1: And I saw in the right hand of him that sat on the throne a book written within and on the backside, sealed with seven seals.
nt/rev/5.2: And I saw a strong angel proclaiming with a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the book, and to loose the seals thereof?
nt/rev/5.3: And no man in heaven, nor in earth, neither under the earth, was able to open the book, neither to look thereon.
nt/rev/5.4: And I wept much, because no man was found worthy to open and to read the book, neither to look thereon.
nt/rev/5.5: And one of the elders saith unto me, Weep not: behold, the Lion of the tribe of Juda, the Root of David, hath prevailed to open the book, and to loose the seven seals thereof.
nt/rev/5.6: And I beheld, and, lo, in the midst of the throne and of the four beasts, and in the midst of the elders, stood a Lamb as it had been slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God sent forth into all the earth.
nt/rev/5.7: And he came and took the book out of the right hand of him that sat upon the throne.
nt/rev/5.8: And when he had taken the book, the four beasts and four [italics]and[/italics] twenty elders fell down before the Lamb, having every one of them harps, and golden vials full of odours, which are the prayers of saints.
nt/rev/5.9: And they sung a new song, saying, Thou art worthy to take the book, and to open the seals thereof: for thou wast slain, and hast redeemed us to God by thy blood out of every kindred, and tongue, and people, and nation;
nt/rev/5.10: And hast made us unto our God kings and priests: and we shall reign on the earth.
nt/rev/5.11: And I beheld, and I heard the voice of many angels round about the throne and the beasts and the elders: and the number of them was ten thousand times ten thousand, and thousands of thousands;
nt/rev/5.12: Saying with a loud voice, Worthy is the Lamb that was slain to receive power, and riches, and wisdom, and strength, and honour, and glory, and blessing.
nt/rev/5.13: And every creature which is in heaven, and on the earth, and under the earth, and such as are in the sea, and all that are in them, heard I saying, Blessing, and honour, and glory, and power, [italics]be[/italics] unto him that sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb for ever and ever.
nt/rev/5.14: And the four beasts said, Amen. And the four [italics]and[/italics] twenty elders fell down and worshipped him that liveth for ever and ever.

nt/rev/7.1: And after these things I saw four angels standing on the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree.
nt/rev/7.2: And I saw another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God: and he cried with a loud voice to the four angels, to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea,
nt/rev/7.3: Saying, Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads.
nt/rev/7.4: And I heard the number of them which were sealed: [italics]and there were[/italics] sealed an hundred [italics]and[/italics] forty [italics]and[/italics] four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel.
nt/rev/7.5: Of the tribe of Juda [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Reuben [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Gad [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand.
nt/rev/7.6: Of the tribe of Aser [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Nepthalim [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Manasses [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand.
nt/rev/7.7: Of the tribe of Simeon [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Levi [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Issachar [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand.
nt/rev/7.8: Of the tribe of Zabulon [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Joseph [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Benjamin [italics]were[/italics] sealed twelve thousand.
nt/rev/7.9: After this I beheld, and, lo, a great multitude, which no man could number, of all nations, and kindreds, and people, and tongues, stood before the throne, and before the Lamb, clothed with white robes, and palms in their hands;
nt/rev/7.10: And cried with a loud voice, saying, Salvation to our God which sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb.
nt/rev/7.11: And all the angels stood round about the throne, and [italics]about[/italics] the elders and the four beasts, and fell before the throne on their faces, and worshipped God,
nt/rev/7.12: Saying, Amen: Blessing, and glory, and wisdom, and thanksgiving, and honour, and power, and might, [italics]be[/italics] unto our God for ever and ever. Amen.
nt/rev/7.13: And one of the elders answered, saying unto me, What are these which are arrayed in white robes? and whence came they?
nt/rev/7.14: And I said unto him, Sir, thou knowest. And he said to me, These are they which came out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
nt/rev/7.15: Therefore are they before the throne of God, and serve him day and night in his temple: and he that sitteth on the throne shall dwell among them.
nt/rev/7.16: They shall hunger no more, neither thirst any more; neither shall the sun light on them, nor any heat.
nt/rev/7.17: For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them, and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters: and God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes.

nt/rom/1.1: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, called [italics]to be[/italics] an apostle, separated unto the gospel of God,
nt/rom/1.2: (Which he had promised afore by his prophets in the holy scriptures,)
nt/rom/1.3: Concerning his Son Jesus Christ our Lord, which was made of the seed of David according to the flesh;
nt/rom/1.4: And declared [italics]to be[/italics] the Son of God with power, according to the spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead:
nt/rom/1.5: By whom we have received grace and apostleship, for obedience to the faith among all nations, for his name:
nt/rom/1.6: Among whom are ye also the called of Jesus Christ:
nt/rom/1.7: To all that be in Rome, beloved of God, called [italics]to be[/italics] saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.
nt/rom/1.8: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for you all, that your faith is spoken of throughout the whole world.
nt/rom/1.9: For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the gospel of his Son, that without ceasing I make mention of you always in my prayers;
nt/rom/1.10: Making request, if by any means now at length I might have a prosperous journey by the will of God to come unto you.
nt/rom/1.11: For I long to see you, that I may impart unto you some spiritual gift, to the end ye may be established;
nt/rom/1.12: That is, that I may be comforted together with you by the mutual faith both of you and me.
nt/rom/1.13: Now I would not have you ignorant, brethren, that oftentimes I purposed to come unto you, (but was let hitherto,) that I might have some fruit among you also, even as among other Gentiles.
nt/rom/1.14: I am debtor both to the Greeks, and to the Barbarians; both to the wise, and to the unwise.
nt/rom/1.15: So, as much as in me is, I am ready to preach the gospel to you that are at Rome also.
nt/rom/1.16: For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation to every one that believeth; to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.
nt/rom/1.17: For therein is the righteousness of God revealed from faith to faith: as it is written, The just shall live by faith.
nt/rom/1.18: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who hold the truth in unrighteousness;
nt/rom/1.19: Because that which may be known of God is manifest in them; for God hath shewed [italics]it[/italics] unto them.
nt/rom/1.20: For the invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made, [italics]even[/italics] his eternal power and Godhead; so that they are without excuse:
nt/rom/1.21: Because that, when they knew God, they glorified [italics]him[/italics] not as God, neither were thankful; but became vain in their imaginations, and their foolish heart was darkened.
nt/rom/1.22: Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools,
nt/rom/1.23: And changed the glory of the uncorruptible God into an image made like to corruptible man, and to birds, and fourfooted beasts, and creeping things.
nt/rom/1.24: Wherefore God also gave them up to uncleanness through the lusts of their own hearts, to dishonour their own bodies between themselves:
nt/rom/1.25: Who changed the truth of God into a lie, and worshipped and served the creature more than the Creator, who is blessed for ever. Amen.
nt/rom/1.26: For this cause God gave them up unto vile affections: for even their women did change the natural use into that which is against nature:
nt/rom/1.27: And likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their lust one toward another; men with men working that which is unseemly, and receiving in themselves that recompence of their error which was meet.
nt/rom/1.28: And even as they did not like to retain God in [italics]their[/italics] knowledge, God gave them over to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not convenient;
nt/rom/1.29: Being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers,
nt/rom/1.30: Backbiters, haters of God, despiteful, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,
nt/rom/1.31: Without understanding, covenantbreakers, without natural affection, implacable, unmerciful:
nt/rom/1.32: Who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them.

nt/rom/11.1: I say then, Hath God cast away his people? God forbid. For I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, [italics]of[/italics] the tribe of Benjamin.
nt/rom/11.2: God hath not cast away his people which he foreknew. Wot ye not what the scripture saith of Elias? how he maketh intercession to God against Israel, saying,
nt/rom/11.3: Lord, they have killed thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am left alone, and they seek my life.
nt/rom/11.4: But what saith the answer of God unto him? I have reserved to myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee to [italics]the image of[/italics] Baal.
nt/rom/11.5: Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the election of grace.
nt/rom/11.6: And if by grace, then [italics]is it[/italics] no more of works: otherwise grace is no more grace. But if [italics]it be[/italics] of works, then is it no more grace: otherwise work is no more work.
nt/rom/11.7: What then? Israel hath not obtained that which he seeketh for; but the election hath obtained it, and the rest were blinded
nt/rom/11.8: (According as it is written, God hath given them the spirit of slumber, eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not hear;) unto this day.
nt/rom/11.9: And David saith, Let their table be made a snare, and a trap, and a stumblingblock, and a recompence unto them:
nt/rom/11.10: Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, and bow down their back alway.
nt/rom/11.11: I say then, Have they stumbled that they should fall? God forbid: but [italics]rather[/italics] through their fall salvation [italics]is come[/italics] unto the Gentiles, for to provoke them to jealousy.
nt/rom/11.12: Now if the fall of them [italics]be[/italics] the riches of the world, and the diminishing of them the riches of the Gentiles; how much more their fulness?
nt/rom/11.13: For I speak to you Gentiles, inasmuch as I am the apostle of the Gentiles, I magnify mine office:
nt/rom/11.14: If by any means I may provoke to emulation [italics]them which are[/italics] my flesh, and might save some of them.
nt/rom/11.15: For if the casting away of them [italics]be[/italics] the reconciling of the world, what [italics]shall[/italics] the receiving [italics]of them be,[/italics] but life from the dead?
nt/rom/11.16: For if the firstfruit [italics]be[/italics] holy, the lump [italics]is[/italics] also [italics]holy:[/italics] and if the root [italics]be[/italics] holy, so [italics]are[/italics] the branches.
nt/rom/11.17: And if some of the branches be broken off, and thou, being a wild olive tree, wert grafted in among them, and with them partakest of the root and fatness of the olive tree;
nt/rom/11.18: Boast not against the branches. But if thou boast, thou bearest not the root, but the root thee.
nt/rom/11.19: Thou wilt say then, The branches were broken off, that I might be grafted in.
nt/rom/11.20: Well; because of unbelief they were broken off, and thou standest by faith. Be not highminded, but fear:
nt/rom/11.21: For if God spared not the natural branches, [italics]take heed[/italics] lest he also spare not thee.
nt/rom/11.22: Behold therefore the goodness and severity of God: on them which fell, severity; but toward thee, goodness, if thou continue in [italics]his[/italics] goodness: otherwise thou also shalt be cut off.
nt/rom/11.23: And they also, if they abide not still in unbelief, shall be grafted in: for God is able to graft them in again.
nt/rom/11.24: For if thou wert cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature, and wert grafted contrary to nature into a good olive tree: how much more shall these, which be the natural [italics]branches,[/italics] be grafted into their own olive tree?
nt/rom/11.25: For I would not, brethren, that ye should be ignorant of this mystery, lest ye should be wise in your own conceits; that blindness in part is happened to Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in.
nt/rom/11.26: And so all Israel shall be saved: as it is written, There shall come out of Sion the Deliverer, and shall turn away ungodliness from Jacob:
nt/rom/11.27: For this [italics]is[/italics] my covenant unto them, when I shall take away their sins.
nt/rom/11.28: As concerning the gospel, [italics]they are[/italics] enemies for your sakes: but as touching the election, [italics]they are[/italics] beloved for the fathers’ sakes.
nt/rom/11.29: For the gifts and calling of God [italics]are[/italics] without repentance.
nt/rom/11.30: For as ye in times past have not believed God, yet have now obtained mercy through their unbelief:
nt/rom/11.31: Even so have these also now not believed, that through your mercy they also may obtain mercy.
nt/rom/11.32: For God hath concluded them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.
nt/rom/11.33: O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God! how unsearchable [italics]are[/italics] his judgments, and his ways past finding out!
nt/rom/11.34: For who hath known the mind of the Lord? or who hath been his counsellor?
nt/rom/11.35: Or who hath first given to him, and it shall be recompensed unto him again?
nt/rom/11.36: For of him, and through him, and to him, [italics]are[/italics] all things: to whom [italics]be[/italics] glory for ever. Amen.

nt/rom/14.1: Him that is weak in the faith receive ye, [italics]but[/italics] not to doubtful disputations.
nt/rom/14.2: For one believeth that he may eat all things: another, who is weak, eateth herbs.
nt/rom/14.3: Let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not; and let not him which eateth not judge him that eateth: for God hath received him.
nt/rom/14.4: Who art thou that judgest another man’s servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.
nt/rom/14.5: One man esteemeth one day above another: another esteemeth every day [italics]alike.[/italics] Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.
nt/rom/14.6: He that regardeth the day, regardeth [italics]it[/italics] unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard [italics]it.[/italics] He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.
nt/rom/14.7: For none of us liveth to himself, and no man dieth to himself.
nt/rom/14.8: For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord’s.
nt/rom/14.9: For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that he might be Lord both of the dead and living.
nt/rom/14.10: But why dost thou judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the judgment seat of Christ.
nt/rom/14.11: For it is written, [italics]As[/italics] I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.
nt/rom/14.12: So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.
nt/rom/14.13: Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a stumblingblock or an occasion to fall in [italics]his[/italics] brother’s way.
nt/rom/14.14: I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that [italics]there is[/italics] nothing unclean of itself: but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him [italics]it is[/italics] unclean.
nt/rom/14.15: But if thy brother be grieved with [italics]thy[/italics] meat, now walkest thou not charitably. Destroy not him with thy meat, for whom Christ died.
nt/rom/14.16: Let not then your good be evil spoken of:
nt/rom/14.17: For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.
nt/rom/14.18: For he that in these things serveth Christ [italics]is[/italics] acceptable to God, and approved of men.
nt/rom/14.19: Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another.
nt/rom/14.20: For meat destroy not the work of God. All things indeed [italics]are[/italics] pure; but [italics]it is[/italics] evil for that man who eateth with offence.
nt/rom/14.21: [italics]It is[/italics] good neither to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor [italics]any thing[/italics] whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is offended, or is made weak.
nt/rom/14.22: Hast thou faith? have [italics]it[/italics] to thyself before God. Happy [italics]is[/italics] he that condemneth not himself in that thing which he alloweth.
nt/rom/14.23: And he that doubteth is damned if he eat, because [italics]he eateth[/italics] not of faith: for whatsoever [italics]is[/italics] not of faith is sin.

nt/rom/5.1: Therefore being justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ:
nt/rom/5.2: By whom also we have access by faith into this grace wherein we stand, and rejoice in hope of the glory of God.
nt/rom/5.3: And not only [italics]so,[/italics] but we glory in tribulations also: knowing that tribulation worketh patience;
nt/rom/5.4: And patience, experience; and experience, hope:
nt/rom/5.5: And hope maketh not ashamed; because the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost which is given unto us.
nt/rom/5.6: For when we were yet without strength, in due time Christ died for the ungodly.
nt/rom/5.7: For scarcely for a righteous man will one die: yet peradventure for a good man some would even dare to die.
nt/rom/5.8: But God commendeth his love toward us, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
nt/rom/5.9: Much more then, being now justified by his blood, we shall be saved from wrath through him.
nt/rom/5.10: For if, when we were enemies, we were reconciled to God by the death of his Son, much more, being reconciled, we shall be saved by his life.
nt/rom/5.11: And not only [italics]so,[/italics] but we also joy in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom we have now received the atonement.
nt/rom/5.12: Wherefore, as by one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin; and so death passed upon all men, for that all have sinned:
nt/rom/5.13: (For until the law sin was in the world: but sin is not imputed when there is no law.
nt/rom/5.14: Nevertheless death reigned from Adam to Moses, even over them that had not sinned after the similitude of Adam’s transgression, who is the figure of him that was to come.
nt/rom/5.15: But not as the offence, so also [italics]is[/italics] the free gift. For if through the offence of one many be dead, much more the grace of God, and the gift by grace, [italics]which is[/italics] by one man, Jesus Christ, hath abounded unto many.
nt/rom/5.16: And not as [italics]it was[/italics] by one that sinned, [italics]so is[/italics] the gift: for the judgment [italics]was[/italics] by one to condemnation, but the free gift [italics]is[/italics] of many offences unto justification.
nt/rom/5.17: For if by one man’s offence death reigned by one; much more they which receive abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness shall reign in life by one, Jesus Christ.)
nt/rom/5.18: Therefore as by the offence of one [italics]judgment came[/italics] upon all men to condemnation; even so by the righteousness of one [italics]the free gift came[/italics] upon all men unto justification of life.
nt/rom/5.19: For as by one man’s disobedience many were made sinners, so by the obedience of one shall many be made righteous.
nt/rom/5.20: Moreover the law entered, that the offence might abound. But where sin abounded, grace did much more abound:
nt/rom/5.21: That as sin hath reigned unto death, even so might grace reign through righteousness unto eternal life by Jesus Christ our Lord.

nt/rom/6.1: What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound?
nt/rom/6.2: God forbid. How shall we, that are dead to sin, live any longer therein?
nt/rom/6.3: Know ye not, that so many of us as were baptized into Jesus Christ were baptized into his death?
nt/rom/6.4: Therefore we are buried with him by baptism into death: that like as Christ was raised up from the dead by the glory of the Father, even so we also should walk in newness of life.
nt/rom/6.5: For if we have been planted together in the likeness of his death, we shall be also [italics]in the likeness[/italics] of [italics]his[/italics] resurrection:
nt/rom/6.6: Knowing this, that our old man is crucified with [italics]him,[/italics] that the body of sin might be destroyed, that henceforth we should not serve sin.
nt/rom/6.7: For he that is dead is freed from sin.
nt/rom/6.8: Now if we be dead with Christ, we believe that we shall also live with him:
nt/rom/6.9: Knowing that Christ being raised from the dead dieth no more; death hath no more dominion over him.
nt/rom/6.10: For in that he died, he died unto sin once: but in that he liveth, he liveth unto God.
nt/rom/6.11: Likewise reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but alive unto God through Jesus Christ our Lord.
nt/rom/6.12: Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof.
nt/rom/6.13: Neither yield ye your members [italics]as[/italics] instruments of unrighteousness unto sin: but yield yourselves unto God, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members [italics]as[/italics] instruments of righteousness unto God.
nt/rom/6.14: For sin shall not have dominion over you: for ye are not under the law, but under grace.
nt/rom/6.15: What then? shall we sin, because we are not under the law, but under grace? God forbid.
nt/rom/6.16: Know ye not, that to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants ye are to whom ye obey; whether of sin unto death, or of obedience unto righteousness?
nt/rom/6.17: But God be thanked, that ye were the servants of sin, but ye have obeyed from the heart that form of doctrine which was delivered you.
nt/rom/6.18: Being then made free from sin, ye became the servants of righteousness.
nt/rom/6.19: I speak after the manner of men because of the infirmity of your flesh: for as ye have yielded your members servants to uncleanness and to iniquity unto iniquity; even so now yield your members servants to righteousness unto holiness.
nt/rom/6.20: For when ye were the servants of sin, ye were free from righteousness.
nt/rom/6.21: What fruit had ye then in those things whereof ye are now ashamed? for the end of those things [italics]is[/italics] death.
nt/rom/6.22: But now being made free from sin, and become servants to God, ye have your fruit unto holiness, and the end everlasting life.
nt/rom/6.23: For the wages of sin [italics]is[/italics] death; but the gift of God [italics]is[/italics] eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.

nt/rom/8.1: [italics]There is[/italics] therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
nt/rom/8.2: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death.
nt/rom/8.3: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:
nt/rom/8.4: That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
nt/rom/8.5: For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
nt/rom/8.6: For to be carnally minded [italics]is[/italics] death; but to be spiritually minded [italics]is[/italics] life and peace.
nt/rom/8.7: Because the carnal mind [italics]is[/italics] enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.
nt/rom/8.8: So then they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
nt/rom/8.9: But ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
nt/rom/8.10: And if Christ [italics]be[/italics] in you, the body [italics]is[/italics] dead because of sin; but the Spirit [italics]is[/italics] life because of righteousness.
nt/rom/8.11: But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
nt/rom/8.12: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.
nt/rom/8.13: For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
nt/rom/8.14: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
nt/rom/8.15: For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
nt/rom/8.16: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:
nt/rom/8.17: And if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with [italics]him,[/italics] that we may be also glorified together.
nt/rom/8.18: For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time [italics]are[/italics] not worthy [italics]to be compared[/italics] with the glory which shall be revealed in us.
nt/rom/8.19: For the earnest expectation of the creature waiteth for the manifestation of the sons of God.
nt/rom/8.20: For the creature was made subject to vanity, not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subjected [italics]the same[/italics] in hope,
nt/rom/8.21: Because the creature itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption into the glorious liberty of the children of God.
nt/rom/8.22: For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.
nt/rom/8.23: And not only [italics]they,[/italics] but ourselves also, which have the firstfruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the adoption, [italics]to wit,[/italics] the redemption of our body.
nt/rom/8.24: For we are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?
nt/rom/8.25: But if we hope for that we see not, [italics]then[/italics] do we with patience wait for [italics]it.[/italics]
nt/rom/8.26: Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.
nt/rom/8.27: And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what [italics]is[/italics] the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh intercession for the saints according to [italics]the will of[/italics] God.
nt/rom/8.28: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to [italics]his[/italics] purpose.
nt/rom/8.29: For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate [italics]to be[/italics] conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren.
nt/rom/8.30: Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.
nt/rom/8.31: What shall we then say to these things? If God [italics]be[/italics] for us, who [italics]can [/italics][italics]be[/italics] against us?
nt/rom/8.32: He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things?
nt/rom/8.33: Who shall lay any thing to the charge of God’s elect? [italics]It is[/italics] God that justifieth.
nt/rom/8.34: Who [italics]is[/italics] he that condemneth? [italics]It is[/italics] Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
nt/rom/8.35: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? [italics]shall[/italics] tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
nt/rom/8.36: As it is written, For thy sake we are killed all the day long; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
nt/rom/8.37: Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him that loved us.
nt/rom/8.38: For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
nt/rom/8.39: Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

nt/titus/2.1: But speak thou the things which become sound doctrine:
nt/titus/2.2: That the aged men be sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience.
nt/titus/2.3: The aged women likewise, that [italics]they be[/italics] in behaviour as becometh holiness, not false accusers, not given to much wine, teachers of good things;
nt/titus/2.4: That they may teach the young women to be sober, to love their husbands, to love their children,
nt/titus/2.5: [italics]To be[/italics] discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.
nt/titus/2.6: Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.
nt/titus/2.7: In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine [italics]shewing[/italics] uncorruptness, gravity, sincerity,
nt/titus/2.8: Sound speech, that cannot be condemned; that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.
nt/titus/2.9: [italics]Exhort[/italics] servants to be obedient unto their own masters, [italics]and[/italics] to please [italics]them[/italics] well in all [italics]things;[/italics] not answering again;
nt/titus/2.10: Not purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.
nt/titus/2.11: For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,
nt/titus/2.12: Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;
nt/titus/2.13: Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;
nt/titus/2.14: Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify unto himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works.
nt/titus/2.15: These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise thee.

ot/2-sam/22.1: And David spake unto the Lord the words of this song in the day [italics]that[/italics] the Lord had delivered him out of the hand of all his enemies, and out of the hand of Saul:
ot/2-sam/22.2: And he said, The Lord [italics]is[/italics] my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer;
ot/2-sam/22.3: The God of my rock; in him will I trust: [italics]he is[/italics] my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower, and my refuge, my saviour; thou savest me from violence.
ot/2-sam/22.4: I will call on the Lord, [italics]who is[/italics] worthy to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.
ot/2-sam/22.5: When the waves of death compassed me, the floods of ungodly men made me afraid;
ot/2-sam/22.6: The sorrows of hell compassed me about; the snares of death prevented me;
ot/2-sam/22.7: In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried to my God: and he did hear my voice out of his temple, and my cry [italics]did enter[/italics] into his ears.
ot/2-sam/22.8: Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations of heaven moved and shook, because he was wroth.
ot/2-sam/22.9: There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it.
ot/2-sam/22.10: He bowed the heavens also, and came down; and darkness [italics]was[/italics] under his feet.
ot/2-sam/22.11: And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly: and he was seen upon the wings of the wind.
ot/2-sam/22.12: And he made darkness pavilions round about him, dark waters, [italics]and[/italics] thick clouds of the skies.
ot/2-sam/22.13: Through the brightness before him were coals of fire kindled.
ot/2-sam/22.14: The Lord thundered from heaven, and the most High uttered his voice.
ot/2-sam/22.15: And he sent out arrows, and scattered them; lightning, and discomfited them.
ot/2-sam/22.16: And the channels of the sea appeared, the foundations of the world were discovered, at the rebuking of the Lord, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
ot/2-sam/22.17: He sent from above, he took me; he drew me out of many waters;
ot/2-sam/22.18: He delivered me from my strong enemy, [italics]and[/italics] from them that hated me: for they were too strong for me.
ot/2-sam/22.19: They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the Lord was my stay.
ot/2-sam/22.20: He brought me forth also into a large place: he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
ot/2-sam/22.21: The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness: according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
ot/2-sam/22.22: For I have kept the ways of the Lord, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
ot/2-sam/22.23: For all his judgments [italics]were[/italics] before me: and [italics]as for[/italics] his statutes, I did not depart from them.
ot/2-sam/22.24: I was also upright before him, and have kept myself from mine iniquity.
ot/2-sam/22.25: Therefore the Lord hath recompensed me according to my righteousness; according to my cleanness in his eye sight.
ot/2-sam/22.26: With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful, [italics]and[/italics] with the upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright.
ot/2-sam/22.27: With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself unsavoury.
ot/2-sam/22.28: And the afflicted people thou wilt save: but thine eyes [italics]are[/italics] upon the haughty, [italics]that[/italics] thou mayest bring [italics]them[/italics] down.
ot/2-sam/22.29: For thou [italics]art[/italics] my lamp, O Lord: and the Lord will lighten my darkness.
ot/2-sam/22.30: For by thee I have run through a troop: by my God have I leaped over a wall.
ot/2-sam/22.31: [italics]As for[/italics] God, his way [italics]is[/italics] perfect; the word of the Lord [italics]is[/italics] tried: he [italics]is[/italics] a buckler to all them that trust in him.
ot/2-sam/22.32: For who [italics]is[/italics] God, save the Lord? and who [italics]is[/italics] a rock, save our God?
ot/2-sam/22.33: God [italics]is[/italics] my strength [italics]and[/italics] power: and he maketh my way perfect.
ot/2-sam/22.34: He maketh my feet like hinds’ [italics]feet:[/italics] and setteth me upon my high places.
ot/2-sam/22.35: He teacheth my hands to war; so that a bow of steel is broken by mine arms.
ot/2-sam/22.36: Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy gentleness hath made me great.
ot/2-sam/22.37: Thou hast enlarged my steps under me; so that my feet did not slip.
ot/2-sam/22.38: I have pursued mine enemies, and destroyed them; and turned not again until I had consumed them.
ot/2-sam/22.39: And I have consumed them, and wounded them, that they could not arise: yea, they are fallen under my feet.
