ot/2-sam/22.40: For thou hast girded me with strength to battle: them that rose up against me hast thou subdued under me.
ot/2-sam/22.41: Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies, that I might destroy them that hate me.
ot/2-sam/22.42: They looked, but [italics]there was[/italics] none to save; [italics]even[/italics] unto the Lord, but he answered them not.
ot/2-sam/22.43: Then did I beat them as small as the dust of the earth, I did stamp them as the mire of the street, [italics]and[/italics] did spread them abroad.
ot/2-sam/22.44: Thou also hast delivered me from the strivings of my people, thou hast kept me [italics]to be[/italics] head of the heathen: a people [italics]which[/italics] I knew not shall serve me.
ot/2-sam/22.45: Strangers shall submit themselves unto me: as soon as they hear, they shall be obedient unto me.
ot/2-sam/22.46: Strangers shall fade away, and they shall be afraid out of their close places.
ot/2-sam/22.47: The Lord liveth; and blessed [italics]be[/italics] my rock; and exalted be the God of the rock of my salvation.
ot/2-sam/22.48: It [italics]is[/italics] God that avengeth me, and that bringeth down the people under me,
ot/2-sam/22.49: And that bringeth me forth from mine enemies: thou also hast lifted me up on high above them that rose up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
ot/2-sam/22.50: Therefore I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and I will sing praises unto thy name.
ot/2-sam/22.51: [italics]He is[/italics] the tower of salvation for his king: and sheweth mercy to his anointed, unto David, and to his seed for evermore.

ot/2-sam/7.1: And it came to pass, when the king sat in his house, and the Lord had given him rest round about from all his enemies;
ot/2-sam/7.2: That the king said unto Nathan the prophet, See now, I dwell in an house of cedar, but the ark of God dwelleth within curtains.
ot/2-sam/7.3: And Nathan said to the king, Go, do all that [italics]is[/italics] in thine heart; for the Lord [italics]is[/italics] with thee.
ot/2-sam/7.4: ¶ And it came to pass that night, that the word of the Lord came unto Nathan, saying,
ot/2-sam/7.5: Go and tell my servant David, Thus saith the Lord, Shalt thou build me an house for me to dwell in?
ot/2-sam/7.6: Whereas I have not dwelt in [italics]any[/italics] house since the time that I brought up the children of Israel out of Egypt, even to this day, but have walked in a tent and in a tabernacle.
ot/2-sam/7.7: In all [italics]the places[/italics] wherein I have walked with all the children of Israel spake I a word with any of the tribes of Israel, whom I commanded to feed my people Israel, saying, Why build ye not me an house of cedar?
ot/2-sam/7.8: Now therefore so shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus saith the Lord of hosts, I took thee from the sheepcote, from following the sheep, to be ruler over my people, over Israel:
ot/2-sam/7.9: And I was with thee whithersoever thou wentest, and have cut off all thine enemies out of thy sight, and have made thee a great name, like unto the name of the great [italics]men[/italics] that [italics]are[/italics] in the earth.
ot/2-sam/7.10: Moreover I will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, that they may dwell in a place of their own, and move no more; neither shall the children of wickedness afflict them any more, as beforetime,
ot/2-sam/7.11: And as since the time that I commanded judges [italics]to be[/italics] over my people Israel, and have caused thee to rest from all thine enemies. Also the Lord telleth thee that he will make thee an house.
ot/2-sam/7.12: ¶ And when thy days be fulfilled, and thou shalt sleep with thy fathers, I will set up thy seed after thee, which shall proceed out of thy bowels, and I will establish his kingdom.
ot/2-sam/7.13: He shall build an house for my name, and I will stablish the throne of his kingdom for ever.
ot/2-sam/7.14: I will be his father, and he shall be my son. If he commit iniquity, I will chasten him with the rod of men, and with the stripes of the children of men:
ot/2-sam/7.15: But my mercy shall not depart away from him, as I took [italics]it[/italics] from Saul, whom I put away before thee.
ot/2-sam/7.16: And thine house and thy kingdom shall be established for ever before thee: thy throne shall be established for ever.
ot/2-sam/7.17: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so did Nathan speak unto David.
ot/2-sam/7.18: ¶ Then went king David in, and sat before the Lord, and he said, Who [italics]am[/italics] I, O Lord God? and what [italics]is[/italics] my house, that thou hast brought me hitherto?
ot/2-sam/7.19: And this was yet a small thing in thy sight, O Lord God; but thou hast spoken also of thy servant’s house for a great while to come. And [italics]is[/italics] this the manner of man, O Lord God?
ot/2-sam/7.20: And what can David say more unto thee? for thou, Lord God, knowest thy servant.
ot/2-sam/7.21: For thy word’s sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all these great things, to make thy servant know [italics]them.[/italics]
ot/2-sam/7.22: Wherefore thou art great, O Lord God: for [italics]there is[/italics] none like thee, neither [italics]is there any[/italics] God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
ot/2-sam/7.23: And what one nation in the earth [italics]is[/italics] like thy people, [italics]even[/italics] like Israel, whom God went to redeem for a people to himself, and to make him a name, and to do for you great things and terrible, for thy land, before thy people, which thou redeemedst to thee from Egypt, [italics]from[/italics] the nations and their gods?
ot/2-sam/7.24: For thou hast confirmed to thyself thy people Israel [italics]to be[/italics] a people unto thee for ever: and thou, Lord, art become their God.
ot/2-sam/7.25: And now, O Lord God, the word that thou hast spoken concerning thy servant, and concerning his house, establish [italics]it[/italics] for ever, and do as thou hast said.
ot/2-sam/7.26: And let thy name be magnified for ever, saying, The Lord of hosts [italics]is[/italics] the God over Israel: and let the house of thy servant David be established before thee.
ot/2-sam/7.27: For thou, O Lord of hosts, God of Israel, hast revealed to thy servant, saying, I will build thee an house: therefore hath thy servant found in his heart to pray this prayer unto thee.
ot/2-sam/7.28: And now, O Lord God, thou [italics]art[/italics] that God, and thy words be true, and thou hast promised this goodness unto thy servant:
ot/2-sam/7.29: Therefore now let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may continue for ever before thee: for thou, O Lord God, hast spoken [italics]it:[/italics] and with thy blessing let the house of thy servant be blessed for ever.

ot/amos/3.1: Hear this word that the Lord hath spoken against you, O children of Israel, against the whole family which I brought up from the land of Egypt, saying,
ot/amos/3.2: You only have I known of all the families of the earth: therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.
ot/amos/3.3: Can two walk together, except they be agreed?
ot/amos/3.4: Will a lion roar in the forest, when he hath no prey? will a young lion cry out of his den, if he have taken nothing?
ot/amos/3.5: Can a bird fall in a snare upon the earth, where no gin [italics]is[/italics] for him? shall [italics]one[/italics] take up a snare from the earth, and have taken nothing at all?
ot/amos/3.6: Shall a trumpet be blown in the city, and the people not be afraid? shall there be evil in a city, and the Lord hath not done [italics]it?[/italics]
ot/amos/3.7: Surely the Lord God will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret unto his servants the prophets.
ot/amos/3.8: The lion hath roared, who will not fear? the Lord God hath spoken, who can but prophesy?
ot/amos/3.9: ¶ Publish in the palaces at Ashdod, and in the palaces in the land of Egypt, and say, Assemble yourselves upon the mountains of Samaria, and behold the great tumults in the midst thereof, and the oppressed in the midst thereof.
ot/amos/3.10: For they know not to do right, saith the Lord, who store up violence and robbery in their palaces.
ot/amos/3.11: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; An adversary [italics]there shall be[/italics] even round about the land; and he shall bring down thy strength from thee, and thy palaces shall be spoiled.
ot/amos/3.12: Thus saith the Lord; As the shepherd taketh out of the mouth of the lion two legs, or a piece of an ear; so shall the children of Israel be taken out that dwell in Samaria in the corner of a bed, and in Damascus [italics]in[/italics] a couch.
ot/amos/3.13: Hear ye, and testify in the house of Jacob, saith the Lord God, the God of hosts,
ot/amos/3.14: That in the day that I shall visit the transgressions of Israel upon him I will also visit the altars of Beth-el: and the horns of the altar shall be cut off, and fall to the ground.
ot/amos/3.15: And I will smite the winter house with the summer house; and the houses of ivory shall perish, and the great houses shall have an end, saith the Lord.

ot/dan/7.1: In the first year of Belshazzar king of Babylon Daniel had a dream and visions of his head upon his bed: then he wrote the dream, [italics]and[/italics] told the sum of the matters.
ot/dan/7.2: Daniel spake and said, I saw in my vision by night, and, behold, the four winds of the heaven strove upon the great sea.
ot/dan/7.3: And four great beasts came up from the sea, diverse one from another.
ot/dan/7.4: The first [italics]was[/italics] like a lion, and had eagle’s wings: I beheld till the wings thereof were plucked, and it was lifted up from the earth, and made stand upon the feet as a man, and a man’s heart was given to it.
ot/dan/7.5: And behold another beast, a second, like to a bear, and it raised up itself on one side, and [italics]it had[/italics] three ribs in the mouth of it between the teeth of it: and they said thus unto it, Arise, devour much flesh.
ot/dan/7.6: After this I beheld, and lo another, like a leopard, which had upon the back of it four wings of a fowl; the beast had also four heads; and dominion was given to it.
ot/dan/7.7: After this I saw in the night visions, and behold a fourth beast, dreadful and terrible, and strong exceedingly; and it had great iron teeth: it devoured and brake in pieces, and stamped the residue with the feet of it: and it [italics]was[/italics] diverse from all the beasts that [italics]were[/italics] before it; and it had ten horns.
ot/dan/7.8: I considered the horns, and, behold, there came up among them another little horn, before whom there were three of the first horns plucked up by the roots: and, behold, in this horn [italics]were[/italics] eyes like the eyes of man, and a mouth speaking great things.
ot/dan/7.9: ¶ I beheld till the thrones were cast down, and the Ancient of days did sit, whose garment [italics]was[/italics] white as snow, and the hair of his head like the pure wool: his throne [italics]was like[/italics] the fiery flame, [italics]and[/italics] his wheels [italics]as[/italics] burning fire.
ot/dan/7.10: A fiery stream issued and came forth from before him: thousand thousands ministered unto him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him: the judgment was set, and the books were opened.
ot/dan/7.11: I beheld then because of the voice of the great words which the horn spake: I beheld [italics]even[/italics] till the beast was slain, and his body destroyed, and given to the burning flame.
ot/dan/7.12: As concerning the rest of the beasts, they had their dominion taken away: yet their lives were prolonged for a season and time.
ot/dan/7.13: I saw in the night visions, and, behold, [italics]one[/italics] like the Son of man came with the clouds of heaven, and came to the Ancient of days, and they brought him near before him.
ot/dan/7.14: And there was given him dominion, and glory, and a kingdom, that all people, nations, and languages, should serve him: his dominion [italics]is[/italics] an everlasting dominion, which shall not pass away, and his kingdom [italics]that[/italics] which shall not be destroyed.
ot/dan/7.15: ¶ I Daniel was grieved in my spirit in the midst of [italics]my[/italics] body, and the visions of my head troubled me.
ot/dan/7.16: I came near unto one of them that stood by, and asked him the truth of all this. So he told me, and made me know the interpretation of the things.
ot/dan/7.17: These great beasts, which are four, [italics]are[/italics] four kings, [italics]which[/italics] shall arise out of the earth.
ot/dan/7.18: But the saints of the most High shall take the kingdom, and possess the kingdom for ever, even for ever and ever.
ot/dan/7.19: Then I would know the truth of the fourth beast, which was diverse from all the others, exceeding dreadful, whose teeth [italics]were of[/italics] iron, and his nails [italics]of[/italics] brass; [italics]which[/italics] devoured, brake in pieces, and stamped the residue with his feet;
ot/dan/7.20: And of the ten horns that [italics]were[/italics] in his head, and [italics]of[/italics] the other which came up, and before whom three fell; even [italics]of[/italics] that horn that had eyes, and a mouth that spake very great things, whose look [italics]was[/italics] more stout than his fellows.
ot/dan/7.21: I beheld, and the same horn made war with the saints, and prevailed against them;
ot/dan/7.22: Until the Ancient of days came, and judgment was given to the saints of the most High; and the time came that the saints possessed the kingdom.
ot/dan/7.23: Thus he said, The fourth beast shall be the fourth kingdom upon earth, which shall be diverse from all kingdoms, and shall devour the whole earth, and shall tread it down, and break it in pieces.
ot/dan/7.24: And the ten horns out of this kingdom [italics]are[/italics] ten kings [italics]that[/italics] shall arise: and another shall rise after them; and he shall be diverse from the first, and he shall subdue three kings.
ot/dan/7.25: And he shall speak [italics]great[/italics] words against the most High, and shall wear out the saints of the most High, and think to change times and laws: and they shall be given into his hand until a time and times and the dividing of time.
ot/dan/7.26: But the judgment shall sit, and they shall take away his dominion, to consume and to destroy [italics]it[/italics] unto the end.
ot/dan/7.27: And the kingdom and dominion, and the greatness of the kingdom under the whole heaven, shall be given to the people of the saints of the most High, whose kingdom [italics]is[/italics] an everlasting kingdom, and all dominions shall serve and obey him.
ot/dan/7.28: Hitherto [italics]is[/italics] the end of the matter. As for me Daniel, my cogitations much troubled me, and my countenance changed in me: but I kept the matter in my heart.

ot/dan/9.1: In the first year of Darius the son of Ahasuerus, of the seed of the Medes, which was made king over the realm of the Chaldeans;
ot/dan/9.2: In the first year of his reign I Daniel understood by books the number of the years, whereof the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah the prophet, that he would accomplish seventy years in the desolations of Jerusalem.
ot/dan/9.3: ¶ And I set my face unto the Lord God, to seek by prayer and supplications, with fasting, and sackcloth, and ashes:
ot/dan/9.4: And I prayed unto the Lord my God, and made my confession, and said, O Lord, the great and dreadful God, keeping the covenant and mercy to them that love him, and to them that keep his commandments;
ot/dan/9.5: We have sinned, and have committed iniquity, and have done wickedly, and have rebelled, even by departing from thy precepts and from thy judgments:
ot/dan/9.6: Neither have we hearkened unto thy servants the prophets, which spake in thy name to our kings, our princes, and our fathers, and to all the people of the land.
ot/dan/9.7: O Lord, righteousness [italics]belongeth[/italics] unto thee, but unto us confusion of faces, as at this day; to the men of Judah, and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and unto all Israel, [italics]that are[/italics] near, and [italics]that are[/italics] far off, through all the countries whither thou hast driven them, because of their trespass that they have trespassed against thee.
ot/dan/9.8: O Lord, to us [italics]belongeth[/italics] confusion of face, to our kings, to our princes, and to our fathers, because we have sinned against thee.
ot/dan/9.9: To the Lord our God [italics]belong[/italics] mercies and forgivenesses, though we have rebelled against him;
ot/dan/9.10: Neither have we obeyed the voice of the Lord our God, to walk in his laws, which he set before us by his servants the prophets.
ot/dan/9.11: Yea, all Israel have transgressed thy law, even by departing, that they might not obey thy voice; therefore the curse is poured upon us, and the oath that [italics]is[/italics] written in the law of Moses the servant of God, because we have sinned against him.
ot/dan/9.12: And he hath confirmed his words, which he spake against us, and against our judges that judged us, by bringing upon us a great evil: for under the whole heaven hath not been done as hath been done upon Jerusalem.
ot/dan/9.13: As [italics]it is[/italics] written in the law of Moses, all this evil is come upon us: yet made we not our prayer before the Lord our God, that we might turn from our iniquities, and understand thy truth.
ot/dan/9.14: Therefore hath the Lord watched upon the evil, and brought it upon us: for the Lord our God [italics]is[/italics] righteous in all his works which he doeth: for we obeyed not his voice.
ot/dan/9.15: And now, O Lord our God, that hast brought thy people forth out of the land of Egypt with a mighty hand, and hast gotten thee renown, as at this day; we have sinned, we have done wickedly.
ot/dan/9.16: ¶ O Lord, according to all thy righteousness, I beseech thee, let thine anger and thy fury be turned away from thy city Jerusalem, thy holy mountain: because for our sins, and for the iniquities of our fathers, Jerusalem and thy people [italics]are become[/italics] a reproach to all [italics]that are[/italics] about us.
ot/dan/9.17: Now therefore, O our God, hear the prayer of thy servant, and his supplications, and cause thy face to shine upon thy sanctuary that is desolate, for the Lord’s sake.
ot/dan/9.18: O my God, incline thine ear, and hear; open thine eyes, and behold our desolations, and the city which is called by thy name: for we do not present our supplications before thee for our righteousnesses, but for thy great mercies.
ot/dan/9.19: O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive; O Lord, hearken and do; defer not, for thine own sake, O my God: for thy city and thy people are called by thy name.
ot/dan/9.20: ¶ And whiles I [italics]was[/italics] speaking, and praying, and confessing my sin and the sin of my people Israel, and presenting my supplication before the Lord my God for the holy mountain of my God;
ot/dan/9.21: Yea, whiles I [italics]was[/italics] speaking in prayer, even the man Gabriel, whom I had seen in the vision at the beginning, being caused to fly swiftly, touched me about the time of the evening oblation.
ot/dan/9.22: And he informed [italics]me,[/italics] and talked with me, and said, O Daniel, I am now come forth to give thee skill and understanding.
ot/dan/9.23: At the beginning of thy supplications the commandment came forth, and I am come to shew [italics]thee;[/italics] for thou [italics]art[/italics] greatly beloved: therefore understand the matter, and consider the vision.
ot/dan/9.24: Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people and upon thy holy city, to finish the transgression, and to make an end of sins, and to make reconciliation for iniquity, and to bring in everlasting righteousness, and to seal up the vision and prophecy, and to anoint the most Holy.
ot/dan/9.25: Know therefore and understand, [italics]that[/italics] from the going forth of the commandment to restore and to build Jerusalem unto the Messiah the Prince [italics]shall be[/italics] seven weeks, and threescore and two weeks: the street shall be built again, and the wall, even in troublous times.
ot/dan/9.26: And after threescore and two weeks shall Messiah be cut off, but not for himself: and the people of the prince that shall come shall destroy the city and the sanctuary; and the end thereof [italics]shall be[/italics] with a flood, and unto the end of the war desolations are determined.
ot/dan/9.27: And he shall confirm the covenant with many for one week: and in the midst of the week he shall cause the sacrifice and the oblation to cease, and for the overspreading of abominations he shall make [italics]it[/italics] desolate, even until the consummation, and that determined shall be poured upon the desolate.

ot/deut/18.1: The priests the Levites, [italics]and[/italics] all the tribe of Levi, shall have no part nor inheritance with Israel: they shall eat the offerings of the Lord made by fire, and his inheritance.
ot/deut/18.2: Therefore shall they have no inheritance among their brethren: the Lord [italics]is[/italics] their inheritance, as he hath said unto them.
ot/deut/18.3: ¶ And this shall be the priest’s due from the people, from them that offer a sacrifice, whether [italics]it be[/italics] ox or sheep; and they shall give unto the priest the shoulder, and the two cheeks, and the maw.
ot/deut/18.4: The firstfruit [italics]also[/italics] of thy corn, of thy wine, and of thine oil, and the first of the fleece of thy sheep, shalt thou give him.
ot/deut/18.5: For the Lord thy God hath chosen him out of all thy tribes, to stand to minister in the name of the Lord, him and his sons for ever.
ot/deut/18.6: ¶ And if a Levite come from any of thy gates out of all Israel, where he sojourned, and come with all the desire of his mind unto the place which the Lord shall choose;
ot/deut/18.7: Then he shall minister in the name of the Lord his God, as all his brethren the Levites [italics]do,[/italics] which stand there before the Lord.
ot/deut/18.8: They shall have like portions to eat, beside that which cometh of the sale of his patrimony.
ot/deut/18.9: ¶ When thou art come into the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee, thou shalt not learn to do after the abominations of those nations.
ot/deut/18.10: There shall not be found among you [italics]any one[/italics] that maketh his son or his daughter to pass through the fire, [italics]or[/italics] that useth divination, [italics]or[/italics] an observer of times, or an enchanter, or a witch,
ot/deut/18.11: Or a charmer, or a consulter with familiar spirits, or a wizard, or a necromancer.
ot/deut/18.12: For all that do these things [italics]are[/italics] an abomination unto the Lord: and because of these abominations the Lord thy God doth drive them out from before thee.
ot/deut/18.13: Thou shalt be perfect with the Lord thy God.
ot/deut/18.14: For these nations, which thou shalt possess, hearkened unto observers of times, and unto diviners: but as for thee, the Lord thy God hath not suffered thee so [italics]to do.[/italics]
ot/deut/18.15: ¶ The Lord thy God will raise up unto thee a Prophet from the midst of thee, of thy brethren, like unto me; unto him ye shall hearken;
ot/deut/18.16: According to all that thou desiredst of the Lord thy God in Horeb in the day of the assembly, saying, Let me not hear again the voice of the Lord my God, neither let me see this great fire any more, that I die not.
ot/deut/18.17: And the Lord said unto me, They have well [italics]spoken that[/italics] which they have spoken.
ot/deut/18.18: I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put my words in his mouth; and he shall speak unto them all that I shall command him.
ot/deut/18.19: And it shall come to pass, [italics]that[/italics] whosoever will not hearken unto my words which he shall speak in my name, I will require [italics]it[/italics] of him.
ot/deut/18.20: But the prophet, which shall presume to speak a word in my name, which I have not commanded him to speak, or that shall speak in the name of other gods, even that prophet shall die.
ot/deut/18.21: And if thou say in thine heart, How shall we know the word which the Lord hath not spoken?
ot/deut/18.22: When a prophet speaketh in the name of the Lord, if the thing follow not, nor come to pass, that [italics]is[/italics] the thing which the Lord hath not spoken, [italics]but[/italics] the prophet hath spoken it presumptuously: thou shalt not be afraid of him.

ot/deut/32.1: Give ear, O ye heavens, and I will speak; and hear, O earth, the words of my mouth.
ot/deut/32.2: My doctrine shall drop as the rain, my speech shall distil as the dew, as the small rain upon the tender herb, and as the showers upon the grass:
ot/deut/32.3: Because I will publish the name of the Lord: ascribe ye greatness unto our God.
ot/deut/32.4: [italics]He is[/italics] the Rock, his work [italics]is[/italics] perfect: for all his ways [italics]are[/italics] judgment: a God of truth and without iniquity, just and right [italics]is[/italics] he.
ot/deut/32.5: They have corrupted themselves, their spot [italics]is[/italics] not [italics]the spot[/italics] of his children: [italics]they are[/italics] a perverse and crooked generation.
ot/deut/32.6: Do ye thus requite the Lord, O foolish people and unwise? [italics]is[/italics] not he thy father [italics]that[/italics] hath bought thee? hath he not made thee, and established thee?
ot/deut/32.7: ¶ Remember the days of old, consider the years of many generations: ask thy father, and he will shew thee; thy elders, and they will tell thee.
ot/deut/32.8: When the most High divided to the nations their inheritance, when he separated the sons of Adam, he set the bounds of the people according to the number of the children of Israel.
ot/deut/32.9: For the Lord’s portion [italics]is[/italics] his people; Jacob [italics]is[/italics] the lot of his inheritance.
ot/deut/32.10: He found him in a desert land, and in the waste howling wilderness; he led him about, he instructed him, he kept him as the apple of his eye.
ot/deut/32.11: As an eagle stirreth up her nest, fluttereth over her young, spreadeth abroad her wings, taketh them, beareth them on her wings:
ot/deut/32.12: [italics]So[/italics] the Lord alone did lead him, and [italics]there was[/italics] no strange god with him.
ot/deut/32.13: He made him ride on the high places of the earth, that he might eat the increase of the fields; and he made him to suck honey out of the rock, and oil out of the flinty rock;
ot/deut/32.14: Butter of kine, and milk of sheep, with fat of lambs, and rams of the breed of Bashan, and goats, with the fat of kidneys of wheat; and thou didst drink the pure blood of the grape.
ot/deut/32.15: ¶ But Jeshurun waxed fat, and kicked: thou art waxen fat, thou art grown thick, thou art covered [italics]with fatness;[/italics] then he forsook God [italics]which[/italics] made him, and lightly esteemed the Rock of his salvation.
ot/deut/32.16: They provoked him to jealousy with strange [italics]gods[/italics][italics],[/italics] with abominations provoked they him to anger.
ot/deut/32.17: They sacrificed unto devils, not to God; to gods whom they knew not, to new [italics]gods[/italics][italics] that[/italics] came newly up, whom your fathers feared not.
ot/deut/32.18: Of the Rock [italics]that[/italics] begat thee thou art unmindful, and hast forgotten God that formed thee.
ot/deut/32.19: And when the Lord saw [italics]it,[/italics] he abhorred [italics]them,[/italics] because of the provoking of his sons, and of his daughters.
ot/deut/32.20: And he said, I will hide my face from them, I will see what their end [italics]shall be:[/italics] for they [italics]are[/italics] a very froward generation, children in whom [italics]is[/italics] no faith.
ot/deut/32.21: They have moved me to jealousy with [italics]that which is[/italics] not God; they have provoked me to anger with their vanities: and I will move them to jealousy with [italics]those which are[/italics] not a people; I will provoke them to anger with a foolish nation.
ot/deut/32.22: For a fire is kindled in mine anger, and shall burn unto the lowest hell, and shall consume the earth with her increase, and set on fire the foundations of the mountains.
ot/deut/32.23: I will heap mischiefs upon them; I will spend mine arrows upon them.
ot/deut/32.24: [italics]They shall be[/italics] burnt with hunger, and devoured with burning heat, and with bitter destruction: I will also send the teeth of beasts upon them, with the poison of serpents of the dust.
ot/deut/32.25: The sword without, and terror within, shall destroy both the young man and the virgin, the suckling [italics]also[/italics] with the man of gray hairs.
ot/deut/32.26: I said, I would scatter them into corners, I would make the remembrance of them to cease from among men:
ot/deut/32.27: Were it not that I feared the wrath of the enemy, lest their adversaries should behave themselves strangely, [italics]and[/italics] lest they should say, Our hand [italics]is[/italics] high, and the Lord hath not done all this.
ot/deut/32.28: For they [italics]are[/italics] a nation void of counsel, neither [italics]is there any[/italics] understanding in them.
ot/deut/32.29: O that they were wise, [italics]that[/italics] they understood this, [italics]that[/italics] they would consider their latter end!
ot/deut/32.30: How should one chase a thousand, and two put ten thousand to flight, except their Rock had sold them, and the Lord had shut them up?
ot/deut/32.31: For their rock [italics]is[/italics] not as our Rock, even our enemies themselves [italics]being[/italics] judges.
ot/deut/32.32: For their vine [italics]is[/italics] of the vine of Sodom, and of the fields of Gomorrah: their grapes [italics]are[/italics] grapes of gall, their clusters [italics]are[/italics] bitter:
ot/deut/32.33: Their wine [italics]is[/italics] the poison of dragons, and the cruel venom of asps.
ot/deut/32.34: [italics]Is[/italics] not this laid up in store with me, [italics]and[/italics] sealed up among my treasures?
ot/deut/32.35: To me [italics]belongeth[/italics] vengeance, and recompence; their foot shall slide in [italics]due[/italics] time: for the day of their calamity [italics]is[/italics] at hand, and the things that shall come upon them make haste.
ot/deut/32.36: For the Lord shall judge his people, and repent himself for his servants, when he seeth that [italics]their[/italics] power is gone, and [italics]there is[/italics] none shut up, or left.
ot/deut/32.37: And he shall say, Where [italics]are[/italics] their gods, [italics]their[/italics] rock in whom they trusted,
ot/deut/32.38: Which did eat the fat of their sacrifices, [italics]and[/italics] drank the wine of their drink offerings? let them rise up and help you, [italics]and[/italics] be your protection.
ot/deut/32.39: See now that I, [italics]even[/italics] I, [italics]am[/italics] he, and [italics]there is[/italics] no god with me: I kill, and I make alive; I wound, and I heal: neither [italics]is there any[/italics] that can deliver out of my hand.
ot/deut/32.40: For I lift up my hand to heaven, and say, I live for ever.
ot/deut/32.41: If I whet my glittering sword, and mine hand take hold on judgment; I will render vengeance to mine enemies, and will reward them that hate me.
ot/deut/32.42: I will make mine arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh; [italics]and that[/italics] with the blood of the slain and of the captives, from the beginning of revenges upon the enemy.
ot/deut/32.43: Rejoice, O ye nations, [italics]with[/italics] his people: for he will avenge the blood of his servants, and will render vengeance to his adversaries, and will be merciful unto his land, [italics]and[/italics] to his people.
ot/deut/32.44: ¶ And Moses came and spake all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he, and Hoshea the son of Nun.
ot/deut/32.45: And Moses made an end of speaking all these words to all Israel:
ot/deut/32.46: And he said unto them, Set your hearts unto all the words which I testify among you this day, which ye shall command your children to observe to do, all the words of this law.
ot/deut/32.47: For it [italics]is[/italics] not a vain thing for you; because it [italics]is[/italics] your life: and through this thing ye shall prolong [italics]your[/italics] days in the land, whither ye go over Jordan to possess it.
ot/deut/32.48: And the Lord spake unto Moses that selfsame day, saying,
ot/deut/32.49: Get thee up into this mountain Abarim, [italics]unto[/italics] mount Nebo, which [italics]is[/italics] in the land of Moab, that [italics]is[/italics] over against Jericho; and behold the land of Canaan, which I give unto the children of Israel for a possession:
ot/deut/32.50: And die in the mount whither thou goest up, and be gathered unto thy people; as Aaron thy brother died in mount Hor, and was gathered unto his people:
ot/deut/32.51: Because ye trespassed against me among the children of Israel at the waters of Meribah-Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin; because ye sanctified me not in the midst of the children of Israel.
ot/deut/32.52: Yet thou shalt see the land before [italics]thee;[/italics] but thou shalt not go thither unto the land which I give the children of Israel.

ot/deut/8.1: All the commandments which I command thee this day shall ye observe to do, that ye may live, and multiply, and go in and possess the land which the Lord sware unto your fathers.
ot/deut/8.2: And thou shalt remember all the way which the Lord thy God led thee these forty years in the wilderness, to humble thee, [italics]and[/italics] to prove thee, to know what [italics]was[/italics] in thine heart, whether thou wouldest keep his commandments, or no.
ot/deut/8.3: And he humbled thee, and suffered thee to hunger, and fed thee with manna, which thou knewest not, neither did thy fathers know; that he might make thee know that man doth not live by bread only, but by every [italics]word[/italics] that proceedeth out of the mouth of the Lord doth man live.
ot/deut/8.4: Thy raiment waxed not old upon thee, neither did thy foot swell, these forty years.
ot/deut/8.5: Thou shalt also consider in thine heart, that, as a man chasteneth his son, [italics]so[/italics] the Lord thy God chasteneth thee.
ot/deut/8.6: Therefore thou shalt keep the commandments of the Lord thy God, to walk in his ways, and to fear him.
ot/deut/8.7: For the Lord thy God bringeth thee into a good land, a land of brooks of water, of fountains and depths that spring out of valleys and hills;
ot/deut/8.8: A land of wheat, and barley, and vines, and fig trees, and pomegranates; a land of oil olive, and honey;
ot/deut/8.9: A land wherein thou shalt eat bread without scarceness, thou shalt not lack any [italics]thing[/italics] in it; a land whose stones [italics]are[/italics] iron, and out of whose hills thou mayest dig brass.
ot/deut/8.10: When thou hast eaten and art full, then thou shalt bless the Lord thy God for the good land which he hath given thee.
ot/deut/8.11: Beware that thou forget not the Lord thy God, in not keeping his commandments, and his judgments, and his statutes, which I command thee this day:
ot/deut/8.12: Lest [italics]when[/italics] thou hast eaten and art full, and hast built goodly houses, and dwelt [italics]therein;[/italics]
ot/deut/8.13: And [italics]when[/italics] thy herds and thy flocks multiply, and thy silver and thy gold is multiplied, and all that thou hast is multiplied;
ot/deut/8.14: Then thine heart be lifted up, and thou forget the Lord thy God, which brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage;
ot/deut/8.15: Who led thee through that great and terrible wilderness, [italics]wherein were[/italics] fiery serpents, and scorpions, and drought, where [italics]there was[/italics] no water; who brought thee forth water out of the rock of flint;
ot/deut/8.16: Who fed thee in the wilderness with manna, which thy fathers knew not, that he might humble thee, and that he might prove thee, to do thee good at thy latter end;
ot/deut/8.17: And thou say in thine heart, My power and the might of [italics]mine[/italics] hand hath gotten me this wealth.
ot/deut/8.18: But thou shalt remember the Lord thy God: for [italics]it is[/italics] he that giveth thee power to get wealth, that he may establish his covenant which he sware unto thy fathers, as [italics]it is[/italics] this day.
ot/deut/8.19: And it shall be, if thou do at all forget the Lord thy God, and walk after other gods, and serve them, and worship them, I testify against you this day that ye shall surely perish.
ot/deut/8.20: As the nations which the Lord destroyeth before your face, so shall ye perish; because ye would not be obedient unto the voice of the Lord your God.

ot/eccl/12.1: Remember now thy Creator in the days of thy youth, while the evil days come not, nor the years draw nigh, when thou shalt say, I have no pleasure in them;
ot/eccl/12.2: While the sun, or the light, or the moon, or the stars, be not darkened, nor the clouds return after the rain:
ot/eccl/12.3: In the day when the keepers of the house shall tremble, and the strong men shall bow themselves, and the grinders cease because they are few, and those that look out of the windows be darkened,
ot/eccl/12.4: And the doors shall be shut in the streets, when the sound of the grinding is low, and he shall rise up at the voice of the bird, and all the daughters of musick shall be brought low;
ot/eccl/12.5: Also [italics]when[/italics] they shall be afraid of [italics]that which is[/italics] high, and fears [italics]shall be[/italics] in the way, and the almond tree shall flourish, and the grasshopper shall be a burden, and desire shall fail: because man goeth to his long home, and the mourners go about the streets:
ot/eccl/12.6: Or ever the silver cord be loosed, or the golden bowl be broken, or the pitcher be broken at the fountain, or the wheel broken at the cistern.
ot/eccl/12.7: Then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.
ot/eccl/12.8: ¶ Vanity of vanities, saith the preacher; all [italics]is[/italics] vanity.
ot/eccl/12.9: And moreover, because the preacher was wise, he still taught the people knowledge; yea, he gave good heed, and sought out, [italics]and[/italics] set in order many proverbs.
ot/eccl/12.10: The preacher sought to find out acceptable words: and [italics]that which was[/italics] written [italics]was[/italics] upright, [italics]even[/italics] words of truth.
ot/eccl/12.11: The words of the wise [italics]are[/italics] as goads, and as nails fastened [italics]by[/italics] the masters of assemblies, [italics]which[/italics] are given from one shepherd.
ot/eccl/12.12: And further, by these, my son, be admonished: of making many books [italics]there is[/italics] no end; and much study [italics]is[/italics] a weariness of the flesh.
ot/eccl/12.13: ¶ Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this [italics]is[/italics] the whole [italics]duty[/italics] of man.
ot/eccl/12.14: For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret thing, whether [italics]it be[/italics] good, or whether [italics]it be[/italics] evil.

ot/ex/12.1: And the Lord spake unto Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying,
ot/ex/12.2: This month [italics]shall be[/italics] unto you the beginning of months: it [italics]shall be[/italics] the first month of the year to you.
ot/ex/12.3: ¶ Speak ye unto all the congregation of Israel, saying, In the tenth [italics]day[/italics] of this month they shall take to them every man a lamb, according to the house of [italics]their[/italics] fathers, a lamb for an house:
ot/ex/12.4: And if the household be too little for the lamb, let him and his neighbour next unto his house take [italics]it[/italics] according to the number of the souls; every man according to his eating shall make your count for the lamb.
ot/ex/12.5: Your lamb shall be without blemish, a male of the first year: ye shall take [italics]it[/italics] out from the sheep, or from the goats:
ot/ex/12.6: And ye shall keep it up until the fourteenth day of the same month: and the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it in the evening.
ot/ex/12.7: And they shall take of the blood, and strike [italics]it[/italics] on the two side posts and on the upper door post of the houses, wherein they shall eat it.
ot/ex/12.8: And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and unleavened bread; [italics]and[/italics] with bitter [italics]herbs[/italics] they shall eat it.
ot/ex/12.9: Eat not of it raw, nor sodden at all with water, but roast [italics]with[/italics] fire; his head with his legs, and with the purtenance thereof.
ot/ex/12.10: And ye shall let nothing of it remain until the morning; and that which remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.
ot/ex/12.11: ¶ And thus shall ye eat it; [italics]with[/italics] your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste: it [italics]is[/italics] the Lord’s passover.
ot/ex/12.12: For I will pass through the land of Egypt this night, and will smite all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgment: I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord.
ot/ex/12.13: And the blood shall be to you for a token upon the houses where ye [italics]are:[/italics] and when I see the blood, I will pass over you, and the plague shall not be upon you to destroy [italics]you,[/italics] when I smite the land of Egypt.
ot/ex/12.14: And this day shall be unto you for a memorial; and ye shall keep it a feast to the Lord throughout your generations; ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever.
ot/ex/12.15: Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread; even the first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses: for whosoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from Israel.
ot/ex/12.16: And in the first day [italics]there shall be[/italics] an holy convocation, and in the seventh day there shall be an holy convocation to you; no manner of work shall be done in them, save [italics]that[/italics] which every man must eat, that only may be done of you.
ot/ex/12.17: And ye shall observe [italics]the [/italics][italics]feast[/italics][italics] of[/italics] unleavened bread; for in this selfsame day have I brought your armies out of the land of Egypt: therefore shall ye observe this day in your generations by an ordinance for ever.
ot/ex/12.18: ¶ In the first [italics]month,[/italics] on the fourteenth day of the month at even, ye shall eat unleavened bread, until the one and twentieth day of the month at even.
ot/ex/12.19: Seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses: for whosoever eateth that which is leavened, even that soul shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he be a stranger, or born in the land.
ot/ex/12.20: Ye shall eat nothing leavened; in all your habitations shall ye eat unleavened bread.
ot/ex/12.21: ¶ Then Moses called for all the elders of Israel, and said unto them, Draw out and take you a lamb according to your families, and kill the passover.
ot/ex/12.22: And ye shall take a bunch of hyssop, and dip [italics]it[/italics] in the blood that [italics]is[/italics] in the basin, and strike the lintel and the two side posts with the blood that [italics]is[/italics] in the basin; and none of you shall go out at the door of his house until the morning.
ot/ex/12.23: For the Lord will pass through to smite the Egyptians; and when he seeth the blood upon the lintel, and on the two side posts, the Lord will pass over the door, and will not suffer the destroyer to come in unto your houses to smite [italics]you.[/italics]
ot/ex/12.24: And ye shall observe this thing for an ordinance to thee and to thy sons for ever.
ot/ex/12.25: And it shall come to pass, when ye be come to the land which the Lord will give you, according as he hath promised, that ye shall keep this service.
ot/ex/12.26: And it shall come to pass, when your children shall say unto you, What mean ye by this service?
ot/ex/12.27: That ye shall say, It [italics]is[/italics] the sacrifice of the Lord’s passover, who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt, when he smote the Egyptians, and delivered our houses. And the people bowed the head and worshipped.
ot/ex/12.28: And the children of Israel went away, and did as the Lord had commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.
ot/ex/12.29: ¶ And it came to pass, that at midnight the Lord smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh that sat on his throne unto the firstborn of the captive that [italics]was[/italics] in the dungeon; and all the firstborn of cattle.
ot/ex/12.30: And Pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt; for [italics]there was[/italics] not a house where [italics]there was[/italics] not one dead.
ot/ex/12.31: ¶ And he called for Moses and Aaron by night, and said, Rise up, [italics]and[/italics] get you forth from among my people, both ye and the children of Israel; and go, serve the Lord, as ye have said.
ot/ex/12.32: Also take your flocks and your herds, as ye have said, and be gone; and bless me also.
ot/ex/12.33: And the Egyptians were urgent upon the people, that they might send them out of the land in haste; for they said, We [italics]be[/italics] all dead [italics]men.[/italics]
ot/ex/12.34: And the people took their dough before it was leavened, their kneadingtroughs being bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.
ot/ex/12.35: And the children of Israel did according to the word of Moses; and they borrowed of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment:
ot/ex/12.36: And the Lord gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they lent unto them [italics]such things as they required.[/italics] And they spoiled the Egyptians.
ot/ex/12.37: ¶ And the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand on foot [italics]that were[/italics] men, beside children.
ot/ex/12.38: And a mixed multitude went up also with them; and flocks, and herds, [italics]even[/italics] very much cattle.
ot/ex/12.39: And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they brought forth out of Egypt, for it was not leavened; because they were thrust out of Egypt, and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any victual.
ot/ex/12.40: ¶ Now the sojourning of the children of Israel, who dwelt in Egypt, [italics]was[/italics] four hundred and thirty years.
ot/ex/12.41: And it came to pass at the end of the four hundred and thirty years, even the selfsame day it came to pass, that all the hosts of the Lord went out from the land of Egypt.
ot/ex/12.42: It [italics]is[/italics] a night to be much observed unto the Lord for bringing them out from the land of Egypt: this [italics]is[/italics] that night of the Lord to be observed of all the children of Israel in their generations.
ot/ex/12.43: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses and Aaron, This [italics]is[/italics] the ordinance of the passover: There shall no stranger eat thereof:
ot/ex/12.44: But every man’s servant that is bought for money, when thou hast circumcised him, then shall he eat thereof.
ot/ex/12.45: A foreigner and an hired servant shall not eat thereof.
ot/ex/12.46: In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth ought of the flesh abroad out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.
ot/ex/12.47: All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.
ot/ex/12.48: And when a stranger shall sojourn with thee, and will keep the passover to the Lord, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land: for no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.
ot/ex/12.49: One law shall be to him that is homeborn, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among you.
ot/ex/12.50: Thus did all the children of Israel; as the Lord commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.
ot/ex/12.51: And it came to pass the selfsame day, [italics]that[/italics] the Lord did bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their armies.

ot/ex/14.1: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
ot/ex/14.2: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they turn and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, over against Baal-zephon: before it shall ye encamp by the sea.
ot/ex/14.3: For Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel, They [italics]are[/italics] entangled in the land, the wilderness hath shut them in.
ot/ex/14.4: And I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, that he shall follow after them; and I will be honoured upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host; that the Egyptians may know that I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord. And they did so.
ot/ex/14.5: ¶ And it was told the king of Egypt that the people fled: and the heart of Pharaoh and of his servants was turned against the people, and they said, Why have we done this, that we have let Israel go from serving us?
ot/ex/14.6: And he made ready his chariot, and took his people with him:
ot/ex/14.7: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt, and captains over every one of them.
ot/ex/14.8: And the Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the children of Israel: and the children of Israel went out with an high hand.
ot/ex/14.9: But the Egyptians pursued after them, all the horses [italics]and[/italics] chariots of Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his army, and overtook them encamping by the sea, beside Pi-hahiroth, before Baal-zephon.
ot/ex/14.10: ¶ And when Pharaoh drew nigh, the children of Israel lifted up their eyes, and, behold, the Egyptians marched after them; and they were sore afraid: and the children of Israel cried out unto the Lord.
ot/ex/14.11: And they said unto Moses, Because [italics]there were[/italics] no graves in Egypt, hast thou taken us away to die in the wilderness? wherefore hast thou dealt thus with us, to carry us forth out of Egypt?
ot/ex/14.12: [italics]Is[/italics] not this the word that we did tell thee in Egypt, saying, Let us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians? For [italics]it had been[/italics] better for us to serve the Egyptians, than that we should die in the wilderness.
ot/ex/14.13: ¶ And Moses said unto the people, Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the Lord, which he will shew to you to day: for the Egyptians whom ye have seen to day, ye shall see them again no more for ever.
ot/ex/14.14: The Lord shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
ot/ex/14.15: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Wherefore criest thou unto me? speak unto the children of Israel, that they go forward:
ot/ex/14.16: But lift thou up thy rod, and stretch out thine hand over the sea, and divide it: and the children of Israel shall go on dry [italics]ground[/italics] through the midst of the sea.
ot/ex/14.17: And I, behold, I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians, and they shall follow them: and I will get me honour upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.
ot/ex/14.18: And the Egyptians shall know that I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord, when I have gotten me honour upon Pharaoh, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.
ot/ex/14.19: ¶ And the angel of God, which went before the camp of Israel, removed and went behind them; and the pillar of the cloud went from before their face, and stood behind them:
ot/ex/14.20: And it came between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel; and it was a cloud and darkness [italics]to them,[/italics] but it gave light by night [italics]to these:[/italics] so that the one came not near the other all the night.
ot/ex/14.21: And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the Lord caused the sea to go [italics]back[/italics] by a strong east wind all that night, and made the sea dry [italics]land,[/italics] and the waters were divided.
ot/ex/14.22: And the children of Israel went into the midst of the sea upon the dry [italics]ground:[/italics] and the waters [italics]were[/italics] a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
ot/ex/14.23: ¶ And the Egyptians pursued, and went in after them to the midst of the sea, [italics]even[/italics] all Pharaoh’s horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.
ot/ex/14.24: And it came to pass, that in the morning watch the Lord looked unto the host of the Egyptians through the pillar of fire and of the cloud, and troubled the host of the Egyptians,
ot/ex/14.25: And took off their chariot wheels, that they drave them heavily: so that the Egyptians said, Let us flee from the face of Israel; for the Lord fighteth for them against the Egyptians.
ot/ex/14.26: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Stretch out thine hand over the sea, that the waters may come again upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.
ot/ex/14.27: And Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to his strength when the morning appeared; and the Egyptians fled against it; and the Lord overthrew the Egyptians in the midst of the sea.
ot/ex/14.28: And the waters returned, and covered the chariots, and the horsemen, [italics]and[/italics] all the host of Pharaoh that came into the sea after them; there remained not so much as one of them.
ot/ex/14.29: But the children of Israel walked upon dry [italics]land[/italics] in the midst of the sea; and the waters [italics]were[/italics] a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
ot/ex/14.30: Thus the Lord saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead upon the sea shore.
ot/ex/14.31: And Israel saw that great work which the Lord did upon the Egyptians: and the people feared the Lord, and believed the Lord, and his servant Moses.

ot/ex/16.1: And they took their journey from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came unto the wilderness of Sin, which [italics]is[/italics] between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departing out of the land of Egypt.
ot/ex/16.2: And the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness:
ot/ex/16.3: And the children of Israel said unto them, Would to God we had died by the hand of the Lord in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the flesh pots, [italics]and[/italics] when we did eat bread to the full; for ye have brought us forth into this wilderness, to kill this whole assembly with hunger.
ot/ex/16.4: ¶ Then said the Lord unto Moses, Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a certain rate every day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in my law, or no.
ot/ex/16.5: And it shall come to pass, that on the sixth day they shall prepare [italics]that[/italics] which they bring in; and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily.
ot/ex/16.6: And Moses and Aaron said unto all the children of Israel, At even, then ye shall know that the Lord hath brought you out from the land of Egypt:
ot/ex/16.7: And in the morning, then ye shall see the glory of the Lord; for that he heareth your murmurings against the Lord: and what [italics]are[/italics] we, that ye murmur against us?
ot/ex/16.8: And Moses said, [italics]This shall be,[/italics] when the Lord shall give you in the evening flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full; for that the Lord heareth your murmurings which ye murmur against him: and what [italics]are[/italics] we? your murmurings [italics]are[/italics] not against us, but against the Lord.
ot/ex/16.9: ¶ And Moses spake unto Aaron, Say unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, Come near before the Lord: for he hath heard your murmurings.
ot/ex/16.10: And it came to pass, as Aaron spake unto the whole congregation of the children of Israel, that they looked toward the wilderness, and, behold, the glory of the Lord appeared in the cloud.
ot/ex/16.11: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
ot/ex/16.12: I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel: speak unto them, saying, At even ye shall eat flesh, and in the morning ye shall be filled with bread; and ye shall know that I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord your God.
ot/ex/16.13: And it came to pass, that at even the quails came up, and covered the camp: and in the morning the dew lay round about the host.
ot/ex/16.14: And when the dew that lay was gone up, behold, upon the face of the wilderness [italics]there lay[/italics] a small round thing, [italics]as[/italics] small as the hoar frost on the ground.
ot/ex/16.15: And when the children of Israel saw [italics]it,[/italics] they said one to another, It [italics]is[/italics] manna: for they wist not what it [italics]was.[/italics] And Moses said unto them, This [italics]is[/italics] the bread which the Lord hath given you to eat.
ot/ex/16.16: ¶ This [italics]is[/italics] the thing which the Lord hath commanded, Gather of it every man according to his eating, an omer for every man, [italics]according to[/italics] the number of your persons; take ye every man for [italics]them[/italics] which [italics]are[/italics] in his tents.
ot/ex/16.17: And the children of Israel did so, and gathered, some more, some less.
ot/ex/16.18: And when they did mete [italics]it[/italics] with an omer, he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little had no lack; they gathered every man according to his eating.
ot/ex/16.19: And Moses said, Let no man leave of it till the morning.
ot/ex/16.20: Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto Moses; but some of them left of it until the morning, and it bred worms, and stank: and Moses was wroth with them.
ot/ex/16.21: And they gathered it every morning, every man according to his eating: and when the sun waxed hot, it melted.
ot/ex/16.22: ¶ And it came to pass, [italics]that[/italics] on the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for one [italics]man:[/italics] and all the rulers of the congregation came and told Moses.
ot/ex/16.23: And he said unto them, This [italics]is that[/italics] which the Lord hath said, To morrow [italics]is[/italics] the rest of the holy sabbath unto the Lord: bake [italics]that[/italics] which ye will bake [italics]to day,[/italics] and seethe that ye will seethe; and that which remaineth over lay up for you to be kept until the morning.
ot/ex/16.24: And they laid it up till the morning, as Moses bade: and it did not stink, neither was there any worm therein.
ot/ex/16.25: And Moses said, Eat that to day; for to day [italics]is[/italics] a sabbath unto the Lord: to day ye shall not find it in the field.
ot/ex/16.26: Six days ye shall gather it; but on the seventh day, [italics]which is[/italics] the sabbath, in it there shall be none.
ot/ex/16.27: ¶ And it came to pass, [italics]that[/italics] there went out [italics]some[/italics] of the people on the seventh day for to gather, and they found none.
ot/ex/16.28: And the Lord said unto Moses, How long refuse ye to keep my commandments and my laws?
ot/ex/16.29: See, for that the Lord hath given you the sabbath, therefore he giveth you on the sixth day the bread of two days; abide ye every man in his place, let no man go out of his place on the seventh day.
ot/ex/16.30: So the people rested on the seventh day.
ot/ex/16.31: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna: and it [italics]was[/italics] like coriander seed, white; and the taste of it [italics]was[/italics] like wafers [italics]made[/italics] with honey.
ot/ex/16.32: ¶ And Moses said, This [italics]is[/italics] the thing which the Lord commandeth, Fill an omer of it to be kept for your generations; that they may see the bread wherewith I have fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you forth from the land of Egypt.
ot/ex/16.33: And Moses said unto Aaron, Take a pot, and put an omer full of manna therein, and lay it up before the Lord, to be kept for your generations.
ot/ex/16.34: As the Lord commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.
ot/ex/16.35: And the children of Israel did eat manna forty years, until they came to a land inhabited; they did eat manna, until they came unto the borders of the land of Canaan.
ot/ex/16.36: Now an omer [italics]is[/italics] the tenth [italics]part[/italics] of an ephah.

ot/ex/19.1: In the third month, when the children of Israel were gone forth out of the land of Egypt, the same day came they [italics]into[/italics] the wilderness of Sinai.
ot/ex/19.2: For they were departed from Rephidim, and were come [italics]to[/italics] the desert of Sinai, and had pitched in the wilderness; and there Israel camped before the mount.
ot/ex/19.3: And Moses went up unto God, and the Lord called unto him out of the mountain, saying, Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel;
ot/ex/19.4: Ye have seen what I did unto the Egyptians, and [italics]how[/italics] I bare you on eagles’ wings, and brought you unto myself.
ot/ex/19.5: Now therefore, if ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure unto me above all people: for all the earth [italics]is[/italics] mine:
ot/ex/19.6: And ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests, and an holy nation. These [italics]are[/italics] the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel.
ot/ex/19.7: ¶ And Moses came and called for the elders of the people, and laid before their faces all these words which the Lord commanded him.
ot/ex/19.8: And all the people answered together, and said, All that the Lord hath spoken we will do. And Moses returned the words of the people unto the Lord.
ot/ex/19.9: And the Lord said unto Moses, Lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and believe thee for ever. And Moses told the words of the people unto the Lord.
ot/ex/19.10: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Go unto the people, and sanctify them to day and to morrow, and let them wash their clothes,
ot/ex/19.11: And be ready against the third day: for the third day the Lord will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.
ot/ex/19.12: And thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying, Take heed to yourselves, [italics]that ye[/italics] go [italics]not[/italics] up into the mount, or touch the border of it: whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death:
ot/ex/19.13: There shall not an hand touch it, but he shall surely be stoned, or shot through; whether [italics]it be[/italics] beast or man, it shall not live: when the trumpet soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount.
ot/ex/19.14: ¶ And Moses went down from the mount unto the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their clothes.
ot/ex/19.15: And he said unto the people, Be ready against the third day: come not at [italics]your[/italics] wives.
ot/ex/19.16: ¶ And it came to pass on the third day in the morning, that there were thunders and lightnings, and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of the trumpet exceeding loud; so that all the people that [italics]was[/italics] in the camp trembled.
ot/ex/19.17: And Moses brought forth the people out of the camp to meet with God; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.
ot/ex/19.18: And mount Sinai was altogether on a smoke, because the Lord descended upon it in fire: and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.
ot/ex/19.19: And when the voice of the trumpet sounded long, and waxed louder and louder, Moses spake, and God answered him by a voice.
ot/ex/19.20: And the Lord came down upon mount Sinai, on the top of the mount: and the Lord called Moses [italics]up[/italics] to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.
ot/ex/19.21: And the Lord said unto Moses, Go down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the Lord to gaze, and many of them perish.
ot/ex/19.22: And let the priests also, which come near to the Lord, sanctify themselves, lest the Lord break forth upon them.
ot/ex/19.23: And Moses said unto the Lord, The people cannot come up to mount Sinai: for thou chargedst us, saying, Set bounds about the mount, and sanctify it.
ot/ex/19.24: And the Lord said unto him, Away, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and Aaron with thee: but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the Lord, lest he break forth upon them.
ot/ex/19.25: So Moses went down unto the people, and spake unto them.

ot/ex/20.1: And God spake all these words, saying,
ot/ex/20.2: I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
ot/ex/20.3: Thou shalt have no other gods before me.
ot/ex/20.4: Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image, or any likeness [italics]of any thing[/italics] that [italics]is[/italics] in heaven above, or that [italics]is[/italics] in the earth beneath, or that [italics]is[/italics] in the water under the earth:
ot/ex/20.5: Thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them: for I the Lord thy God [italics]am[/italics] a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth [italics]generation[/italics] of them that hate me;
ot/ex/20.6: And shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me, and keep my commandments.
ot/ex/20.7: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain; for the Lord will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain.
ot/ex/20.8: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
ot/ex/20.9: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work:
ot/ex/20.10: But the seventh day [italics]is[/italics] the sabbath of the Lord thy God: [italics]in it[/italics] thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy manservant, nor thy maidservant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that [italics]is[/italics] within thy gates:
ot/ex/20.11: For [italics]in[/italics] six days the Lord made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them [italics]is,[/italics] and rested the seventh day: wherefore the Lord blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.
ot/ex/20.12: ¶ Honour thy father and thy mother: that thy days may be long upon the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
ot/ex/20.16: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
ot/ex/20.17: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s house, thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife, nor his manservant, nor his maidservant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that [italics]is[/italics] thy neighbour’s.
ot/ex/20.18: ¶ And all the people saw the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the noise of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking: and when the people saw [italics]it,[/italics] they removed, and stood afar off.
ot/ex/20.19: And they said unto Moses, Speak thou with us, and we will hear: but let not God speak with us, lest we die.
ot/ex/20.20: And Moses said unto the people, Fear not: for God is come to prove you, and that his fear may be before your faces, that ye sin not.
ot/ex/20.21: And the people stood afar off, and Moses drew near unto the thick darkness where God [italics]was.[/italics]
ot/ex/20.22: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Thus thou shalt say unto the children of Israel, Ye have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
ot/ex/20.23: Ye shall not make with me gods of silver, neither shall ye make unto you gods of gold.
ot/ex/20.24: ¶ An altar of earth thou shalt make unto me, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt offerings, and thy peace offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen: in all places where I record my name I will come unto thee, and I will bless thee.
ot/ex/20.25: And if thou wilt make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stone: for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast polluted it.
ot/ex/20.26: Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto mine altar, that thy nakedness be not discovered thereon.

ot/ex/3.1: Now Moses kept the flock of Jethro his father in law, the priest of Midian: and he led the flock to the backside of the desert, and came to the mountain of God, [italics]even[/italics] to Horeb.
ot/ex/3.2: And the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush: and he looked, and, behold, the bush burned with fire, and the bush [italics]was[/italics] not consumed.
ot/ex/3.3: And Moses said, I will now turn aside, and see this great sight, why the bush is not burnt.
ot/ex/3.4: And when the Lord saw that he turned aside to see, God called unto him out of the midst of the bush, and said, Moses, Moses. And he said, Here [italics]am[/italics] I.
ot/ex/3.5: And he said, Draw not nigh hither: put off thy shoes from off thy feet, for the place whereon thou standest [italics]is[/italics] holy ground.
ot/ex/3.6: Moreover he said, I [italics]am[/italics] the God of thy father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. And Moses hid his face; for he was afraid to look upon God.
ot/ex/3.7: ¶ And the Lord said, I have surely seen the affliction of my people which [italics]are[/italics] in Egypt, and have heard their cry by reason of their taskmasters; for I know their sorrows;
ot/ex/3.8: And I am come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians, and to bring them up out of that land unto a good land and a large, unto a land flowing with milk and honey; unto the place of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
ot/ex/3.9: Now therefore, behold, the cry of the children of Israel is come unto me: and I have also seen the oppression wherewith the Egyptians oppress them.
ot/ex/3.10: Come now therefore, and I will send thee unto Pharaoh, that thou mayest bring forth my people the children of Israel out of Egypt.
ot/ex/3.11: ¶ And Moses said unto God, Who [italics]am[/italics] I, that I should go unto Pharaoh, and that I should bring forth the children of Israel out of Egypt?
ot/ex/3.12: And he said, Certainly I will be with thee; and this [italics]shall be[/italics] a token unto thee, that I have sent thee: When thou hast brought forth the people out of Egypt, ye shall serve God upon this mountain.
ot/ex/3.13: And Moses said unto God, Behold, [italics]when[/italics] I come unto the children of Israel, and shall say unto them, The God of your fathers hath sent me unto you; and they shall say to me, What [italics]is[/italics] his name? what shall I say unto them?
ot/ex/3.14: And God said unto Moses, I Am That I Am: and he said, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel, I Am hath sent me unto you.
ot/ex/3.15: And God said moreover unto Moses, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel, The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, hath sent me unto you: this [italics]is[/italics] my name for ever, and this [italics]is[/italics] my memorial unto all generations.
ot/ex/3.16: Go, and gather the elders of Israel together, and say unto them, The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, appeared unto me, saying, I have surely visited you, and [italics]seen[/italics] that which is done to you in Egypt:
ot/ex/3.17: And I have said, I will bring you up out of the affliction of Egypt unto the land of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, unto a land flowing with milk and honey.
ot/ex/3.18: And they shall hearken to thy voice: and thou shalt come, thou and the elders of Israel, unto the king of Egypt, and ye shall say unto him, The Lord God of the Hebrews hath met with us: and now let us go, we beseech thee, three days’ journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to the Lord our God.
ot/ex/3.19: ¶ And I am sure that the king of Egypt will not let you go, no, not by a mighty hand.
ot/ex/3.20: And I will stretch out my hand, and smite Egypt with all my wonders which I will do in the midst thereof: and after that he will let you go.
ot/ex/3.21: And I will give this people favour in the sight of the Egyptians: and it shall come to pass, that, when ye go, ye shall not go empty:
ot/ex/3.22: But every woman shall borrow of her neighbour, and of her that sojourneth in her house, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment: and ye shall put [italics]them[/italics] upon your sons, and upon your daughters; and ye shall spoil the Egyptians.

ot/ex/30.1: And thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon: [italics]of[/italics] shittim wood shalt thou make it.
ot/ex/30.2: A cubit [italics]shall be[/italics] the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits [italics]shall be[/italics] the height thereof: the horns thereof [italics]shall be[/italics] of the same.
ot/ex/30.3: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about.
ot/ex/30.4: And two golden rings shalt thou make to it under the crown of it, by the two corners thereof, upon the two sides of it shalt thou make [italics]it;[/italics] and they shall be for places for the staves to bear it withal.
ot/ex/30.5: And thou shalt make the staves [italics]of[/italics] shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.
ot/ex/30.6: And thou shalt put it before the veil that [italics]is[/italics] by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that [italics]is[/italics] over the testimony, where I will meet with thee.
ot/ex/30.7: And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning: when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it.
ot/ex/30.8: And when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the Lord throughout your generations.
ot/ex/30.9: Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; neither shall ye pour drink offering thereon.
ot/ex/30.10: And Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements: once in the year shall he make atonement upon it throughout your generations: it [italics]is[/italics] most holy unto the Lord.
ot/ex/30.11: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
ot/ex/30.12: When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel after their number, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the Lord, when thou numberest them; that there be no plague among them, when [italics]thou[/italics] numberest them.
ot/ex/30.13: This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary: (a shekel [italics]is[/italics] twenty gerahs:) an half shekel [italics]shall be[/italics] the offering of the Lord.
ot/ex/30.14: Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the Lord.
ot/ex/30.15: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when [italics]they[/italics] give an offering unto the Lord, to make an atonement for your souls.
ot/ex/30.16: And thou shalt take the atonement money of the children of Israel, and shalt appoint it for the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; that it may be a memorial unto the children of Israel before the Lord, to make an atonement for your souls.
ot/ex/30.17: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
ot/ex/30.18: Thou shalt also make a laver [italics]of[/italics] brass, and his foot [italics]also of[/italics] brass, to wash [italics]withal:[/italics] and thou shalt put it between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar, and thou shalt put water therein.
ot/ex/30.19: For Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet thereat:
ot/ex/30.20: When they go into the tabernacle of the congregation, they shall wash with water, that they die not; or when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn offering made by fire unto the Lord:
ot/ex/30.21: So they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they die not: and it shall be a statute for ever to them, [italics]even[/italics] to him and to his seed throughout their generations.
ot/ex/30.22: ¶ Moreover the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
ot/ex/30.23: Take thou also unto thee principal spices, of pure myrrh five hundred [italics]shekels,[/italics] and of sweet cinnamon half so much, [italics]even[/italics] two hundred and fifty [italics]shekels,[/italics] and of sweet calamus two hundred and fifty [italics]shekels,[/italics]
ot/ex/30.24: And of cassia five hundred [italics]shekels,[/italics] after the shekel of the sanctuary, and of oil olive an hin:
ot/ex/30.25: And thou shalt make it an oil of holy ointment, an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary: it shall be an holy anointing oil.
ot/ex/30.26: And thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the congregation therewith, and the ark of the testimony,
ot/ex/30.27: And the table and all his vessels, and the candlestick and his vessels, and the altar of incense,
ot/ex/30.28: And the altar of burnt offering with all his vessels, and the laver and his foot.
ot/ex/30.29: And thou shalt sanctify them, that they may be most holy: whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy.
ot/ex/30.30: And thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, that [italics]they[/italics] may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
ot/ex/30.31: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations.
ot/ex/30.32: Upon man’s flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make [italics]any other[/italics] like it, after the composition of it: it [italics]is[/italics] holy, [italics]and[/italics] it shall be holy unto you.
ot/ex/30.33: Whosoever compoundeth [italics]any[/italics] like it, or whosoever putteth [italics]any[/italics] of it upon a stranger, shall even be cut off from his people.
ot/ex/30.34: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Take unto thee sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum; [italics]these[/italics] sweet spices with pure frankincense: of each shall there be a like [italics]weight:[/italics]
ot/ex/30.35: And thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure [italics]and[/italics] holy:
ot/ex/30.36: And thou shalt beat [italics]some[/italics] of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy.
ot/ex/30.37: And [italics]as for[/italics] the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the Lord.
ot/ex/30.38: Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people.

ot/ex/33.1: And the Lord said unto Moses, Depart, [italics]and[/italics] go up hence, thou and the people which thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt, unto the land which I sware unto Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed will I give it:
ot/ex/33.2: And I will send an angel before thee; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:
ot/ex/33.3: Unto a land flowing with milk and honey: for I will not go up in the midst of thee; for thou [italics]art[/italics] a stiffnecked people: lest I consume thee in the way.
ot/ex/33.4: ¶ And when the people heard these evil tidings, they mourned: and no man did put on him his ornaments.
