ot/ex/33.5: For the Lord had said unto Moses, Say unto the children of Israel, Ye [italics]are[/italics] a stiffnecked people: I will come up into the midst of thee in a moment, and consume thee: therefore now put off thy ornaments from thee, that I may know what to do unto thee.
ot/ex/33.6: And the children of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments by the mount Horeb.
ot/ex/33.7: And Moses took the tabernacle, and pitched it without the camp, afar off from the camp, and called it the Tabernacle of the congregation. And it came to pass, [italics]that[/italics] every one which sought the Lord went out unto the tabernacle of the congregation, which [italics]was[/italics] without the camp.
ot/ex/33.8: And it came to pass, when Moses went out unto the tabernacle, [italics]that[/italics] all the people rose up, and stood every man [italics]at[/italics] his tent door, and looked after Moses, until he was gone into the tabernacle.
ot/ex/33.9: And it came to pass, as Moses entered into the tabernacle, the cloudy pillar descended, and stood [italics]at[/italics] the door of the tabernacle, and [italics]the Lord[/italics] talked with Moses.
ot/ex/33.10: And all the people saw the cloudy pillar stand [italics]at[/italics] the tabernacle door: and all the people rose up and worshipped, every man [italics]in[/italics] his tent door.
ot/ex/33.11: And the Lord spake unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend. And he turned again into the camp: but his servant Joshua, the son of Nun, a young man, departed not out of the tabernacle.
ot/ex/33.12: ¶ And Moses said unto the Lord, See, thou sayest unto me, Bring up this people: and thou hast not let me know whom thou wilt send with me. Yet thou hast said, I know thee by name, and thou hast also found grace in my sight.
ot/ex/33.13: Now therefore, I pray thee, if I have found grace in thy sight, shew me now thy way, that I may know thee, that I may find grace in thy sight: and consider that this nation [italics]is[/italics] thy people.
ot/ex/33.14: And he said, My presence shall go [italics]with thee,[/italics] and I will give thee rest.
ot/ex/33.15: And he said unto him, If thy presence go not [italics]with me,[/italics] carry us not up hence.
ot/ex/33.16: For wherein shall it be known here that I and thy people have found grace in thy sight? [italics]is it[/italics] not in that thou goest with us? so shall we be separated, I and thy people, from all the people that [italics]are[/italics] upon the face of the earth.
ot/ex/33.17: And the Lord said unto Moses, I will do this thing also that thou hast spoken: for thou hast found grace in my sight, and I know thee by name.
ot/ex/33.18: And he said, I beseech thee, shew me thy glory.
ot/ex/33.19: And he said, I will make all my goodness pass before thee, and I will proclaim the name of the Lord before thee; and will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will shew mercy on whom I will shew mercy.
ot/ex/33.20: And he said, Thou canst not see my face: for there shall no man see me, and live.
ot/ex/33.21: And the Lord said, Behold, [italics]there is[/italics] a place by me, and thou shalt stand upon a rock:
ot/ex/33.22: And it shall come to pass, while my glory passeth by, that I will put thee in a clift of the rock, and will cover thee with my hand while I pass by:
ot/ex/33.23: And I will take away mine hand, and thou shalt see my back parts: but my face shall not be seen.

ot/ex/6.1: Then the Lord said unto Moses, Now shalt thou see what I will do to Pharaoh: for with a strong hand shall he let them go, and with a strong hand shall he drive them out of his land.
ot/ex/6.2: And God spake unto Moses, and said unto him, I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord:
ot/ex/6.3: And I appeared unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, by [italics]the name of[/italics] God Almighty, but by my name Jehovah was I not known to them.
ot/ex/6.4: And I have also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage, wherein they were strangers.
ot/ex/6.5: And I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel, whom the Egyptians keep in bondage; and I have remembered my covenant.
ot/ex/6.6: Wherefore say unto the children of Israel, I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will rid you out of their bondage, and I will redeem you with a stretched out arm, and with great judgments:
ot/ex/6.7: And I will take you to me for a people, and I will be to you a God: and ye shall know that I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord your God, which bringeth you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.
ot/ex/6.8: And I will bring you in unto the land, concerning the which I did swear to give it to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob; and I will give it you for an heritage: I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord.
ot/ex/6.9: ¶ And Moses spake so unto the children of Israel: but they hearkened not unto Moses for anguish of spirit, and for cruel bondage.
ot/ex/6.10: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
ot/ex/6.11: Go in, speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.
ot/ex/6.12: And Moses spake before the Lord, saying, Behold, the children of Israel have not hearkened unto me; how then shall Pharaoh hear me, who [italics]am[/italics] of uncircumcised lips?
ot/ex/6.13: And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, and gave them a charge unto the children of Israel, and unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.
ot/ex/6.14: ¶ These [italics]be[/italics] the heads of their fathers’ houses: The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel; Hanoch, and Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi: these [italics]be[/italics] the families of Reuben.
ot/ex/6.15: And the sons of Simeon; Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanitish woman: these [italics]are[/italics] the families of Simeon.
ot/ex/6.16: ¶ And these [italics]are[/italics] the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations; Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari: and the years of the life of Levi [italics]were[/italics] an hundred thirty and seven years.
ot/ex/6.17: The sons of Gershon; Libni, and Shimi, according to their families.
ot/ex/6.18: And the sons of Kohath; Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel: and the years of the life of Kohath [italics]were[/italics] an hundred thirty and three years.
ot/ex/6.19: And the sons of Merari; Mahali and Mushi: these [italics]are[/italics] the families of Levi according to their generations.
ot/ex/6.20: And Amram took him Jochebed his father’s sister to wife; and she bare him Aaron and Moses: and the years of the life of Amram [italics]were[/italics] an hundred and thirty and seven years.
ot/ex/6.21: ¶ And the sons of Izhar; Korah, and Nepheg, and Zichri.
ot/ex/6.22: And the sons of Uzziel; Mishael, and Elzaphan, and Zithri.
ot/ex/6.23: And Aaron took him Elisheba, daughter of Amminadab, sister of Naashon, to wife; and she bare him Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
ot/ex/6.24: And the sons of Korah; Assir, and Elkanah, and Abiasaph: these [italics]are[/italics] the families of the Korhites.
ot/ex/6.25: And Eleazar Aaron’s son took him [italics]one[/italics] of the daughters of Putiel to wife; and she bare him Phinehas: these [italics]are[/italics] the heads of the fathers of the Levites according to their families.
ot/ex/6.26: These [italics]are[/italics] that Aaron and Moses, to whom the Lord said, Bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt according to their armies.
ot/ex/6.27: These [italics]are[/italics] they which spake to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt: these [italics]are[/italics] that Moses and Aaron.
ot/ex/6.28: ¶ And it came to pass on the day [italics]when[/italics] the Lord spake unto Moses in the land of Egypt,
ot/ex/6.29: That the Lord spake unto Moses, saying, I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord: speak thou unto Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I say unto thee.
ot/ex/6.30: And Moses said before the Lord, Behold, I [italics]am[/italics] of uncircumcised lips, and how shall Pharaoh hearken unto me?

ot/ezek/34.1: And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
ot/ezek/34.2: Son of man, prophesy against the shepherds of Israel, prophesy, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God unto the shepherds; Woe [italics]be[/italics] to the shepherds of Israel that do feed themselves! should not the shepherds feed the flocks?
ot/ezek/34.3: Ye eat the fat, and ye clothe you with the wool, ye kill them that are fed: [italics]but[/italics] ye feed not the flock.
ot/ezek/34.4: The diseased have ye not strengthened, neither have ye healed that which was sick, neither have ye bound up [italics]that which was[/italics] broken, neither have ye brought again that which was driven away, neither have ye sought that which was lost; but with force and with cruelty have ye ruled them.
ot/ezek/34.5: And they were scattered, because [italics]there is[/italics] no shepherd: and they became meat to all the beasts of the field, when they were scattered.
ot/ezek/34.6: My sheep wandered through all the mountains, and upon every high hill: yea, my flock was scattered upon all the face of the earth, and none did search or seek [italics]after them.[/italics]
ot/ezek/34.7: ¶ Therefore, ye shepherds, hear the word of the Lord;
ot/ezek/34.8: [italics]As[/italics] I live, saith the Lord God, surely because my flock became a prey, and my flock became meat to every beast of the field, because [italics]there was[/italics] no shepherd, neither did my shepherds search for my flock, but the shepherds fed themselves, and fed not my flock;
ot/ezek/34.9: Therefore, O ye shepherds, hear the word of the Lord;
ot/ezek/34.10: Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I [italics]am[/italics] against the shepherds; and I will require my flock at their hand, and cause them to cease from feeding the flock; neither shall the shepherds feed themselves any more; for I will deliver my flock from their mouth, that they may not be meat for them.
ot/ezek/34.11: ¶ For thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I, [italics]even[/italics] I, will both search my sheep, and seek them out.
ot/ezek/34.12: As a shepherd seeketh out his flock in the day that he is among his sheep [italics]that are[/italics] scattered; so will I seek out my sheep, and will deliver them out of all places where they have been scattered in the cloudy and dark day.
ot/ezek/34.13: And I will bring them out from the people, and gather them from the countries, and will bring them to their own land, and feed them upon the mountains of Israel by the rivers, and in all the inhabited places of the country.
ot/ezek/34.14: I will feed them in a good pasture, and upon the high mountains of Israel shall their fold be: there shall they lie in a good fold, and [italics]in[/italics] a fat pasture shall they feed upon the mountains of Israel.
ot/ezek/34.15: I will feed my flock, and I will cause them to lie down, saith the Lord God.
ot/ezek/34.16: I will seek that which was lost, and bring again that which was driven away, and will bind up [italics]that which was[/italics] broken, and will strengthen that which was sick: but I will destroy the fat and the strong; I will feed them with judgment.
ot/ezek/34.17: And [italics]as for[/italics] you, O my flock, thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I judge between cattle and cattle, between the rams and the he goats.
ot/ezek/34.18: [italics]Seemeth it[/italics] a small thing unto you to have eaten up the good pasture, but ye must tread down with your feet the residue of your pastures? and to have drunk of the deep waters, but ye must foul the residue with your feet?
ot/ezek/34.19: And [italics]as for[/italics] my flock, they eat that which ye have trodden with your feet; and they drink that which ye have fouled with your feet.
ot/ezek/34.20: ¶ Therefore thus saith the Lord God unto them; Behold, I, [italics]even[/italics] I, will judge between the fat cattle and between the lean cattle.
ot/ezek/34.21: Because ye have thrust with side and with shoulder, and pushed all the diseased with your horns, till ye have scattered them abroad;
ot/ezek/34.22: Therefore will I save my flock, and they shall no more be a prey; and I will judge between cattle and cattle.
ot/ezek/34.23: And I will set up one shepherd over them, and he shall feed them, [italics]even[/italics] my servant David; he shall feed them, and he shall be their shepherd.
ot/ezek/34.24: And I the Lord will be their God, and my servant David a prince among them; I the Lord have spoken [italics]it.[/italics]
ot/ezek/34.25: And I will make with them a covenant of peace, and will cause the evil beasts to cease out of the land: and they shall dwell safely in the wilderness, and sleep in the woods.
ot/ezek/34.26: And I will make them and the places round about my hill a blessing; and I will cause the shower to come down in his season; there shall be showers of blessing.
ot/ezek/34.27: And the tree of the field shall yield her fruit, and the earth shall yield her increase, and they shall be safe in their land, and shall know that I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord, when I have broken the bands of their yoke, and delivered them out of the hand of those that served themselves of them.
ot/ezek/34.28: And they shall no more be a prey to the heathen, neither shall the beast of the land devour them; but they shall dwell safely, and none shall make [italics]them[/italics] afraid.
ot/ezek/34.29: And I will raise up for them a plant of renown, and they shall be no more consumed with hunger in the land, neither bear the shame of the heathen any more.
ot/ezek/34.30: Thus shall they know that I the Lord their God [italics]am[/italics] with them, and [italics]that[/italics] they, [italics]even[/italics] the house of Israel, [italics]are[/italics] my people, saith the Lord God.
ot/ezek/34.31: And ye my flock, the flock of my pasture, [italics]are[/italics] men, [italics]and[/italics] I [italics]am[/italics] your God, saith the Lord God.

ot/ezek/37.1: The hand of the Lord was upon me, and carried me out in the spirit of the Lord, and set me down in the midst of the valley which [italics]was[/italics] full of bones,
ot/ezek/37.2: And caused me to pass by them round about: and, behold, [italics]there were[/italics] very many in the open valley; and, lo, [italics]they were[/italics] very dry.
ot/ezek/37.3: And he said unto me, Son of man, can these bones live? And I answered, O Lord God, thou knowest.
ot/ezek/37.4: Again he said unto me, Prophesy upon these bones, and say unto them, O ye dry bones, hear the word of the Lord.
ot/ezek/37.5: Thus saith the Lord God unto these bones; Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live:
ot/ezek/37.6: And I will lay sinews upon you, and will bring up flesh upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and ye shall live; and ye shall know that I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord.
ot/ezek/37.7: So I prophesied as I was commanded: and as I prophesied, there was a noise, and behold a shaking, and the bones came together, bone to his bone.
ot/ezek/37.8: And when I beheld, lo, the sinews and the flesh came up upon them, and the skin covered them above: but [italics]there was[/italics] no breath in them.
ot/ezek/37.9: Then said he unto me, Prophesy unto the wind, prophesy, son of man, and say to the wind, Thus saith the Lord God; Come from the four winds, O breath, and breathe upon these slain, that they may live.
ot/ezek/37.10: So I prophesied as he commanded me, and the breath came into them, and they lived, and stood up upon their feet, an exceeding great army.
ot/ezek/37.11: ¶ Then he said unto me, Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel: behold, they say, Our bones are dried, and our hope is lost: we are cut off for our parts.
ot/ezek/37.12: Therefore prophesy and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, O my people, I will open your graves, and cause you to come up out of your graves, and bring you into the land of Israel.
ot/ezek/37.13: And ye shall know that I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord, when I have opened your graves, O my people, and brought you up out of your graves,
ot/ezek/37.14: And shall put my spirit in you, and ye shall live, and I shall place you in your own land: then shall ye know that I the Lord have spoken [italics]it,[/italics] and performed [italics]it,[/italics] saith the Lord.
ot/ezek/37.15: ¶ The word of the Lord came again unto me, saying,
ot/ezek/37.16: Moreover, thou son of man, take thee one stick, and write upon it, For Judah, and for the children of Israel his companions: then take another stick, and write upon it, For Joseph, the stick of Ephraim, and [italics]for[/italics] all the house of Israel his companions:
ot/ezek/37.17: And join them one to another into one stick; and they shall become one in thine hand.
ot/ezek/37.18: ¶ And when the children of thy people shall speak unto thee, saying, Wilt thou not shew us what thou [italics]meanest[/italics] by these?
ot/ezek/37.19: Say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will take the stick of Joseph, which [italics]is[/italics] in the hand of Ephraim, and the tribes of Israel his fellows, and will put them with him, [italics]even[/italics] with the stick of Judah, and make them one stick, and they shall be one in mine hand.
ot/ezek/37.20: ¶ And the sticks whereon thou writest shall be in thine hand before their eyes.
ot/ezek/37.21: And say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will take the children of Israel from among the heathen, whither they be gone, and will gather them on every side, and bring them into their own land:
ot/ezek/37.22: And I will make them one nation in the land upon the mountains of Israel; and one king shall be king to them all: and they shall be no more two nations, neither shall they be divided into two kingdoms any more at all:
ot/ezek/37.23: Neither shall they defile themselves any more with their idols, nor with their detestable things, nor with any of their transgressions: but I will save them out of all their dwellingplaces, wherein they have sinned, and will cleanse them: so shall they be my people, and I will be their God.
ot/ezek/37.24: And David my servant [italics]shall be[/italics] king over them; and they all shall have one shepherd: they shall also walk in my judgments, and observe my statutes, and do them.
ot/ezek/37.25: And they shall dwell in the land that I have given unto Jacob my servant, wherein your fathers have dwelt; and they shall dwell therein, [italics]even[/italics] they, and their children, and their children’s children for ever: and my servant David [italics]shall be[/italics] their prince for ever.
ot/ezek/37.26: Moreover I will make a covenant of peace with them; it shall be an everlasting covenant with them: and I will place them, and multiply them, and will set my sanctuary in the midst of them for evermore.
ot/ezek/37.27: My tabernacle also shall be with them: yea, I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
ot/ezek/37.28: And the heathen shall know that I the Lord do sanctify Israel, when my sanctuary shall be in the midst of them for evermore.

ot/ezek/43.1: Afterward he brought me to the gate, [italics]even[/italics] the gate that looketh toward the east:
ot/ezek/43.2: And, behold, the glory of the God of Israel came from the way of the east: and his voice [italics]was[/italics] like a noise of many waters: and the earth shined with his glory.
ot/ezek/43.3: And [italics]it was[/italics] according to the appearance of the vision which I saw, [italics]even[/italics] according to the vision that I saw when I came to destroy the city: and the visions [italics]were[/italics] like the vision that I saw by the river Chebar; and I fell upon my face.
ot/ezek/43.4: And the glory of the Lord came into the house by the way of the gate whose prospect [italics]is[/italics] toward the east.
ot/ezek/43.5: So the spirit took me up, and brought me into the inner court; and, behold, the glory of the Lord filled the house.
ot/ezek/43.6: And I heard [italics]him[/italics] speaking unto me out of the house; and the man stood by me.
ot/ezek/43.7: ¶ And he said unto me, Son of man, the place of my throne, and the place of the soles of my feet, where I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel for ever, and my holy name, shall the house of Israel no more defile, [italics]neither[/italics] they, nor their kings, by their whoredom, nor by the carcases of their kings in their high places.
ot/ezek/43.8: In their setting of their threshold by my thresholds, and their post by my posts, and the wall between me and them, they have even defiled my holy name by their abominations that they have committed: wherefore I have consumed them in mine anger.
ot/ezek/43.9: Now let them put away their whoredom, and the carcases of their kings, far from me, and I will dwell in the midst of them for ever.
ot/ezek/43.10: ¶ Thou son of man, shew the house to the house of Israel, that they may be ashamed of their iniquities: and let them measure the pattern.
ot/ezek/43.11: And if they be ashamed of all that they have done, shew them the form of the house, and the fashion thereof, and the goings out thereof, and the comings in thereof, and all the forms thereof, and all the ordinances thereof, and all the forms thereof, and all the laws thereof: and write [italics]it[/italics] in their sight, that they may keep the whole form thereof, and all the ordinances thereof, and do them.
ot/ezek/43.12: This [italics]is[/italics] the law of the house; Upon the top of the mountain the whole limit thereof round about [italics]shall be[/italics] most holy. Behold, this [italics]is[/italics] the law of the house.
ot/ezek/43.13: ¶ And these [italics]are[/italics] the measures of the altar after the cubits: The cubit [italics]is[/italics] a cubit and an hand breadth; even the bottom [italics]shall be[/italics] a cubit, and the breadth a cubit, and the border thereof by the edge thereof round about [italics]shall be[/italics] a span: and this [italics]shall be[/italics] the higher place of the altar.
ot/ezek/43.14: And from the bottom [italics]upon[/italics] the ground [italics]even[/italics] to the lower settle [italics]shall be[/italics] two cubits, and the breadth one cubit; and from the lesser settle [italics]even[/italics] to the greater settle [italics]shall be[/italics] four cubits, and the breadth [italics]one[/italics] cubit.
ot/ezek/43.15: So the altar [italics]shall be[/italics] four cubits; and from the altar and upward [italics]shall be[/italics] four horns.
ot/ezek/43.16: And the altar [italics]shall be[/italics] twelve [italics]cubits[/italics] long, twelve broad, square in the four squares thereof.
ot/ezek/43.17: And the settle [italics]shall be[/italics] fourteen [italics]cubits[/italics] long and fourteen broad in the four squares thereof; and the border about it [italics]shall be[/italics] half a cubit; and the bottom thereof [italics]shall be[/italics] a cubit about; and his stairs shall look toward the east.
ot/ezek/43.18: ¶ And he said unto me, Son of man, thus saith the Lord God; These [italics]are[/italics] the ordinances of the altar in the day when they shall make it, to offer burnt offerings thereon, and to sprinkle blood thereon.
ot/ezek/43.19: And thou shalt give to the priests the Levites that be of the seed of Zadok, which approach unto me, to minister unto me, saith the Lord God, a young bullock for a sin offering.
ot/ezek/43.20: And thou shalt take of the blood thereof, and put [italics]it[/italics] on the four horns of it, and on the four corners of the settle, and upon the border round about: thus shalt thou cleanse and purge it.
ot/ezek/43.21: Thou shalt take the bullock also of the sin offering, and he shall burn it in the appointed place of the house, without the sanctuary.
ot/ezek/43.22: And on the second day thou shalt offer a kid of the goats without blemish for a sin offering; and they shall cleanse the altar, as they did cleanse [italics]it[/italics] with the bullock.
ot/ezek/43.23: When thou hast made an end of cleansing [italics]it,[/italics] thou shalt offer a young bullock without blemish, and a ram out of the flock without blemish.
ot/ezek/43.24: And thou shalt offer them before the Lord, and the priests shall cast salt upon them, and they shall offer them up [italics]for[/italics] a burnt offering unto the Lord.
ot/ezek/43.25: Seven days shalt thou prepare every day a goat [italics]for[/italics] a sin offering: they shall also prepare a young bullock, and a ram out of the flock, without blemish.
ot/ezek/43.26: Seven days shall they purge the altar and purify it; and they shall consecrate themselves.
ot/ezek/43.27: And when these days are expired, it shall be, [italics]that[/italics] upon the eighth day, and [italics]so[/italics] forward, the priests shall make your burnt offerings upon the altar, and your peace offerings; and I will accept you, saith the Lord God.

ot/gen/1.1: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
ot/gen/1.2: And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness [italics]was[/italics] upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.
ot/gen/1.3: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.
ot/gen/1.4: And God saw the light, that [italics]it was[/italics] good: and God divided the light from the darkness.
ot/gen/1.5: And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day.
ot/gen/1.6: ¶ And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.
ot/gen/1.7: And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which [italics]were[/italics] under the firmament from the waters which [italics]were[/italics] above the firmament: and it was so.
ot/gen/1.8: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.
ot/gen/1.9: ¶ And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry [italics]land[/italics] appear: and it was so.
ot/gen/1.10: And God called the dry [italics]land[/italics] Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that [italics]it was[/italics] good.
ot/gen/1.11: And God said, Let the earth bring forth grass, the herb yielding seed, [italics]and[/italics] the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind, whose seed [italics]is[/italics] in itself, upon the earth: and it was so.
ot/gen/1.12: And the earth brought forth grass, [italics]and[/italics] herb yielding seed after his kind, and the tree yielding fruit, whose seed [italics]was[/italics] in itself, after his kind: and God saw that [italics]it was[/italics] good.
ot/gen/1.13: And the evening and the morning were the third day.
ot/gen/1.14: ¶ And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years:
ot/gen/1.15: And let them be for lights in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth: and it was so.
ot/gen/1.16: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: [italics]he made[/italics] the stars also.
ot/gen/1.17: And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,
ot/gen/1.18: And to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness: and God saw that [italics]it was[/italics] good.
ot/gen/1.19: And the evening and the morning were the fourth day.
ot/gen/1.20: And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl [italics]that[/italics] may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.
ot/gen/1.21: And God created great whales, and every living creature that moveth, which the waters brought forth abundantly, after their kind, and every winged fowl after his kind: and God saw that [italics]it was[/italics] good.
ot/gen/1.22: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
ot/gen/1.23: And the evening and the morning were the fifth day.
ot/gen/1.24: ¶ And God said, Let the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kind: and it was so.
ot/gen/1.25: And God made the beast of the earth after his kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind: and God saw that [italics]it was[/italics] good.
ot/gen/1.26: ¶ And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.
ot/gen/1.27: So God created man in his [italics]own[/italics] image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.
ot/gen/1.28: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
ot/gen/1.29: ¶ And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which [italics]is[/italics] upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which [italics]is[/italics] the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat.
ot/gen/1.30: And to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein [italics]there is[/italics] life, [italics]I have [/italics]given every green herb for meat: and it was so.
ot/gen/1.31: And God saw every thing that he had made, and, behold, [italics]it was[/italics] very good. And the evening and the morning were the sixth day.

ot/gen/14.1: And it came to pass in the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of nations;
ot/gen/14.2: [italics]That these[/italics] made war with Bera king of Sodom, and with Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, and Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, which is Zoar.
ot/gen/14.3: All these were joined together in the vale of Siddim, which is the salt sea.
ot/gen/14.4: Twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and in the thirteenth year they rebelled.
ot/gen/14.5: And in the fourteenth year came Chedorlaomer, and the kings that [italics]were[/italics] with him, and smote the Rephaims in Ashteroth Karnaim, and the Zuzims in Ham, and the Emims in Shaveh Kiriathaim,
ot/gen/14.6: And the Horites in their mount Seir, unto El-paran, which [italics]is[/italics] by the wilderness.
ot/gen/14.7: And they returned, and came to En-mishpat, which [italics]is[/italics] Kadesh, and smote all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites, that dwelt in Hazezon-tamar.
ot/gen/14.8: And there went out the king of Sodom, and the king of Gomorrah, and the king of Admah, and the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (the same [italics]is[/italics] Zoar;) and they joined battle with them in the vale of Siddim;
ot/gen/14.9: With Chedorlaomer the king of Elam, and with Tidal king of nations, and Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar; four kings with five.
ot/gen/14.10: And the vale of Siddim [italics]was full of[/italics] slimepits; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, and fell there; and they that remained fled to the mountain.
ot/gen/14.11: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their victuals, and went their way.
ot/gen/14.12: And they took Lot, Abram’s brother’s son, who dwelt in Sodom, and his goods, and departed.
ot/gen/14.13: ¶ And there came one that had escaped, and told Abram the Hebrew; for he dwelt in the plain of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol, and brother of Aner: and these [italics]were[/italics] confederate with Abram.
ot/gen/14.14: And when Abram heard that his brother was taken captive, he armed his trained [italics]servants,[/italics] born in his own house, three hundred and eighteen, and pursued [italics]them[/italics] unto Dan.
ot/gen/14.15: And he divided himself against them, he and his servants, by night, and smote them, and pursued them unto Hobah, which [italics]is[/italics] on the left hand of Damascus.
ot/gen/14.16: And he brought back all the goods, and also brought again his brother Lot, and his goods, and the women also, and the people.
ot/gen/14.17: ¶ And the king of Sodom went out to meet him after his return from the slaughter of Chedorlaomer, and of the kings that [italics]were[/italics] with him, at the valley of Shaveh, which [italics]is[/italics] the king’s dale.
ot/gen/14.18: And Melchizedek king of Salem brought forth bread and wine: and he [italics]was[/italics] the priest of the most high God.
ot/gen/14.19: And he blessed him, and said, Blessed [italics]be[/italics] Abram of the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth:
ot/gen/14.20: And blessed be the most high God, which hath delivered thine enemies into thy hand. And he gave him tithes of all.
ot/gen/14.21: And the king of Sodom said unto Abram, Give me the persons, and take the goods to thyself.
ot/gen/14.22: And Abram said to the king of Sodom, I have lift up mine hand unto the Lord, the most high God, the possessor of heaven and earth,
ot/gen/14.23: That I will not [italics]take[/italics] from a thread even to a shoelatchet, and that I will not take any thing that [italics]is[/italics] thine, lest thou shouldest say, I have made Abram rich:
ot/gen/14.24: Save only that which the young men have eaten, and the portion of the men which went with me, Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre; let them take their portion.

ot/gen/17.1: And when Abram was ninety years old and nine, the Lord appeared to Abram, and said unto him, I [italics]am[/italics] the Almighty God; walk before me, and be thou perfect.
ot/gen/17.2: And I will make my covenant between me and thee, and will multiply thee exceedingly.
ot/gen/17.3: And Abram fell on his face: and God talked with him, saying,
ot/gen/17.4: As for me, behold, my covenant [italics]is[/italics] with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.
ot/gen/17.5: Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but thy name shall be Abraham; for a father of many nations have I made thee.
ot/gen/17.6: And I will make thee exceeding fruitful, and I will make nations of thee, and kings shall come out of thee.
ot/gen/17.7: And I will establish my covenant between me and thee and thy seed after thee in their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be a God unto thee, and to thy seed after thee.
ot/gen/17.8: And I will give unto thee, and to thy seed after thee, the land wherein thou art a stranger, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession; and I will be their God.
ot/gen/17.9: ¶ And God said unto Abraham, Thou shalt keep my covenant therefore, thou, and thy seed after thee in their generations.
ot/gen/17.10: This [italics]is[/italics] my covenant, which ye shall keep, between me and you and thy seed after thee; Every man child among you shall be circumcised.
ot/gen/17.11: And ye shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin; and it shall be a token of the covenant betwixt me and you.
ot/gen/17.12: And he that is eight days old shall be circumcised among you, every man child in your generations, he that is born in the house, or bought with money of any stranger, which [italics]is[/italics] not of thy seed.
ot/gen/17.13: He that is born in thy house, and he that is bought with thy money, must needs be circumcised: and my covenant shall be in your flesh for an everlasting covenant.
ot/gen/17.14: And the uncircumcised man child whose flesh of his foreskin is not circumcised, that soul shall be cut off from his people; he hath broken my covenant.
ot/gen/17.15: ¶ And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah [italics]shall[/italics] her name [italics]be.[/italics]
ot/gen/17.16: And I will bless her, and give thee a son also of her: yea, I will bless her, and she shall be [italics]a [/italics][italics]mother[/italics] of nations; kings of people shall be of her.
ot/gen/17.17: Then Abraham fell upon his face, and laughed, and said in his heart, Shall [italics]a child[/italics] be born unto him that is an hundred years old? and shall Sarah, that is ninety years old, bear?
ot/gen/17.18: And Abraham said unto God, O that Ishmael might live before thee!
ot/gen/17.19: And God said, Sarah thy wife shall bear thee a son indeed; and thou shalt call his name Isaac: and I will establish my covenant with him for an everlasting covenant, [italics]and[/italics] with his seed after him.
ot/gen/17.20: And as for Ishmael, I have heard thee: Behold, I have blessed him, and will make him fruitful, and will multiply him exceedingly; twelve princes shall he beget, and I will make him a great nation.
ot/gen/17.21: But my covenant will I establish with Isaac, which Sarah shall bear unto thee at this set time in the next year.
ot/gen/17.22: And he left off talking with him, and God went up from Abraham.
ot/gen/17.23: ¶ And Abraham took Ishmael his son, and all that were born in his house, and all that were bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham’s house; and circumcised the flesh of their foreskin in the selfsame day, as God had said unto him.
ot/gen/17.24: And Abraham [italics]was[/italics] ninety years old and nine, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
ot/gen/17.25: And Ishmael his son [italics]was[/italics] thirteen years old, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
ot/gen/17.26: In the selfsame day was Abraham circumcised, and Ishmael his son.
ot/gen/17.27: And all the men of his house, born in the house, and bought with money of the stranger, were circumcised with him.

ot/gen/2.1: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them.
ot/gen/2.2: And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made.
ot/gen/2.3: And God blessed the seventh day, and sanctified it: because that in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made.
ot/gen/2.4: ¶ These [italics]are[/italics] the generations of the heavens and of the earth when they were created, in the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens,
ot/gen/2.5: And every plant of the field before it was in the earth, and every herb of the field before it grew: for the Lord God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and [italics]there was[/italics] not a man to till the ground.
ot/gen/2.6: But there went up a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground.
ot/gen/2.7: And the Lord God formed man [italics]of[/italics] the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
ot/gen/2.8: ¶ And the Lord God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
ot/gen/2.9: And out of the ground made the Lord God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.
ot/gen/2.10: And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became into four heads.
ot/gen/2.11: The name of the first [italics]is[/italics] Pison: that [italics]is[/italics] it which compasseth the whole land of Havilah, where [italics]there is[/italics] gold;
ot/gen/2.12: And the gold of that land [italics]is[/italics] good: there [italics]is[/italics] bdellium and the onyx stone.
ot/gen/2.13: And the name of the second river [italics]is[/italics] Gihon: the same [italics]is[/italics] it that compasseth the whole land of Ethiopia.
ot/gen/2.14: And the name of the third river [italics]is[/italics] Hiddekel: that [italics]is[/italics] it which goeth toward the east of Assyria. And the fourth river [italics]is[/italics] Euphrates.
ot/gen/2.15: And the Lord God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.
ot/gen/2.16: And the Lord God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat:
ot/gen/2.17: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.
ot/gen/2.18: ¶ And the Lord God said, [italics]It is[/italics] not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.
ot/gen/2.19: And out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought [italics]them[/italics] unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that [italics]was[/italics] the name thereof.
ot/gen/2.20: And Adam gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found an help meet for him.
ot/gen/2.21: And the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept: and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof;
ot/gen/2.22: And the rib, which the Lord God had taken from man, made he a woman, and brought her unto the man.
ot/gen/2.23: And Adam said, This [italics]is[/italics] now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.
ot/gen/2.24: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
ot/gen/2.25: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and were not ashamed.

ot/gen/22.1: And it came to pass after these things, that God did tempt Abraham, and said unto him, Abraham: and he said, Behold, [italics]here[/italics] I [italics]am.[/italics]
ot/gen/22.2: And he said, Take now thy son, thine only [italics]son[/italics] Isaac, whom thou lovest, and get thee into the land of Moriah; and offer him there for a burnt offering upon one of the mountains which I will tell thee of.
ot/gen/22.3: ¶ And Abraham rose up early in the morning, and saddled his ass, and took two of his young men with him, and Isaac his son, and clave the wood for the burnt offering, and rose up, and went unto the place of which God had told him.
ot/gen/22.4: Then on the third day Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw the place afar off.
ot/gen/22.5: And Abraham said unto his young men, Abide ye here with the ass; and I and the lad will go yonder and worship, and come again to you.
ot/gen/22.6: And Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering, and laid [italics]it[/italics] upon Isaac his son; and he took the fire in his hand, and a knife; and they went both of them together.
ot/gen/22.7: And Isaac spake unto Abraham his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here [italics]am[/italics] I, my son. And he said, Behold the fire and the wood: but where [italics]is[/italics] the lamb for a burnt offering?
ot/gen/22.8: And Abraham said, My son, God will provide himself a lamb for a burnt offering: so they went both of them together.
ot/gen/22.9: And they came to the place which God had told him of; and Abraham built an altar there, and laid the wood in order, and bound Isaac his son, and laid him on the altar upon the wood.
ot/gen/22.10: And Abraham stretched forth his hand, and took the knife to slay his son.
ot/gen/22.11: And the angel of the Lord called unto him out of heaven, and said, Abraham, Abraham: and he said, Here [italics]am[/italics] I.
ot/gen/22.12: And he said, Lay not thine hand upon the lad, neither do thou any thing unto him: for now I know that thou fearest God, seeing thou hast not withheld thy son, thine only [italics]son[/italics] from me.
ot/gen/22.13: And Abraham lifted up his eyes, and looked, and behold behind [italics]him[/italics] a ram caught in a thicket by his horns: and Abraham went and took the ram, and offered him up for a burnt offering in the stead of his son.
ot/gen/22.14: And Abraham called the name of that place Jehovah-jireh: as it is said [italics]to[/italics] this day, In the mount of the Lord it shall be seen.
ot/gen/22.15: ¶ And the angel of the Lord called unto Abraham out of heaven the second time,
ot/gen/22.16: And said, By myself have I sworn, saith the Lord, for because thou hast done this thing, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only [italics]son:[/italics]
ot/gen/22.17: That in blessing I will bless thee, and in multiplying I will multiply thy seed as the stars of the heaven, and as the sand which [italics]is[/italics] upon the sea shore; and thy seed shall possess the gate of his enemies;
ot/gen/22.18: And in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed; because thou hast obeyed my voice.
ot/gen/22.19: So Abraham returned unto his young men, and they rose up and went together to Beer-sheba; and Abraham dwelt at Beer-sheba.
ot/gen/22.20: ¶ And it came to pass after these things, that it was told Abraham, saying, Behold, Milcah, she hath also born children unto thy brother Nahor;
ot/gen/22.21: Huz his firstborn, and Buz his brother, and Kemuel the father of Aram,
ot/gen/22.22: And Chesed, and Hazo, and Pildash, and Jidlaph, and Bethuel.
ot/gen/22.23: And Bethuel begat Rebekah: these eight Milcah did bear to Nahor, Abraham’s brother.
ot/gen/22.24: And his concubine, whose name [italics]was[/italics] Reumah, she bare also Tebah, and Gaham, and Thahash, and Maachah.

ot/gen/3.1: Now the serpent was more subtil than any beast of the field which the Lord God had made. And he said unto the woman, Yea, hath God said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?
ot/gen/3.2: And the woman said unto the serpent, We may eat of the fruit of the trees of the garden:
ot/gen/3.3: But of the fruit of the tree which [italics]is[/italics] in the midst of the garden, God hath said, Ye shall not eat of it, neither shall ye touch it, lest ye die.
ot/gen/3.4: And the serpent said unto the woman, Ye shall not surely die:
ot/gen/3.5: For God doth know that in the day ye eat thereof, then your eyes shall be opened, and ye shall be as gods, knowing good and evil.
ot/gen/3.6: And when the woman saw that the tree [italics]was[/italics] good for food, and that it [italics]was[/italics] pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make [italics]one[/italics] wise, she took of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and gave also unto her husband with her; and he did eat.
ot/gen/3.7: And the eyes of them both were opened, and they knew that they [italics]were[/italics] naked; and they sewed fig leaves together, and made themselves aprons.
ot/gen/3.8: And they heard the voice of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day: and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the Lord God amongst the trees of the garden.
ot/gen/3.9: And the Lord God called unto Adam, and said unto him, Where [italics]art[/italics] thou?
ot/gen/3.10: And he said, I heard thy voice in the garden, and I was afraid, because I [italics]was[/italics] naked; and I hid myself.
ot/gen/3.11: And he said, Who told thee that thou [italics]wast[/italics] naked? Hast thou eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat?
ot/gen/3.12: And the man said, The woman whom thou gavest [italics]to be[/italics] with me, she gave me of the tree, and I did eat.
ot/gen/3.13: And the Lord God said unto the woman, What [italics]is[/italics] this [italics]that[/italics] thou hast done? And the woman said, The serpent beguiled me, and I did eat.
ot/gen/3.14: And the Lord God said unto the serpent, Because thou hast done this, thou [italics]art[/italics] cursed above all cattle, and above every beast of the field; upon thy belly shalt thou go, and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life:
ot/gen/3.15: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
ot/gen/3.16: Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire [italics]shall be[/italics] to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.
ot/gen/3.17: And unto Adam he said, Because thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree, of which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it: cursed [italics]is[/italics] the ground for thy sake; in sorrow shalt thou eat [italics]of[/italics] it all the days of thy life;
ot/gen/3.18: Thorns also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee; and thou shalt eat the herb of the field;
ot/gen/3.19: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou [italics]art,[/italics] and unto dust shalt thou return.
ot/gen/3.20: And Adam called his wife’s name Eve; because she was the mother of all living.
ot/gen/3.21: Unto Adam also and to his wife did the Lord God make coats of skins, and clothed them.
ot/gen/3.22: ¶ And the Lord God said, Behold, the man is become as one of us, to know good and evil: and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever:
ot/gen/3.23: Therefore the Lord God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from whence he was taken.
ot/gen/3.24: So he drove out the man; and he placed at the east of the garden of Eden Cherubims, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the way of the tree of life.

ot/gen/32.1: And Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.
ot/gen/32.2: And when Jacob saw them, he said, This [italics]is[/italics] God’s host: and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.
ot/gen/32.3: And Jacob sent messengers before him to Esau his brother unto the land of Seir, the country of Edom.
ot/gen/32.4: And he commanded them, saying, Thus shall ye speak unto my lord Esau; Thy servant Jacob saith thus, I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed there until now:
ot/gen/32.5: And I have oxen, and asses, flocks, and menservants, and womenservants: and I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find grace in thy sight.
ot/gen/32.6: ¶ And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, We came to thy brother Esau, and also he cometh to meet thee, and four hundred men with him.
ot/gen/32.7: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed: and he divided the people that [italics]was[/italics] with him, and the flocks, and herds, and the camels, into two bands;
ot/gen/32.8: And said, If Esau come to the one company, and smite it, then the other company which is left shall escape.
ot/gen/32.9: ¶ And Jacob said, O God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac, the Lord which saidst unto me, Return unto thy country, and to thy kindred, and I will deal well with thee:
ot/gen/32.10: I am not worthy of the least of all the mercies, and of all the truth, which thou hast shewed unto thy servant; for with my staff I passed over this Jordan; and now I am become two bands.
ot/gen/32.11: Deliver me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I fear him, lest he will come and smite me, [italics]and[/italics] the mother with the children.
ot/gen/32.12: And thou saidst, I will surely do thee good, and make thy seed as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.
ot/gen/32.13: ¶ And he lodged there that same night; and took of that which came to his hand a present for Esau his brother;
ot/gen/32.14: Two hundred she goats, and twenty he goats, two hundred ewes, and twenty rams,
ot/gen/32.15: Thirty milch camels with their colts, forty kine, and ten bulls, twenty she asses, and ten foals.
ot/gen/32.16: And he delivered [italics]them[/italics] into the hand of his servants, every drove by themselves; and said unto his servants, Pass over before me, and put a space betwixt drove and drove.
ot/gen/32.17: And he commanded the foremost, saying, When Esau my brother meeteth thee, and asketh thee, saying, Whose [italics]art[/italics] thou? and whither goest thou? and whose [italics]are[/italics] these before thee?
ot/gen/32.18: Then thou shalt say, [italics]They be[/italics] thy servant Jacob’s; it [italics]is[/italics] a present sent unto my lord Esau: and, behold, also he [italics]is[/italics] behind us.
ot/gen/32.19: And so commanded he the second, and the third, and all that followed the droves, saying, On this manner shall ye speak unto Esau, when ye find him.
ot/gen/32.20: And say ye moreover, Behold, thy servant Jacob [italics]is[/italics] behind us. For he said, I will appease him with the present that goeth before me, and afterward I will see his face; peradventure he will accept of me.
ot/gen/32.21: So went the present over before him: and himself lodged that night in the company.
ot/gen/32.22: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two womenservants, and his eleven sons, and passed over the ford Jabbok.
ot/gen/32.23: And he took them, and sent them over the brook, and sent over that he had.
ot/gen/32.24: ¶ And Jacob was left alone; and there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of the day.
ot/gen/32.25: And when he saw that he prevailed not against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh; and the hollow of Jacob’s thigh was out of joint, as he wrestled with him.
ot/gen/32.26: And he said, Let me go, for the day breaketh. And he said, I will not let thee go, except thou bless me.
ot/gen/32.27: And he said unto him, What [italics]is[/italics] thy name? And he said, Jacob.
ot/gen/32.28: And he said, Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed.
ot/gen/32.29: And Jacob asked [italics]him,[/italics] and said, Tell [italics]me,[/italics] I pray thee, thy name. And he said, Wherefore [italics]is[/italics] it [italics]that[/italics] thou dost ask after my name? And he blessed him there.
ot/gen/32.30: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel: for I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.
ot/gen/32.31: And as he passed over Penuel the sun rose upon him, and he halted upon his thigh.
ot/gen/32.32: Therefore the children of Israel eat not [italics]of[/italics] the sinew which shrank, which [italics]is[/italics] upon the hollow of the thigh, unto this day: because he touched the hollow of Jacob’s thigh in the sinew that shrank.

ot/hosea/11.1: When Israel [italics]was[/italics] a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.
ot/hosea/11.2: [italics]As[/italics] they called them, so they went from them: they sacrificed unto Baalim, and burned incense to graven images.
ot/hosea/11.3: I taught Ephraim also to go, taking them by their arms; but they knew not that I healed them.
ot/hosea/11.4: I drew them with cords of a man, with bands of love: and I was to them as they that take off the yoke on their jaws, and I laid meat unto them.
ot/hosea/11.5: ¶ He shall not return into the land of Egypt, but the Assyrian shall be his king, because they refused to return.
ot/hosea/11.6: And the sword shall abide on his cities, and shall consume his branches, and devour [italics]them,[/italics] because of their own counsels.
ot/hosea/11.7: And my people are bent to backsliding from me: though they called them to the most High, none at all would exalt [italics]him.[/italics]
ot/hosea/11.8: How shall I give thee up, Ephraim? [italics]how[/italics] shall I deliver thee, Israel? how shall I make thee as Admah? [italics]how[/italics] shall I set thee as Zeboim? mine heart is turned within me, my repentings are kindled together.
ot/hosea/11.9: I will not execute the fierceness of mine anger, I will not return to destroy Ephraim: for I [italics]am[/italics] God, and not man; the Holy One in the midst of thee: and I will not enter into the city.
ot/hosea/11.10: They shall walk after the Lord: he shall roar like a lion: when he shall roar, then the children shall tremble from the west.
ot/hosea/11.11: They shall tremble as a bird out of Egypt, and as a dove out of the land of Assyria: and I will place them in their houses, saith the Lord.
ot/hosea/11.12: Ephraim compasseth me about with lies, and the house of Israel with deceit: but Judah yet ruleth with God, and is faithful with the saints.

ot/hosea/12.1: Ephraim feedeth on wind, and followeth after the east wind: he daily increaseth lies and desolation; and they do make a covenant with the Assyrians, and oil is carried into Egypt.
ot/hosea/12.2: The Lord hath also a controversy with Judah, and will punish Jacob according to his ways; according to his doings will he recompense him.
ot/hosea/12.3: ¶ He took his brother by the heel in the womb, and by his strength he had power with God:
ot/hosea/12.4: Yea, he had power over the angel, and prevailed: he wept, and made supplication unto him: he found him [italics]in[/italics] Beth-el, and there he spake with us;
ot/hosea/12.5: Even the Lord God of hosts; the Lord [italics]is[/italics] his memorial.
ot/hosea/12.6: Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait on thy God continually.
ot/hosea/12.7: ¶ [italics]He is[/italics] a merchant, the balances of deceit [italics]are[/italics] in his hand: he loveth to oppress.
ot/hosea/12.8: And Ephraim said, Yet I am become rich, I have found me out substance: [italics]in[/italics] all my labours they shall find none iniquity in me that [italics]were[/italics] sin.
ot/hosea/12.9: And I [italics]that am[/italics] the Lord thy God from the land of Egypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast.
ot/hosea/12.10: I have also spoken by the prophets, and I have multiplied visions, and used similitudes, by the ministry of the prophets.
ot/hosea/12.11: [italics]Is there[/italics] iniquity [italics]in[/italics] Gilead? surely they are vanity: they sacrifice bullocks in Gilgal; yea, their altars [italics]are[/italics] as heaps in the furrows of the fields.
ot/hosea/12.12: And Jacob fled into the country of Syria, and Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he kept [italics]sheep.[/italics]
ot/hosea/12.13: And by a prophet the Lord brought Israel out of Egypt, and by a prophet was he preserved.
ot/hosea/12.14: Ephraim provoked [italics]him[/italics] to anger most bitterly: therefore shall he leave his blood upon him, and his reproach shall his Lord return unto him.

ot/isa/11.1: And there shall come forth a rod out of the stem of Jesse, and a Branch shall grow out of his roots:
ot/isa/11.2: And the spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him, the spirit of wisdom and understanding, the spirit of counsel and might, the spirit of knowledge and of the fear of the Lord;
ot/isa/11.3: And shall make him of quick understanding in the fear of the Lord: and he shall not judge after the sight of his eyes, neither reprove after the hearing of his ears:
ot/isa/11.4: But with righteousness shall he judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth: and he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips shall he slay the wicked.
ot/isa/11.5: And righteousness shall be the girdle of his loins, and faithfulness the girdle of his reins.
ot/isa/11.6: The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the kid; and the calf and the young lion and the fatling together; and a little child shall lead them.
ot/isa/11.7: And the cow and the bear shall feed; their young ones shall lie down together: and the lion shall eat straw like the ox.
ot/isa/11.8: And the sucking child shall play on the hole of the asp, and the weaned child shall put his hand on the cockatrice’ den.
ot/isa/11.9: They shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain: for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the Lord, as the waters cover the sea.
ot/isa/11.10: ¶ And in that day there shall be a root of Jesse, which shall stand for an ensign of the people; to it shall the Gentiles seek: and his rest shall be glorious.
ot/isa/11.11: And it shall come to pass in that day, [italics]that[/italics] the Lord shall set his hand again the second time to recover the remnant of his people, which shall be left, from Assyria, and from Egypt, and from Pathros, and from Cush, and from Elam, and from Shinar, and from Hamath, and from the islands of the sea.
ot/isa/11.12: And he shall set up an ensign for the nations, and shall assemble the outcasts of Israel, and gather together the dispersed of Judah from the four corners of the earth.
ot/isa/11.13: The envy also of Ephraim shall depart, and the adversaries of Judah shall be cut off: Ephraim shall not envy Judah, and Judah shall not vex Ephraim.
ot/isa/11.14: But they shall fly upon the shoulders of the Philistines toward the west; they shall spoil them of the east together: they shall lay their hand upon Edom and Moab; and the children of Ammon shall obey them.
ot/isa/11.15: And the Lord shall utterly destroy the tongue of the Egyptian sea; and with his mighty wind shall he shake his hand over the river, and shall smite it in the seven streams, and make [italics]men[/italics] go over dryshod.
ot/isa/11.16: And there shall be an highway for the remnant of his people, which shall be left, from Assyria; like as it was to Israel in the day that he came up out of the land of Egypt.

ot/isa/14.1: For the Lord will have mercy on Jacob, and will yet choose Israel, and set them in their own land: and the strangers shall be joined with them, and they shall cleave to the house of Jacob.
ot/isa/14.2: And the people shall take them, and bring them to their place: and the house of Israel shall possess them in the land of the Lord for servants and handmaids: and they shall take them captives, whose captives they were; and they shall rule over their oppressors.
ot/isa/14.3: And it shall come to pass in the day that the Lord shall give thee rest from thy sorrow, and from thy fear, and from the hard bondage wherein thou wast made to serve,
ot/isa/14.4: ¶ That thou shalt take up this proverb against the king of Babylon, and say, How hath the oppressor ceased! the golden city ceased!
ot/isa/14.5: The Lord hath broken the staff of the wicked, [italics]and[/italics] the sceptre of the rulers.
ot/isa/14.6: He who smote the people in wrath with a continual stroke, he that ruled the nations in anger, is persecuted, [italics]and[/italics] none hindereth.
ot/isa/14.7: The whole earth is at rest, [italics]and[/italics] is quiet: they break forth into singing.
ot/isa/14.8: Yea, the fir trees rejoice at thee, [italics]and[/italics] the cedars of Lebanon, [italics]saying,[/italics] Since thou art laid down, no feller is come up against us.
ot/isa/14.9: Hell from beneath is moved for thee to meet [italics]thee[/italics] at thy coming: it stirreth up the dead for thee, [italics]even[/italics] all the chief ones of the earth; it hath raised up from their thrones all the kings of the nations.
ot/isa/14.10: All they shall speak and say unto thee, Art thou also become weak as we? art thou become like unto us?
ot/isa/14.11: Thy pomp is brought down to the grave, [italics]and[/italics] the noise of thy viols: the worm is spread under thee, and the worms cover thee.
ot/isa/14.12: How art thou fallen from heaven, O Lucifer, son of the morning! [italics]how[/italics] art thou cut down to the ground, which didst weaken the nations!
ot/isa/14.13: For thou hast said in thine heart, I will ascend into heaven, I will exalt my throne above the stars of God: I will sit also upon the mount of the congregation, in the sides of the north:
ot/isa/14.14: I will ascend above the heights of the clouds; I will be like the most High.
ot/isa/14.15: Yet thou shalt be brought down to hell, to the sides of the pit.
ot/isa/14.16: They that see thee shall narrowly look upon thee, [italics]and[/italics] consider thee, [italics]saying, Is[/italics] this the man that made the earth to tremble, that did shake kingdoms;
ot/isa/14.17: [italics]That[/italics] made the world as a wilderness, and destroyed the cities thereof; [italics]that[/italics] opened not the house of his prisoners?
ot/isa/14.18: All the kings of the nations, [italics]even[/italics] all of them, lie in glory, every one in his own house.
ot/isa/14.19: But thou art cast out of thy grave like an abominable branch, [italics]and as[/italics] the raiment of those that are slain, thrust through with a sword, that go down to the stones of the pit; as a carcase trodden under feet.
ot/isa/14.20: Thou shalt not be joined with them in burial, because thou hast destroyed thy land, [italics]and[/italics] slain thy people: the seed of evildoers shall never be renowned.
ot/isa/14.21: Prepare slaughter for his children for the iniquity of their fathers; that they do not rise, nor possess the land, nor fill the face of the world with cities.
ot/isa/14.22: For I will rise up against them, saith the Lord of hosts, and cut off from Babylon the name, and remnant, and son, and nephew, saith the Lord.
ot/isa/14.23: I will also make it a possession for the bittern, and pools of water: and I will sweep it with the besom of destruction, saith the Lord of hosts.
ot/isa/14.24: ¶ The Lord of hosts hath sworn, saying, Surely as I have thought, so shall it come to pass; and as I have purposed, [italics]so[/italics] shall it stand:
ot/isa/14.25: That I will break the Assyrian in my land, and upon my mountains tread him under foot: then shall his yoke depart from off them, and his burden depart from off their shoulders.
ot/isa/14.26: This [italics]is[/italics] the purpose that is purposed upon the whole earth: and this [italics]is[/italics] the hand that is stretched out upon all the nations.
ot/isa/14.27: For the Lord of hosts hath purposed, and who shall disannul [italics]it?[/italics] and his hand [italics]is[/italics] stretched out, and who shall turn it back?
ot/isa/14.28: In the year that king Ahaz died was this burden.
ot/isa/14.29: ¶ Rejoice not thou, whole Palestina, because the rod of him that smote thee is broken: for out of the serpent’s root shall come forth a cockatrice, and his fruit [italics]shall be[/italics] a fiery flying serpent.
ot/isa/14.30: And the firstborn of the poor shall feed, and the needy shall lie down in safety: and I will kill thy root with famine, and he shall slay thy remnant.
ot/isa/14.31: Howl, O gate; cry, O city; thou, whole Palestina, [italics]art[/italics] dissolved: for there shall come from the north a smoke, and none [italics]shall be[/italics] alone in his appointed times.
ot/isa/14.32: What shall [italics]one[/italics] then answer the messengers of the nation? That the Lord hath founded Zion, and the poor of his people shall trust in it.

ot/isa/2.1: The word that Isaiah the son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
ot/isa/2.2: And it shall come to pass in the last days, [italics]that[/italics] the mountain of the Lord’s house shall be established in the top of the mountains, and shall be exalted above the hills; and all nations shall flow unto it.
ot/isa/2.3: And many people shall go and say, Come ye, and let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, to the house of the God of Jacob; and he will teach us of his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for out of Zion shall go forth the law, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
ot/isa/2.4: And he shall judge among the nations, and shall rebuke many people: and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks: nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.
ot/isa/2.5: O house of Jacob, come ye, and let us walk in the light of the Lord.
ot/isa/2.6: ¶ Therefore thou hast forsaken thy people the house of Jacob, because they be replenished from the east, and [italics]are[/italics] soothsayers like the Philistines, and they please themselves in the children of strangers.
ot/isa/2.7: Their land also is full of silver and gold, neither [italics]is there any[/italics] end of their treasures; their land is also full of horses, neither [italics]is there any[/italics] end of their chariots:
ot/isa/2.8: Their land also is full of idols; they worship the work of their own hands, that which their own fingers have made:
ot/isa/2.9: And the mean man boweth down, and the great man humbleth himself: therefore forgive them not.
ot/isa/2.10: ¶ Enter into the rock, and hide thee in the dust, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of his majesty.
ot/isa/2.11: The lofty looks of man shall be humbled, and the haughtiness of men shall be bowed down, and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day.
ot/isa/2.12: For the day of the Lord of hosts [italics]shall be[/italics] upon every [italics]one that is[/italics] proud and lofty, and upon every [italics]one that is[/italics] lifted up; and he shall be brought low:
ot/isa/2.13: And upon all the cedars of Lebanon, [italics]that are[/italics] high and lifted up, and upon all the oaks of Bashan,
ot/isa/2.14: And upon all the high mountains, and upon all the hills [italics]that are[/italics] lifted up,
ot/isa/2.15: And upon every high tower, and upon every fenced wall,
ot/isa/2.16: And upon all the ships of Tarshish, and upon all pleasant pictures.
ot/isa/2.17: And the loftiness of man shall be bowed down, and the haughtiness of men shall be made low: and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day.
ot/isa/2.18: And the idols he shall utterly abolish.
ot/isa/2.19: And they shall go into the holes of the rocks, and into the caves of the earth, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of his majesty, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
ot/isa/2.20: In that day a man shall cast his idols of silver, and his idols of gold, which they made [italics]each one[/italics] for himself to worship, to the moles and to the bats;
ot/isa/2.21: To go into the clefts of the rocks, and into the tops of the ragged rocks, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of his majesty, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
ot/isa/2.22: Cease ye from man, whose breath [italics]is[/italics] in his nostrils: for wherein is he to be accounted of?

ot/isa/32.1: Behold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule in judgment.
ot/isa/32.2: And a man shall be as an hiding place from the wind, and a covert from the tempest; as rivers of water in a dry place, as the shadow of a great rock in a weary land.
ot/isa/32.3: And the eyes of them that see shall not be dim, and the ears of them that hear shall hearken.
ot/isa/32.4: The heart also of the rash shall understand knowledge, and the tongue of the stammerers shall be ready to speak plainly.
ot/isa/32.5: The vile person shall be no more called liberal, nor the churl said [italics]to be[/italics] bountiful.
ot/isa/32.6: For the vile person will speak villany, and his heart will work iniquity, to practise hypocrisy, and to utter error against the Lord, to make empty the soul of the hungry, and he will cause the drink of the thirsty to fail.
ot/isa/32.7: The instruments also of the churl [italics]are[/italics] evil: he deviseth wicked devices to destroy the poor with lying words, even when the needy speaketh right.
ot/isa/32.8: But the liberal deviseth liberal things; and by liberal things shall he stand.
ot/isa/32.9: ¶ Rise up, ye women that are at ease; hear my voice, ye careless daughters; give ear unto my speech.
ot/isa/32.10: Many days and years shall ye be troubled, ye careless women: for the vintage shall fail, the gathering shall not come.
ot/isa/32.11: Tremble, ye women that are at ease; be troubled, ye careless ones: strip you, and make you bare, and gird [italics]sackcloth[/italics] upon [italics]your[/italics] loins.
ot/isa/32.12: They shall lament for the teats, for the pleasant fields, for the fruitful vine.
ot/isa/32.13: Upon the land of my people shall come up thorns [italics]and[/italics] briers; yea, upon all the houses of joy [italics]in[/italics] the joyous city:
ot/isa/32.14: Because the palaces shall be forsaken; the multitude of the city shall be left; the forts and towers shall be for dens for ever, a joy of wild asses, a pasture of flocks;
ot/isa/32.15: Until the spirit be poured upon us from on high, and the wilderness be a fruitful field, and the fruitful field be counted for a forest.
ot/isa/32.16: Then judgment shall dwell in the wilderness, and righteousness remain in the fruitful field.
ot/isa/32.17: And the work of righteousness shall be peace; and the effect of righteousness quietness and assurance for ever.
ot/isa/32.18: And my people shall dwell in a peaceable habitation, and in sure dwellings, and in quiet resting places;
ot/isa/32.19: When it shall hail, coming down on the forest; and the city shall be low in a low place.
ot/isa/32.20: Blessed [italics]are[/italics] ye that sow beside all waters, that send forth [italics]thither[/italics] the feet of the ox and the ass.

ot/isa/40.1: Comfort ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God.
ot/isa/40.2: Speak ye comfortably to Jerusalem, and cry unto her, that her warfare is accomplished, that her iniquity is pardoned: for she hath received of the Lord’s hand double for all her sins.
ot/isa/40.3: ¶ The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
ot/isa/40.4: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low: and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain:
ot/isa/40.5: And the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see [italics]it[/italics] together: for the mouth of the Lord hath spoken [italics]it.[/italics]
ot/isa/40.6: The voice said, Cry. And he said, What shall I cry? All flesh [italics]is[/italics] grass, and all the goodliness thereof [italics]is[/italics] as the flower of the field:
ot/isa/40.7: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: because the spirit of the Lord bloweth upon it: surely the people [italics]is[/italics] grass.
ot/isa/40.8: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.
ot/isa/40.9: ¶ O Zion, that bringest good tidings, get thee up into the high mountain; O Jerusalem, that bringest good tidings, lift up thy voice with strength; lift [italics]it[/italics] up, be not afraid; say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!
ot/isa/40.10: Behold, the Lord God will come with strong [italics]hand,[/italics] and his arm shall rule for him: behold, his reward [italics]is[/italics] with him, and his work before him.
ot/isa/40.11: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather the lambs with his arm, and carry [italics]them[/italics] in his bosom, [italics]and[/italics] shall gently lead those that are with young.
ot/isa/40.12: ¶ Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and meted out heaven with the span, and comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?
ot/isa/40.13: Who hath directed the Spirit of the Lord, or [italics]being[/italics] his counsellor hath taught him?
ot/isa/40.14: With whom took he counsel, and [italics]who[/italics] instructed him, and taught him in the path of judgment, and taught him knowledge, and shewed to him the way of understanding?
ot/isa/40.15: Behold, the nations [italics]are[/italics] as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the small dust of the balance: behold, he taketh up the isles as a very little thing.
ot/isa/40.16: And Lebanon [italics]is[/italics] not sufficient to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.
ot/isa/40.17: All nations before him [italics]are[/italics] as nothing; and they are counted to him less than nothing, and vanity.
ot/isa/40.18: ¶ To whom then will ye liken God? or what likeness will ye compare unto him?
ot/isa/40.19: The workman melteth a graven image, and the goldsmith spreadeth it over with gold, and casteth silver chains.
ot/isa/40.20: He that [italics]is[/italics] so impoverished that he hath no oblation chooseth a tree [italics]that[/italics] will not rot; he seeketh unto him a cunning workman to prepare a graven image, [italics]that[/italics] shall not be moved.
ot/isa/40.21: Have ye not known? have ye not heard? hath it not been told you from the beginning? have ye not understood from the foundations of the earth?
ot/isa/40.22: [italics]It is[/italics] he that sitteth upon the circle of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof [italics]are[/italics] as grasshoppers; that stretcheth out the heavens as a curtain, and spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in:
ot/isa/40.23: That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
ot/isa/40.24: Yea, they shall not be planted; yea, they shall not be sown: yea, their stock shall not take root in the earth: and he shall also blow upon them, and they shall wither, and the whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.
ot/isa/40.25: To whom then will ye liken me, or shall I be equal? saith the Holy One.
ot/isa/40.26: Lift up your eyes on high, and behold who hath created these [italics]things,[/italics] that bringeth out their host by number: he calleth them all by names by the greatness of his might, for that [italics]he is[/italics] strong in power; not one faileth.
ot/isa/40.27: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel, My way is hid from the Lord, and my judgment is passed over from my God?
ot/isa/40.28: ¶ Hast thou not known? hast thou not heard, [italics]that[/italics] the everlasting God, the Lord, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary? [italics]there is[/italics] no searching of his understanding.
ot/isa/40.29: He giveth power to the faint; and to [italics]them that have[/italics] no might he increaseth strength.
ot/isa/40.30: Even the youths shall faint and be weary, and the young men shall utterly fall:
ot/isa/40.31: But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew [italics]their[/italics] strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; [italics]and[/italics] they shall walk, and not faint.

ot/isa/42.1: Behold my servant, whom I uphold; mine elect, [italics]in whom[/italics] my soul delighteth; I have put my spirit upon him: he shall bring forth judgment to the Gentiles.
ot/isa/42.2: He shall not cry, nor lift up, nor cause his voice to be heard in the street.
ot/isa/42.3: A bruised reed shall he not break, and the smoking flax shall he not quench: he shall bring forth judgment unto truth.
ot/isa/42.4: He shall not fail nor be discouraged, till he have set judgment in the earth: and the isles shall wait for his law.
ot/isa/42.5: ¶ Thus saith God the Lord, he that created the heavens, and stretched them out; he that spread forth the earth, and that which cometh out of it; he that giveth breath unto the people upon it, and spirit to them that walk therein:
ot/isa/42.6: I the Lord have called thee in righteousness, and will hold thine hand, and will keep thee, and give thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles;
ot/isa/42.7: To open the blind eyes, to bring out the prisoners from the prison, [italics]and[/italics] them that sit in darkness out of the prison house.
ot/isa/42.8: I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord: that [italics]is[/italics] my name: and my glory will I not give to another, neither my praise to graven images.
ot/isa/42.9: Behold, the former things are come to pass, and new things do I declare: before they spring forth I tell you of them.
ot/isa/42.10: Sing unto the Lord a new song, [italics]and[/italics] his praise from the end of the earth, ye that go down to the sea, and all that is therein; the isles, and the inhabitants thereof.
ot/isa/42.11: Let the wilderness and the cities thereof lift up [italics]their voice,[/italics] the villages [italics]that[/italics] Kedar doth inhabit: let the inhabitants of the rock sing, let them shout from the top of the mountains.
