ot/isa/42.12: Let them give glory unto the Lord, and declare his praise in the islands.
ot/isa/42.13: The Lord shall go forth as a mighty man, he shall stir up jealousy like a man of war: he shall cry, yea, roar; he shall prevail against his enemies.
ot/isa/42.14: I have long time holden my peace; I have been still, [italics]and[/italics] refrained myself: [italics]now[/italics] will I cry like a travailing woman; I will destroy and devour at once.
ot/isa/42.15: I will make waste mountains and hills, and dry up all their herbs; and I will make the rivers islands, and I will dry up the pools.
ot/isa/42.16: And I will bring the blind by a way [italics]that[/italics] they knew not; I will lead them in paths [italics]that[/italics] they have not known: I will make darkness light before them, and crooked things straight. These things will I do unto them, and not forsake them.
ot/isa/42.17: ¶ They shall be turned back, they shall be greatly ashamed, that trust in graven images, that say to the molten images, Ye [italics]are[/italics] our gods.
ot/isa/42.18: Hear, ye deaf; and look, ye blind, that ye may see.
ot/isa/42.19: Who [italics]is[/italics] blind, but my servant? or deaf, as my messenger [italics]that[/italics] I sent? who [italics]is[/italics] blind as [italics]he that is[/italics] perfect, and blind as the Lord’s servant?
ot/isa/42.20: Seeing many things, but thou observest not; opening the ears, but he heareth not.
ot/isa/42.21: The Lord is well pleased for his righteousness’ sake; he will magnify the law, and make [italics]it[/italics] honourable.
ot/isa/42.22: But this [italics]is[/italics] a people robbed and spoiled; [italics]they are[/italics] all of them snared in holes, and they are hid in prison houses: they are for a prey, and none delivereth; for a spoil, and none saith, Restore.
ot/isa/42.23: Who among you will give ear to this? [italics]who[/italics] will hearken and hear for the time to come?
ot/isa/42.24: Who gave Jacob for a spoil, and Israel to the robbers? did not the Lord, he against whom we have sinned? for they would not walk in his ways, neither were they obedient unto his law.
ot/isa/42.25: Therefore he hath poured upon him the fury of his anger, and the strength of battle: and it hath set him on fire round about, yet he knew not; and it burned him, yet he laid [italics]it[/italics] not to heart.

ot/isa/43.1: But now thus saith the Lord that created thee, O Jacob, and he that formed thee, O Israel, Fear not: for I have redeemed thee, I have called [italics]thee[/italics] by thy name; thou [italics]art[/italics] mine.
ot/isa/43.2: When thou passest through the waters, I [italics]will be[/italics] with thee; and through the rivers, they shall not overflow thee: when thou walkest through the fire, thou shalt not be burned; neither shall the flame kindle upon thee.
ot/isa/43.3: For I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord thy God, the Holy One of Israel, thy Saviour: I gave Egypt [italics]for[/italics] thy ransom, Ethiopia and Seba for thee.
ot/isa/43.4: Since thou wast precious in my sight, thou hast been honourable, and I have loved thee: therefore will I give men for thee, and people for thy life.
ot/isa/43.5: Fear not: for I [italics]am[/italics] with thee: I will bring thy seed from the east, and gather thee from the west;
ot/isa/43.6: I will say to the north, Give up; and to the south, Keep not back: bring my sons from far, and my daughters from the ends of the earth;
ot/isa/43.7: [italics]Even[/italics] every one that is called by my name: for I have created him for my glory, I have formed him; yea, I have made him.
ot/isa/43.8: ¶ Bring forth the blind people that have eyes, and the deaf that have ears.
ot/isa/43.9: Let all the nations be gathered together, and let the people be assembled: who among them can declare this, and shew us former things? let them bring forth their witnesses, that they may be justified: or let them hear, and say, [italics]It is[/italics] truth.
ot/isa/43.10: Ye [italics]are[/italics] my witnesses, saith the Lord, and my servant whom I have chosen: that ye may know and believe me, and understand that I [italics]am[/italics] he: before me there was no God formed, neither shall there be after me.
ot/isa/43.11: I, [italics]even[/italics] I, [italics]am[/italics] the Lord; and beside me [italics]there is[/italics] no saviour.
ot/isa/43.12: I have declared, and have saved, and I have shewed, when [italics]there was[/italics] no strange [italics]god[/italics] among you: therefore ye [italics]are[/italics] my witnesses, saith the Lord, that I [italics]am[/italics] God.
ot/isa/43.13: Yea, before the day [italics]was[/italics] I [italics]am[/italics] he; and [italics]there is[/italics] none that can deliver out of my hand: I will work, and who shall let it?
ot/isa/43.14: ¶ Thus saith the Lord, your redeemer, the Holy One of Israel; For your sake I have sent to Babylon, and have brought down all their nobles, and the Chaldeans, whose cry [italics]is[/italics] in the ships.
ot/isa/43.15: I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord, your Holy One, the creator of Israel, your King.
ot/isa/43.16: Thus saith the Lord, which maketh a way in the sea, and a path in the mighty waters;
ot/isa/43.17: Which bringeth forth the chariot and horse, the army and the power; they shall lie down together, they shall not rise: they are extinct, they are quenched as tow.
ot/isa/43.18: ¶ Remember ye not the former things, neither consider the things of old.
ot/isa/43.19: Behold, I will do a new thing; now it shall spring forth; shall ye not know it? I will even make a way in the wilderness, [italics]and[/italics] rivers in the desert.
ot/isa/43.20: The beast of the field shall honour me, the dragons and the owls: because I give waters in the wilderness, [italics]and[/italics] rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, my chosen.
ot/isa/43.21: This people have I formed for myself; they shall shew forth my praise.
ot/isa/43.22: ¶ But thou hast not called upon me, O Jacob; but thou hast been weary of me, O Israel.
ot/isa/43.23: Thou hast not brought me the small cattle of thy burnt offerings; neither hast thou honoured me with thy sacrifices. I have not caused thee to serve with an offering, nor wearied thee with incense.
ot/isa/43.24: Thou hast bought me no sweet cane with money, neither hast thou filled me with the fat of thy sacrifices: but thou hast made me to serve with thy sins, thou hast wearied me with thine iniquities.
ot/isa/43.25: I, [italics]even[/italics] I, [italics]am[/italics] he that blotteth out thy transgressions for mine own sake, and will not remember thy sins.
ot/isa/43.26: Put me in remembrance: let us plead together: declare thou, that thou mayest be justified.
ot/isa/43.27: Thy first father hath sinned, and thy teachers have transgressed against me.
ot/isa/43.28: Therefore I have profaned the princes of the sanctuary, and have given Jacob to the curse, and Israel to reproaches.

ot/isa/44.1: Yet now hear, O Jacob my servant; and Israel, whom I have chosen:
ot/isa/44.2: Thus saith the Lord that made thee, and formed thee from the womb, [italics]which[/italics] will help thee; Fear not, O Jacob, my servant; and thou, Jesurun, whom I have chosen.
ot/isa/44.3: For I will pour water upon him that is thirsty, and floods upon the dry ground: I will pour my spirit upon thy seed, and my blessing upon thine offspring:
ot/isa/44.4: And they shall spring up [italics]as[/italics] among the grass, as willows by the water courses.
ot/isa/44.5: One shall say, I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord’s; and another shall call [italics]himself[/italics] by the name of Jacob; and another shall subscribe [italics]with[/italics] his hand unto the Lord, and surname [italics]himself[/italics] by the name of Israel.
ot/isa/44.6: Thus saith the Lord the King of Israel, and his redeemer the Lord of hosts; I [italics]am[/italics] the first, and I [italics]am[/italics] the last; and beside me [italics]there is[/italics] no God.
ot/isa/44.7: And who, as I, shall call, and shall declare it, and set it in order for me, since I appointed the ancient people? and the things that are coming, and shall come, let them shew unto them.
ot/isa/44.8: Fear ye not, neither be afraid: have not I told thee from that time, and have declared [italics]it?[/italics] ye [italics]are[/italics] even my witnesses. Is there a God beside me? yea, [italics]there is[/italics] no God; I know not [italics]any.[/italics]
ot/isa/44.9: ¶ They that make a graven image [italics]are[/italics] all of them vanity; and their delectable things shall not profit; and they [italics]are[/italics] their own witnesses; they see not, nor know; that they may be ashamed.
ot/isa/44.10: Who hath formed a god, or molten a graven image [italics]that[/italics] is profitable for nothing?
ot/isa/44.11: Behold, all his fellows shall be ashamed: and the workmen, they [italics]are[/italics] of men: let them all be gathered together, let them stand up; [italics]yet[/italics] they shall fear, [italics]and[/italics] they shall be ashamed together.
ot/isa/44.12: The smith with the tongs both worketh in the coals, and fashioneth it with hammers, and worketh it with the strength of his arms: yea, he is hungry, and his strength faileth: he drinketh no water, and is faint.
ot/isa/44.13: The carpenter stretcheth out [italics]his[/italics] rule; he marketh it out with a line; he fitteth it with planes, and he marketh it out with the compass, and maketh it after the figure of a man, according to the beauty of a man; that it may remain in the house.
ot/isa/44.14: He heweth him down cedars, and taketh the cypress and the oak, which he strengtheneth for himself among the trees of the forest: he planteth an ash, and the rain doth nourish [italics]it.[/italics]
ot/isa/44.15: Then shall it be for a man to burn: for he will take thereof, and warm himself; yea, he kindleth [italics]it,[/italics] and baketh bread; yea, he maketh a god, and worshippeth [italics]it;[/italics] he maketh it a graven image, and falleth down thereto.
ot/isa/44.16: He burneth part thereof in the fire; with part thereof he eateth flesh; he roasteth roast, and is satisfied: yea, he warmeth [italics]himself,[/italics] and saith, Aha, I am warm, I have seen the fire:
ot/isa/44.17: And the residue thereof he maketh a god, [italics]even[/italics] his graven image: he falleth down unto it, and worshippeth [italics]it,[/italics] and prayeth unto it, and saith, Deliver me; for thou [italics]art[/italics] my god.
ot/isa/44.18: They have not known nor understood: for he hath shut their eyes, that they cannot see; [italics]and[/italics] their hearts, that they cannot understand.
ot/isa/44.19: And none considereth in his heart, neither [italics]is there[/italics] knowledge nor understanding to say, I have burned part of it in the fire; yea, also I have baked bread upon the coals thereof; I have roasted flesh, and eaten [italics]it:[/italics] and shall I make the residue thereof an abomination? shall I fall down to the stock of a tree?
ot/isa/44.20: He feedeth on ashes: a deceived heart hath turned him aside, that he cannot deliver his soul, nor say, [italics]Is there[/italics] not a lie in my right hand?
ot/isa/44.21: ¶ Remember these, O Jacob and Israel; for thou [italics]art[/italics] my servant: I have formed thee; thou [italics]art[/italics] my servant: O Israel, thou shalt not be forgotten of me.
ot/isa/44.22: I have blotted out, as a thick cloud, thy transgressions, and, as a cloud, thy sins: return unto me; for I have redeemed thee.
ot/isa/44.23: Sing, O ye heavens; for the Lord hath done [italics]it:[/italics] shout, ye lower parts of the earth: break forth into singing, ye mountains, O forest, and every tree therein: for the Lord hath redeemed Jacob, and glorified himself in Israel.
ot/isa/44.24: Thus saith the Lord, thy redeemer, and he that formed thee from the womb, I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord that maketh all [italics]things;[/italics] that stretcheth forth the heavens alone; that spreadeth abroad the earth by myself;
ot/isa/44.25: That frustrateth the tokens of the liars, and maketh diviners mad; that turneth wise [italics]men[/italics] backward, and maketh their knowledge foolish;
ot/isa/44.26: That confirmeth the word of his servant, and performeth the counsel of his messengers; that saith to Jerusalem, Thou shalt be inhabited; and to the cities of Judah, Ye shall be built, and I will raise up the decayed places thereof:
ot/isa/44.27: That saith to the deep, Be dry, and I will dry up thy rivers:
ot/isa/44.28: That saith of Cyrus, [italics]He is[/italics] my shepherd, and shall perform all my pleasure: even saying to Jerusalem, Thou shalt be built; and to the temple, Thy foundation shall be laid.

ot/isa/45.1: Thus saith the Lord to his anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have holden, to subdue nations before him; and I will loose the loins of kings, to open before him the two leaved gates; and the gates shall not be shut;
ot/isa/45.2: I will go before thee, and make the crooked places straight: I will break in pieces the gates of brass, and cut in sunder the bars of iron:
ot/isa/45.3: And I will give thee the treasures of darkness, and hidden riches of secret places, that thou mayest know that I, the Lord, which call [italics]thee[/italics] by thy name, [italics]am[/italics] the God of Israel.
ot/isa/45.4: For Jacob my servant’s sake, and Israel mine elect, I have even called thee by thy name: I have surnamed thee, though thou hast not known me.
ot/isa/45.5: ¶ I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord, and [italics]there is[/italics] none else, [italics]there is[/italics] no God beside me: I girded thee, though thou hast not known me:
ot/isa/45.6: That they may know from the rising of the sun, and from the west, that [italics]there is[/italics] none beside me. I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord, and [italics]there is[/italics] none else.
ot/isa/45.7: I form the light, and create darkness: I make peace, and create evil: I the Lord do all these [italics]things.[/italics]
ot/isa/45.8: Drop down, ye heavens, from above, and let the skies pour down righteousness: let the earth open, and let them bring forth salvation, and let righteousness spring up together; I the Lord have created it.
ot/isa/45.9: Woe unto him that striveth with his Maker! [italics]Let[/italics] the potsherd [italics]strive[/italics] with the potsherds of the earth. Shall the clay say to him that fashioneth it, What makest thou? or thy work, He hath no hands?
ot/isa/45.10: Woe unto him that saith unto [italics]his[/italics] father, What begettest thou? or to the woman, What hast thou brought forth?
ot/isa/45.11: Thus saith the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, and his Maker, Ask me of things to come concerning my sons, and concerning the work of my hands command ye me.
ot/isa/45.12: I have made the earth, and created man upon it: I, [italics]even[/italics] my hands, have stretched out the heavens, and all their host have I commanded.
ot/isa/45.13: I have raised him up in righteousness, and I will direct all his ways: he shall build my city, and he shall let go my captives, not for price nor reward, saith the Lord of hosts.
ot/isa/45.14: Thus saith the Lord, The labour of Egypt, and merchandise of Ethiopia and of the Sabeans, men of stature, shall come over unto thee, and they shall be thine: they shall come after thee; in chains they shall come over, and they shall fall down unto thee, they shall make supplication unto thee, [italics]saying,[/italics] Surely God [italics]is[/italics] in thee; and [italics]there is[/italics] none else, [italics]there is[/italics] no God.
ot/isa/45.15: Verily thou [italics]art[/italics] a God that hidest thyself, O God of Israel, the Saviour.
ot/isa/45.16: They shall be ashamed, and also confounded, all of them: they shall go to confusion together [italics]that are[/italics] makers of idols.
ot/isa/45.17: [italics]But[/italics] Israel shall be saved in the Lord with an everlasting salvation: ye shall not be ashamed nor confounded world without end.
ot/isa/45.18: For thus saith the Lord that created the heavens; God himself that formed the earth and made it; he hath established it, he created it not in vain, he formed it to be inhabited: I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord; and [italics]there is[/italics] none else.
ot/isa/45.19: I have not spoken in secret, in a dark place of the earth: I said not unto the seed of Jacob, Seek ye me in vain: I the Lord speak righteousness, I declare things that are right.
ot/isa/45.20: ¶ Assemble yourselves and come; draw near together, ye [italics]that are[/italics] escaped of the nations: they have no knowledge that set up the wood of their graven image, and pray unto a god [italics]that[/italics] cannot save.
ot/isa/45.21: Tell ye, and bring [italics]them[/italics] near; yea, let them take counsel together: who hath declared this from ancient time? [italics]who[/italics] hath told it from that time? [italics]have[/italics] not I the Lord? and [italics]there is[/italics] no God else beside me; a just God and a Saviour; [italics]there is[/italics] none beside me.
ot/isa/45.22: Look unto me, and be ye saved, all the ends of the earth: for I [italics]am[/italics] God, and [italics]there is[/italics] none else.
ot/isa/45.23: I have sworn by myself, the word is gone out of my mouth [italics]in[/italics] righteousness, and shall not return, That unto me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear.
ot/isa/45.24: Surely, shall [italics]one[/italics] say, in the Lord have I righteousness and strength: [italics]even[/italics] to him shall [italics]men[/italics] come; and all that are incensed against him shall be ashamed.
ot/isa/45.25: In the Lord shall all the seed of Israel be justified, and shall glory.

ot/isa/49.1: Listen, O isles, unto me; and hearken, ye people, from far; The Lord hath called me from the womb; from the bowels of my mother hath he made mention of my name.
ot/isa/49.2: And he hath made my mouth like a sharp sword; in the shadow of his hand hath he hid me, and made me a polished shaft; in his quiver hath he hid me;
ot/isa/49.3: And said unto me, Thou [italics]art[/italics] my servant, O Israel, in whom I will be glorified.
ot/isa/49.4: Then I said, I have laboured in vain, I have spent my strength for nought, and in vain: [italics]yet[/italics] surely my judgment [italics]is[/italics] with the Lord, and my work with my God.
ot/isa/49.5: ¶ And now, saith the Lord that formed me from the womb [italics]to be[/italics] his servant, to bring Jacob again to him, Though Israel be not gathered, yet shall I be glorious in the eyes of the Lord, and my God shall be my strength.
ot/isa/49.6: And he said, It is a light thing that thou shouldest be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob, and to restore the preserved of Israel: I will also give thee for a light to the Gentiles, that thou mayest be my salvation unto the end of the earth.
ot/isa/49.7: Thus saith the Lord, the Redeemer of Israel, [italics]and[/italics] his Holy One, to him whom man despiseth, to him whom the nation abhorreth, to a servant of rulers, Kings shall see and arise, princes also shall worship, because of the Lord that is faithful, [italics]and[/italics] the Holy One of Israel, and he shall choose thee.
ot/isa/49.8: Thus saith the Lord, In an acceptable time have I heard thee, and in a day of salvation have I helped thee: and I will preserve thee, and give thee for a covenant of the people, to establish the earth, to cause to inherit the desolate heritages;
ot/isa/49.9: That thou mayest say to the prisoners, Go forth; to them that [italics]are[/italics] in darkness, Shew yourselves. They shall feed in the ways, and their pastures [italics]shall be[/italics] in all high places.
ot/isa/49.10: They shall not hunger nor thirst; neither shall the heat nor sun smite them: for he that hath mercy on them shall lead them, even by the springs of water shall he guide them.
ot/isa/49.11: And I will make all my mountains a way, and my highways shall be exalted.
ot/isa/49.12: Behold, these shall come from far: and, lo, these from the north and from the west; and these from the land of Sinim.
ot/isa/49.13: ¶ Sing, O heavens; and be joyful, O earth; and break forth into singing, O mountains: for the Lord hath comforted his people, and will have mercy upon his afflicted.
ot/isa/49.14: But Zion said, The Lord hath forsaken me, and my Lord hath forgotten me.
ot/isa/49.15: Can a woman forget her sucking child, that she should not have compassion on the son of her womb? yea, they may forget, yet will I not forget thee.
ot/isa/49.16: Behold, I have graven thee upon the palms of [italics]my[/italics] hands; thy walls [italics]are[/italics] continually before me.
ot/isa/49.17: Thy children shall make haste; thy destroyers and they that made thee waste shall go forth of thee.
ot/isa/49.18: ¶ Lift up thine eyes round about, and behold: all these gather themselves together, [italics]and[/italics] come to thee. [italics]As[/italics] I live, saith the Lord, thou shalt surely clothe thee with them all, as with an ornament, and bind them [italics]on thee,[/italics] as a bride [italics]doeth.[/italics]
ot/isa/49.19: For thy waste and thy desolate places, and the land of thy destruction, shall even now be too narrow by reason of the inhabitants, and they that swallowed thee up shall be far away.
ot/isa/49.20: The children which thou shalt have, after thou hast lost the other, shall say again in thine ears, The place [italics]is[/italics] too strait for me: give place to me that I may dwell.
ot/isa/49.21: Then shalt thou say in thine heart, Who hath begotten me these, seeing I have lost my children, and am desolate, a captive, and removing to and fro? and who hath brought up these? Behold, I was left alone; these, where [italics]had[/italics] they [italics]been?[/italics]
ot/isa/49.22: Thus saith the Lord God, Behold, I will lift up mine hand to the Gentiles, and set up my standard to the people: and they shall bring thy sons in [italics]their[/italics] arms, and thy daughters shall be carried upon [italics]their[/italics] shoulders.
ot/isa/49.23: And kings shall be thy nursing fathers, and their queens thy nursing mothers: they shall bow down to thee with [italics]their[/italics] face toward the earth, and lick up the dust of thy feet; and thou shalt know that I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord: for they shall not be ashamed that wait for me.
ot/isa/49.24: ¶ Shall the prey be taken from the mighty, or the lawful captive delivered?
ot/isa/49.25: But thus saith the Lord, Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible shall be delivered: for I will contend with him that contendeth with thee, and I will save thy children.
ot/isa/49.26: And I will feed them that oppress thee with their own flesh; and they shall be drunken with their own blood, as with sweet wine: and all flesh shall know that I the Lord [italics]am[/italics] thy Saviour and thy Redeemer, the mighty One of Jacob.

ot/isa/53.1: Who hath believed our report? and to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?
ot/isa/53.2: For he shall grow up before him as a tender plant, and as a root out of a dry ground: he hath no form nor comeliness; and when we shall see him, [italics]there is[/italics] no beauty that we should desire him.
ot/isa/53.3: He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief: and we hid as it were [italics]our[/italics] faces from him; he was despised, and we esteemed him not.
ot/isa/53.4: ¶ Surely he hath borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows: yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted.
ot/isa/53.5: But he [italics]was[/italics] wounded for our transgressions, [italics]he was[/italics] bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace [italics]was[/italics] upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.
ot/isa/53.6: All we like sheep have gone astray; we have turned every one to his own way; and the Lord hath laid on him the iniquity of us all.
ot/isa/53.7: He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he opened not his mouth: he is brought as a lamb to the slaughter, and as a sheep before her shearers is dumb, so he openeth not his mouth.
ot/isa/53.8: He was taken from prison and from judgment: and who shall declare his generation? for he was cut off out of the land of the living: for the transgression of my people was he stricken.
ot/isa/53.9: And he made his grave with the wicked, and with the rich in his death; because he had done no violence, neither [italics]was any[/italics] deceit in his mouth.
ot/isa/53.10: ¶ Yet it pleased the Lord to bruise him; he hath put [italics]him[/italics] to grief: when thou shalt make his soul an offering for sin, he shall see [italics]his[/italics] seed, he shall prolong [italics]his[/italics] days, and the pleasure of the Lord shall prosper in his hand.
ot/isa/53.11: He shall see of the travail of his soul, [italics]and[/italics] shall be satisfied: by his knowledge shall my righteous servant justify many; for he shall bear their iniquities.
ot/isa/53.12: Therefore will I divide him [italics]a portion[/italics] with the great, and he shall divide the spoil with the strong; because he hath poured out his soul unto death: and he was numbered with the transgressors; and he bare the sin of many, and made intercession for the transgressors.

ot/isa/6.1: In the year that king Uzziah died I saw also the Lord sitting upon a throne, high and lifted up, and his train filled the temple.
ot/isa/6.2: Above it stood the seraphims: each one had six wings; with twain he covered his face, and with twain he covered his feet, and with twain he did fly.
ot/isa/6.3: And one cried unto another, and said, Holy, holy, holy, [italics]is[/italics] the Lord of hosts: the whole earth [italics]is[/italics] full of his glory.
ot/isa/6.4: And the posts of the door moved at the voice of him that cried, and the house was filled with smoke.
ot/isa/6.5: ¶ Then said I, Woe [italics]is[/italics] me! for I am undone; because I [italics]am[/italics] a man of unclean lips, and I dwell in the midst of a people of unclean lips: for mine eyes have seen the King, the Lord of hosts.
ot/isa/6.6: Then flew one of the seraphims unto me, having a live coal in his hand, [italics]which[/italics] he had taken with the tongs from off the altar:
ot/isa/6.7: And he laid [italics]it[/italics] upon my mouth, and said, Lo, this hath touched thy lips; and thine iniquity is taken away, and thy sin purged.
ot/isa/6.8: Also I heard the voice of the Lord, saying, Whom shall I send, and who will go for us? Then said I, Here [italics]am[/italics] I; send me.
ot/isa/6.9: ¶ And he said, Go, and tell this people, Hear ye indeed, but understand not; and see ye indeed, but perceive not.
ot/isa/6.10: Make the heart of this people fat, and make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes; lest they see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and convert, and be healed.
ot/isa/6.11: Then said I, Lord, how long? And he answered, Until the cities be wasted without inhabitant, and the houses without man, and the land be utterly desolate,
ot/isa/6.12: And the Lord have removed men far away, and [italics]there be[/italics] a great forsaking in the midst of the land.
ot/isa/6.13: ¶ But yet in it [italics]shall be[/italics] a tenth, and [italics]it[/italics] shall return, and shall be eaten: as a teil tree, and as an oak, whose substance [italics]is[/italics] in them, when they cast [italics]their leaves: so[/italics] the holy seed [italics]shall be[/italics] the substance thereof.

ot/isa/60.1: Arise, shine; for thy light is come, and the glory of the Lord is risen upon thee.
ot/isa/60.2: For, behold, the darkness shall cover the earth, and gross darkness the people: but the Lord shall arise upon thee, and his glory shall be seen upon thee.
ot/isa/60.3: And the Gentiles shall come to thy light, and kings to the brightness of thy rising.
ot/isa/60.4: Lift up thine eyes round about, and see: all they gather themselves together, they come to thee: thy sons shall come from far, and thy daughters shall be nursed at [italics]thy[/italics] side.
ot/isa/60.5: Then thou shalt see, and flow together, and thine heart shall fear, and be enlarged; because the abundance of the sea shall be converted unto thee, the forces of the Gentiles shall come unto thee.
ot/isa/60.6: The multitude of camels shall cover thee, the dromedaries of Midian and Ephah; all they from Sheba shall come: they shall bring gold and incense; and they shall shew forth the praises of the Lord.
ot/isa/60.7: All the flocks of Kedar shall be gathered together unto thee, the rams of Nebaioth shall minister unto thee: they shall come up with acceptance on mine altar, and I will glorify the house of my glory.
ot/isa/60.8: Who [italics]are[/italics] these [italics]that[/italics] fly as a cloud, and as the doves to their windows?
ot/isa/60.9: Surely the isles shall wait for me, and the ships of Tarshish first, to bring thy sons from far, their silver and their gold with them, unto the name of the Lord thy God, and to the Holy One of Israel, because he hath glorified thee.
ot/isa/60.10: And the sons of strangers shall build up thy walls, and their kings shall minister unto thee: for in my wrath I smote thee, but in my favour have I had mercy on thee.
ot/isa/60.11: Therefore thy gates shall be open continually; they shall not be shut day nor night; that [italics]men[/italics] may bring unto thee the forces of the Gentiles, and [italics]that[/italics] their kings [italics]may be[/italics] brought.
ot/isa/60.12: For the nation and kingdom that will not serve thee shall perish; yea, [italics]those[/italics] nations shall be utterly wasted.
ot/isa/60.13: The glory of Lebanon shall come unto thee, the fir tree, the pine tree, and the box together, to beautify the place of my sanctuary; and I will make the place of my feet glorious.
ot/isa/60.14: The sons also of them that afflicted thee shall come bending unto thee; and all they that despised thee shall bow themselves down at the soles of thy feet; and they shall call thee, The city of the Lord, The Zion of the Holy One of Israel.
ot/isa/60.15: Whereas thou hast been forsaken and hated, so that no man went through [italics]thee,[/italics] I will make thee an eternal excellency, a joy of many generations.
ot/isa/60.16: Thou shalt also suck the milk of the Gentiles, and shalt suck the breast of kings: and thou shalt know that I the Lord [italics]am[/italics] thy Saviour and thy Redeemer, the mighty One of Jacob.
ot/isa/60.17: For brass I will bring gold, and for iron I will bring silver, and for wood brass, and for stones iron: I will also make thy officers peace, and thine exactors righteousness.
ot/isa/60.18: Violence shall no more be heard in thy land, wasting nor destruction within thy borders; but thou shalt call thy walls Salvation, and thy gates Praise.
ot/isa/60.19: The sun shall be no more thy light by day; neither for brightness shall the moon give light unto thee: but the Lord shall be unto thee an everlasting light, and thy God thy glory.
ot/isa/60.20: Thy sun shall no more go down; neither shall thy moon withdraw itself: for the Lord shall be thine everlasting light, and the days of thy mourning shall be ended.
ot/isa/60.21: Thy people also [italics]shall be[/italics] all righteous: they shall inherit the land for ever, the branch of my planting, the work of my hands, that I may be glorified.
ot/isa/60.22: A little one shall become a thousand, and a small one a strong nation: I the Lord will hasten it in his time.

ot/isa/61.1: The Spirit of the Lord God [italics]is[/italics] upon me; because the Lord hath anointed me to preach good tidings unto the meek; he hath sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to [italics]them that are[/italics] bound;
ot/isa/61.2: To proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord, and the day of vengeance of our God; to comfort all that mourn;
ot/isa/61.3: To appoint unto them that mourn in Zion, to give unto them beauty for ashes, the oil of joy for mourning, the garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness; that they might be called trees of righteousness, the planting of the Lord, that he might be glorified.
ot/isa/61.4: ¶ And they shall build the old wastes, they shall raise up the former desolations, and they shall repair the waste cities, the desolations of many generations.
ot/isa/61.5: And strangers shall stand and feed your flocks, and the sons of the alien [italics]shall be[/italics] your plowmen and your vinedressers.
ot/isa/61.6: But ye shall be named the Priests of the Lord: [italics]men[/italics] shall call you the Ministers of our God: ye shall eat the riches of the Gentiles, and in their glory shall ye boast yourselves.
ot/isa/61.7: ¶ For your shame [italics]ye shall have[/italics] double; and [italics]for[/italics] confusion they shall rejoice in their portion: therefore in their land they shall possess the double: everlasting joy shall be unto them.
ot/isa/61.8: For I the Lord love judgment, I hate robbery for burnt offering; and I will direct their work in truth, and I will make an everlasting covenant with them.
ot/isa/61.9: And their seed shall be known among the Gentiles, and their offspring among the people: all that see them shall acknowledge them, that they [italics]are[/italics] the seed [italics]which[/italics] the Lord hath blessed.
ot/isa/61.10: I will greatly rejoice in the Lord, my soul shall be joyful in my God; for he hath clothed me with the garments of salvation, he hath covered me with the robe of righteousness, as a bridegroom decketh [italics]himself[/italics] with ornaments, and as a bride adorneth [italics]herself[/italics] with her jewels.
ot/isa/61.11: For as the earth bringeth forth her bud, and as the garden causeth the things that are sown in it to spring forth; so the Lord God will cause righteousness and praise to spring forth before all the nations.

ot/isa/65.1: I am sought of [italics]them that[/italics] asked not [italics]for me;[/italics] I am found of [italics]them that[/italics] sought me not: I said, Behold me, behold me, unto a nation [italics]that[/italics] was not called by my name.
ot/isa/65.2: I have spread out my hands all the day unto a rebellious people, which walketh in a way [italics]that was[/italics] not good, after their own thoughts;
ot/isa/65.3: A people that provoketh me to anger continually to my face; that sacrificeth in gardens, and burneth incense upon altars of brick;
ot/isa/65.4: Which remain among the graves, and lodge in the monuments, which eat swine’s flesh, and broth of abominable [italics]things is in[/italics] their vessels;
ot/isa/65.5: Which say, Stand by thyself, come not near to me; for I am holier than thou. These [italics]are[/italics] a smoke in my nose, a fire that burneth all the day.
ot/isa/65.6: Behold, [italics]it is[/italics] written before me: I will not keep silence, but will recompense, even recompense into their bosom,
ot/isa/65.7: Your iniquities, and the iniquities of your fathers together, saith the Lord, which have burned incense upon the mountains, and blasphemed me upon the hills: therefore will I measure their former work into their bosom.
ot/isa/65.8: ¶ Thus saith the Lord, As the new wine is found in the cluster, and [italics]one[/italics] saith, Destroy it not; for a blessing [italics]is[/italics] in it: so will I do for my servants’ sakes, that I may not destroy them all.
ot/isa/65.9: And I will bring forth a seed out of Jacob, and out of Judah an inheritor of my mountains: and mine elect shall inherit it, and my servants shall dwell there.
ot/isa/65.10: And Sharon shall be a fold of flocks, and the valley of Achor a place for the herds to lie down in, for my people that have sought me.
ot/isa/65.11: ¶ But ye [italics]are[/italics] they that forsake the Lord, that forget my holy mountain, that prepare a table for that troop, and that furnish the drink offering unto that number.
ot/isa/65.12: Therefore will I number you to the sword, and ye shall all bow down to the slaughter: because when I called, ye did not answer; when I spake, ye did not hear; but did evil before mine eyes, and did choose [italics]that[/italics] wherein I delighted not.
ot/isa/65.13: Therefore thus saith the Lord God, Behold, my servants shall eat, but ye shall be hungry: behold, my servants shall drink, but ye shall be thirsty: behold, my servants shall rejoice, but ye shall be ashamed:
ot/isa/65.14: Behold, my servants shall sing for joy of heart, but ye shall cry for sorrow of heart, and shall howl for vexation of spirit.
ot/isa/65.15: And ye shall leave your name for a curse unto my chosen: for the Lord God shall slay thee, and call his servants by another name:
ot/isa/65.16: That he who blesseth himself in the earth shall bless himself in the God of truth; and he that sweareth in the earth shall swear by the God of truth; because the former troubles are forgotten, and because they are hid from mine eyes.
ot/isa/65.17: ¶ For, behold, I create new heavens and a new earth: and the former shall not be remembered, nor come into mind.
ot/isa/65.18: But be ye glad and rejoice for ever [italics]in that[/italics] which I create: for, behold, I create Jerusalem a rejoicing, and her people a joy.
ot/isa/65.19: And I will rejoice in Jerusalem, and joy in my people: and the voice of weeping shall be no more heard in her, nor the voice of crying.
ot/isa/65.20: There shall be no more thence an infant of days, nor an old man that hath not filled his days: for the child shall die an hundred years old; but the sinner [italics]being[/italics] an hundred years old shall be accursed.
ot/isa/65.21: And they shall build houses, and inhabit [italics]them;[/italics] and they shall plant vineyards, and eat the fruit of them.
ot/isa/65.22: They shall not build, and another inhabit; they shall not plant, and another eat: for as the days of a tree [italics]are[/italics] the days of my people, and mine elect shall long enjoy the work of their hands.
ot/isa/65.23: They shall not labour in vain, nor bring forth for trouble; for they [italics]are[/italics] the seed of the blessed of the Lord, and their offspring with them.
ot/isa/65.24: And it shall come to pass, that before they call, I will answer; and while they are yet speaking, I will hear.
ot/isa/65.25: The wolf and the lamb shall feed together, and the lion shall eat straw like the bullock: and dust [italics]shall be[/italics] the serpent’s meat. They shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain, saith the Lord.

ot/isa/7.1: And it came to pass in the days of Ahaz the son of Jotham, the son of Uzziah, king of Judah, [italics]that[/italics] Rezin the king of Syria, and Pekah the son of Remaliah, king of Israel, went up toward Jerusalem to war against it, but could not prevail against it.
ot/isa/7.2: And it was told the house of David, saying, Syria is confederate with Ephraim. And his heart was moved, and the heart of his people, as the trees of the wood are moved with the wind.
ot/isa/7.3: Then said the Lord unto Isaiah, Go forth now to meet Ahaz, thou, and Shear-jashub thy son, at the end of the conduit of the upper pool in the highway of the fuller’s field;
ot/isa/7.4: And say unto him, Take heed, and be quiet; fear not, neither be fainthearted for the two tails of these smoking firebrands, for the fierce anger of Rezin with Syria, and of the son of Remaliah.
ot/isa/7.5: Because Syria, Ephraim, and the son of Remaliah, have taken evil counsel against thee, saying,
ot/isa/7.6: Let us go up against Judah, and vex it, and let us make a breach therein for us, and set a king in the midst of it, [italics]even[/italics] the son of Tabeal:
ot/isa/7.7: Thus saith the Lord God, It shall not stand, neither shall it come to pass.
ot/isa/7.8: For the head of Syria [italics]is[/italics] Damascus, and the head of Damascus [italics]is[/italics] Rezin; and within threescore and five years shall Ephraim be broken, that it be not a people.
ot/isa/7.9: And the head of Ephraim [italics]is[/italics] Samaria, and the head of Samaria [italics]is[/italics] Remaliah’s son. If ye will not believe, surely ye shall not be established.
ot/isa/7.10: ¶ Moreover the Lord spake again unto Ahaz, saying,
ot/isa/7.11: Ask thee a sign of the Lord thy God; ask it either in the depth, or in the height above.
ot/isa/7.12: But Ahaz said, I will not ask, neither will I tempt the Lord.
ot/isa/7.13: And he said, Hear ye now, O house of David; [italics]Is it[/italics] a small thing for you to weary men, but will ye weary my God also?
ot/isa/7.14: Therefore the Lord himself shall give you a sign; Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.
ot/isa/7.15: Butter and honey shall he eat, that he may know to refuse the evil, and choose the good.
ot/isa/7.16: For before the child shall know to refuse the evil, and choose the good, the land that thou abhorrest shall be forsaken of both her kings.
ot/isa/7.17: ¶ The Lord shall bring upon thee, and upon thy people, and upon thy father’s house, days that have not come, from the day that Ephraim departed from Judah; [italics]even[/italics] the king of Assyria.
ot/isa/7.18: And it shall come to pass in that day, [italics]that[/italics] the Lord shall hiss for the fly that [italics]is[/italics] in the uttermost part of the rivers of Egypt, and for the bee that [italics]is[/italics] in the land of Assyria.
ot/isa/7.19: And they shall come, and shall rest all of them in the desolate valleys, and in the holes of the rocks, and upon all thorns, and upon all bushes.
ot/isa/7.20: In the same day shall the Lord shave with a razor that is hired, [italics]namely,[/italics] by them beyond the river, by the king of Assyria, the head, and the hair of the feet: and it shall also consume the beard.
ot/isa/7.21: And it shall come to pass in that day, [italics]that[/italics] a man shall nourish a young cow, and two sheep;
ot/isa/7.22: And it shall come to pass, for the abundance of milk [italics]that[/italics] they shall give he shall eat butter: for butter and honey shall every one eat that is left in the land.
ot/isa/7.23: And it shall come to pass in that day, [italics]that[/italics] every place shall be, where there were a thousand vines at a thousand silverlings, it shall [italics]even[/italics] be for briers and thorns.
ot/isa/7.24: With arrows and with bows shall [italics]men[/italics] come thither; because all the land shall become briers and thorns.
ot/isa/7.25: And [italics]on[/italics] all hills that shall be digged with the mattock, there shall not come thither the fear of briers and thorns: but it shall be for the sending forth of oxen, and for the treading of lesser cattle.

ot/isa/9.1: Nevertheless the dimness [italics]shall[/italics] not [italics]be[/italics] such as [italics]was[/italics] in her vexation, when at the first he lightly afflicted the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, and afterward did more grievously afflict [italics]her by[/italics] the way of the sea, beyond Jordan, in Galilee of the nations.
ot/isa/9.2: The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light: they that dwell in the land of the shadow of death, upon them hath the light shined.
ot/isa/9.3: Thou hast multiplied the nation, [italics]and[/italics] not increased the joy: they joy before thee according to the joy in harvest, [italics]and[/italics] as [italics]men[/italics] rejoice when they divide the spoil.
ot/isa/9.4: For thou hast broken the yoke of his burden, and the staff of his shoulder, the rod of his oppressor, as in the day of Midian.
ot/isa/9.5: For every battle of the warrior [italics]is[/italics] with confused noise, and garments rolled in blood; but [italics]this[/italics] shall be with burning [italics]and[/italics] fuel of fire.
ot/isa/9.6: For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given: and the government shall be upon his shoulder: and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace.
ot/isa/9.7: Of the increase of [italics]his[/italics] government and peace [italics]there shall be[/italics] no end, upon the throne of David, and upon his kingdom, to order it, and to establish it with judgment and with justice from henceforth even for ever. The zeal of the Lord of hosts will perform this.
ot/isa/9.8: ¶ The Lord sent a word into Jacob, and it hath lighted upon Israel.
ot/isa/9.9: And all the people shall know, [italics]even[/italics] Ephraim and the inhabitant of Samaria, that say in the pride and stoutness of heart,
ot/isa/9.10: The bricks are fallen down, but we will build with hewn stones: the sycomores are cut down, but we will change [italics]them into[/italics] cedars.
ot/isa/9.11: Therefore the Lord shall set up the adversaries of Rezin against him, and join his enemies together;
ot/isa/9.12: The Syrians before, and the Philistines behind; and they shall devour Israel with open mouth. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand [italics]is[/italics] stretched out still.
ot/isa/9.13: ¶ For the people turneth not unto him that smiteth them, neither do they seek the Lord of hosts.
ot/isa/9.14: Therefore the Lord will cut off from Israel head and tail, branch and rush, in one day.
ot/isa/9.15: The ancient and honourable, he [italics]is[/italics] the head; and the prophet that teacheth lies, he [italics]is[/italics] the tail.
ot/isa/9.16: For the leaders of this people cause [italics]them[/italics] to err; and [italics]they that are[/italics] led of them [italics]are[/italics] destroyed.
ot/isa/9.17: Therefore the Lord shall have no joy in their young men, neither shall have mercy on their fatherless and widows: for every one [italics]is[/italics] an hypocrite and an evildoer, and every mouth speaketh folly. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand [italics]is[/italics] stretched out still.
ot/isa/9.18: ¶ For wickedness burneth as the fire: it shall devour the briers and thorns, and shall kindle in the thickets of the forest, and they shall mount up [italics]like[/italics] the lifting up of smoke.
ot/isa/9.19: Through the wrath of the Lord of hosts is the land darkened, and the people shall be as the fuel of the fire: no man shall spare his brother.
ot/isa/9.20: And he shall snatch on the right hand, and be hungry; and he shall eat on the left hand, and they shall not be satisfied: they shall eat every man the flesh of his own arm:
ot/isa/9.21: Manasseh, Ephraim; and Ephraim, Manasseh: [italics]and[/italics] they together [italics]shall be[/italics] against Judah. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand [italics]is[/italics] stretched out still.

ot/jer/1.1: The words of Jeremiah the son of Hilkiah, of the priests that [italics]were[/italics] in Anathoth in the land of Benjamin:
ot/jer/1.2: To whom the word of the Lord came in the days of Josiah the son of Amon king of Judah, in the thirteenth year of his reign.
ot/jer/1.3: It came also in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah king of Judah, unto the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah the son of Josiah king of Judah, unto the carrying away of Jerusalem captive in the fifth month.
ot/jer/1.4: Then the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
ot/jer/1.5: Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee; and before thou camest forth out of the womb I sanctified thee, [italics]and[/italics] I ordained thee a prophet unto the nations.
ot/jer/1.6: Then said I, Ah, Lord God! behold, I cannot speak: for I [italics]am[/italics] a child.
ot/jer/1.7: ¶ But the Lord said unto me, Say not, I [italics]am[/italics] a child: for thou shalt go to all that I shall send thee, and whatsoever I command thee thou shalt speak.
ot/jer/1.8: Be not afraid of their faces: for I [italics]am[/italics] with thee to deliver thee, saith the Lord.
ot/jer/1.9: Then the Lord put forth his hand, and touched my mouth. And the Lord said unto me, Behold, I have put my words in thy mouth.
ot/jer/1.10: See, I have this day set thee over the nations and over the kingdoms, to root out, and to pull down, and to destroy, and to throw down, to build, and to plant.
ot/jer/1.11: ¶ Moreover the word of the Lord came unto me, saying, Jeremiah, what seest thou? And I said, I see a rod of an almond tree.
ot/jer/1.12: Then said the Lord unto me, Thou hast well seen: for I will hasten my word to perform it.
ot/jer/1.13: And the word of the Lord came unto me the second time, saying, What seest thou? And I said, I see a seething pot; and the face thereof [italics]is[/italics] toward the north.
ot/jer/1.14: Then the Lord said unto me, Out of the north an evil shall break forth upon all the inhabitants of the land.
ot/jer/1.15: For, lo, I will call all the families of the kingdoms of the north, saith the Lord; and they shall come, and they shall set every one his throne at the entering of the gates of Jerusalem, and against all the walls thereof round about, and against all the cities of Judah.
ot/jer/1.16: And I will utter my judgments against them touching all their wickedness, who have forsaken me, and have burned incense unto other gods, and worshipped the works of their own hands.
ot/jer/1.17: ¶ Thou therefore gird up thy loins, and arise, and speak unto them all that I command thee: be not dismayed at their faces, lest I confound thee before them.
ot/jer/1.18: For, behold, I have made thee this day a defenced city, and an iron pillar, and brasen walls against the whole land, against the kings of Judah, against the princes thereof, against the priests thereof, and against the people of the land.
ot/jer/1.19: And they shall fight against thee; but they shall not prevail against thee; for I [italics]am[/italics] with thee, saith the Lord, to deliver thee.

ot/jer/23.1: Woe be unto the pastors that destroy and scatter the sheep of my pasture! saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.2: Therefore thus saith the Lord God of Israel against the pastors that feed my people; Ye have scattered my flock, and driven them away, and have not visited them: behold, I will visit upon you the evil of your doings, saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.3: And I will gather the remnant of my flock out of all countries whither I have driven them, and will bring them again to their folds; and they shall be fruitful and increase.
ot/jer/23.4: And I will set up shepherds over them which shall feed them: and they shall fear no more, nor be dismayed, neither shall they be lacking, saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.5: ¶ Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that I will raise unto David a righteous Branch, and a King shall reign and prosper, and shall execute judgment and justice in the earth.
ot/jer/23.6: In his days Judah shall be saved, and Israel shall dwell safely: and this [italics]is[/italics] his name whereby he shall be called, The Lord Our Righteousness.
ot/jer/23.7: Therefore, behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that they shall no more say, The Lord liveth, which brought up the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt;
ot/jer/23.8: But, The Lord liveth, which brought up and which led the seed of the house of Israel out of the north country, and from all countries whither I had driven them; and they shall dwell in their own land.
ot/jer/23.9: ¶ Mine heart within me is broken because of the prophets; all my bones shake; I am like a drunken man, and like a man whom wine hath overcome, because of the Lord, and because of the words of his holiness.
ot/jer/23.10: For the land is full of adulterers; for because of swearing the land mourneth; the pleasant places of the wilderness are dried up, and their course is evil, and their force [italics]is[/italics] not right.
ot/jer/23.11: For both prophet and priest are profane; yea, in my house have I found their wickedness, saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.12: Wherefore their way shall be unto them as slippery [italics]ways[/italics] in the darkness: they shall be driven on, and fall therein: for I will bring evil upon them, [italics]even[/italics] the year of their visitation, saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.13: And I have seen folly in the prophets of Samaria; they prophesied in Baal, and caused my people Israel to err.
ot/jer/23.14: I have seen also in the prophets of Jerusalem an horrible thing: they commit adultery, and walk in lies: they strengthen also the hands of evildoers, that none doth return from his wickedness: they are all of them unto me as Sodom, and the inhabitants thereof as Gomorrah.
ot/jer/23.15: Therefore thus saith the Lord of hosts concerning the prophets; Behold, I will feed them with wormwood, and make them drink the water of gall: for from the prophets of Jerusalem is profaneness gone forth into all the land.
ot/jer/23.16: Thus saith the Lord of hosts, Hearken not unto the words of the prophets that prophesy unto you: they make you vain: they speak a vision of their own heart, [italics]and[/italics] not out of the mouth of the Lord.
ot/jer/23.17: They say still unto them that despise me, The Lord hath said, Ye shall have peace; and they say unto every one that walketh after the imagination of his own heart, No evil shall come upon you.
ot/jer/23.18: For who hath stood in the counsel of the Lord, and hath perceived and heard his word? who hath marked his word, and heard [italics]it?[/italics]
ot/jer/23.19: Behold, a whirlwind of the Lord is gone forth in fury, even a grievous whirlwind: it shall fall grievously upon the head of the wicked.
ot/jer/23.20: The anger of the Lord shall not return, until he have executed, and till he have performed the thoughts of his heart: in the latter days ye shall consider it perfectly.
ot/jer/23.21: I have not sent these prophets, yet they ran: I have not spoken to them, yet they prophesied.
ot/jer/23.22: But if they had stood in my counsel, and had caused my people to hear my words, then they should have turned them from their evil way, and from the evil of their doings.
ot/jer/23.23: [italics]Am[/italics] I a God at hand, saith the Lord, and not a God afar off?
ot/jer/23.24: Can any hide himself in secret places that I shall not see him? saith the Lord. Do not I fill heaven and earth? saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.25: I have heard what the prophets said, that prophesy lies in my name, saying, I have dreamed, I have dreamed.
ot/jer/23.26: How long shall [italics]this[/italics] be in the heart of the prophets that prophesy lies? yea, [italics]they are[/italics] prophets of the deceit of their own heart;
ot/jer/23.27: Which think to cause my people to forget my name by their dreams which they tell every man to his neighbour, as their fathers have forgotten my name for Baal.
ot/jer/23.28: The prophet that hath a dream, let him tell a dream; and he that hath my word, let him speak my word faithfully. What [italics]is[/italics] the chaff to the wheat? saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.29: [italics]Is[/italics] not my word like as a fire? saith the Lord; and like a hammer [italics]that[/italics] breaketh the rock in pieces?
ot/jer/23.30: Therefore, behold, I [italics]am[/italics] against the prophets, saith the Lord, that steal my words every one from his neighbour.
ot/jer/23.31: Behold, I [italics]am[/italics] against the prophets, saith the Lord, that use their tongues, and say, He saith.
ot/jer/23.32: Behold, I [italics]am[/italics] against them that prophesy false dreams, saith the Lord, and do tell them, and cause my people to err by their lies, and by their lightness; yet I sent them not, nor commanded them: therefore they shall not profit this people at all, saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.33: ¶ And when this people, or the prophet, or a priest, shall ask thee, saying, What [italics]is[/italics] the burden of the Lord? thou shalt then say unto them, What burden? I will even forsake you, saith the Lord.
ot/jer/23.34: And [italics]as for[/italics] the prophet, and the priest, and the people, that shall say, The burden of the Lord, I will even punish that man and his house.
ot/jer/23.35: Thus shall ye say every one to his neighbour, and every one to his brother, What hath the Lord answered? and, What hath the Lord spoken?
ot/jer/23.36: And the burden of the Lord shall ye mention no more: for every man’s word shall be his burden; for ye have perverted the words of the living God, of the Lord of hosts our God.
ot/jer/23.37: Thus shalt thou say to the prophet, What hath the Lord answered thee? and, What hath the Lord spoken?
ot/jer/23.38: But since ye say, The burden of the Lord; therefore thus saith the Lord; Because ye say this word, The burden of the Lord, and I have sent unto you, saying, Ye shall not say, The burden of the Lord;
ot/jer/23.39: Therefore, behold, I, even I, will utterly forget you, and I will forsake you, and the city that I gave you and your fathers, [italics]and cast you[/italics] out of my presence:
ot/jer/23.40: And I will bring an everlasting reproach upon you, and a perpetual shame, which shall not be forgotten.

ot/job/19.2: How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?
ot/job/19.3: These ten times have ye reproached me: ye are not ashamed [italics]that[/italics] ye make yourselves strange to me.
ot/job/19.4: And be it indeed [italics]that[/italics] I have erred, mine error remaineth with myself.
ot/job/19.5: If indeed ye will magnify [italics]yourselves[/italics] against me, and plead against me my reproach:
ot/job/19.6: Know now that God hath overthrown me, and hath compassed me with his net.
ot/job/19.7: Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard: I cry aloud, but [italics]there is[/italics] no judgment.
ot/job/19.8: He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.
ot/job/19.9: He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown [italics]from[/italics] my head.
ot/job/19.10: He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.
ot/job/19.11: He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as [italics]one of[/italics] his enemies.
ot/job/19.12: His troops come together, and raise up their way against me, and encamp round about my tabernacle.
ot/job/19.13: He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.
ot/job/19.14: My kinsfolk have failed, and my familiar friends have forgotten me.
ot/job/19.15: They that dwell in mine house, and my maids, count me for a stranger: I am an alien in their sight.
ot/job/19.16: I called my servant, and he gave [italics]me[/italics] no answer; I entreated him with my mouth.
ot/job/19.17: My breath is strange to my wife, though I entreated for the children’s [italics]sake[/italics] of mine own body.
ot/job/19.18: Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.
ot/job/19.19: All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.
ot/job/19.20: My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.
ot/job/19.21: Have pity upon me, have pity upon me, O ye my friends; for the hand of God hath touched me.
ot/job/19.22: Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?
ot/job/19.23: Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book!
ot/job/19.24: That they were graven with an iron pen and lead in the rock for ever!
ot/job/19.25: For I know [italics]that[/italics] my redeemer liveth, and [italics]that[/italics] he shall stand at the latter [italics]day[/italics] upon the earth:
ot/job/19.26: And [italics]though[/italics] after my skin [italics]worms[/italics] destroy this [italics]body[/italics][italics],[/italics] yet in my flesh shall I see God:
ot/job/19.27: Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another; [italics]though[/italics] my reins be consumed within me.
ot/job/19.28: But ye should say, Why persecute we him, seeing the root of the matter is found in me?
ot/job/19.29: Be ye afraid of the sword: for wrath [italics]bringeth[/italics] the punishments of the sword, that ye may know [italics]there is[/italics] a judgment.

ot/lev/16.1: And the Lord spake unto Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron, when they offered before the Lord, and died;
ot/lev/16.2: And the Lord said unto Moses, Speak unto Aaron thy brother, that he come not at all times into the holy [italics]place[/italics] within the veil before the mercy seat, which [italics]is[/italics] upon the ark; that he die not: for I will appear in the cloud upon the mercy seat.
ot/lev/16.3: Thus shall Aaron come into the holy [italics]place:[/italics] with a young bullock for a sin offering, and a ram for a burnt offering.
ot/lev/16.4: He shall put on the holy linen coat, and he shall have the linen breeches upon his flesh, and shall be girded with a linen girdle, and with the linen mitre shall he be attired: these [italics]are[/italics] holy garments; therefore shall he wash his flesh in water, and [italics]so[/italics] put them on.
ot/lev/16.5: And he shall take of the congregation of the children of Israel two kids of the goats for a sin offering, and one ram for a burnt offering.
ot/lev/16.6: And Aaron shall offer his bullock of the sin offering, which [italics]is[/italics] for himself, and make an atonement for himself, and for his house.
ot/lev/16.7: And he shall take the two goats, and present them before the Lord [italics]at[/italics] the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
ot/lev/16.8: And Aaron shall cast lots upon the two goats; one lot for the Lord, and the other lot for the scapegoat.
ot/lev/16.9: And Aaron shall bring the goat upon which the Lord’s lot fell, and offer him [italics]for[/italics] a sin offering.
ot/lev/16.10: But the goat, on which the lot fell to be the scapegoat, shall be presented alive before the Lord, to make an atonement with him, [italics]and[/italics] to let him go for a scapegoat into the wilderness.
ot/lev/16.11: And Aaron shall bring the bullock of the sin offering, which [italics]is[/italics] for himself, and shall make an atonement for himself, and for his house, and shall kill the bullock of the sin offering which [italics]is[/italics] for himself:
ot/lev/16.12: And he shall take a censer full of burning coals of fire from off the altar before the Lord, and his hands full of sweet incense beaten small, and bring [italics]it[/italics] within the veil:
ot/lev/16.13: And he shall put the incense upon the fire before the Lord, that the cloud of the incense may cover the mercy seat that [italics]is[/italics] upon the testimony, that he die not:
ot/lev/16.14: And he shall take of the blood of the bullock, and sprinkle [italics]it[/italics] with his finger upon the mercy seat eastward; and before the mercy seat shall he sprinkle of the blood with his finger seven times.
ot/lev/16.15: ¶ Then shall he kill the goat of the sin offering, that [italics]is[/italics] for the people, and bring his blood within the veil, and do with that blood as he did with the blood of the bullock, and sprinkle it upon the mercy seat, and before the mercy seat:
ot/lev/16.16: And he shall make an atonement for the holy [italics]place,[/italics] because of the uncleanness of the children of Israel, and because of their transgressions in all their sins: and so shall he do for the tabernacle of the congregation, that remaineth among them in the midst of their uncleanness.
ot/lev/16.17: And there shall be no man in the tabernacle of the congregation when he goeth in to make an atonement in the holy [italics]place,[/italics] until he come out, and have made an atonement for himself, and for his household, and for all the congregation of Israel.
ot/lev/16.18: And he shall go out unto the altar that [italics]is[/italics] before the Lord, and make an atonement for it; and shall take of the blood of the bullock, and of the blood of the goat, and put [italics]it[/italics] upon the horns of the altar round about.
ot/lev/16.19: And he shall sprinkle of the blood upon it with his finger seven times, and cleanse it, and hallow it from the uncleanness of the children of Israel.
ot/lev/16.20: ¶ And when he hath made an end of reconciling the holy [italics]place,[/italics] and the tabernacle of the congregation, and the altar, he shall bring the live goat:
ot/lev/16.21: And Aaron shall lay both his hands upon the head of the live goat, and confess over him all the iniquities of the children of Israel, and all their transgressions in all their sins, putting them upon the head of the goat, and shall send [italics]him[/italics] away by the hand of a fit man into the wilderness:
ot/lev/16.22: And the goat shall bear upon him all their iniquities unto a land not inhabited: and he shall let go the goat in the wilderness.
ot/lev/16.23: And Aaron shall come into the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall put off the linen garments, which he put on when he went into the holy [italics]place[/italics][italics],[/italics] and shall leave them there:
ot/lev/16.24: And he shall wash his flesh with water in the holy place, and put on his garments, and come forth, and offer his burnt offering, and the burnt offering of the people, and make an atonement for himself, and for the people.
ot/lev/16.25: And the fat of the sin offering shall he burn upon the altar.
ot/lev/16.26: And he that let go the goat for the scapegoat shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward come into the camp.
ot/lev/16.27: And the bullock [italics]for[/italics] the sin offering, and the goat [italics]for[/italics] the sin offering, whose blood was brought in to make atonement in the holy [italics]place,[/italics] shall [italics]one[/italics] carry forth without the camp; and they shall burn in the fire their skins, and their flesh, and their dung.
ot/lev/16.28: And he that burneth them shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he shall come into the camp.
ot/lev/16.29: ¶ And [italics]this[/italics] shall be a statute for ever unto you: [italics]that[/italics] in the seventh month, on the tenth [italics]day[/italics] of the month, ye shall afflict your souls, and do no work at all, [italics]whether it be[/italics] one of your own country, or a stranger that sojourneth among you:
ot/lev/16.30: For on that day shall [italics]the priest[/italics] make an atonement for you, to cleanse you, [italics]that[/italics] ye may be clean from all your sins before the Lord.
ot/lev/16.31: It [italics]shall be[/italics] a sabbath of rest unto you, and ye shall afflict your souls, by a statute for ever.
ot/lev/16.32: And the priest, whom he shall anoint, and whom he shall consecrate to minister in the priest’s office in his father’s stead, shall make the atonement, and shall put on the linen clothes, [italics]even[/italics] the holy garments:
ot/lev/16.33: And he shall make an atonement for the holy sanctuary, and he shall make an atonement for the tabernacle of the congregation, and for the altar, and he shall make an atonement for the priests, and for all the people of the congregation.
ot/lev/16.34: And this shall be an everlasting statute unto you, to make an atonement for the children of Israel for all their sins once a year. And he did as the Lord commanded Moses.

ot/lev/8.1: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
ot/lev/8.2: Take Aaron and his sons with him, and the garments, and the anointing oil, and a bullock for the sin offering, and two rams, and a basket of unleavened bread;
ot/lev/8.3: And gather thou all the congregation together unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
ot/lev/8.4: And Moses did as the Lord commanded him; and the assembly was gathered together unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
ot/lev/8.5: And Moses said unto the congregation, This [italics]is[/italics] the thing which the Lord commanded to be done.
ot/lev/8.6: And Moses brought Aaron and his sons, and washed them with water.
ot/lev/8.7: And he put upon him the coat, and girded him with the girdle, and clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod upon him, and he girded him with the curious girdle of the ephod, and bound [italics]it[/italics] unto him therewith.
ot/lev/8.8: And he put the breastplate upon him: also he put in the breastplate the Urim and the Thummim.
ot/lev/8.9: And he put the mitre upon his head; also upon the mitre, [italics]even[/italics] upon his forefront, did he put the golden plate, the holy crown; as the Lord commanded Moses.
ot/lev/8.10: And Moses took the anointing oil, and anointed the tabernacle and all that [italics]was[/italics] therein, and sanctified them.
ot/lev/8.11: And he sprinkled thereof upon the altar seven times, and anointed the altar and all his vessels, both the laver and his foot, to sanctify them.
ot/lev/8.12: And he poured of the anointing oil upon Aaron’s head, and anointed him, to sanctify him.
ot/lev/8.13: And Moses brought Aaron’s sons, and put coats upon them, and girded them with girdles, and put bonnets upon them; as the Lord commanded Moses.
ot/lev/8.14: And he brought the bullock for the sin offering: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the bullock for the sin offering.
ot/lev/8.15: And he slew [italics]it;[/italics] and Moses took the blood, and put [italics]it[/italics] upon the horns of the altar round about with his finger, and purified the altar, and poured the blood at the bottom of the altar, and sanctified it, to make reconciliation upon it.
ot/lev/8.16: And he took all the fat that [italics]was[/italics] upon the inwards, and the caul [italics]above[/italics] the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and Moses burned [italics]it[/italics] upon the altar.
ot/lev/8.17: But the bullock, and his hide, his flesh, and his dung, he burnt with fire without the camp; as the Lord commanded Moses.
ot/lev/8.18: ¶ And he brought the ram for the burnt offering: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.
ot/lev/8.19: And he killed [italics]it;[/italics] and Moses sprinkled the blood upon the altar round about.
ot/lev/8.20: And he cut the ram into pieces; and Moses burnt the head, and the pieces, and the fat.
ot/lev/8.21: And he washed the inwards and the legs in water; and Moses burnt the whole ram upon the altar: it [italics]was[/italics] a burnt sacrifice for a sweet savour, [italics]and[/italics] an offering made by fire unto the Lord; as the Lord commanded Moses.
ot/lev/8.22: ¶ And he brought the other ram, the ram of consecration: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.
ot/lev/8.23: And he slew [italics]it;[/italics] and Moses took of the blood of it, and put [italics]it[/italics] upon the tip of Aaron’s right ear, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot.
ot/lev/8.24: And he brought Aaron’s sons, and Moses put of the blood upon the tip of their right ear, and upon the thumbs of their right hands, and upon the great toes of their right feet: and Moses sprinkled the blood upon the altar round about.
ot/lev/8.25: And he took the fat, and the rump, and all the fat that [italics]was[/italics] upon the inwards, and the caul [italics]above[/italics] the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and the right shoulder:
ot/lev/8.26: And out of the basket of unleavened bread, that [italics]was[/italics] before the Lord, he took one unleavened cake, and a cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, and put [italics]them[/italics] on the fat, and upon the right shoulder:
ot/lev/8.27: And he put all upon Aaron’s hands, and upon his sons’ hands, and waved them [italics]for[/italics] a wave offering before the Lord.
ot/lev/8.28: And Moses took them from off their hands, and burnt [italics]them[/italics] on the altar upon the burnt offering: they [italics]were[/italics] consecrations for a sweet savour: it [italics]is[/italics] an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
ot/lev/8.29: And Moses took the breast, and waved it [italics]for[/italics] a wave offering before the Lord: [italics]for[/italics] of the ram of consecration it was Moses’ part; as the Lord commanded Moses.
ot/lev/8.30: And Moses took of the anointing oil, and of the blood which [italics]was[/italics] upon the altar, and sprinkled [italics]it[/italics] upon Aaron, [italics]and[/italics] upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon his sons’ garments with him; and sanctified Aaron, [italics]and[/italics] his garments, and his sons, and his sons’ garments with him.
ot/lev/8.31: ¶ And Moses said unto Aaron and to his sons, Boil the flesh [italics]at[/italics] the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and there eat it with the bread that [italics]is[/italics] in the basket of consecrations, as I commanded, saying, Aaron and his sons shall eat it.
ot/lev/8.32: And that which remaineth of the flesh and of the bread shall ye burn with fire.
ot/lev/8.33: And ye shall not go out of the door of the tabernacle of the congregation [italics]in[/italics] seven days, until the days of your consecration be at an end: for seven days shall he consecrate you.
ot/lev/8.34: As he hath done this day, [italics]so[/italics] the Lord hath commanded to do, to make an atonement for you.
ot/lev/8.35: Therefore shall ye abide [italics]at[/italics] the door of the tabernacle of the congregation day and night seven days, and keep the charge of the Lord, that ye die not: for so I am commanded.
ot/lev/8.36: So Aaron and his sons did all things which the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses.

ot/mal/3.1: Behold, I will send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me: and the Lord, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple, even the messenger of the covenant, whom ye delight in: behold, he shall come, saith the Lord of hosts.
ot/mal/3.2: But who may abide the day of his coming? and who shall stand when he appeareth? for he [italics]is[/italics] like a refiner’s fire, and like fullers’ soap:
ot/mal/3.3: And he shall sit [italics]as[/italics] a refiner and purifier of silver: and he shall purify the sons of Levi, and purge them as gold and silver, that they may offer unto the Lord an offering in righteousness.
ot/mal/3.4: Then shall the offering of Judah and Jerusalem be pleasant unto the Lord, as in the days of old, and as in former years.
ot/mal/3.5: And I will come near to you to judgment; and I will be a swift witness against the sorcerers, and against the adulterers, and against false swearers, and against those that oppress the hireling in [italics]his[/italics] wages, the widow, and the fatherless, and that turn aside the stranger [italics]from his right,[/italics] and fear not me, saith the Lord of hosts.
ot/mal/3.6: For I [italics]am[/italics] the Lord, I change not; therefore ye sons of Jacob are not consumed.
ot/mal/3.7: ¶ Even from the days of your fathers ye are gone away from mine ordinances, and have not kept [italics]them.[/italics] Return unto me, and I will return unto you, saith the Lord of hosts. But ye said, Wherein shall we return?
ot/mal/3.8: ¶ Will a man rob God? Yet ye have robbed me. But ye say, Wherein have we robbed thee? In tithes and offerings.
ot/mal/3.9: Ye [italics]are[/italics] cursed with a curse: for ye have robbed me, [italics]even[/italics] this whole nation.
ot/mal/3.10: Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse, that there may be meat in mine house, and prove me now herewith, saith the Lord of hosts, if I will not open you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that [italics]there shall[/italics] not [italics]be room[/italics] enough [italics]to receive it.[/italics]
ot/mal/3.11: And I will rebuke the devourer for your sakes, and he shall not destroy the fruits of your ground; neither shall your vine cast her fruit before the time in the field, saith the Lord of hosts.
ot/mal/3.12: And all nations shall call you blessed: for ye shall be a delightsome land, saith the Lord of hosts.
ot/mal/3.13: ¶ Your words have been stout against me, saith the Lord. Yet ye say, What have we spoken [italics]so much[/italics] against thee?
ot/mal/3.14: Ye have said, It [italics]is[/italics] vain to serve God: and what profit [italics]is it[/italics] that we have kept his ordinance, and that we have walked mournfully before the Lord of hosts?
ot/mal/3.15: And now we call the proud happy; yea, they that work wickedness are set up; yea, [italics]they that[/italics] tempt God are even delivered.
ot/mal/3.16: ¶ Then they that feared the Lord spake often one to another: and the Lord hearkened, and heard [italics]it,[/italics] and a book of remembrance was written before him for them that feared the Lord, and that thought upon his name.
ot/mal/3.17: And they shall be mine, saith the Lord of hosts, in that day when I make up my jewels; and I will spare them, as a man spareth his own son that serveth him.
ot/mal/3.18: Then shall ye return, and discern between the righteous and the wicked, between him that serveth God and him that serveth him not.

ot/mal/4.1: For, behold, the day cometh, that shall burn as an oven; and all the proud, yea, and all that do wickedly, shall be stubble: and the day that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of hosts, that it shall leave them neither root nor branch.
ot/mal/4.2: ¶ But unto you that fear my name shall the Sun of righteousness arise with healing in his wings; and ye shall go forth, and grow up as calves of the stall.
ot/mal/4.3: And ye shall tread down the wicked; for they shall be ashes under the soles of your feet in the day that I shall do [italics]this,[/italics] saith the Lord of hosts.
ot/mal/4.4: ¶ Remember ye the law of Moses my servant, which I commanded unto him in Horeb for all Israel, [italics]with[/italics] the statutes and judgments.
ot/mal/4.5: ¶ Behold, I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the Lord:
ot/mal/4.6: And he shall turn the heart of the fathers to the children, and the heart of the children to their fathers, lest I come and smite the earth with a curse.

ot/micah/4.1: But in the last days it shall come to pass, [italics]that[/italics] the mountain of the house of the Lord shall be established in the top of the mountains, and it shall be exalted above the hills; and people shall flow unto it.
ot/micah/4.2: And many nations shall come, and say, Come, and let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, and to the house of the God of Jacob; and he will teach us of his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for the law shall go forth of Zion, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
ot/micah/4.3: ¶ And he shall judge among many people, and rebuke strong nations afar off; and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks: nation shall not lift up a sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.
ot/micah/4.4: But they shall sit every man under his vine and under his fig tree; and none shall make [italics]them[/italics] afraid: for the mouth of the Lord of hosts hath spoken [italics]it.[/italics]
ot/micah/4.5: For all people will walk every one in the name of his god, and we will walk in the name of the Lord our God for ever and ever.
ot/micah/4.6: In that day, saith the Lord, will I assemble her that halteth, and I will gather her that is driven out, and her that I have afflicted;
ot/micah/4.7: And I will make her that halted a remnant, and her that was cast far off a strong nation: and the Lord shall reign over them in mount Zion from henceforth, even for ever.
ot/micah/4.8: ¶ And thou, O tower of the flock, the strong hold of the daughter of Zion, unto thee shall it come, even the first dominion; the kingdom shall come to the daughter of Jerusalem.
ot/micah/4.9: Now why dost thou cry out aloud? [italics]is there[/italics] no king in thee? is thy counsellor perished? for pangs have taken thee as a woman in travail.
