ot/micah/4.10: Be in pain, and labour to bring forth, O daughter of Zion, like a woman in travail: for now shalt thou go forth out of the city, and thou shalt dwell in the field, and thou shalt go [italics]even[/italics] to Babylon; there shalt thou be delivered; there the Lord shall redeem thee from the hand of thine enemies.
ot/micah/4.11: ¶ Now also many nations are gathered against thee, that say, Let her be defiled, and let our eye look upon Zion.
ot/micah/4.12: But they know not the thoughts of the Lord, neither understand they his counsel: for he shall gather them as the sheaves into the floor.
ot/micah/4.13: Arise and thresh, O daughter of Zion: for I will make thine horn iron, and I will make thy hoofs brass: and thou shalt beat in pieces many people: and I will consecrate their gain unto the Lord, and their substance unto the Lord of the whole earth.

ot/micah/5.1: Now gather thyself in troops, O daughter of troops: he hath laid siege against us: they shall smite the judge of Israel with a rod upon the cheek.
ot/micah/5.2: But thou, Beth-lehem Ephratah, [italics]though[/italics] thou be little among the thousands of Judah, [italics]yet[/italics] out of thee shall he come forth unto me [italics]that is[/italics] to be ruler in Israel; whose goings forth [italics]have been[/italics] from of old, from everlasting.
ot/micah/5.3: Therefore will he give them up, until the time [italics]that[/italics] she which travaileth hath brought forth: then the remnant of his brethren shall return unto the children of Israel.
ot/micah/5.4: ¶ And he shall stand and feed in the strength of the Lord, in the majesty of the name of the Lord his God; and they shall abide: for now shall he be great unto the ends of the earth.
ot/micah/5.5: And this [italics]man[/italics] shall be the peace, when the Assyrian shall come into our land: and when he shall tread in our palaces, then shall we raise against him seven shepherds, and eight principal men.
ot/micah/5.6: And they shall waste the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nimrod in the entrances thereof: thus shall he deliver [italics]us[/italics] from the Assyrian, when he cometh into our land, and when he treadeth within our borders.
ot/micah/5.7: And the remnant of Jacob shall be in the midst of many people as a dew from the Lord, as the showers upon the grass, that tarrieth not for man, nor waiteth for the sons of men.
ot/micah/5.8: ¶ And the remnant of Jacob shall be among the Gentiles in the midst of many people as a lion among the beasts of the forest, as a young lion among the flocks of sheep: who, if he go through, both treadeth down, and teareth in pieces, and none can deliver.
ot/micah/5.9: Thine hand shall be lifted up upon thine adversaries, and all thine enemies shall be cut off.
ot/micah/5.10: And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Lord, that I will cut off thy horses out of the midst of thee, and I will destroy thy chariots:
ot/micah/5.11: And I will cut off the cities of thy land, and throw down all thy strong holds:
ot/micah/5.12: And I will cut off witchcrafts out of thine hand; and thou shalt have no [italics]more[/italics] soothsayers:
ot/micah/5.13: Thy graven images also will I cut off, and thy standing images out of the midst of thee; and thou shalt no more worship the work of thine hands.
ot/micah/5.14: And I will pluck up thy groves out of the midst of thee: so will I destroy thy cities.
ot/micah/5.15: And I will execute vengeance in anger and fury upon the heathen, such as they have not heard.

ot/num/21.1: And [italics]when[/italics] king Arad the Canaanite, which dwelt in the south, heard tell that Israel came by the way of the spies; then he fought against Israel, and took [italics]some[/italics] of them prisoners.
ot/num/21.2: And Israel vowed a vow unto the Lord, and said, If thou wilt indeed deliver this people into my hand, then I will utterly destroy their cities.
ot/num/21.3: And the Lord hearkened to the voice of Israel, and delivered up the Canaanites; and they utterly destroyed them and their cities: and he called the name of the place Hormah.
ot/num/21.4: ¶ And they journeyed from mount Hor by the way of the Red sea, to compass the land of Edom: and the soul of the people was much discouraged because of the way.
ot/num/21.5: And the people spake against God, and against Moses, Wherefore have ye brought us up out of Egypt to die in the wilderness? for [italics]there is[/italics] no bread, neither [italics]is there any[/italics] water; and our soul loatheth this light bread.
ot/num/21.6: And the Lord sent fiery serpents among the people, and they bit the people; and much people of Israel died.
ot/num/21.7: ¶ Therefore the people came to Moses, and said, We have sinned, for we have spoken against the Lord, and against thee; pray unto the Lord, that he take away the serpents from us. And Moses prayed for the people.
ot/num/21.8: And the Lord said unto Moses, Make thee a fiery serpent, and set it upon a pole: and it shall come to pass, that every one that is bitten, when he looketh upon it, shall live.
ot/num/21.9: And Moses made a serpent of brass, and put it upon a pole, and it came to pass, that if a serpent had bitten any man, when he beheld the serpent of brass, he lived.
ot/num/21.10: ¶ And the children of Israel set forward, and pitched in Oboth.
ot/num/21.11: And they journeyed from Oboth, and pitched at Ije-abarim, in the wilderness which [italics]is[/italics] before Moab, toward the sunrising.
ot/num/21.12: ¶ From thence they removed, and pitched in the valley of Zared.
ot/num/21.13: From thence they removed, and pitched on the other side of Arnon, which [italics]is[/italics] in the wilderness that cometh out of the coasts of the Amorites: for Arnon [italics]is[/italics] the border of Moab, between Moab and the Amorites.
ot/num/21.14: Wherefore it is said in the book of the wars of the Lord, What he did in the Red sea, and in the brooks of Arnon,
ot/num/21.15: And at the stream of the brooks that goeth down to the dwelling of Ar, and lieth upon the border of Moab.
ot/num/21.16: And from thence [italics]they went[/italics] to Beer: that [italics]is[/italics] the well whereof the Lord spake unto Moses, Gather the people together, and I will give them water.
ot/num/21.17: ¶ Then Israel sang this song, Spring up, O well; sing ye unto it:
ot/num/21.18: The princes digged the well, the nobles of the people digged it, by [italics]the direction of[/italics] the lawgiver, with their staves. And from the wilderness [italics]they went[/italics] to Mattanah:
ot/num/21.19: And from Mattanah to Nahaliel: and from Nahaliel to Bamoth:
ot/num/21.20: And from Bamoth [italics]in[/italics] the valley, that [italics]is[/italics] in the country of Moab, to the top of Pisgah, which looketh toward Jeshimon.
ot/num/21.21: ¶ And Israel sent messengers unto Sihon king of the Amorites, saying,
ot/num/21.22: Let me pass through thy land: we will not turn into the fields, or into the vineyards; we will not drink [italics]of[/italics] the waters of the well: [italics]but[/italics] we will go along by the king’s [italics]high[/italics] way, until we be past thy borders.
ot/num/21.23: And Sihon would not suffer Israel to pass through his border: but Sihon gathered all his people together, and went out against Israel into the wilderness: and he came to Jahaz, and fought against Israel.
ot/num/21.24: And Israel smote him with the edge of the sword, and possessed his land from Arnon unto Jabbok, even unto the children of Ammon: for the border of the children of Ammon [italics]was[/italics] strong.
ot/num/21.25: And Israel took all these cities: and Israel dwelt in all the cities of the Amorites, in Heshbon, and in all the villages thereof.
ot/num/21.26: For Heshbon [italics]was[/italics] the city of Sihon the king of the Amorites, who had fought against the former king of Moab, and taken all his land out of his hand, even unto Arnon.
ot/num/21.27: Wherefore they that speak in proverbs say, Come into Heshbon, let the city of Sihon be built and prepared:
ot/num/21.28: For there is a fire gone out of Heshbon, a flame from the city of Sihon: it hath consumed Ar of Moab, [italics]and[/italics] the lords of the high places of Arnon.
ot/num/21.29: Woe to thee, Moab! thou art undone, O people of Chemosh: he hath given his sons that escaped, and his daughters, into captivity unto Sihon king of the Amorites.
ot/num/21.30: We have shot at them; Heshbon is perished even unto Dibon, and we have laid them waste even unto Nophah, which [italics]reacheth[/italics] unto Medeba.
ot/num/21.31: ¶ Thus Israel dwelt in the land of the Amorites.
ot/num/21.32: And Moses sent to spy out Jaazer, and they took the villages thereof, and drove out the Amorites that [italics]were[/italics] there.
ot/num/21.33: ¶ And they turned and went up by the way of Bashan: and Og the king of Bashan went out against them, he, and all his people, to the battle at Edrei.
ot/num/21.34: And the Lord said unto Moses, Fear him not: for I have delivered him into thy hand, and all his people, and his land; and thou shalt do to him as thou didst unto Sihon king of the Amorites, which dwelt at Heshbon.
ot/num/21.35: So they smote him, and his sons, and all his people, until there was none left him alive: and they possessed his land.

ot/ps/110.1: The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.
ot/ps/110.2: The Lord shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.
ot/ps/110.3: Thy people [italics]shall be[/italics] willing in the day of thy power, in the beauties of holiness from the womb of the morning: thou hast the dew of thy youth.
ot/ps/110.4: The Lord hath sworn, and will not repent, Thou [italics]art[/italics] a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek.
ot/ps/110.5: The Lord at thy right hand shall strike through kings in the day of his wrath.
ot/ps/110.6: He shall judge among the heathen, he shall fill [italics]the places[/italics] with the dead bodies; he shall wound the heads over many countries.
ot/ps/110.7: He shall drink of the brook in the way: therefore shall he lift up the head.

ot/ps/130.1: Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O Lord.
ot/ps/130.2: Lord, hear my voice: let thine ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.
ot/ps/130.3: If thou, Lord, shouldest mark iniquities, O Lord, who shall stand?
ot/ps/130.4: But [italics]there is[/italics] forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.
ot/ps/130.5: I wait for the Lord, my soul doth wait, and in his word do I hope.
ot/ps/130.6: My soul [italics]waiteth[/italics] for the Lord more than they that watch for the morning: [italics]I say, more than[/italics] they that watch for the morning.
ot/ps/130.7: Let Israel hope in the Lord: for with the Lord [italics]there is[/italics] mercy, and with him [italics]is[/italics] plenteous redemption.
ot/ps/130.8: And he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.

ot/ps/132.1: Lord, remember David, [italics]and[/italics] all his afflictions:
ot/ps/132.2: How he sware unto the Lord, [italics]and[/italics] vowed unto the mighty [italics]God[/italics] of Jacob;
ot/ps/132.3: Surely I will not come into the tabernacle of my house, nor go up into my bed;
ot/ps/132.4: I will not give sleep to mine eyes, [italics]or[/italics] slumber to mine eyelids,
ot/ps/132.5: Until I find out a place for the Lord, an habitation for the mighty [italics]God[/italics] of Jacob.
ot/ps/132.6: Lo, we heard of it at Ephratah: we found it in the fields of the wood.
ot/ps/132.7: We will go into his tabernacles: we will worship at his footstool.
ot/ps/132.8: Arise, O Lord, into thy rest; thou, and the ark of thy strength.
ot/ps/132.9: Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness; and let thy saints shout for joy.
ot/ps/132.10: For thy servant David’s sake turn not away the face of thine anointed.
ot/ps/132.11: The Lord hath sworn [italics]in[/italics] truth unto David; he will not turn from it; Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.
ot/ps/132.12: If thy children will keep my covenant and my testimony that I shall teach them, their children shall also sit upon thy throne for evermore.
ot/ps/132.13: For the Lord hath chosen Zion; he hath desired [italics]it[/italics] for his habitation.
ot/ps/132.14: This [italics]is[/italics] my rest for ever: here will I dwell; for I have desired it.
ot/ps/132.15: I will abundantly bless her provision: I will satisfy her poor with bread.
ot/ps/132.16: I will also clothe her priests with salvation: and her saints shall shout aloud for joy.
ot/ps/132.17: There will I make the horn of David to bud: I have ordained a lamp for mine anointed.
ot/ps/132.18: His enemies will I clothe with shame: but upon himself shall his crown flourish.

ot/ps/18.1: I will love thee, O Lord, my strength.
ot/ps/18.2: The Lord [italics]is[/italics] my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust; my buckler, and the horn of my salvation, [italics]and[/italics] my high tower.
ot/ps/18.3: I will call upon the Lord, [italics]who is [/italics][italics]worthy[/italics] to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.
ot/ps/18.4: The sorrows of death compassed me, and the floods of ungodly men made me afraid.
ot/ps/18.5: The sorrows of hell compassed me about: the snares of death prevented me.
ot/ps/18.6: In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, [italics]even[/italics] into his ears.
ot/ps/18.7: Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations also of the hills moved and were shaken, because he was wroth.
ot/ps/18.8: There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it.
ot/ps/18.9: He bowed the heavens also, and came down: and darkness [italics]was[/italics] under his feet.
ot/ps/18.10: And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly: yea, he did fly upon the wings of the wind.
ot/ps/18.11: He made darkness his secret place; his pavilion round about him [italics]were[/italics] dark waters [italics]and[/italics] thick clouds of the skies.
ot/ps/18.12: At the brightness [italics]that was[/italics] before him his thick clouds passed, hail [italics]stones[/italics] and coals of fire.
ot/ps/18.13: The Lord also thundered in the heavens, and the Highest gave his voice; hail [italics]stones[/italics] and coals of fire.
ot/ps/18.14: Yea, he sent out his arrows, and scattered them; and he shot out lightnings, and discomfited them.
ot/ps/18.15: Then the channels of waters were seen, and the foundations of the world were discovered at thy rebuke, O Lord, at the blast of the breath of thy nostrils.
ot/ps/18.16: He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.
ot/ps/18.17: He delivered me from my strong enemy, and from them which hated me: for they were too strong for me.
ot/ps/18.18: They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the Lord was my stay.
ot/ps/18.19: He brought me forth also into a large place; he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
ot/ps/18.20: The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
ot/ps/18.21: For I have kept the ways of the Lord, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
ot/ps/18.22: For all his judgments [italics]were[/italics] before me, and I did not put away his statutes from me.
ot/ps/18.23: I was also upright before him, and I kept myself from mine iniquity.
ot/ps/18.24: Therefore hath the Lord recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight.
ot/ps/18.25: With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful; with an upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright;
ot/ps/18.26: With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself froward.
ot/ps/18.27: For thou wilt save the afflicted people; but wilt bring down high looks.
ot/ps/18.28: For thou wilt light my candle: the Lord my God will enlighten my darkness.
ot/ps/18.29: For by thee I have run through a troop; and by my God have I leaped over a wall.
ot/ps/18.30: [italics]As for[/italics] God, his way [italics]is[/italics] perfect: the word of the Lord is tried: he [italics]is[/italics] a buckler to all those that trust in him.
ot/ps/18.31: For who [italics]is[/italics] God save the Lord? or who [italics]is[/italics] a rock save our God?
ot/ps/18.32: [italics]It is[/italics] God that girdeth me with strength, and maketh my way perfect.
ot/ps/18.33: He maketh my feet like hinds’ [italics]feet,[/italics] and setteth me upon my high places.
ot/ps/18.34: He teacheth my hands to war, so that a bow of steel is broken by mine arms.
ot/ps/18.35: Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up, and thy gentleness hath made me great.
ot/ps/18.36: Thou hast enlarged my steps under me, that my feet did not slip.
ot/ps/18.37: I have pursued mine enemies, and overtaken them: neither did I turn again till they were consumed.
ot/ps/18.38: I have wounded them that they were not able to rise: they are fallen under my feet.
ot/ps/18.39: For thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle: thou hast subdued under me those that rose up against me.
ot/ps/18.40: Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies; that I might destroy them that hate me.
ot/ps/18.41: They cried, but [italics]there was[/italics] none to save [italics]them: even[/italics] unto the Lord, but he answered them not.
ot/ps/18.42: Then did I beat them small as the dust before the wind: I did cast them out as the dirt in the streets.
ot/ps/18.43: Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; [italics]and[/italics] thou hast made me the head of the heathen: a people [italics]whom[/italics] I have not known shall serve me.
ot/ps/18.44: As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me: the strangers shall submit themselves unto me.
ot/ps/18.45: The strangers shall fade away, and be afraid out of their close places.
ot/ps/18.46: The Lord liveth; and blessed [italics]be[/italics] my rock; and let the God of my salvation be exalted.
ot/ps/18.47: [italics]It is[/italics] God that avengeth me, and subdueth the people under me.
ot/ps/18.48: He delivereth me from mine enemies: yea, thou liftest me up above those that rise up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
ot/ps/18.49: Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.
ot/ps/18.50: Great deliverance giveth he to his king; and sheweth mercy to his anointed, to David, and to his seed for evermore.

ot/ps/2.1: Why do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing?
ot/ps/2.2: The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the Lord, and against his anointed, [italics]saying,[/italics]
ot/ps/2.3: Let us break their bands asunder, and cast away their cords from us.
ot/ps/2.4: He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh: the Lord shall have them in derision.
ot/ps/2.5: Then shall he speak unto them in his wrath, and vex them in his sore displeasure.
ot/ps/2.6: Yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.
ot/ps/2.7: I will declare the decree: the Lord hath said unto me, Thou [italics]art[/italics] my Son; this day have I begotten thee.
ot/ps/2.8: Ask of me, and I shall give [italics]thee[/italics] the heathen [italics]for[/italics] thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the earth [italics]for[/italics] thy possession.
ot/ps/2.9: Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron; thou shalt dash them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.
ot/ps/2.10: Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be instructed, ye judges of the earth.
ot/ps/2.11: Serve the Lord with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
ot/ps/2.12: Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and ye perish [italics]from[/italics] the way, when his wrath is kindled but a little. Blessed [italics]are[/italics] all they that put their trust in him.

ot/ps/22.1: My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me? [italics]why art thou so[/italics] far from helping me, [italics]and from[/italics] the words of my roaring?
ot/ps/22.2: O my God, I cry in the daytime, but thou hearest not; and in the night season, and am not silent.
ot/ps/22.3: But thou [italics]art[/italics] holy, [italics]O thou[/italics] that inhabitest the praises of Israel.
ot/ps/22.4: Our fathers trusted in thee: they trusted, and thou didst deliver them.
ot/ps/22.5: They cried unto thee, and were delivered: they trusted in thee, and were not confounded.
ot/ps/22.6: But I [italics]am[/italics] a worm, and no man; a reproach of men, and despised of the people.
ot/ps/22.7: All they that see me laugh me to scorn: they shoot out the lip, they shake the head, [italics]saying,[/italics]
ot/ps/22.8: He trusted on the Lord [italics]that[/italics] he would deliver him: let him deliver him, seeing he delighted in him.
ot/ps/22.9: But thou [italics]art[/italics] he that took me out of the womb: thou didst make me hope [italics]when I was[/italics] upon my mother’s breasts.
ot/ps/22.10: I was cast upon thee from the womb: thou [italics]art[/italics] my God from my mother’s belly.
ot/ps/22.11: Be not far from me; for trouble [italics]is[/italics] near; for [italics]there is[/italics] none to help.
ot/ps/22.12: Many bulls have compassed me: strong [italics]bulls[/italics] of Bashan have beset me round.
ot/ps/22.13: They gaped upon me [italics]with[/italics] their mouths, [italics]as[/italics] a ravening and a roaring lion.
ot/ps/22.14: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart is like wax; it is melted in the midst of my bowels.
ot/ps/22.15: My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
ot/ps/22.16: For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.
ot/ps/22.17: I may tell all my bones: they look [italics]and[/italics] stare upon me.
ot/ps/22.18: They part my garments among them, and cast lots upon my vesture.
ot/ps/22.19: But be not thou far from me, O Lord: O my strength, haste thee to help me.
ot/ps/22.20: Deliver my soul from the sword; my darling from the power of the dog.
ot/ps/22.21: Save me from the lion’s mouth: for thou hast heard me from the horns of the unicorns.
ot/ps/22.22: I will declare thy name unto my brethren: in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.
ot/ps/22.23: Ye that fear the Lord, praise him; all ye the seed of Jacob, glorify him; and fear him, all ye the seed of Israel.
ot/ps/22.24: For he hath not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; neither hath he hid his face from him; but when he cried unto him, he heard.
ot/ps/22.25: My praise [italics]shall be[/italics] of thee in the great congregation: I will pay my vows before them that fear him.
ot/ps/22.26: The meek shall eat and be satisfied: they shall praise the Lord that seek him: your heart shall live for ever.
ot/ps/22.27: All the ends of the world shall remember and turn unto the Lord: and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before thee.
ot/ps/22.28: For the kingdom [italics]is[/italics] the Lord’s: and he [italics]is[/italics] the governor among the nations.
ot/ps/22.29: All [italics]they that be[/italics] fat upon earth shall eat and worship: all they that go down to the dust shall bow before him: and none can keep alive his own soul.
ot/ps/22.30: A seed shall serve him; it shall be accounted to the Lord for a generation.
ot/ps/22.31: They shall come, and shall declare his righteousness unto a people that shall be born, that he hath done [italics]this.[/italics]

ot/ps/23.1: The Lord [italics]is[/italics] my shepherd; I shall not want.
ot/ps/23.2: He maketh me to lie down in green pastures: he leadeth me beside the still waters.
ot/ps/23.3: He restoreth my soul: he leadeth me in the paths of righteousness for his name’s sake.
ot/ps/23.4: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou [italics]art[/italics] with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
ot/ps/23.5: Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine enemies: thou anointest my head with oil; my cup runneth over.
ot/ps/23.6: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the Lord for ever.

ot/ps/24.1: The earth [italics]is[/italics] the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.
ot/ps/24.2: For he hath founded it upon the seas, and established it upon the floods.
ot/ps/24.3: Who shall ascend into the hill of the Lord? or who shall stand in his holy place?
ot/ps/24.4: He that hath clean hands, and a pure heart; who hath not lifted up his soul unto vanity, nor sworn deceitfully.
ot/ps/24.5: He shall receive the blessing from the Lord, and righteousness from the God of his salvation.
ot/ps/24.6: This [italics]is[/italics] the generation of them that seek him, that seek thy face, O Jacob. Selah.
ot/ps/24.7: Lift up your heads, O ye gates; and be ye lift up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.
ot/ps/24.8: Who [italics]is[/italics] this King of glory? The Lord strong and mighty, the Lord mighty in battle.
ot/ps/24.9: Lift up your heads, O ye gates; even lift [italics]them[/italics] up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.
ot/ps/24.10: Who is this King of glory? The Lord of hosts, he [italics]is[/italics] the King of glory. Selah.

ot/ps/27.1: The Lord [italics]is[/italics] my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the Lord [italics]is[/italics] the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
ot/ps/27.2: When the wicked, [italics]even[/italics] mine enemies and my foes, came upon me to eat up my flesh, they stumbled and fell.
ot/ps/27.3: Though an host should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear: though war should rise against me, in this [italics]will[/italics] I [italics]be[/italics] confident.
ot/ps/27.4: One [italics]thing[/italics] have I desired of the Lord, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the Lord, and to inquire in his temple.
ot/ps/27.5: For in the time of trouble he shall hide me in his pavilion: in the secret of his tabernacle shall he hide me; he shall set me up upon a rock.
ot/ps/27.6: And now shall mine head be lifted up above mine enemies round about me: therefore will I offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of joy; I will sing, yea, I will sing praises unto the Lord.
ot/ps/27.7: Hear, O Lord, [italics]when[/italics] I cry with my voice: have mercy also upon me, and answer me.
ot/ps/27.8: [italics]When thou saidst,[/italics] Seek ye my face; my heart said unto thee, Thy face, Lord, will I seek.
ot/ps/27.9: Hide not thy face [italics]far[/italics] from me; put not thy servant away in anger: thou hast been my help; leave me not, neither forsake me, O God of my salvation.
ot/ps/27.10: When my father and my mother forsake me, then the Lord will take me up.
ot/ps/27.11: Teach me thy way, O Lord, and lead me in a plain path, because of mine enemies.
ot/ps/27.12: Deliver me not over unto the will of mine enemies: for false witnesses are risen up against me, and such as breathe out cruelty.
ot/ps/27.13: [italics]I had fainted,[/italics] unless I had believed to see the goodness of the Lord in the land of the living.
ot/ps/27.14: Wait on the Lord: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart: wait, I say, on the Lord.

ot/ps/30.1: I will extol thee, O Lord; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.
ot/ps/30.2: O Lord my God, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me.
ot/ps/30.3: O Lord, thou hast brought up my soul from the grave: thou hast kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.
ot/ps/30.4: Sing unto the Lord, O ye saints of his, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.
ot/ps/30.5: For his anger [italics]endureth but[/italics] a moment; in his favour [italics]is[/italics] life: weeping may endure for a night, but joy [italics]cometh[/italics] in the morning.
ot/ps/30.6: And in my prosperity I said, I shall never be moved.
ot/ps/30.7: Lord, by thy favour thou hast made my mountain to stand strong: thou didst hide thy face, [italics]and[/italics] I was troubled.
ot/ps/30.8: I cried to thee, O Lord; and unto the Lord I made supplication.
ot/ps/30.9: What profit [italics]is there[/italics] in my blood, when I go down to the pit? Shall the dust praise thee? shall it declare thy truth?
ot/ps/30.10: Hear, O Lord, and have mercy upon me: Lord, be thou my helper.
ot/ps/30.11: Thou hast turned for me my mourning into dancing: thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness;
ot/ps/30.12: To the end that [italics]my[/italics] glory may sing praise to thee, and not be silent. O Lord my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.

ot/ps/41.1: Blessed [italics]is[/italics] he that considereth the poor: the Lord will deliver him in time of trouble.
ot/ps/41.2: The Lord will preserve him, and keep him alive; [italics]and[/italics] he shall be blessed upon the earth: and thou wilt not deliver him unto the will of his enemies.
ot/ps/41.3: The Lord will strengthen him upon the bed of languishing: thou wilt make all his bed in his sickness.
ot/ps/41.4: I said, Lord, be merciful unto me: heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee.
ot/ps/41.5: Mine enemies speak evil of me, When shall he die, and his name perish?
ot/ps/41.6: And if he come to see [italics]me,[/italics] he speaketh vanity: his heart gathereth iniquity to itself; [italics]when[/italics] he goeth abroad, he telleth [italics]it.[/italics]
ot/ps/41.7: All that hate me whisper together against me: against me do they devise my hurt.
ot/ps/41.8: An evil disease, [italics]say they,[/italics] cleaveth fast unto him: and [italics]now[/italics] that he lieth he shall rise up no more.
ot/ps/41.9: Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, which did eat of my bread, hath lifted up [italics]his[/italics] heel against me.
ot/ps/41.10: But thou, O Lord, be merciful unto me, and raise me up, that I may requite them.
ot/ps/41.11: By this I know that thou favourest me, because mine enemy doth not triumph over me.
ot/ps/41.12: And as for me, thou upholdest me in mine integrity, and settest me before thy face for ever.
ot/ps/41.13: Blessed [italics]be[/italics] the Lord God of Israel from everlasting, and to everlasting. Amen, and Amen.

ot/ps/62.1: Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him [italics]cometh[/italics] my salvation.
ot/ps/62.2: He only [italics]is[/italics] my rock and my salvation; [italics]he is[/italics] my defence; I shall not be greatly moved.
ot/ps/62.3: How long will ye imagine mischief against a man? ye shall be slain all of you: as a bowing wall [italics]shall ye be, and as[/italics] a tottering fence.
ot/ps/62.4: They only consult to cast [italics]him[/italics] down from his excellency: they delight in lies: they bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.
ot/ps/62.5: My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation [italics]is[/italics] from him.
ot/ps/62.6: He only [italics]is[/italics] my rock and my salvation: [italics]he is[/italics] my defence; I shall not be moved.
ot/ps/62.7: In God [italics]is[/italics] my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, [italics]and[/italics] my refuge, [italics]is[/italics] in God.
ot/ps/62.8: Trust in him at all times; ye people, pour out your heart before him: God [italics]is[/italics] a refuge for us. Selah.
ot/ps/62.9: Surely men of low degree [italics]are[/italics] vanity, [italics]and[/italics] men of high degree [italics]are[/italics] a lie: to be laid in the balance, they [italics]are[/italics] altogether [italics]lighter[/italics] than vanity.
ot/ps/62.10: Trust not in oppression, and become not vain in robbery: if riches increase, set not your heart [italics]upon them.[/italics]
ot/ps/62.11: God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power [italics]belongeth[/italics] unto God.
ot/ps/62.12: Also unto thee, O Lord, [italics]belongeth[/italics] mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.

ot/ps/69.1: Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto [italics]my[/italics] soul.
ot/ps/69.2: I sink in deep mire, where [italics]there is[/italics] no standing: I am come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.
ot/ps/69.3: I am weary of my crying: my throat is dried: mine eyes fail while I wait for my God.
ot/ps/69.4: They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of mine head: they that would destroy me, [italics]being[/italics] mine enemies wrongfully, are mighty: then I restored [italics]that[/italics] which I took not away.
ot/ps/69.5: O God, thou knowest my foolishness; and my sins are not hid from thee.
ot/ps/69.6: Let not them that wait on thee, O Lord God of hosts, be ashamed for my sake: let not those that seek thee be confounded for my sake, O God of Israel.
ot/ps/69.7: Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; shame hath covered my face.
ot/ps/69.8: I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my mother’s children.
ot/ps/69.9: For the zeal of thine house hath eaten me up; and the reproaches of them that reproached thee are fallen upon me.
ot/ps/69.10: When I wept, [italics]and chastened[/italics] my soul with fasting, that was to my reproach.
ot/ps/69.11: I made sackcloth also my garment; and I became a proverb to them.
ot/ps/69.12: They that sit in the gate speak against me; and I [italics]was[/italics] the song of the drunkards.
ot/ps/69.13: But as for me, my prayer [italics]is[/italics] unto thee, O Lord, [italics]in[/italics] an acceptable time: O God, in the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation.
ot/ps/69.14: Deliver me out of the mire, and let me not sink: let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the deep waters.
ot/ps/69.15: Let not the waterflood overflow me, neither let the deep swallow me up, and let not the pit shut her mouth upon me.
ot/ps/69.16: Hear me, O Lord; for thy lovingkindness [italics]is[/italics] good: turn unto me according to the multitude of thy tender mercies.
ot/ps/69.17: And hide not thy face from thy servant; for I am in trouble: hear me speedily.
ot/ps/69.18: Draw nigh unto my soul, [italics]and[/italics] redeem it: deliver me because of mine enemies.
ot/ps/69.19: Thou hast known my reproach, and my shame, and my dishonour: mine adversaries [italics]are[/italics] all before thee.
ot/ps/69.20: Reproach hath broken my heart; and I am full of heaviness: and I looked [italics]for some[/italics] to take pity, but [italics]there was[/italics] none; and for comforters, but I found none.
ot/ps/69.21: They gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
ot/ps/69.22: Let their table become a snare before them: and [italics]that which should have been[/italics] for [italics]their[/italics] welfare, [italics]let it become[/italics] a trap.
ot/ps/69.23: Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not; and make their loins continually to shake.
ot/ps/69.24: Pour out thine indignation upon them, and let thy wrathful anger take hold of them.
ot/ps/69.25: Let their habitation be desolate; [italics]and[/italics] let none dwell in their tents.
ot/ps/69.26: For they persecute [italics]him[/italics] whom thou hast smitten; and they talk to the grief of those whom thou hast wounded.
ot/ps/69.27: Add iniquity unto their iniquity: and let them not come into thy righteousness.
ot/ps/69.28: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and not be written with the righteous.
ot/ps/69.29: But I [italics]am[/italics] poor and sorrowful: let thy salvation, O God, set me up on high.
ot/ps/69.30: I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.
ot/ps/69.31: [italics]This[/italics] also shall please the Lord better than an ox [italics]or[/italics] bullock that hath horns and hoofs.
ot/ps/69.32: The humble shall see [italics]this, and[/italics] be glad: and your heart shall live that seek God.
ot/ps/69.33: For the Lord heareth the poor, and despiseth not his prisoners.
ot/ps/69.34: Let the heaven and earth praise him, the seas, and every thing that moveth therein.
ot/ps/69.35: For God will save Zion, and will build the cities of Judah: that they may dwell there, and have it in possession.
ot/ps/69.36: The seed also of his servants shall inherit it: and they that love his name shall dwell therein.

ot/ps/72.1: Give the king thy judgments, O God, and thy righteousness unto the king’s son.
ot/ps/72.2: He shall judge thy people with righteousness, and thy poor with judgment.
ot/ps/72.3: The mountains shall bring peace to the people, and the little hills, by righteousness.
ot/ps/72.4: He shall judge the poor of the people, he shall save the children of the needy, and shall break in pieces the oppressor.
ot/ps/72.5: They shall fear thee as long as the sun and moon endure, throughout all generations.
ot/ps/72.6: He shall come down like rain upon the mown grass: as showers [italics]that[/italics] water the earth.
ot/ps/72.7: In his days shall the righteous flourish; and abundance of peace so long as the moon endureth.
ot/ps/72.8: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earth.
ot/ps/72.9: They that dwell in the wilderness shall bow before him; and his enemies shall lick the dust.
ot/ps/72.10: The kings of Tarshish and of the isles shall bring presents: the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.
ot/ps/72.11: Yea, all kings shall fall down before him: all nations shall serve him.
ot/ps/72.12: For he shall deliver the needy when he crieth; the poor also, and [italics]him[/italics] that hath no helper.
ot/ps/72.13: He shall spare the poor and needy, and shall save the souls of the needy.
ot/ps/72.14: He shall redeem their soul from deceit and violence: and precious shall their blood be in his sight.
ot/ps/72.15: And he shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba: prayer also shall be made for him continually; [italics]and[/italics] daily shall he be praised.
ot/ps/72.16: There shall be an handful of corn in the earth upon the top of the mountains; the fruit thereof shall shake like Lebanon: and [italics]they[/italics] of the city shall flourish like grass of the earth.
ot/ps/72.17: His name shall endure for ever: his name shall be continued as long as the sun: and [italics]men[/italics] shall be blessed in him: all nations shall call him blessed.
ot/ps/72.18: Blessed [italics]be[/italics] the Lord God, the God of Israel, who only doeth wondrous things.
ot/ps/72.19: And blessed [italics]be[/italics] his glorious name for ever: and let the whole earth be filled [italics]with[/italics] his glory; Amen, and Amen.
ot/ps/72.20: The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.

ot/ps/83.1: Keep not thou silence, O God: hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God.
ot/ps/83.2: For, lo, thine enemies make a tumult: and they that hate thee have lifted up the head.
ot/ps/83.3: They have taken crafty counsel against thy people, and consulted against thy hidden ones.
ot/ps/83.4: They have said, Come, and let us cut them off from [italics]being[/italics] a nation; that the name of Israel may be no more in remembrance.
ot/ps/83.5: For they have consulted together with one consent: they are confederate against thee:
ot/ps/83.6: The tabernacles of Edom, and the Ishmaelites; of Moab, and the Hagarenes;
ot/ps/83.7: Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek; the Philistines with the inhabitants of Tyre;
ot/ps/83.8: Assur also is joined with them: they have holpen the children of Lot. Selah.
ot/ps/83.9: Do unto them as [italics]unto[/italics] the Midianites; as [italics]to[/italics] Sisera, as [italics]to[/italics] Jabin, at the brook of Kison:
ot/ps/83.10: [italics]Which[/italics] perished at En-dor: they became [italics]as[/italics] dung for the earth.
ot/ps/83.11: Make their nobles like Oreb, and like Zeeb: yea, all their princes as Zebah, and as Zalmunna:
ot/ps/83.12: Who said, Let us take to ourselves the houses of God in possession.
ot/ps/83.13: O my God, make them like a wheel; as the stubble before the wind.
ot/ps/83.14: As the fire burneth a wood, and as the flame setteth the mountains on fire;
ot/ps/83.15: So persecute them with thy tempest, and make them afraid with thy storm.
ot/ps/83.16: Fill their faces with shame; that they may seek thy name, O Lord.
ot/ps/83.17: Let them be confounded and troubled for ever; yea, let them be put to shame, and perish:
ot/ps/83.18: That [italics]men[/italics] may know that thou, whose name alone [italics]is[/italics] Jehovah, [italics]art[/italics] the most high over all the earth.

ot/zech/12.1: The burden of the word of the Lord for Israel, saith the Lord, which stretcheth forth the heavens, and layeth the foundation of the earth, and formeth the spirit of man within him.
ot/zech/12.2: Behold, I will make Jerusalem a cup of trembling unto all the people round about, when they shall be in the siege both against Judah [italics]and[/italics] against Jerusalem.
ot/zech/12.3: ¶ And in that day will I make Jerusalem a burdensome stone for all people: all that burden themselves with it shall be cut in pieces, though all the people of the earth be gathered together against it.
ot/zech/12.4: In that day, saith the Lord, I will smite every horse with astonishment, and his rider with madness: and I will open mine eyes upon the house of Judah, and will smite every horse of the people with blindness.
ot/zech/12.5: And the governors of Judah shall say in their heart, The inhabitants of Jerusalem [italics]shall be[/italics] my strength in the Lord of hosts their God.
ot/zech/12.6: ¶ In that day will I make the governors of Judah like an hearth of fire among the wood, and like a torch of fire in a sheaf; and they shall devour all the people round about, on the right hand and on the left: and Jerusalem shall be inhabited again in her own place, [italics]even[/italics] in Jerusalem.
ot/zech/12.7: The Lord also shall save the tents of Judah first, that the glory of the house of David and the glory of the inhabitants of Jerusalem do not magnify [italics]themselves[/italics] against Judah.
ot/zech/12.8: In that day shall the Lord defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem; and he that is feeble among them at that day shall be as David; and the house of David [italics]shall be[/italics] as God, as the angel of the Lord before them.
ot/zech/12.9: ¶ And it shall come to pass in that day, [italics]that[/italics] I will seek to destroy all the nations that come against Jerusalem.
ot/zech/12.10: And I will pour upon the house of David, and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the spirit of grace and of supplications: and they shall look upon me whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn for him, as one mourneth for [italics]his[/italics] only [italics]son,[/italics] and shall be in bitterness for him, as one that is in bitterness for [italics]his[/italics] firstborn.
ot/zech/12.11: In that day shall there be a great mourning in Jerusalem, as the mourning of Hadadrimmon in the valley of Megiddon.
ot/zech/12.12: And the land shall mourn, every family apart; the family of the house of David apart, and their wives apart; the family of the house of Nathan apart, and their wives apart;
ot/zech/12.13: The family of the house of Levi apart, and their wives apart; the family of Shimei apart, and their wives apart;
ot/zech/12.14: All the families that remain, every family apart, and their wives apart.

ot/zech/13.1: In that day there shall be a fountain opened to the house of David and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem for sin and for uncleanness.
ot/zech/13.2: ¶ And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Lord of hosts, [italics]that[/italics] I will cut off the names of the idols out of the land, and they shall no more be remembered: and also I will cause the prophets and the unclean spirit to pass out of the land.
ot/zech/13.3: And it shall come to pass, [italics]that[/italics] when any shall yet prophesy, then his father and his mother that begat him shall say unto him, Thou shalt not live; for thou speakest lies in the name of the Lord: and his father and his mother that begat him shall thrust him through when he prophesieth.
ot/zech/13.4: And it shall come to pass in that day, [italics]that[/italics] the prophets shall be ashamed every one of his vision, when he hath prophesied; neither shall they wear a rough garment to deceive:
ot/zech/13.5: But he shall say, I [italics]am[/italics] no prophet, I [italics]am[/italics] an husbandman; for man taught me to keep cattle from my youth.
ot/zech/13.6: And [italics]one[/italics] shall say unto him, What [italics]are[/italics] these wounds in thine hands? Then he shall answer, [italics]Those[/italics] with which I was wounded [italics]in[/italics] the house of my friends.
ot/zech/13.7: ¶ Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, and against the man [italics]that is[/italics] my fellow, saith the Lord of hosts: smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered: and I will turn mine hand upon the little ones.
ot/zech/13.8: And it shall come to pass, [italics]that[/italics] in all the land, saith the Lord, two parts therein shall be cut off [italics]and[/italics] die; but the third shall be left therein.
ot/zech/13.9: And I will bring the third part through the fire, and will refine them as silver is refined, and will try them as gold is tried: they shall call on my name, and I will hear them: I will say, It [italics]is[/italics] my people: and they shall say, The Lord [italics]is[/italics] my God.

ot/zech/14.1: Behold, the day of the Lord cometh, and thy spoil shall be divided in the midst of thee.
ot/zech/14.2: For I will gather all nations against Jerusalem to battle; and the city shall be taken, and the houses rifled, and the women ravished; and half of the city shall go forth into captivity, and the residue of the people shall not be cut off from the city.
ot/zech/14.3: Then shall the Lord go forth, and fight against those nations, as when he fought in the day of battle.
ot/zech/14.4: ¶ And his feet shall stand in that day upon the mount of Olives, which [italics]is[/italics] before Jerusalem on the east, and the mount of Olives shall cleave in the midst thereof toward the east and toward the west, [italics]and there shall be[/italics] a very great valley; and half of the mountain shall remove toward the north, and half of it toward the south.
ot/zech/14.5: And ye shall flee [italics]to[/italics] the valley of the mountains; for the valley of the mountains shall reach unto Azal: yea, ye shall flee, like as ye fled from before the earthquake in the days of Uzziah king of Judah: and the Lord my God shall come, [italics]and[/italics] all the saints with thee.
ot/zech/14.6: And it shall come to pass in that day, [italics]that[/italics] the light shall not be clear, [italics]nor[/italics] dark:
ot/zech/14.7: But it shall be one day which shall be known to the Lord, not day, nor night: but it shall come to pass, [italics]that[/italics] at evening time it shall be light.
ot/zech/14.8: And it shall be in that day, [italics]that[/italics] living waters shall go out from Jerusalem; half of them toward the former sea, and half of them toward the hinder sea: in summer and in winter shall it be.
ot/zech/14.9: And the Lord shall be king over all the earth: in that day shall there be one Lord, and his name one.
ot/zech/14.10: All the land shall be turned as a plain from Geba to Rimmon south of Jerusalem: and it shall be lifted up, and inhabited in her place, from Benjamin’s gate unto the place of the first gate, unto the corner gate, and [italics]from[/italics] the tower of Hananeel unto the king’s winepresses.
ot/zech/14.11: And [italics]men[/italics] shall dwell in it, and there shall be no more utter destruction; but Jerusalem shall be safely inhabited.
ot/zech/14.12: ¶ And this shall be the plague wherewith the Lord will smite all the people that have fought against Jerusalem; Their flesh shall consume away while they stand upon their feet, and their eyes shall consume away in their holes, and their tongue shall consume away in their mouth.
ot/zech/14.13: And it shall come to pass in that day, [italics]that[/italics] a great tumult from the Lord shall be among them; and they shall lay hold every one on the hand of his neighbour, and his hand shall rise up against the hand of his neighbour.
ot/zech/14.14: And Judah also shall fight at Jerusalem; and the wealth of all the heathen round about shall be gathered together, gold, and silver, and apparel, in great abundance.
ot/zech/14.15: And so shall be the plague of the horse, of the mule, of the camel, and of the ass, and of all the beasts that shall be in these tents, as this plague.
ot/zech/14.16: ¶ And it shall come to pass, [italics]that[/italics] every one that is left of all the nations which came against Jerusalem shall even go up from year to year to worship the King, the Lord of hosts, and to keep the feast of tabernacles.
ot/zech/14.17: And it shall be, [italics]that[/italics] whoso will not come up of [italics]all[/italics] the families of the earth unto Jerusalem to worship the King, the Lord of hosts, even upon them shall be no rain.
ot/zech/14.18: And if the family of Egypt go not up, and come not, that [italics]have[/italics] no [italics]rain;[/italics] there shall be the plague, wherewith the Lord will smite the heathen that come not up to keep the feast of tabernacles.
ot/zech/14.19: This shall be the punishment of Egypt, and the punishment of all nations that come not up to keep the feast of tabernacles.
ot/zech/14.20: ¶ In that day shall there be upon the bells of the horses, Holiness unto the Lord; and the pots in the Lord’s house shall be like the bowls before the altar.
ot/zech/14.21: Yea, every pot in Jerusalem and in Judah shall be holiness unto the Lord of hosts: and all they that sacrifice shall come and take of them, and seethe therein: and in that day there shall be no more the Canaanite in the house of the Lord of hosts.

ot/zech/9.1: The burden of the word of the Lord in the land of Hadrach, and Damascus [italics]shall be[/italics] the rest thereof: when the eyes of man, as of all the tribes of Israel, [italics]shall be[/italics] toward the Lord.
ot/zech/9.2: And Hamath also shall border thereby; Tyrus, and Zidon, though it be very wise.
ot/zech/9.3: And Tyrus did build herself a strong hold, and heaped up silver as the dust, and fine gold as the mire of the streets.
ot/zech/9.4: Behold, the Lord will cast her out, and he will smite her power in the sea; and she shall be devoured with fire.
ot/zech/9.5: Ashkelon shall see [italics]it,[/italics] and fear; Gaza also [italics]shall see it,[/italics] and be very sorrowful, and Ekron; for her expectation shall be ashamed; and the king shall perish from Gaza, and Ashkelon shall not be inhabited.
ot/zech/9.6: And a bastard shall dwell in Ashdod, and I will cut off the pride of the Philistines.
ot/zech/9.7: And I will take away his blood out of his mouth, and his abominations from between his teeth: but he that remaineth, even he, [italics]shall be[/italics] for our God, and he shall be as a governor in Judah, and Ekron as a Jebusite.
ot/zech/9.8: And I will encamp about mine house because of the army, because of him that passeth by, and because of him that returneth: and no oppressor shall pass through them any more: for now have I seen with mine eyes.
ot/zech/9.9: ¶ Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion; shout, O daughter of Jerusalem: behold, thy King cometh unto thee: he [italics]is[/italics] just, and having salvation; lowly, and riding upon an ass, and upon a colt the foal of an ass.
ot/zech/9.10: And I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim, and the horse from Jerusalem, and the battle bow shall be cut off: and he shall speak peace unto the heathen: and his dominion [italics]shall be[/italics] from sea [italics]even[/italics] to sea, and from the river [italics]even[/italics] to the ends of the earth.
ot/zech/9.11: As for thee also, by the blood of thy covenant I have sent forth thy prisoners out of the pit wherein [italics]is[/italics] no water.
ot/zech/9.12: ¶ Turn you to the strong hold, ye prisoners of hope: even to day do I declare [italics]that[/italics] I will render double unto thee;
ot/zech/9.13: When I have bent Judah for me, filled the bow with Ephraim, and raised up thy sons, O Zion, against thy sons, O Greece, and made thee as the sword of a mighty man.
ot/zech/9.14: And the Lord shall be seen over them, and his arrow shall go forth as the lightning: and the Lord God shall blow the trumpet, and shall go with whirlwinds of the south.
ot/zech/9.15: The Lord of hosts shall defend them; and they shall devour, and subdue with sling stones; and they shall drink, [italics]and[/italics] make a noise as through wine; and they shall be filled like bowls, [italics]and[/italics] as the corners of the altar.
ot/zech/9.16: And the Lord their God shall save them in that day as the flock of his people: for [italics]they shall be as[/italics] the stones of a crown, lifted up as an ensign upon his land.
ot/zech/9.17: For how great [italics]is[/italics] his goodness, and how great [italics]is[/italics] his beauty! corn shall make the young men cheerful, and new wine the maids.

pgp/abr/1.1: In the land of the Chaldeans, at the residence of my fathers, I, Abraham, saw that it was needful for me to obtain another place of residence;
pgp/abr/1.2: And, finding there was greater happiness and peace and rest for me, I sought for the blessings of the fathers, and the right whereunto I should be ordained to administer the same; having been myself a follower of righteousness, desiring also to be one who possessed great knowledge, and to be a greater follower of righteousness, and to possess a greater knowledge, and to be a father of many nations, a prince of peace, and desiring to receive instructions, and to keep the commandments of God, I became a rightful heir, a High Priest, holding the right belonging to the fathers.
pgp/abr/1.3: It was conferred upon me from the fathers; it came down from the fathers, from the beginning of time, yea, even from the beginning, or before the foundation of the earth, down to the present time, even the right of the firstborn, or the first man, who is Adam, or first father, through the fathers unto me.
pgp/abr/1.4: I sought for mine appointment unto the Priesthood according to the appointment of God unto the fathers concerning the seed.
pgp/abr/1.5: My fathers, having turned from their righteousness, and from the holy commandments which the Lord their God had given unto them, unto the worshiping of the gods of the heathen, utterly refused to hearken to my voice;
pgp/abr/1.6: For their hearts were set to do evil, and were wholly turned to the god of Elkenah, and the god of Libnah, and the god of Mahmackrah, and the god of Korash, and the god of Pharaoh, king of Egypt;
pgp/abr/1.7: Therefore they turned their hearts to the sacrifice of the heathen in offering up their children unto these dumb idols, and hearkened not unto my voice, but endeavored to take away my life by the hand of the priest of Elkenah. The priest of Elkenah was also the priest of Pharaoh.
pgp/abr/1.8: Now, at this time it was the custom of the priest of Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, to offer up upon the altar which was built in the land of Chaldea, for the offering unto these strange gods, men, women, and children.
pgp/abr/1.9: And it came to pass that the priest made an offering unto the god of Pharaoh, and also unto the god of Shagreel, even after the manner of the Egyptians. Now the god of Shagreel was the sun.
pgp/abr/1.10: Even the thank-offering of a child did the priest of Pharaoh offer upon the altar which stood by the hill called Potiphar’s Hill, at the head of the plain of Olishem.
pgp/abr/1.11: Now, this priest had offered upon this altar three virgins at one time, who were the daughters of Onitah, one of the royal descent directly from the loins of Ham. These virgins were offered up because of their virtue; they would not bow down to worship gods of wood or of stone, therefore they were killed upon this altar, and it was done after the manner of the Egyptians.
pgp/abr/1.12: And it came to pass that the priests laid violence upon me, that they might slay me also, as they did those virgins upon this altar; and that you may have a knowledge of this altar, I will refer you to the representation at the commencement of this record.
pgp/abr/1.13: It was made after the form of a bedstead, such as was had among the Chaldeans, and it stood before the gods of Elkenah, Libnah, Mahmackrah, Korash, and also a god like unto that of Pharaoh, king of Egypt.
pgp/abr/1.14: That you may have an understanding of these gods, I have given you the fashion of them in the figures at the beginning, which manner of figures is called by the Chaldeans Rahleenos, which signifies hieroglyphics.
pgp/abr/1.15: And as they lifted up their hands upon me, that they might offer me up and take away my life, behold, I lifted up my voice unto the Lord my God, and the Lord hearkened and heard, and he filled me with the vision of the Almighty, and the angel of his presence stood by me, and immediately unloosed my bands;
pgp/abr/1.16: And his voice was unto me: Abraham, Abraham, behold, my name is Jehovah, and I have heard thee, and have come down to deliver thee, and to take thee away from thy father’s house, and from all thy kinsfolk, into a strange land which thou knowest not of;
pgp/abr/1.17: And this because they have turned their hearts away from me, to worship the god of Elkenah, and the god of Libnah, and the god of Mahmackrah, and the god of Korash, and the god of Pharaoh, king of Egypt; therefore I have come down to visit them, and to destroy him who hath lifted up his hand against thee, Abraham, my son, to take away thy life.
pgp/abr/1.18: Behold, I will lead thee by my hand, and I will take thee, to put upon thee my name, even the Priesthood of thy father, and my power shall be over thee.
pgp/abr/1.19: As it was with Noah so shall it be with thee; but through thy ministry my name shall be known in the earth forever, for I am thy God.
pgp/abr/1.20: Behold, Potiphar’s Hill was in the land of Ur, of Chaldea. And the Lord broke down the altar of Elkenah, and of the gods of the land, and utterly destroyed them, and smote the priest that he died; and there was great mourning in Chaldea, and also in the court of Pharaoh; which Pharaoh signifies king by royal blood.
pgp/abr/1.21: Now this king of Egypt was a descendant from the loins of Ham, and was a partaker of the blood of the Canaanites by birth.
pgp/abr/1.22: From this descent sprang all the Egyptians, and thus the blood of the Canaanites was preserved in the land.
pgp/abr/1.23: The land of Egypt being first discovered by a woman, who was the daughter of Ham, and the daughter of Egyptus, which in the Chaldean signifies Egypt, which signifies that which is forbidden;
pgp/abr/1.24: When this woman discovered the land it was under water, who afterward settled her sons in it; and thus, from Ham, sprang that race which preserved the curse in the land.
pgp/abr/1.25: Now the first government of Egypt was established by Pharaoh, the eldest son of Egyptus, the daughter of Ham, and it was after the manner of the government of Ham, which was patriarchal.
pgp/abr/1.26: Pharaoh, being a righteous man, established his kingdom and judged his people wisely and justly all his days, seeking earnestly to imitate that order established by the fathers in the first generations, in the days of the first patriarchal reign, even in the reign of Adam, and also of Noah, his father, who blessed him with the blessings of the earth, and with the blessings of wisdom, but cursed him as pertaining to the Priesthood.
pgp/abr/1.27: Now, Pharaoh being of that lineage by which he could not have the right of Priesthood, notwithstanding the Pharaohs would fain claim it from Noah, through Ham, therefore my father was led away by their idolatry;
pgp/abr/1.28: But I shall endeavor, hereafter, to delineate the chronology running back from myself to the beginning of the creation, for the records have come into my hands, which I hold unto this present time.
pgp/abr/1.29: Now, after the priest of Elkenah was smitten that he died, there came a fulfilment of those things which were said unto me concerning the land of Chaldea, that there should be a famine in the land.
pgp/abr/1.30: Accordingly a famine prevailed throughout all the land of Chaldea, and my father was sorely tormented because of the famine, and he repented of the evil which he had determined against me, to take away my life.
pgp/abr/1.31: But the records of the fathers, even the patriarchs, concerning the right of Priesthood, the Lord my God preserved in mine own hands; therefore a knowledge of the beginning of the creation, and also of the planets, and of the stars, as they were made known unto the fathers, have I kept even unto this day, and I shall endeavor to write some of these things upon this record, for the benefit of my posterity that shall come after me.

pgp/abr/2.1: Now the Lord God caused the famine to wax sore in the land of Ur, insomuch that Haran, my brother, died; but Terah, my father, yet lived in the land of Ur, of the Chaldees.
pgp/abr/2.2: And it came to pass that I, Abraham, took Sarai to wife, and Nahor, my brother, took Milcah to wife, who was the daughter of Haran.
pgp/abr/2.3: Now the Lord had said unto me: Abraham, get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father’s house, unto a land that I will show thee.
pgp/abr/2.4: Therefore I left the land of Ur, of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan; and I took Lot, my brother’s son, and his wife, and Sarai my wife; and also my father followed after me, unto the land which we denominated Haran.
pgp/abr/2.5: And the famine abated; and my father tarried in Haran and dwelt there, as there were many flocks in Haran; and my father turned again unto his idolatry, therefore he continued in Haran.
pgp/abr/2.6: But I, Abraham, and Lot, my brother’s son, prayed unto the Lord, and the Lord appeared unto me, and said unto me: Arise, and take Lot with thee; for I have purposed to take thee away out of Haran, and to make of thee a minister to bear my name in a strange land which I will give unto thy seed after thee for an everlasting possession, when they hearken to my voice.
pgp/abr/2.7: For I am the Lord thy God; I dwell in heaven; the earth is my footstool; I stretch my hand over the sea, and it obeys my voice; I cause the wind and the fire to be my chariot; I say to the mountains—Depart hence—and behold, they are taken away by a whirlwind, in an instant, suddenly.
pgp/abr/2.8: My name is Jehovah, and I know the end from the beginning; therefore my hand shall be over thee.
pgp/abr/2.9: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee above measure, and make thy name great among all nations, and thou shalt be a blessing unto thy seed after thee, that in their hands they shall bear this ministry and Priesthood unto all nations;
pgp/abr/2.10: And I will bless them through thy name; for as many as receive this Gospel shall be called after thy name, and shall be accounted thy seed, and shall rise up and bless thee, as their father;
pgp/abr/2.11: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse them that curse thee; and in thee (that is, in thy Priesthood) and in thy seed (that is, thy Priesthood), for I give unto thee a promise that this right shall continue in thee, and in thy seed after thee (that is to say, the literal seed, or the seed of the body) shall all the families of the earth be blessed, even with the blessings of the Gospel, which are the blessings of salvation, even of life eternal.
pgp/abr/2.12: Now, after the Lord had withdrawn from speaking to me, and withdrawn his face from me, I said in my heart: Thy servant has sought thee earnestly; now I have found thee;
pgp/abr/2.13: Thou didst send thine angel to deliver me from the gods of Elkenah, and I will do well to hearken unto thy voice, therefore let thy servant rise up and depart in peace.
pgp/abr/2.14: So I, Abraham, departed as the Lord had said unto me, and Lot with me; and I, Abraham, was sixty and two years old when I departed out of Haran.
pgp/abr/2.15: And I took Sarai, whom I took to wife when I was in Ur, in Chaldea, and Lot, my brother’s son, and all our substance that we had gathered, and the souls that we had won in Haran, and came forth in the way to the land of Canaan, and dwelt in tents as we came on our way;
pgp/abr/2.16: Therefore, eternity was our covering and our rock and our salvation, as we journeyed from Haran by the way of Jershon, to come to the land of Canaan.
pgp/abr/2.17: Now I, Abraham, built an altar in the land of Jershon, and made an offering unto the Lord, and prayed that the famine might be turned away from my father’s house, that they might not perish.
pgp/abr/2.18: And then we passed from Jershon through the land unto the place of Sechem; it was situated in the plains of Moreh, and we had already come into the borders of the land of the Canaanites, and I offered sacrifice there in the plains of Moreh, and called on the Lord devoutly, because we had already come into the land of this idolatrous nation.
pgp/abr/2.19: And the Lord appeared unto me in answer to my prayers, and said unto me: Unto thy seed will I give this land.
pgp/abr/2.20: And I, Abraham, arose from the place of the altar which I had built unto the Lord, and removed from thence unto a mountain on the east of Bethel, and pitched my tent there, Bethel on the west, and Hai on the east; and there I built another altar unto the Lord, and called again upon the name of the Lord.
pgp/abr/2.21: And I, Abraham, journeyed, going on still towards the south; and there was a continuation of a famine in the land; and I, Abraham, concluded to go down into Egypt, to sojourn there, for the famine became very grievous.
pgp/abr/2.22: And it came to pass when I was come near to enter into Egypt, the Lord said unto me: Behold, Sarai, thy wife, is a very fair woman to look upon;
pgp/abr/2.23: Therefore it shall come to pass, when the Egyptians shall see her, they will say—She is his wife; and they will kill you, but they will save her alive; therefore see that ye do on this wise:
pgp/abr/2.24: Let her say unto the Egyptians, she is thy sister, and thy soul shall live.
pgp/abr/2.25: And it came to pass that I, Abraham, told Sarai, my wife, all that the Lord had said unto me—Therefore say unto them, I pray thee, thou art my sister, that it may be well with me for thy sake, and my soul shall live because of thee.

pgp/abr/3.1: And I, Abraham, had the Urim and Thummim, which the Lord my God had given unto me, in Ur of the Chaldees;
pgp/abr/3.2: And I saw the stars, that they were very great, and that one of them was nearest unto the throne of God; and there were many great ones which were near unto it;
pgp/abr/3.3: And the Lord said unto me: These are the governing ones; and the name of the great one is Kolob, because it is near unto me, for I am the Lord thy God: I have set this one to govern all those which belong to the same order as that upon which thou standest.
pgp/abr/3.4: And the Lord said unto me, by the Urim and Thummim, that Kolob was after the manner of the Lord, according to its times and seasons in the revolutions thereof; that one revolution was a day unto the Lord, after his manner of reckoning, it being one thousand years according to the time appointed unto that whereon thou standest. This is the reckoning of the Lord’s time, according to the reckoning of Kolob.
pgp/abr/3.5: And the Lord said unto me: The planet which is the lesser light, lesser than that which is to rule the day, even the night, is above or greater than that upon which thou standest in point of reckoning, for it moveth in order more slow; this is in order because it standeth above the earth upon which thou standest, therefore the reckoning of its time is not so many as to its number of days, and of months, and of years.
pgp/abr/3.6: And the Lord said unto me: Now, Abraham, these two facts exist, behold thine eyes see it; it is given unto thee to know the times of reckoning, and the set time, yea, the set time of the earth upon which thou standest, and the set time of the greater light which is set to rule the day, and the set time of the lesser light which is set to rule the night.
pgp/abr/3.7: Now the set time of the lesser light is a longer time as to its reckoning than the reckoning of the time of the earth upon which thou standest.
pgp/abr/3.8: And where these two facts exist, there shall be another fact above them, that is, there shall be another planet whose reckoning of time shall be longer still;
pgp/abr/3.9: And thus there shall be the reckoning of the time of one planet above another, until thou come nigh unto Kolob, which Kolob is after the reckoning of the Lord’s time; which Kolob is set nigh unto the throne of God, to govern all those planets which belong to the same order as that upon which thou standest.
pgp/abr/3.10: And it is given unto thee to know the set time of all the stars that are set to give light, until thou come near unto the throne of God.
pgp/abr/3.11: Thus I, Abraham, talked with the Lord, face to face, as one man talketh with another; and he told me of the works which his hands had made;
pgp/abr/3.12: And he said unto me: My son, my son (and his hand was stretched out), behold I will show you all these. And he put his hand upon mine eyes, and I saw those things which his hands had made, which were many; and they multiplied before mine eyes, and I could not see the end thereof.
pgp/abr/3.13: And he said unto me: This is Shinehah, which is the sun. And he said unto me: Kokob, which is star. And he said unto me: Olea, which is the moon. And he said unto me: Kokaubeam, which signifies stars, or all the great lights, which were in the firmament of heaven.
pgp/abr/3.14: And it was in the night time when the Lord spake these words unto me: I will multiply thee, and thy seed after thee, like unto these; and if thou canst count the number of sands, so shall be the number of thy seeds.
pgp/abr/3.15: And the Lord said unto me: Abraham, I show these things unto thee before ye go into Egypt, that ye may declare all these words.
pgp/abr/3.16: If two things exist, and there be one above the other, there shall be greater things above them; therefore Kolob is the greatest of all the Kokaubeam that thou hast seen, because it is nearest unto me.
pgp/abr/3.17: Now, if there be two things, one above the other, and the moon be above the earth, then it may be that a planet or a star may exist above it; and there is nothing that the Lord thy God shall take in his heart to do but what he will do it.
pgp/abr/3.18: Howbeit that he made the greater star; as, also, if there be two spirits, and one shall be more intelligent than the other, yet these two spirits, notwithstanding one is more intelligent than the other, have no beginning; they existed before, they shall have no end, they shall exist after, for they are gnolaum, or eternal.
pgp/abr/3.19: And the Lord said unto me: These two facts do exist, that there are two spirits, one being more intelligent than the other; there shall be another more intelligent than they; I am the Lord thy God, I am more intelligent than they all.
pgp/abr/3.20: The Lord thy God sent his angel to deliver thee from the hands of the priest of Elkenah.
pgp/abr/3.21: I dwell in the midst of them all; I now, therefore, have come down unto thee to declare unto thee the works which my hands have made, wherein my wisdom excelleth them all, for I rule in the heavens above, and in the earth beneath, in all wisdom and prudence, over all the intelligences thine eyes have seen from the beginning; I came down in the beginning in the midst of all the intelligences thou hast seen.
pgp/abr/3.22: Now the Lord had shown unto me, Abraham, the intelligences that were organized before the world was; and among all these there were many of the noble and great ones;
pgp/abr/3.23: And God saw these souls that they were good, and he stood in the midst of them, and he said: These I will make my rulers; for he stood among those that were spirits, and he saw that they were good; and he said unto me: Abraham, thou art one of them; thou wast chosen before thou wast born.
pgp/abr/3.24: And there stood one among them that was like unto God, and he said unto those who were with him: We will go down, for there is space there, and we will take of these materials, and we will make an earth whereon these may dwell;
pgp/abr/3.25: And we will prove them herewith, to see if they will do all things whatsoever the Lord their God shall command them;
pgp/abr/3.26: And they who keep their first estate shall be added upon; and they who keep not their first estate shall not have glory in the same kingdom with those who keep their first estate; and they who keep their second estate shall have glory added upon their heads for ever and ever.
pgp/abr/3.27: And the Lord said: Whom shall I send? And one answered like unto the Son of Man: Here am I, send me. And another answered and said: Here am I, send me. And the Lord said: I will send the first.
pgp/abr/3.28: And the second was angry, and kept not his first estate; and, at that day, many followed after him.

pgp/abr/4.1: And then the Lord said: Let us go down. And they went down at the beginning, and they, that is the Gods, organized and formed the heavens and the earth.
pgp/abr/4.2: And the earth, after it was formed, was empty and desolate, because they had not formed anything but the earth; and darkness reigned upon the face of the deep, and the Spirit of the Gods was brooding upon the face of the waters.
pgp/abr/4.3: And they (the Gods) said: Let there be light; and there was light.
pgp/abr/4.4: And they (the Gods) comprehended the light, for it was bright; and they divided the light, or caused it to be divided, from the darkness.
pgp/abr/4.5: And the Gods called the light Day, and the darkness they called Night. And it came to pass that from the evening until morning they called night; and from the morning until the evening they called day; and this was the first, or the beginning, of that which they called day and night.
pgp/abr/4.6: And the Gods also said: Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and it shall divide the waters from the waters.
pgp/abr/4.7: And the Gods ordered the expanse, so that it divided the waters which were under the expanse from the waters which were above the expanse; and it was so, even as they ordered.
pgp/abr/4.8: And the Gods called the expanse, Heaven. And it came to pass that it was from evening until morning that they called night; and it came to pass that it was from morning until evening that they called day; and this was the second time that they called night and day.
pgp/abr/4.9: And the Gods ordered, saying: Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the earth come up dry; and it was so as they ordered;
pgp/abr/4.10: And the Gods pronounced the dry land, Earth; and the gathering together of the waters, pronounced they, Great Waters; and the Gods saw that they were obeyed.
pgp/abr/4.11: And the Gods said: Let us prepare the earth to bring forth grass; the herb yielding seed; the fruit tree yielding fruit, after his kind, whose seed in itself yieldeth its own likeness upon the earth; and it was so, even as they ordered.
pgp/abr/4.12: And the Gods organized the earth to bring forth grass from its own seed, and the herb to bring forth herb from its own seed, yielding seed after his kind; and the earth to bring forth the tree from its own seed, yielding fruit, whose seed could only bring forth the same in itself, after his kind; and the Gods saw that they were obeyed.
pgp/abr/4.13: And it came to pass that they numbered the days; from the evening until the morning they called night; and it came to pass, from the morning until the evening they called day; and it was the third time.
pgp/abr/4.14: And the Gods organized the lights in the expanse of the heaven, and caused them to divide the day from the night; and organized them to be for signs and for seasons, and for days and for years;
pgp/abr/4.15: And organized them to be for lights in the expanse of the heaven to give light upon the earth; and it was so.
pgp/abr/4.16: And the Gods organized the two great lights, the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night; with the lesser light they set the stars also;
pgp/abr/4.17: And the Gods set them in the expanse of the heavens, to give light upon the earth, and to rule over the day and over the night, and to cause to divide the light from the darkness.
pgp/abr/4.18: And the Gods watched those things which they had ordered until they obeyed.
pgp/abr/4.19: And it came to pass that it was from evening until morning that it was night; and it came to pass that it was from morning until evening that it was day; and it was the fourth time.
pgp/abr/4.20: And the Gods said: Let us prepare the waters to bring forth abundantly the moving creatures that have life; and the fowl, that they may fly above the earth in the open expanse of heaven.
pgp/abr/4.21: And the Gods prepared the waters that they might bring forth great whales, and every living creature that moveth, which the waters were to bring forth abundantly after their kind; and every winged fowl after their kind. And the Gods saw that they would be obeyed, and that their plan was good.
pgp/abr/4.22: And the Gods said: We will bless them, and cause them to be fruitful and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas or great waters; and cause the fowl to multiply in the earth.
pgp/abr/4.23: And it came to pass that it was from evening until morning that they called night; and it came to pass that it was from morning until evening that they called day; and it was the fifth time.
pgp/abr/4.24: And the Gods prepared the earth to bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle and creeping things, and beasts of the earth after their kind; and it was so, as they had said.
pgp/abr/4.25: And the Gods organized the earth to bring forth the beasts after their kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after its kind; and the Gods saw they would obey.
pgp/abr/4.26: And the Gods took counsel among themselves and said: Let us go down and form man in our image, after our likeness; and we will give them dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.
pgp/abr/4.27: So the Gods went down to organize man in their own image, in the image of the Gods to form they him, male and female to form they them.
pgp/abr/4.28: And the Gods said: We will bless them. And the Gods said: We will cause them to be fruitful and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it, and to have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
pgp/abr/4.29: And the Gods said: Behold, we will give them every herb bearing seed that shall come upon the face of all the earth, and every tree which shall have fruit upon it; yea, the fruit of the tree yielding seed to them we will give it; it shall be for their meat.
